Physical Rename Rule

old_path -> proposed_path | classification | move_status | evidence

For gitlinks, also reconcile .gitmodules before moving.

000/03_overview_canonical_map.md

Overview Canonical Map

This map resolves the apparent duplication between /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ and /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/ without deleting or moving files.

Canonical Rule

  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ is the root overview index and short-entry reading spine.
  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/ is the deep overview source set.
  • If the same filename exists in both folders and content differs, keep both:
    • read /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/<name> first for orientation,
    • read /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/<name> for detailed analysis.
  • If content is identical, treat /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/<name> as the navigation copy and /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/<name> as the deep-overview location until a later link audit decides whether deduplication is safe.

000/00_co_overview.md

Codex Agent Engine Review Overview

This series is no longer a document that broadly explains â€œCan you create an MVP with Codex?â€‌ The purpose of this version is much narrower and more practical. The goal is to analyze on a code-by-code basis where and how we can extract the parts we actually need from the openai/codex repository, namely prompt assembly, context injection, tool execution loop, skill loading, and subagent collaboration.

If I were to reduce the conclusion I read this time to one sentence, it would be as follows. Codex is not a simple CLI, but a huge engine with prompt assembly + tool router + skill injector + app-server control plane + subagent runtime already separated. So, rather than â€œuse it as is,â€‌ a much more accurate approach is â€œselect only the necessary layers and reassemble them with our engine.â€‌

The core is five-pronged. First, base directives and developer directives are combined in different channels. Second, AGENTS.md and SKILL.md enter contextual user fragment rather than developer messages. Third, tool execution returns to the ToolRouter -> ToolRegistry -> handler structure, and dynamic tools and MCP tools converge on the same event layer. Fourth, loading and injection of skills are separated, and only explicit mentions lead to actual text injection. Fifth, a subagent is not a separate runtime, but a child thread that inherits the settings of the current turn.

This document bundle is now organized into an axis-specific folder structure. First, we look at the TUI and local client runtime in [[001_tui-design/00-overview]]. Next, see the order in which base instructions, developer instructions, AGENTS.md, skills, and plugin hints are mixed in [[002_prompt-context/00-overview]]. Next, see how the tool item called by the model flows to the actual handler in [[003_tool-runtime/00-overview]]. Next, in [[004_skill-system/00-overview]], the differences between skill root, trigger, explicit injection, and implicit invocation are summarized. In [[005_subagents-collab/00-overview]], we look at the inheritance structure and delegate bridge of the spawn_agent, send_input, and wait_agent series, and finally, in [[006_app-server-client-surfaces/00-overview]], we look at the app-server, protocol, and SDK surfaces.

Codebase size

The Codex Rust workspace (codex-rs/) is a large monorepo consisting of 83 crates. The main groups are:

group representative crate Quantity
Core runtime codex-core, codex-protocol, codex-config, codex-connectors 4
Tools & Execution codex-exec, codex-exec-server, codex-shell-command, codex-apply-patch 4
Skills & Plugins codex-skills, codex-plugins (core built-in) 2
Serverآ·Protocolآ·Client codex-app-server, codex-app-server-protocol, codex-app-server-client 3
MCP codex-mcp-server, codex-rmcp-client 2
CLI & TUI codex-cli, codex-tui, codex-tui-app-server 3
model backend codex-backend-client, codex-lmstudio, codex-ollama, codex-chatgpt 4
Securityآ·Sandbox codex-sandboxing, codex-linux-sandbox, codex-windows-sandbox, codex-execpolicy 4+
utilities (utils/*) codex-utils-path, codex-utils-image, codex-utils-pty, etc. 19
Infrastructureآ·Others codex-otel, codex-rollout, codex-hooks, codex-features, etc. 38+

There are 43 pub modules declared in the src/ directory of the single codex-core crate.

turn processing sequence

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى‍…ë ¥

    end
    Registry-->>Router: AnyToolResult
    Router-->>Session: tool output ë°کي™ک

technical reference

Core structures and source locations

The table below summarizes the structures that form the backbone of the Codex engine and the corresponding source files.

struct file Role
Session core/src/codex.rs Main Orchestrator. Controls the entire flow from turn input to output
ToolRouter core/src/tools/router.rs Route tool calls requested by the model to the appropriate handler
ToolRegistry core/src/tools/registry.rs Centrally register and manage specs and handlers of all tools
SkillsManager core/src/skills/manager.rs Manages skill navigation, loading, caching, and injection
PluginsManager core/src/plugins/manager.rs Manages plugin marketplace, installation, and synchronization
ModelsManager core/src/models_manager/manager.rs Manage model presets, settings, and collaboration modes
McpManager core/src/mcp/mod.rs Manages MCP server connection, authentication, and tool registration
AgentControl core/src/agent/control.rs Subagent spawnآ·sendآ·waitآ·close life stock

000/00_harness-index.md (excerpt)

Harness Source Index

This file is the non-conflicting 000/ index for source-repository harnesses.

The canonical deep overview files stay under /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/. Files in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000 named 00_harness-index_* are short routing/index notes only. They intentionally avoid the canonical 00_<prefix>_overview.md basename so wiki/backlink indexers do not see two files with the same 00_*_overview identity.

Routing

Prefix Index stub Canonical overview Classification
hermes /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_hermes.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_hermes_overview.md standalone source-based agent runtime
omc /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_omc.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_omc_overview.md Claude Code host-dependent orchestration harness
omx /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_omx.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_omx_overview.md Codex host-dependent control plane
omo /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_omo.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_omo_overview.md OpenCode host-dependent harness plus Codex Light adapter
lc n/a /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_lc_overview.md LazyCodex / Codex Light distribution wrapper for OmO
sp n/a /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_sp_overview.md Superpowers multi-harness skills/bootstrap methodology layer

000/01_corpus_navigation_map.md

Corpus Navigation Map

This repository is a comparative research corpus for coding agents and harnesses. Use this map to decide where to read first without physically moving existing data.

For reversible old-to-new bucket proposals, see /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/02_corpus_move_candidates.md.

For the canonical relationship between /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ and /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/, see /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/03_overview_canonical_map.md.

Physical Rename Rule

Before a folder is moved, add an explicit mapping row using:

old_path -> proposed_path | classification | move_status | evidence

For gitlinks, also reconcile .gitmodules before moving.

Top-Level Triage

Use this when scanning the root directory. Status is intentionally conservative: keep-current means the current path remains the source of truth for now.

Current Path Triage Status
`/Users/jun/Developer/codex/.agen

000/03_overview_canonical_map.md

Overview Canonical Map

This map resolves the apparent duplication between /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ and /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/ without deleting or moving files.

Canonical Rule

  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ is the root overview index and short-entry reading spine.
  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/ is the deep overview source set.
  • If the same filename exists in both folders and content differs, keep both:
    • read /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/<name> first for orientation,
    • read /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/<name> for detailed analysis.
  • If content is identical, treat /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/<name> as the navigation copy and /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/<name> as the deep-overview location until a later link audit decides whether deduplication is safe.

Paired Overview Files

Filename /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/ bytes /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ bytes Equal Rule
00_ag_overview.md 27224 15790 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_ai_overview.md 25716 25716 yes duplicate content; defer dedupe until link audit
00_ay_overview.md 3815 798 no keep both: orientation plus detail

ي„´ ى²کë¦¬ ى‹œي€€ىٹ¤

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى‍…ë ¥
    participant Session as Session (codex.rs)
    participant Skills as SkillsManager
    participant Plugins as PluginsManager
    participant Router as ToolRouter
    participant Registry as ToolRegistry
    participant Handler as handler (37ى¢…)
    participant Agent as AgentControl
    participant Exec as UnifiedExecRuntime

    User->>Session: turn input
    Session->>Skills: load_skills_from_roots â†’ injection
    Session->>Plugins: startup_sync â†’ injection
    Session->>Router: model tool call dispatch
    Router->>Registry: resolve handler by name
    alt shell آ· apply_patch آ· unified_exec
        Registry->>Exec: ShellRuntime / ApplyPatchRuntime / UnifiedExecRuntime
        Exec-->>Registry: ToolOutput
    else mcp tool
        Registry->>Handler: mcp.rs â†’ McpManager
        Handler-->>Registry: ToolOutput
    else subagent tool
        Registry->>Agent: spawn_agent / send_input / wait_agent
        Agent-->>Session: ي•کىœ„ thread ىƒپيƒœى™€ ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ë°کي™ک
        Agent-->>Registry: ToolOutput
    end
    Registry-->>Router: AnyToolResult
    Router-->>Session: tool output ë°کي™ک

000/00_harness-index.md (ë°œى·Œ)

Harness Source Index

This file is the non-conflicting 000/ index for source-repository harnesses.

The canonical deep overview files stay under /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/. Files in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000 named 00_harness-index_* are short routing/index notes only. They intentionally avoid the canonical 00_<prefix>_overview.md basename so wiki/backlink indexers do not see two files with the same 00_*_overview identity.

Routing

Prefix Index stub Canonical overview Classification
hermes /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_hermes.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_hermes_overview.md standalone source-based agent runtime
omc /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_omc.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_omc_overview.md Claude Code host-dependent orchestration harness

// 120_codex-cli/src/main.rs (ى‍¬êµ¬ى„±)
fn main() {
    // 1. ëھ…ë ¹ى¤„ ى‌¸ى‍گ يŒŒى‹± (clap ê¸°ë°ک)
    let args = Cli::parse();
    
    // 2. ى„¤ى • ë،œë“œ
    //    ~/.codex/config.toml â†’ ى „ى—­
    //    .codex/config.toml â†’ ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸
    //    ي”„ë،œي•„ â†’ dev/qa/prod ë¶„ê¸°
    let config = Config::load(&args)?;
    
    // 3. ي”¼ى²ک ي”Œë‍کê·¸ ى´ˆê¸°ي™” (50+ ي”¼ى²ک)
    //    FeatureStage: UnderDevelopment â†’ Experimental â†’ Preview â†’ Stable â†’ Removed
    let features = Features::from_config(&config);
    features.normalize_dependencies(); // ى‌کى،´ى„± ى‍گëڈ™ ي™œى„±ي™”
    
    // 4. OTel ي…”ë ˆë©”يٹ¸ë¦¬ ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
    //    trace_safe vs log_only يƒ€ê²ں ë¶„ë¦¬
    //    Statsig OTLP HTTP ى—”ë“œيڈ¬ى‌¸يٹ¸
    let otel = init_otel(&config)?;
    
    // 5. ى‌¸ى¦‌ ي™•ى‌¸
    //    ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„: CODEX_API_KEY > OPENAI_API_KEY > ChatGPT OAuth > AuthTokens
    //    OS ي‚¤ى²´ى‌¸ ي†µي•© (keyring-store يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸)
    let auth = resolve_auth(&config)?;
    
    // 6. Session ىƒ‌ى„±
    let session = Session::new(config, auth, features)?;
    
    // 7. TUI ë Œëچ”ë§پ ى‹œى‍‘ (React/Ink)
    tui::run(session)?;
}

README / docs

// core/src/codex.rs (ى‍¬êµ¬ى„±)
pub struct Session {
    // ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ
    tool_router: ToolRouter,          // ëڈ„êµ¬ ë‌¼ىڑ°يŒ… (router.rs)
    
    // ىٹ¤ي‚¬آ·ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸
    skills_manager: Arc<SkillsManager>,    // ىٹ¤ي‚¬ يƒگىƒ‰آ·ë،œë”©آ·ى؛گى‹±
    plugins_manager: Arc<PluginsManager>,  // ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ ë§ˆى¼“ي”Œë ˆى‌´ىٹ¤
    
    // ëھ¨ëچ¸آ·MCP
    models_manager: ModelsManager,         // ëھ¨ëچ¸ ي”„ë¦¬ى…‹آ·يک‘ى—… ëھ¨ë“œ
    mcp_manager: McpManager,               // MCP ى„œë²„ ى—°ê²°آ·ى‌¸ى¦‌
    
    // ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸
    agent_control: AgentControl,           // ى„œë¸Œى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ëھ…ى£¼ê¸°
    agent_registry: AgentRegistry,         // ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ى—­ي•  ë“±ë،‌
    
    // ى‹¤ي–‰ ي™کê²½
    unified_exec: UnifiedExecRuntime,      // ي†µي•© ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ê´€ë¦¬
    sandboxing: SandboxPolicy,             // OS-level ê²©ë¦¬
    
    // ىƒپيƒœ
    threads: HashMap<ThreadId, Thread>,    // ëŒ€ي™” ى“°ë ˆë“œ
    event_tx: async_channel::Sender<ThreadEvent>,  // ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ى±„ë„گ
}

  4. McpManager ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
     â†’ ى„¤ى •ى—گى„œ MCP ى„œë²„ ëھ©ë،‌ ë،œë“œ
     â†’ ê°پ ى„œë²„ى—گ stdio ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ى‹œى‍‘
     â†’ MCP ي”„ë،œي† ى½œ ى´ˆê¸°ي™” (initialize â†’ tools/list)
     â†’ auth.rs: ى‌¸ى¦‌ ىƒپيƒœ ê´€ë¦¬
     â†’ skill_dependencies.rs: ىٹ¤ي‚¬ MCP ى‌کى،´ى„± ي•´ê²°
  5. ModelsManager ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
     â†’ model_presets.rs: ëھ¨ëچ¸ ي”„ë¦¬ى…‹ ë،œë“œ
     â†’ cache.rs: ëھ¨ëچ¸ ى •ë³´ ى؛گى‹±
  6. ToolRouter::from_config()
     â†’ build_specs_with_discoverable_tools()
     â†’ ë‚´ى‍¥ ëڈ„êµ¬ + MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ + app ëڈ„êµ¬ + ëڈ™ى پ ëڈ„êµ¬ ي†µي•©
     â†’ (specs, registry) = builder.build()
  7. AgentControl + AgentRegistry ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
     â†’ builtins/awaiter.toml ë“± ë¹Œيٹ¸ى‌¸ ى—­ي•  ë،œë“œ
  8. UnifiedExecRuntime ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
     â†’ process_manager.rs: ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ي’€
     â†’ async_watcher.rs: ë¹„ëڈ™ê¸° ى¶œë ¥ ê°گى‹œ
     â†’ head_tail_buffer.rs: ى¶œë ¥ ë²„يچ¼
  9. SandboxPolicy ë،œë“œ
     â†’ OSë³„: Seatbelt (macOS), Landlock (Linux)

README / docs

  ى„œë²„ â†گ {"event": "tool_call_start", "data": {"name": "read_file", ...}}
  ى„œë²„ â†گ {"event": "tool_call_output", "data": {"output": "..."}}

SessionSource enum:
  VSCode       â†’ VS Code ي™•ى‍¥ى—گى„œ ى—°ê²°
  Atlas        â†’ Atlas يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ى—گى„œ ى—°ê²°

Main analysis

1. System startup

1. System startup

1.1 CLI entry point

Codex CLI is a native binary written in Rust:

// 120_codex-cli/src/main.rs (ى‍¬êµ¬ى„±)
fn main() {
    // 1. ëھ…ë ¹ى¤„ ى‌¸ى‍گ يŒŒى‹± (clap ê¸°ë°ک)
    let args = Cli::parse();
    
    // 2. ى„¤ى • ë،œë“œ
    //    ~/.codex/config.toml â†’ ى „ى—­
    //    .codex/config.toml â†’ ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸
    //    ي”„ë،œي•„ â†’ dev/qa/prod ë¶„ê¸°
    let config = Config::load(&args)?;
    
    // 3. ي”¼ى²ک ي”Œë‍کê·¸ ى´ˆê¸°ي™” (50+ ي”¼ى²ک)
    //    FeatureStage: UnderDevelopment â†’ Experimental â†’ Preview â†’ Stable â†’ Removed
    let features = Features::from_config(&config);
    features.normalize_dependencies(); // ى‌کى،´ى„± ى‍گëڈ™ ي™œى„±ي™”
    
    // 4. OTel ي…”ë ˆë©”يٹ¸ë¦¬ ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
    //    trace_safe vs log_only يƒ€ê²ں ë¶„ë¦¬
    //    Statsig OTLP HTTP ى—”ë“œيڈ¬ى‌¸يٹ¸
    let otel = init_otel(&config)?;
    
    // 5. ى‌¸ى¦‌ ي™•ى‌¸
    //    ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„: CODEX_API_KEY > OPENAI_API_KEY > ChatGPT OAuth > AuthTokens
    //    OS ي‚¤ى²´ى‌¸ ي†µي•© (keyring-store يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸)
    let auth = resolve_auth(&config)?;
    
    // 6. Session ىƒ‌ى„±
    let session = Session::new(config, auth, features)?;
    
    // 7. TUI ë Œëچ”ë§پ ى‹œى‍‘ (React/Ink)
    tui::run(session)?;
}

1.2 Session initialization

Session is the central orchestrator of Codex. Located at the top of the 43 pub modules:

// core/src/codex.rs (ى‍¬êµ¬ى„±)
pub struct Session {
    // ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ
    tool_router: ToolRouter,          // ëڈ„êµ¬ ë‌¼ىڑ°يŒ… (router.rs)
    
    // ىٹ¤ي‚¬آ·ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸
    skills_manager: Arc<SkillsManager>,    // ىٹ¤ي‚¬ يƒگىƒ‰آ·ë،œë”©آ·ى؛گى‹±
    plugins_manager: Arc<PluginsManager>,  // ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ ë§ˆى¼“ي”Œë ˆى‌´ىٹ¤
    
    // ëھ¨ëچ¸آ·MCP
    models_manager: ModelsManager,         // ëھ¨ëچ¸ ي”„ë¦¬ى…‹آ·يک‘ى—… ëھ¨ë“œ
    mcp_manager: McpManager,               // MCP ى„œë²„ ى—°ê²°آ·ى‌¸ى¦‌
    
    // ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸
    agent_control: AgentControl,           // ى„œë¸Œى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ëھ…ى£¼ê¸°
    agent_registry: AgentRegistry,         // ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ى—­ي•  ë“±ë،‌
    
    // ى‹¤ي–‰ ي™کê²½
    unified_exec: UnifiedExecRuntime,      // ي†µي•© ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ê´€ë¦¬
    sandboxing: SandboxPolicy,             // OS-level ê²©ë¦¬
    
    // ىƒپيƒœ
    threads: HashMap<ThreadId, Thread>,    // ëŒ€ي™” ى“°ë ˆë“œ
    event_tx: async_channel::Sender<ThreadEvent>,  // ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ى±„ë„گ
}

Session initialization sequence:

Session::new():
  1. Config ىœ يڑ¨ى„± ê²€ى¦‌
  2. PluginsManager::startup_sync()
     â†’ ى›گê²© ë§ˆى¼“ي”Œë ˆى‌´ىٹ¤ى™€ ëڈ™ê¸°ي™”
     â†’ ى„¤ى¹کëگœ ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ ë،œë“œ
     â†’ discoverable ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ ë“±ë،‌
  3. SkillsManager ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
     â†’ ىٹ¤ي‚¬ ë£¨يٹ¸ يƒگىƒ‰: ~/.codex/skills/, .agents/skills/
     â†’ ê°پ SKILL.md frontmatter يŒŒى‹±
     â†’ ى‌´ë¦„+ى„¤ëھ… ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤ ىƒ‌ى„±
  4. McpManager ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
     â†’ ى„¤ى •ى—گى„œ MCP ى„œë²„ ëھ©ë،‌ ë،œë“œ
     â†’ ê°پ ى„œë²„ى—گ stdio ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ى‹œى‍‘
     â†’ MCP ي”„ë،œي† ى½œ ى´ˆê¸°ي™” (initialize â†’ tools/list)
     â†’ auth.rs: ى‌¸ى¦‌ ىƒپيƒœ ê´€ë¦¬
     â†’ skill_dependencies.rs: ىٹ¤ي‚¬ MCP ى‌کى،´ى„± ي•´ê²°
  5. ModelsManager ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
     â†’ model_presets.rs: ëھ¨ëچ¸ ي”„ë¦¬ى…‹ ë،œë“œ
     â†’ cache.rs: ëھ¨ëچ¸ ى •ë³´ ى؛گى‹±
  6. ToolRouter::from_config()
     â†’ build_specs_with_discoverable_tools()
     â†’ ë‚´ى‍¥ ëڈ„êµ¬ + MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ + app ëڈ„êµ¬ + ëڈ™ى پ ëڈ„êµ¬ ي†µي•©
     â†’ (specs, registry) = builder.build()
  7. AgentControl + AgentRegistry ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
     â†’ builtins/awaiter.toml ë“± ë¹Œيٹ¸ى‌¸ ى—­ي•  ë،œë“œ
  8. UnifiedExecRuntime ى´ˆê¸°ي™”
     â†’ process_manager.rs: ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ي’€
     â†’ async_watcher.rs: ë¹„ëڈ™ê¸° ى¶œë ¥ ê°گى‹œ

1.3 App-Server mode

In addition to CLI, communicate with external clients using the App-Server Protocol:

Overview

Main analysis

2. Prompt assembly

2. Prompt assembly

Overview

Overview آ§3 â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • sequenceDiagram
  • participant Session as Session (codex.rs)
  • participant Config as ToolsConfig
  • participant Router as ToolRouter (router.rs)
  • participant Builder as SpecsBuilder
  • participant Registry as ToolRegistry (registry.rs)
  • participant Handler as Handler (37ى¢…+)
  • participant Runtime as Runtime (3ى¢…)
  • Note over Session,Config: â”€â”€ ى´ˆê¸°ي™” ë‹¨ê³„ â”€â”€
  • Session->>Config: ëڈ„êµ¬ ى„¤ى • ë،œë“œ
  • Config->>Builder: build_specs_with_discoverable_tools()
  • Builder->>Builder: ë‚´ى‍¥ ëڈ„êµ¬ spec ىƒ‌ى„±

    Builder->>Builder: ë‚´ى‍¥ ëڈ„êµ¬ spec ىƒ‌ى„±
    Builder->>Builder: MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ spec ى¶”ê°€
    Builder->>Builder: App ëڈ„êµ¬ spec ى¶”ê°€
    Builder->>Builder: ëڈ™ى پ(discoverable) ëڈ„êµ¬ spec ى¶”ê°€
    Builder-->>Router: (specs, registry) = build()
    
    Note over Session,Handler: â”€â”€ ى‹¤ي–‰ ë‹¨ê³„ â”€â”€
    Session->>Router: modelى‌´ ë°کي™کي•œ tool call
    Router->>Router: tool nameىœ¼ë،œ spec ى،°يڑŒ
    Router->>Registry: resolve handler
    Registry->>Handler: handler.call(invocation)
    
    alt Shell ëڈ„êµ¬ (shell.rs)
        Handler->>Runtime: ShellRuntime.execute(command, cwd)
        Runtime->>Runtime: sandbox ى •ى±… ي™•ى‌¸
        Runtime->>Runtime: pty ى„¸ى…کى—گى„œ ى‹¤ي–‰
        Runtime-->>Handler: stdout + stderr + exit_code
    end
    
    alt يŒŒى‌¼ ëڈ„êµ¬ (apply_patch.rs)
        Handler->>Runtime: ApplyPatchRuntime.apply(patch)
        Runtime->>Runtime: Ghost Commit ى²´يپ¬يڈ¬ى‌¸يٹ¸
        Runtime->>Runtime: git apply --3way
        Runtime-->>Handler: ى پىڑ© ê²°ê³¼
    end
    
    alt ي†µي•© ى‹¤ي–‰ (unified_exec/)
        Handler->>Runtime: UnifiedExecRuntime.run(spec)
        Runtime->>Runtime: process_manager.spawn()
        Runtime->>Runtime: async_watcher.monitor()
        Runtime-->>Handler: ى‹¤ي–‰ ê²°ê³¼
    end
    
    alt MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ (mcp.rs)
        Handler->>Runtime: McpManager.call_tool(server, tool, args)
        Runtime-->>Handler: MCP ê²°ê³¼
    end
    
    alt ى„œë¸Œى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ (multi_agents/)
        Handler->>Runtime: AgentControl.spawn_agent(role, config)
        Runtime-->>Handler: ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ê²°ê³¼
    end
    
    Handler-->>Registry: ToolOutput (يƒ€ى‍…ë³„)
    Registry-->>Router: AnyToolResult
    Router-->>Session: ê²°ê³¼ â†’ ëŒ€ي™” ى‌´ë ¥ى—گ ى¶”ê°€

// core/src/tools/registry.rs
#[async_trait]
pub trait ToolHandler: Send + Sync {
    type Output: ToolOutput + 'static;
    
    /// ëڈ„êµ¬ê°€ Functionى‌¸ى§€ MCPى‌¸ى§€

    /// ى‹¤ى œ ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‹¤ي–‰

Main analysis

3. Tool routing and execution

3. Tool routing and execution

3.1 Three-stage dispatch pipeline

sequenceDiagram
    participant Session as Session (codex.rs)
    participant Config as ToolsConfig
    participant Router as ToolRouter (router.rs)
    participant Builder as SpecsBuilder
    participant Registry as ToolRegistry (registry.rs)
    participant Handler as Handler (37ى¢…+)
    participant Runtime as Runtime (3ى¢…)

    Builder-->>Router: (specs, registry) = build()
    
    Note over Session,Handler: â”€â”€ ى‹¤ي–‰ ë‹¨ê³„ â”€â”€
    Session->>Router: modelى‌´ ë°کي™کي•œ tool call
    Router->>Router: tool nameىœ¼ë،œ spec ى،°يڑŒ
    Router->>Registry: resolve handler
    Registry->>Handler: handler.call(invocation)

        Runtime->>Runtime: sandbox ى •ى±… ي™•ى‌¸
        Runtime->>Runtime: pty ى„¸ى…کى—گى„œ ى‹¤ي–‰

    alt يŒŒى‌¼ ëڈ„êµ¬ (apply_patch.rs)

3.2 ToolHandler trait

Common interface for all tool handlers:

// core/src/tools/registry.rs
#[async_trait]
pub trait ToolHandler: Send + Sync {
    type Output: ToolOutput + 'static;
    
    /// ëڈ„êµ¬ê°€ Functionى‌¸ى§€ MCPى‌¸ى§€
    fn kind(&self) -> ToolKind;
    
    /// يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œى‌„ ىˆکى •ي•کëٹ” ëڈ„êµ¬ى‌¸ى§€ يŒگى •
    /// trueى‌´ë©´ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى „ Ghost Commit ى²´يپ¬يڈ¬ى‌¸يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ى„±
    async fn is_mutating(&self, invocation: &ToolInvocation) -> bool;
    
    /// ى‹¤ى œ ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‹¤ي–‰
    async fn call(&self, invocation: ToolInvocation) -> Result<Self::Output>;
}

Why the is_mutating() test is important:

  • true â†’ Unconditionally create checkpoint with Ghost Commit
  • Error during execution â†’ Safely restore to checkpoint
  • User can restore directly with /undo command

3.2 ToolHandler trait

ëھ¨ë“  ëڈ„êµ¬ handlerى‌ک ê³µي†µ ى‌¸ي„°يژکى‌´ىٹ¤:

// core/src/tools/registry.rs
#[async_trait]
pub trait ToolHandler: Send + Sync {
    type Output: ToolOutput + 'static;
    
    /// ëڈ„êµ¬ê°€ Functionى‌¸ى§€ MCPى‌¸ى§€
    fn kind(&self) -> ToolKind;
    
    /// يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œى‌„ ىˆکى •ي•کëٹ” ëڈ„êµ¬ى‌¸ى§€ يŒگى •
    /// trueى‌´ë©´ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى „ Ghost Commit ى²´يپ¬يڈ¬ى‌¸يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ى„±
    async fn is_mutating(&self, invocation: &ToolInvocation) -> bool;
    
    /// ى‹¤ى œ ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‹¤ي–‰
    async fn call(&self, invocation: ToolInvocation) -> Result<Self::Output>;
}

is_mutating() يŒگى •ى‌´ ى¤‘ىڑ”ي•œ ى‌´ىœ :

  • true â†’ Ghost Commitىœ¼ë،œ ë¬´ى،°ê±´ ى²´يپ¬يڈ¬ى‌¸يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ى„±

[filesystem]
read_allow = ["/usr", "/etc", "${CWD}"]
write_allow = ["${CWD}", "/tmp"]
deny = ["/etc/shadow", "${HOME}/.ssh"]

[network]
mode = "deny_all"  # ê¸°ë³¸: ى™„ى „ ى°¨ë‹¨

Main analysis

4. Run sandbox

4. Run sandbox

4.1 OS-level isolation mechanism

Codex provides real OS kernel level isolation:

â”Œâ”€â”€ macOS: Seatbelt â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”گ

â”‚  ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ى‹¤ي–‰: ي—ˆىڑ© ë°”ى‌´ë„ˆë¦¬ ëھ©ë،‌                        â”‚

4.2 exec-policy (TOML)

# ى‹¤ي–‰ ى •ى±… ىکˆى‹œ
[filesystem]
read_allow = ["/usr", "/etc", "${CWD}"]
write_allow = ["${CWD}", "/tmp"]
deny = ["/etc/shadow", "${HOME}/.ssh"]

[network]
mode = "deny_all"  # ê¸°ë³¸: ى™„ى „ ى°¨ë‹¨
allow = []          # ي•„ىڑ” ى‹œ ëڈ„ë©”ى‌¸/IP ي™”ى‌´يٹ¸ë¦¬ىٹ¤يٹ¸

[process]
allow = ["git", "node", "python", "cargo", "make"]
deny = ["rm", "dd", "mkfs", "fdisk"]

[starlark]
# Starlark ىٹ¤يپ¬ë¦½يٹ¸ë،œ ëچ” ى„¸ë°€ي•œ ى •ى±… ى •ى‌ک ê°€ëٹ¥
script = "policy.star"

Ghost Commit ى „ى²´ ي‌گë¦„:

  1. ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ي„´ ى‹œى‍‘
     â†’ يک„ى‍¬ ى‍‘ى—… يٹ¸ë¦¬ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ· â†’ git commit (ghost)
     â†’ ghost commitى‌€ ى‌¼ë°ک git logى—گ ي‘œى‹œëگکى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ

  2. mutating ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى „
     â†’ is_mutating() = true ي™•ى‌¸
     â†’ ى¶”ê°€ ghost commit ىƒ‌ى„±

  3. ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‹¤ي–‰
     â†’ يŒŒى‌¼ ىˆکى •, ëھ…ë ¹ ى‹¤ي–‰ ë“±

  4. ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ي„´ ى¢…ë£Œ
     â†’ ىµœى¢… ghost commit

  5. /undo ىڑ”ى²­ ى‹œ
     â†’ ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ ghost commitىœ¼ë،œ ى‍‘ى—… يٹ¸ë¦¬ ë³µى›گ
     â†’ git reset --hard <ghost_commit_sha>

Ghost Commitى‌ک ى‍¥ى گ:
  - ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ى‌ک ى‌¼ë°ک git ى‌´ë ¥ى—گ ë‚کيƒ€ë‚کى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ
  - ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ي™œëڈ™ ى „ى²´ë¥¼ ى¶”ى پ ê°€ëٹ¥

ىڑ”ى²­:
  POST https://api.openai.com/v1/responses

5.2 Responses API single wire

Codex uses only the OpenAI Responses API. The Chat Completions API has been completely removed:

ىڑ”ى²­:
  POST https://api.openai.com/v1/responses
  {
    "model": "codex-mini-latest",
    "input": [
      {"role": "system", "content": "..."},
      {"role": "user", "content": "Fix the auth bug"},
      {"role": "assistant", "content": [
        {"type": "function_call", "name": "read_file", ...},
      ]},
      {"type": "function_call_output", "call_id": "...", "output": "..."},
    ],
    "tools": [...],
    "stream": true
  }

ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ى‌‘ë‹µ (SSE):
  data: {"type": "response.output_item.added", ...}
  data: {"type": "response.content_part.added", ...}
  data: {"type": "response.output_text.delta", "delta": "Let me..."}
  data: {"type": "response.function_call_arguments.delta", ...}
  data: {"type": "response.output_item.done", ...}
  data: {"type": "response.done", ...}

ى‌´ ى„¤ê³„ى‌ک ى‌کë¯¸:
  - Chat APIى‌ک messages ë°°ى—´ ëŒ€ى‹  input ë°°ى—´ ى‚¬ىڑ©
  - function_callê³¼ function_call_outputى‌´ inputى—گ ى§پى ‘ يڈ¬ي•¨
  - ëŒ€ي™” ى‌´ë ¥ ê´€ë¦¬ê°€ يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ ى¸،ى—گى„œ ëچ” ىœ ى—°

README / docs

v1 API (core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/):
  spawn_agent(role, instructions)
    â†’ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ى„±, ID ë°کي™ک

v2 API (core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents_v2/):

    â†’ v1ê³¼ ىœ ى‚¬ي•کë‚ک ي™•ى‍¥ëگœ ى„¤ى •

  send_message(agent_id, message)
    â†’ ى‍گىœ يک• ë©”ى‹œى§€ (ى‍‘ى—…ê³¼ ë³„ëڈ„)

    â†’ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ى‌ک يٹ¹ى • ëڈ„êµ¬ë¥¼ ى§پى ‘ يک¸ى¶œ
  list_agents()
    â†’ يک„ى‍¬ ي™œى„± ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ëھ©ë،‌ ë°کي™ک

v2ى‌ک ي•µى‹¬ ى°¨ى‌´:

Main analysis

6. Subagent system

6. Subagent system

6.1 Thread inheritance structure

A subagent is not an independent process, but a sub-thread that inherits the settings of the current Session:

AgentControl.spawn_agent(role, config):
  1. AgentRegistryى—گى„œ ى—­ي• (role) ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„° ى،°يڑŒ
     â†’ builtins/awaiter.toml: ëŒ€ê¸°ى‍گ ى—­ي• 
     â†’ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى •ى‌ک roles/
  2. ë¶€ëھ¨ Sessionى‌ک ى„¤ى • ë³µى œ
     â†’ ëڈ„êµ¬ ى œي•œ (ى—­ي• ë³„ ي—ˆىڑ© ëڈ„êµ¬ ëھ©ë،‌)
     â†’ ëھ¨ëچ¸ ى„¤ى • (ى—­ي• ë³„ ëھ¨ëچ¸ ى§€ى • ê°€ëٹ¥)
     â†’ sandbox ى •ى±… ىƒپى†چ
  3. ë³„ëڈ„ threadى—گى„œ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ë£¨ي”„ ى‹¤ي–‰
     â†’ ى‍گى²´ ëŒ€ي™” ى‌´ë ¥ ê´€ë¦¬
     â†’ ى‍گى²´ ëڈ„êµ¬ يک¸ى¶œ ê°€ëٹ¥
  4. ë¶€ëھ¨ threadى™€ ى±„ë„گ ي†µى‹ 
     â†’ send_input: ë¶€ëھ¨ â†’ ى‍گى‹‌ ë©”ى‹œى§€
     â†’ ê²°ê³¼ ë°کي™ک: ى‍گى‹‌ â†’ ë¶€ëھ¨

// codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs
pub enum FeatureStage {
    UnderDevelopment,  // ê°œë°œ ى´ˆê¸° â€” ë‚´ë¶€ ê°œë°œى‍گë§Œ
    Experimental,      // ى‹¤ي—کى پ â€” ىکµيٹ¸ى‌¸ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ
    Preview,           // ë¯¸ë¦¬ë³´ê¸° â€” ë„“ى‌€ ë² يƒ€
    Stable,            // ى•ˆى • â€” ى „ى²´ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ
    Removed,           // ى œê±°ëگ¨ â€” ë ˆê±°ى‹œ يک¸ي™کىڑ© ىٹ¤ي…پ
}

// 50+ ي”¼ى²ک ىکˆى‹œ:
//   SpawnCsv          â†’ CSV ê¸°ë°ک ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ى„±
//   Collab            â†’ يک‘ى—… ëھ¨ë“œ
//   MultiAgentsV2     â†’ ى°¨ى„¸ëŒ€ ë©€ي‹°ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸
//   RealtimeConv      â†’ ى‹¤ى‹œê°„ ëŒ€ي™” (audio)
//   McpAuth           â†’ MCP ى‌¸ى¦‌
//   AppServer         â†’ App-Server ëھ¨ë“œ
//   StreamingExec     â†’ ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ى‹¤ي–‰

README / docs

fn normalize_dependencies(features: &mut FeatureSet) {
    // SpawnCsv ي™œى„±ي™” â†’ Collab ى‍گëڈ™ ي™œى„±ي™”
    if features.is_enabled(SpawnCsv) {
        features.enable(Collab);
    }
    // MultiAgentsV2 ي™œى„±ي™” â†’ ê´€ë ¨ ê¸°ëٹ¥ ى‍گëڈ™ ي™œى„±ي™”

Main analysis

8. Feature flags and settings

8. Feature flags and settings

8.1 5-step stage management

// codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs
pub enum FeatureStage {
    UnderDevelopment,  // ê°œë°œ ى´ˆê¸° â€” ë‚´ë¶€ ê°œë°œى‍گë§Œ
    Experimental,      // ى‹¤ي—کى پ â€” ىکµيٹ¸ى‌¸ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ
    Preview,           // ë¯¸ë¦¬ë³´ê¸° â€” ë„“ى‌€ ë² يƒ€
    Stable,            // ى•ˆى • â€” ى „ى²´ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ
    Removed,           // ى œê±°ëگ¨ â€” ë ˆê±°ى‹œ يک¸ي™کىڑ© ىٹ¤ي…پ
}

// 50+ ي”¼ى²ک ىکˆى‹œ:
//   SpawnCsv          â†’ CSV ê¸°ë°ک ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ى„±
//   Collab            â†’ يک‘ى—… ëھ¨ë“œ
//   MultiAgentsV2     â†’ ى°¨ى„¸ëŒ€ ë©€ي‹°ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸

//   AppServer         â†’ App-Server ëھ¨ë“œ
//   StreamingExec     â†’ ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ى‹¤ي–‰

8.2 Dependency normalization

fn normalize_dependencies(features: &mut FeatureSet) {
    // SpawnCsv ي™œى„±ي™” â†’ Collab ى‍گëڈ™ ي™œى„±ي™”
    if features.is_enabled(SpawnCsv) {
        features.enable(Collab);
    }
    // MultiAgentsV2 ي™œى„±ي™” â†’ ê´€ë ¨ ê¸°ëٹ¥ ى‍گëڈ™ ي™œى„±ي™”
    // ...
}

// core/src/error.rs
pub enum CodexErr {
    // API ى—گëں¬
    ContextWindowExceeded { model: String, limit: usize },
    UsageLimitReached { plan: Plan, message: String },
    ApiError { status: u16, body: String },
    NetworkError { source: reqwest::Error },
    
    // ى‹¤ي–‰ ى—گëں¬
    SandboxViolation { path: String, action: String },
    ToolExecutionFailed { tool: String, error: String },
    PatchApplyFailed { file: String, hunks_failed: usize },
    
    // ى‌¸ى¦‌ ى—گëں¬
    AuthenticationFailed { provider: String },
    InvalidApiKey { key_prefix: String },
    
    // ë¦¬ى†Œىٹ¤ ى—گëں¬
    FileNotFound { path: PathBuf },
    PermissionDenied { path: PathBuf },
    
    // ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ى—گëں¬
    SubagentTimeout { agent_id: String, duration: Duration },
    SubagentFailed { agent_id: String, error: String },
    
    // MCP ى—گëں¬
    McpServerUnreachable { server: String },
    McpToolFailed { server: String, tool: String },
    
    // + ى•½ 5ê°œ ى¶”ê°€
}

  is_retryable() يŒگى •:
    â†’ 429 Rate Limit â†’ ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„ (ى§€ىˆک ë°±ىک¤ي”„)

    â†’ ECONNRESET, ETIMEDOUT â†’ ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„

Source code snippets

README / docs

graph TD
    subgraph Surface["ي‘œë©´ ê³„ى¸µ"]
        TUI["codex-tui<br/>(React/Ink)"]
        CLI["codex-cli<br/>(clap)"]
        AppServer["codex-app-server<br/>(JSON-RPC)"]
    end

    subgraph Core["codex-core (43 pub modules)"]
        Session["Session<br/>(codex.rs)"]
        CtxAssembly["Context Assembly<br/>instructions آ· AGENTS.md<br/>skills آ· plugins آ· MCP"]
        Router["ToolRouter<br/>(router.rs)"]
        Registry["ToolRegistry<br/>(37+ handlers)"]
        SkillsMgr["SkillsManager<br/>(15 files)"]
        PluginsMgr["PluginsManager<br/>(20 files)"]
        ModelsMgr["ModelsManager<br/>(9 files)"]
        McpMgr["McpManager<br/>(5 files)"]
        AgentCtrl["AgentControl<br/>(v1: 5h + v2: 5h)"]
        Features["Features<br/>(50+ flags, 5 stages)"]
    end

    subgraph Exec["ى‹¤ي–‰ ê³„ى¸µ"]
        UnifiedExec["UnifiedExecRuntime<br/>(10 files)"]
        Sandbox["Seatbelt / Landlock<br/>(OS-level)"]
        Ghost["Ghost Commits<br/>(git-utils)"]
        Shell["ShellRuntime<br/>(pty)"]
    end

    subgraph Protocol["ي”„ë،œي† ى½œ ê³„ى¸µ"]
        Proto["codex-protocol"]
        AppProto["app-server-protocol"]
        AppClient["app-server-client"]
    end

    subgraph Model["ëھ¨ëچ¸"]
        API["Responses API<br/>(SSE stream)"]
    end

    TUI --> Session
    CLI --> Session
    AppServer --> AppProto --> Session

    Session --> CtxAssembly
    CtxAssembly --> SkillsMgr
    CtxAssembly --> PluginsMgr
    CtxAssembly --> ModelsMgr
    
    Session --> API
    API --> Router
    Router --> Registry
    
    Registry --> McpMgr
    Registry --> AgentCtrl
    Registry --> Shell
    Registry --> UnifiedExec
    
    Shell --> Sandbox
    UnifiedExec --> Sandbox
    Ghost --> Sandbox
    
    AgentCtrl -->|"spawn آ· task آ· msg"| Session
    Features --> Session

    AppClient --> AppProto

Axis 01: TUI design

README / docs

// chatwidget.rs â€” queued_message_edit_binding_for_terminal (211â€“235ي–‰)
fn queued_message_edit_binding_for_terminal(terminal_info: TerminalInfo) -> KeyBinding {
    if matches!(terminal_info.multiplexer, Some(Multiplexer::Tmux { .. })) {
        return key_hint::shift(KeyCode::Left);  // tmuxëٹ” Alt+Upى‌„ ى‚¼ي‚¨ë‹¤
    }
    match terminal_info.name {
        AppleTerminal | WarpTerminal | VsCode => key_hint::shift(KeyCode::Left),
        Ghostty | Iterm2 | WezTerm | Kitty | Alacritty | Konsole
        | GnomeTerminal | Vte | WindowsTerminal | Dumb | Unknown
            => key_hint::alt(KeyCode::Up),
    }
}

README / docs

// chatwidget.rs â€” RateLimitWarningState (414â€“477ي–‰)
const RATE_LIMIT_WARNING_THRESHOLDS: [f64; 3] = [75.0, 90.0, 95.0];

struct RateLimitWarningState {
    secondary_index: usize,  // ى£¼ê°„ ى œي•œ ى‍„ê³„ê°’ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
    primary_index: usize,    // 5ى‹œê°„ ى œي•œ ى‍„ê³„ê°’ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
}

impl RateLimitWarningState {
    fn take_warnings(&mut self, secondary_pct: Option<f64>, primary_pct: Option<f64>) -> Vec<String> {
        // 100%ى—گ ëڈ„ë‹¬ي•کë©´ ê²½ê³  ى—†ى‌Œ (ë³„ëڈ„ UIë،œ ى „ي™ک)
        // ê°پ ë ˆë²¨(75/90/95)ى‌€ ي•œ ë²ˆë§Œ ê²½ê³ , index ى „ى§„ىœ¼ë،œ ى‍¬ë°œ ë°©ى§€
    }
}

// chatwidget.rs â€” PendingGuardianReviewStatus (598â€“664ي–‰)

}

    fn start_or_update(&mut self, id: String, detail: String);

    fn status_indicator_state(&self) -> Option<StatusIndicatorState> {
        // 1ê°œ ë¦¬ë·°: "Reviewing approval request" + ë‹¨ى‌¼ ىƒپى„¸
        // Nê°œ ë¦¬ë·°: "Reviewing N approval requests" + ê¸€ë¨¸ë¦¬ ëھ©ë،‌ (ىµœëŒ€ 3ê°œ + "+N more")
    }
}

// chatwidget.rs â€” active_cell_revision (684â€“692ي–‰)
/// ëھ¨ë…¸ي† ë‹‰ ى¹´ىڑ´ي„°. active cellى‌ک transcript ى¶œë ¥ى‌´ ë³€ي•  ىˆک ى‍ˆى‌„ ë•Œ bump.
/// ى‌کëڈ„ى پىœ¼ë،œ wrappingى‌„ ي—ˆىڑ©. ê·¹ي‍ˆ ë“œë¬¸ 1يڑŒ ى؛گى‹œ ى¶©ëڈŒ ê°€ëٹ¥ (ë¬´ي•´).
active_cell_revision: u64,

Main analysis

Codex TUI design analysis

Analysis Snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ (Rust source, workspace). Primary crates: tui/, tui_app_server/, app-server-*. Host-delegated harnesses (lc, sp) render via this surface.

The TUI layer within openai/codex is not just a CLI screen, but is almost a local front-end runtime. codex-rs/tui is a full-screen interface based on Ratatui, and codex-rs/tui_app_server is a parallel implementation that uses the app-server session as a first-class data source while maintaining almost the same UI.

The core structure is three chunks. The main transcript viewport is handled by ChatWidget, the input, status, and approval UI are handled by bottom_pane, and the overlay layer places auxiliary screens such as transcript pager, approval modal, model selection, plugin pop-up, file search, and request_user_input form. tui.rs is the terminal runtime responsible for raw mode, alt screen, event stream, and frame scheduling, and app.rs is effectively the entire UI state machine.

An important observation is that even codex-rs/tui is not a pure core direct UI. Looking at Cargo dependencies, it directly supports codex-app-server-client, codex-app-server-protocol, and codex-tui-app-server, and app.rs also uses InProcessAppServerClient. In other words, â€œlocal TUIâ€‌ is also on top of app-server protocol thinking. This is a big hint for future extraction.

graph TD
    A["tui.rs â€” terminal runtime<br/>raw mode آ· alt screen آ· EventBroker"] --> B["app.rs â€” App state machine<br/>AppEvent dispatch آ· overlay ê´€ë¦¬"]
    B --> C["ChatWidget<br/>transcript cells آ· active cell آ· viewport"]
    B --> D["BottomPane<br/>ChatComposer آ· BottomPaneView stack"]
    B --> E["Overlay layer<br/>TranscriptOverlay آ· StaticOverlay"]
    C --> F["history_cell.rs<br/>protocol â†’ render cell ë³€ي™ک"]
    C --> G["streaming pipeline<br/>StreamController آ· AdaptiveChunkingPolicy"]
    D --> H["popup / overlay ىœ„ى ¯ë“¤"]
    H --> H1["ApprovalOverlay"]
    H --> H2["CommandPopup آ· SkillPopup"]
    H --> H3["FileSearchPopup"]
    H --> H4["RequestUserInputOverlay"]
    G --> G1["chunking.rs â€” Smooth/CatchUp ى •ى±…"]
    G --> G2["commit_tick.rs â€” ë“œë ˆى‌¸ ىک¤ى¼€ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë ˆى‌´ى…ک"]
    G --> G3["controller.rs â€” Stream/PlanStream ى»¨يٹ¸ë،¤ëں¬"]

technical reference

Screen layer decomposition

floor Role representative file
terminal runtime raw mode, alt screen, EventBroker, frame scheduling tui/src/tui.rs, tui/src/tui/event_stream.rs
App Status Full UI state, AppEvent dispatch, overlay management tui/src/app.rs, tui/src/app_event.rs
transcript Message/event rendering, active cell management tui/src/chatwidget.rs, tui/src/chatwidget/*
history cell Convert protocol item to render cell tui/src/history_cell.rs
input layer composer, footer, slash, approval, popup stack tui/src/bottom_pane/*
overlay layer transcript pager, static overlay, approval modal tui/src/pager_overlay.rs
Streaming Policy adaptive chunking, commit tick, smooth/catch-up tui/src/streaming/*
Model Catalog Model list, collaboration mode preset tui/src/model_catalog.rs (tui_app_server)
Render/Style Syntax Highlighting, Line Utilities, Markdown Render tui/src/render/*, tui/src/markdown_render.rs

data flow

steps Description
Receive terminal input EventBroker in tui.rs normalizes crossterm events to TuiEvent
Update app status app.rs centralizes keystrokes and session events on the AppEvent bus
transcript calculation ChatWidget maintains committed cell and in-flight active cell
Cell Rendering history_cell.rs creates a HistoryCell trait implementation for each item type
Bottom input processing BottomPane manages popups, mentions, and approvals with the BottomPaneView stack
streaming commit streaming/* achieves readable output speed with adaptive chunking policy

Overlayآ·Popup component details

Pager Overlay (pager_overlay.rs)

There are two types of Overlay enums.

// tui/src/pager_overlay.rs
pub(crate) enum Overlay {
    Transcript(TranscriptOverlay),
    Static(StaticOverlay),
}

TranscriptOverlay is a full transcript viewer opened with Ctrl+T. In addition to the committed cell list, the live tail of the currently streaming active cell is updated based on the cache key. The cache key is a combination of terminal width, active cell revision, stream-continuation flag, and animation tick.

StaticOverlay displays a list of arbitrary Line or Renderable as a static page. It is used in auxiliary screens such as model information screen and debug view.

Approval Overlay (bottom_pane/approval_overlay.rs)

This is a modal that receives user approval when the agent attempts to run a tool, request permission, apply a patch, or elicit MCP.

// tui/src/bottom_pane/approval_overlay.rs
pub(crate) enum ApprovalRequest {
    Exec { thread_id, id, command, reason, available_decisions, .. },
    Permissions { thread_id, call_id, reason, permissions, .. },
    ApplyPatch { thread_id, id, reason, cwd, changes, .. },
    McpElicitation { thread_id, server_name, request_id, message, .. },
}

Each variant tracks which agent thread the request is from through thread_id. The UI includes Bash command highlighting, diff summary, and permission profile display.

Command Popup (bottom_pane/command_popup.rs)

This is an autocomplete pop-up that opens when you type / slash. Shows a combined list of built-in SlashCommand and user-defined CustomPrompt.

// tui/src/bottom_pane/command_popup.rs
pub(crate) enum CommandItem {
    Builtin(SlashCommand),
    UserPrompt(usize),  // prompts ë²،ي„° ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
}

Among built-ins, alias commands (quit â†’ exit, approvals â†’ permissions) are hidden from the default list. With CommandPopupFlags, displayed items vary depending on feature flags such as collaboration modes, connectors, plugins, and realtime.

Skill Popup (bottom_pane/skill_popup.rs)

This is a skill mention pop-up that opens when you type @. Filter the MentionItem list by fuzzy match.

// tui/src/bottom_pane/skill_popup.rs
pub(crate) struct MentionItem {
    pub display_name: String,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub insert_text: String,
    pub search_terms: Vec<String>,
    pub path: Option<String>,
    pub category_tag: Option<String>,
    pub sort_rank: u8,
}

Sort in the following order: sort_rank â†’ fuzzy score â†’ display_name, and cut and display with MAX_POPUP_ROWS. Render the Enter to insert / Esc to close hint at the bottom.

File Search Popup (bottom_pane/file_search_popup.rs)

This is a file search pop-up that opens when a file is mentioned in the form @filename.

// tui/src/bottom_pane/file_search_popup.rs
pub(crate) struct FileSearchPopup {
    display_query: String,
    pending_query: String,
    waiting: bool,
    matches: Vec<FileMatch>,
    state: ScrollState,
}

By separating pending_query and display_query, existing results are reliably displayed even while the search is in-flight. Stale results are discarded by query comparison in set_matches.

Request User Input Overlay (bottom_pane/request_user_input/)

This is a form overlay that appears when the MCP server or agent requests structured input from the user. It consists of three files.

bottom_pane/request_user_input/
â”œâ”€â”€ mod.rs     â€” ىƒپيƒœ ë¨¸ى‹  (Focus::Options / Focus::Notes, ى§ˆë¬¸ë³„ ë‹µى•ˆ)
â”œâ”€â”€ layout.rs  â€” ى پى‌‘يک• ë ˆى‌´ى•„ى›ƒ ê³„ى‚° (LayoutSections, LayoutPlan)
â””â”€â”€ render.rs  â€” Renderable êµ¬يک„, ى»¤ى„œ ىœ„ى¹ک, unanswered ي™•ى‌¸ ë‹¤ى‌´ى–¼ë،œê·¸

Core operations:

  • For each question, combine option selection + free text (notes) input.
  • If you type during option focus, it automatically switches to notes focus.
  • Enter to advance to next question, submit in full from last question
  • If there are unanswered questions, a confirmation dialog will appear: "Submit with unanswered questions?"
// tui/src/bottom_pane/request_user_input/mod.rs
enum Focus { Options, Notes }

The layout adaptively distributes the progress â†’ question â†’ options â†’ notes â†’ footer area according to the available height. Divide the cases into layout_with_options and layout_without_options.

Model Catalog / Selection

tui/src/model_catalog.rs (or the same file in tui_app_server) manages the model list and collaboration mode presets.

// tui/src/model_catalog.rs (tui_app_server ë‚´)
pub(crate) struct ModelCatalog {
    models: Vec<ModelPreset>,
    collaboration_modes_config: CollaborationModesConfig,
}

When executing the /model slash command, it is converted to a SelectionItem list and displayed in ListSelectionView.

Streaming Pipeline Details

Architecture Overview

graph LR
    D["LLM delta"] --> SC["MarkdownStreamCollector"]
    SC -->|"newline-gated<br/>commit"| Q["StreamState.queued_lines<br/>(VecDeque&lt;QueuedLine&gt;)"]
    Q --> P["AdaptiveChunkingPolicy<br/>Smooth / CatchUp"]
    P -->|DrainPlan| CT["commit_tick<br/>run_commit_tick()"]
    CT --> HC["HistoryCell emission<br/>AgentMessageCell"]

    style P fill:#ffd,stroke:#333

StreamState (streaming/mod.rs)

This is the basic structure of all streaming states. Owns a newline-gated markdown collection and a FIFO queue.

// tui/src/streaming/mod.rs
struct QueuedLine {
    line: Line<'static>,
    enqueued_at: Instant,
}

pub(crate) struct StreamState {
    pub collector: MarkdownStreamCollector,
    queued_lines: VecDeque<QueuedLine>,
    pub has_seen_delta: bool,
}

enqueued_at timestamp tracks the "age" of queue pressure. step() takes one line, drain_n(max) takes up to max lines, and drain_all() takes the entire line from the front of the queue.

StreamController / PlanStreamController (streaming/controller.rs)

Two controllers wrap StreamState and set HistoryCell emission rules.

// tui/src/streaming/controller.rs
pub(crate) struct StreamController {
    state: StreamState,
    finishing_after_drain: bool,
    header_emitted: bool,
}

pub(crate) struct PlanStreamController {
    state: StreamState,
    header_emitted: bool,
    top_padding_emitted: bool,
}
  • push(delta): Add delta to the collector. When a newline is included, the completed line is queued.
  • on_commit_tick(): Drain one line and release it as AgentMessageCell
  • on_commit_tick_batch(max): Multi-line drain for adaptive catch-up
  • finalize(): Flush all remaining data and release it into the final cell.

PlanStreamController adds the "â€¢ Proposed Plan" header, proposed_plan_style() application, and padding management.

Adaptive Chunking Policy (streaming/chunking.rs)

A two-speed gear system controls the streaming display speed.

// tui/src/streaming/chunking.rs
pub(crate) enum ChunkingMode {
    Smooth,   // ê¸°ë³¸: tickë‹¹ 1ى¤„ ë“œë ˆى‌¸
    CatchUp,  // يپگ ى••ë ¥ ë†’ى‌„ ë•Œ: ى „ى²´ backlog ë“œë ˆى‌¸
}

pub(crate) enum DrainPlan {
    Single,         // 1ى¤„ ë°©ى¶œ
    Batch(usize),   // ىµœëŒ€ Nى¤„ ë°©ى¶œ
}
constant value Role
ENTER_QUEUE_DEPTH_LINES 8 Smoothâ†’CatchUp transition cue depth
ENTER_OLDEST_AGE 120ms Smoothâ†’CatchUp transition cue age
EXIT_QUEUE_DEPTH_LINES 2 CatchUpâ†’Smooth Start Queue Depth
EXIT_OLDEST_AGE 40ms CatchUpâ†’Smooth Start Q Age
EXIT_HOLD 250ms Time required to maintain exit condition (hysteresis)
REENTER_CATCH_UP_HOLD 250ms Re-entry cooldown after CatchUp ends
SEVERE_QUEUE_DEPTH_LINES 64 Severe backlog (ignore cooldown)
SEVERE_OLDEST_AGE 300ms Severe backlog (ignore cooldown)

Hysteresis prevents frequent switching (gear-flapping) between Smoothâ†”CatchUp. Severe backlogs will ignore the cooldown and immediately re-enter CatchUp.

Commit Tick Orchestration (streaming/commit_tick.rs)

This is an adhesive module that connects the chunking policy to a specific controller drain.

// tui/src/streaming/commit_tick.rs
pub(crate) fn run_commit_tick(
    policy: &mut AdaptiveChunkingPolicy,
    stream_controller: Option<&mut StreamController>,
    plan_stream_controller: Option<&mut PlanStreamController>,
    scope: CommitTickScope,
    now: Instant,
) -> CommitTickOutput { ... }

Flow: stream_queue_snapshot() â†’ resolve_chunking_plan() â†’ apply_commit_tick_plan()

If you use CommitTickScope::CatchUpOnly, actual drain occurs only in CatchUp mode, and the rest returns as noop. In this way, we can separate the baseline tick and catch-up tick.

Terminal runtime details (tui.rs, tui/event_stream.rs)

EventBroker Structure

// tui/src/tui/event_stream.rs
pub struct EventBroker<S: EventSource = CrosstermEventSource> {
    state: Mutex<EventBrokerState<S>>,
    resume_events_tx: watch::Sender<()>,
}

enum EventBrokerState<S: EventSource> {
    Paused,     // crossterm EventStream dropped
    Start,      // ë‹¤ى‌Œ poll ى‹œ ىƒˆ ى†Œىٹ¤ ىƒ‌ى„±
    Running(S), // ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ى†Œىٹ¤ ê°€ëڈ™ ى¤‘
}

When EventBroker shares a crossterm stream and passes stdin by calling an external editor, etc., dropâ†’recreate is possible. When testing, replace it with FakeEventSource.

Frame Scheduling

Control the frame interval with the TARGET_FRAME_INTERVAL constant. If you issue a redraw request with FrameRequester, the TUI runtime will reflect it in the next frame.

tui_app_server parallel implementation details

codex-rs/tui_app_server/ is a parallel implementation that maintains almost the same UI code as codex-rs/tui/ and replaces the session management layer with AppServerClient based.

structural differences

side tui (local) tui_app_server
Session Connection InProcessAppServerClient AppServerClient (both remote/local available)
Certification AuthManager, directly owned by CodexAuth Delegation to server side
Model list ModelCatalog in model_catalog.rs ModelListParams RPC with AppServerSession
Config Management ConfigBuilder + ConfigOverrides directly Query the server with the ConfigLayerSource protocol
Thread Management ThreadManager, ForkSnapshot directly ThreadStartParams/ThreadForkParams RPC

AppServerSession (tui_app_server/src/app_server_session.rs)

Wraps a JSON-RPC session with the server. Provides typed request methods for thread CRUD (start, fork, resume, rollback, interrupt), model list, account information, skill list, realtime audio, etc.

// tui_app_server/src/app_server_session.rs â€” ى£¼ىڑ” import
use codex_app_server_client::AppServerClient;
use codex_app_server_protocol::{
    ThreadStartParams, ThreadForkParams, ThreadResumeParams,
    ThreadRollbackParams, TurnStartParams, TurnInterruptParams,
    ModelListParams, SkillsListParams, ...
};

Shared code and branching points

Both crates have most of the rendering code the same, including bottom_pane/, chatwidget/, streaming/, pager_overlay.rs, and history_cell.rs (copy source). The differences are mainly in the initialization/session connection part of app.rs and authentication/config loading. This structural redundancy becomes the main motivation for later common crate extraction.

Anatomy of ChatWidget â€” 9,707 lines of state machine

chatwidget.rs is the largest single file in Codex TUI. It consumes protocol events, configures history cells, and drives the transcript viewport and overlay UI.

Core status field

// chatwidget.rs â€” ChatWidget êµ¬ى،°ى²´ (678â€“800ي–‰, ë°œى·Œ)
pub(crate) struct ChatWidget {
    active_cell: Option<Box<dyn HistoryCell>>,  // يک„ى‍¬ ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ى¤‘ى‌¸ ى…€
    active_cell_revision: u64,                   // ى؛گى‹œ ë¬´يڑ¨ي™” ى¹´ىڑ´ي„°
    adaptive_chunking: AdaptiveChunkingPolicy,   // Smooth/CatchUp ى •ى±…
    stream_controller: Option<StreamController>, // ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¼ ë‌¼ى‌´ي”„ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´
    agent_turn_running: bool,                    // ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ي„´ ى§„ي–‰ ى¤‘
    mcp_startup_status: Option<HashMap<String, McpStartupStatus>>,
    running_commands: HashMap<String, RunningCommand>,
    queued_user_messages: VecDeque<UserMessage>,  // ي„´ ى¤‘ ëŒ€ê¸° ë©”ى‹œى§€
    pending_steers: VecDeque<PendingSteer>,       // ى œى¶œëگœ ë¯¸ي™•ى‌¸ steer
    interrupts: InterruptManager,                 // ى‌¸ي„°ëں½يٹ¸ يپگ
    current_status: StatusIndicatorState,         // يک„ى‍¬ ي‘¸ي„° ىƒپيƒœ
    // ... ى´‌ 60+ ي•„ë“œ
}

Event Mapping â€” 120+ EventMsg types

ChatWidget directly matches 120+ types of codex_protocol::protocol::EventMsg. Main Categories:

Category Representative event processing
agent message AgentMessageDelta, AgentMessage active_cell update/commit
Run ExecCommandBegin/End/OutputDelta RunningCommand tracking
Approved ApplyPatchApprovalRequest, ExecApprovalRequest Create BottomPane Overlay
MCP McpToolCallBegin/End, McpStartupUpdate/Complete state machine transition
Guardian GuardianAssessment Track pending review status
Tokens/Limits TokenUsage, RateLimitSnapshot Warning threshold check
web search WebSearchBegin/End Create WebSearchCell
create image ImageGenerationBegin/End Separate cell tracking

Keybinding adaptation based on terminal detection

// chatwidget.rs â€” queued_message_edit_binding_for_terminal (211â€“235ي–‰)
fn queued_message_edit_binding_for_terminal(terminal_info: TerminalInfo) -> KeyBinding {
    if matches!(terminal_info.multiplexer, Some(Multiplexer::Tmux { .. })) {
        return key_hint::shift(KeyCode::Left);  // tmuxëٹ” Alt+Upى‌„ ى‚¼ي‚¨ë‹¤
    }
    match terminal_info.name {
        AppleTerminal | WarpTerminal | VsCode => key_hint::shift(KeyCode::Left),
        Ghostty | Iterm2 | WezTerm | Kitty | Alacritty | Konsole
        | GnomeTerminal | Vte | WindowsTerminal | Dumb | Unknown
            => key_hint::alt(KeyCode::Up),
    }
}

Since match is exhaustive, adding a new TerminalName variant will cause a compilation error, forcing a binding decision.

Rate Limit Alert State Machine

// chatwidget.rs â€” RateLimitWarningState (414â€“477ي–‰)
const RATE_LIMIT_WARNING_THRESHOLDS: [f64; 3] = [75.0, 90.0, 95.0];

struct RateLimitWarningState {
    secondary_index: usize,  // ى£¼ê°„ ى œي•œ ى‍„ê³„ê°’ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
    primary_index: usize,    // 5ى‹œê°„ ى œي•œ ى‍„ê³„ê°’ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
}

impl RateLimitWarningState {
    fn take_warnings(&mut self, secondary_pct: Option<f64>, primary_pct: Option<f64>) -> Vec<String> {
        // 100%ى—گ ëڈ„ë‹¬ي•کë©´ ê²½ê³  ى—†ى‌Œ (ë³„ëڈ„ UIë،œ ى „ي™ک)
        // ê°پ ë ˆë²¨(75/90/95)ى‌€ ي•œ ë²ˆë§Œ ê²½ê³ , index ى „ى§„ىœ¼ë،œ ى‍¬ë°œ ë°©ى§€
    }
}

It advances monotonically in the order 75% â†’ 90% â†’ 95%, and the same threshold warning is not repeated. When 100% is reached, a separate UI dedicated to the rate limit is activated instead of a warning.

Guardian Review Status Tracking

// chatwidget.rs â€” PendingGuardianReviewStatus (598â€“664ي–‰)
struct PendingGuardianReviewStatus {
    entries: Vec<PendingGuardianReviewStatusEntry>,
}

impl PendingGuardianReviewStatus {
    fn start_or_update(&mut self, id: String, detail: String);
    fn finish(&mut self, id: &str) -> bool;
    fn status_indicator_state(&self) -> Option<StatusIndicatorState> {
        // 1ê°œ ë¦¬ë·°: "Reviewing approval request" + ë‹¨ى‌¼ ىƒپى„¸
        // Nê°œ ë¦¬ë·°: "Reviewing N approval requests" + ê¸€ë¨¸ë¦¬ ëھ©ë،‌ (ىµœëŒ€ 3ê°œ + "+N more")
    }
}

Guardian review status has its own tracking system, separate from the ChatWidget's current_status. When multiple reviews are running simultaneously, a summary UI is displayed in the footer.

ChatComposer input pipeline â€” 9,874-line state machine

Architecture Overview

ChatComposer is a reusable input component whose behavior scope is controlled by ChatComposerConfig:

// chat_composer.rs â€” ChatComposerConfig (291â€“307ي–‰)
struct ChatComposerConfig {
    popups_enabled: bool,         // ىٹ¬ë‍کى‹œ/يŒŒى‌¼/ىٹ¤ي‚¬ يŒ‌ى—… ي—ˆىڑ©
    slash_commands_enabled: bool, // /ëھ…ë ¹ يŒŒى‹± ي™œى„±ي™”
    image_paste_enabled: bool,    // ى‌´ë¯¸ى§€ ê²½ë،œ ى²¨ë¶€ ي™œى„±ي™”
}

// ىµœى†Œ ي”„ë¦¬ى…‹: request_user_input ê°™ى‌€ ë‹¨ىˆœ ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ى‍…ë ¥ى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©
const fn plain_text() -> Self { /* ëھ¨ë‘گ false */ }

Popup status â€” limit 1 at a time

// chat_composer.rs (431â€“436ي–‰)
enum ActivePopup {
    None,
    Command(CommandPopup),    // /ىٹ¬ë‍کى‹œ ëھ…ë ¹ ى‍گëڈ™ى™„ى„±
    File(FileSearchPopup),    // يŒŒى‌¼ ê²€ىƒ‰
    Skill(SkillPopup),        // @ë©کى…ک (ىٹ¤ي‚¬/ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸/ى»¤ë„¥ي„°)
}

All key events go through handle_key_event(), and if there is an active popup, they are consumed first by the popup handler. After key processing, sync_popups() is called to automatically open or close the popup depending on the buffer/cursor state.

Non-Bracketed Paste Burst Detection

Some terminals, such as Windows, do not send the paste as bracketed paste, but as a quick KeyCode::Char sequence. The PasteBurst state machine detects this:

ASCII ى‍…ë ¥:
  ى²« ë¹ ë¥¸ ë¬¸ى‍گ â†’ ي™€ë“œ (ê¹œë¹،ى‍„ ى–µى œ)
  â†’ ي›„ى†چ ë¹ ë¥¸ ى‍…ë ¥ ي™•ى‌¸ â†’ paste-likeë،œ يŒگى • â†’ handle_paste()ë،œ ë‌¼ىڑ°يŒ…

Non-ASCII (IME):
  ى²« ë¬¸ى‍گ â†’ ى¦‰ى‹œ ى‚½ى‍… (IME ى‍…ë ¥ ëˆ„ë‌½ ë°©ى§€)
  â†’ ي›„ى†چ ê°گى§€ëٹ” ى—¬ى „ي‍ˆ ëڈ™ى‍‘ â†’ retro-capture ê²½ë،œ

When LARGE_PASTE_CHAR_THRESHOLD = 1000 characters are exceeded, the text is not inserted directly but replaced with a [Pasted N chars] placeholder and expanded upon submission.

Voice Hold-to-Talk

// chat_composer.rs â€” VoiceState (324â€“341ي–‰)
struct VoiceState {
    transcription_enabled: bool,
    space_hold_started_at: Option<Instant>,    // ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ëˆ„ë¥¸ ى‹œى گ
    space_hold_trigger: Option<Arc<AtomicBool>>,
    key_release_supported: bool,               // ي„°ë¯¸ë„گى‌´ Release ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ى§€ى›گ?
    space_hold_repeat_seen: bool,              // ë°کë³µ ي‚¤ ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ê°گى§€ëگ¨?
}

In terminals that support KeyRelease, you can record while pressing the space button, and transcribe when you release it. On terminals that do not support KeyRelease, use repeated space key events as "still held" evidence, and end recording when the events are disconnected.

InputResult â€” Submission path branch

// chat_composer.rs (250â€“262ي–‰)
enum InputResult {
    Submitted { text, text_elements },           // Enter â†’ ى¦‰ى‹œ ى „ى†،
    Queued { text, text_elements },              // Tab â†’ ëŒ€ê¸°ى—´ ى¶”ê°€
    Command(SlashCommand),                        // /ëھ…ë ¹ ë””ىٹ¤يŒ¨ى¹ک
    CommandWithArgs(SlashCommand, String, Vec<TextElement>), // /ëھ…ë ¹ + ى‌¸ى‍گ
    None,                                         // ي‚¤ ى†Œë¹„ ى—†ى‌Œ
}

Enter = Submit immediately, Tab = Queue if task is running (Submit immediately if not running). In the submission path, pending paste placeholder expansion, space trim, text element rebase, and custom prompt expansion are executed sequentially.

active_cell_revision invalidate cache

// chatwidget.rs â€” active_cell_revision (684â€“692ي–‰)
/// ëھ¨ë…¸ي† ë‹‰ ى¹´ىڑ´ي„°. active cellى‌ک transcript ى¶œë ¥ى‌´ ë³€ي•  ىˆک ى‍ˆى‌„ ë•Œ bump.
/// ى‌کëڈ„ى پىœ¼ë،œ wrappingى‌„ ي—ˆىڑ©. ê·¹ي‍ˆ ë“œë¬¸ 1يڑŒ ى؛گى‹œ ى¶©ëڈŒ ê°€ëٹ¥ (ë¬´ي•´).
active_cell_revision: u64,

Transcript overlay caches live tail with active_cell_transcript_key() and active_cell_transcript_lines(). If active_cell_revision does not change, it will not be reorganized for every draw. Without this optimization, the 9,700-line transcript would be re-rendered every frame, drastically reducing performance.

reuse seam

  • tui.rs + tui/event_stream.rs is the easiest terminal runtime layer to remove
  • history_cell.rs is a protocol-aware adapter layer, so it has high reuse value.
  • render/*, markdown_render.rs are portable to other UI surfaces
  • streaming/* is a generalizable streaming marking policy â€” chunking policy is source agnostic
  • selection_popup_common.rs + scroll_state.rs is a general-purpose popup framework
  • chat_composer.rs is valuable, but requires stripping out Codex-specific slash and mention rules.
  • app.rs is too big so we need to break the seam into smaller pieces first
  • request_user_input/ can be extracted with a structured form engine, but is currently directly connected to the Codex protocol.

Tightly coupled risk

risk Description
app.rs non-dialog State and orchestration are concentrated in one file (tui/tui_app_server) (thousands of lines each)
Direct dependence on protocol type UI knows codex_protocol::* events deeply
Copy parallel implementation source tui and tui_app_server have overlapping rendering code
Input Model Complexity slash, skill mention, app mention, approval, attachment, request_user_input are intertwined
increase overlay type ApprovalOverlay, RequestUserInputOverlay, McpServerElicitationOverlay, etc. are each independent state machines

Core File List

tui crate:

  • tui/src/tui.rs â€” Terminal runtime
  • tui/src/tui/event_stream.rs â€” EventBroker, TuiEventStream
  • tui/src/app.rs â€” App state machine
  • tui/src/app_event.rs â€” AppEvent message bus
  • tui/src/chatwidget.rs â€” transcript surface
  • tui/src/chatwidget/agent.rs, interrupts.rs, plugins.rs, realtime.rs, skills.rs
  • tui/src/history_cell.rs â€” HistoryCell trait + implementations
  • tui/src/pager_overlay.rs â€” Overlay enum (Transcript, Static)
  • tui/src/bottom_pane/chat_composer.rs â€” Prompt editor
  • tui/src/bottom_pane/approval_overlay.rs â€” Approval modal
  • tui/src/bottom_pane/command_popup.rs â€” slash autocompletion
  • tui/src/bottom_pane/skill_popup.rs â€” Skill mention popup
  • tui/src/bottom_pane/file_search_popup.rs â€” File search popup
  • tui/src/bottom_pane/request_user_input/ â€” structured input form
  • tui/src/streaming/mod.rs â€” StreamState
  • tui/src/streaming/controller.rs â€” StreamController, PlanStreamController
  • tui/src/streaming/chunking.rs â€” AdaptiveChunkingPolicy
  • tui/src/streaming/commit_tick.rs â€” run_commit_tick orchestration
  • tui/src/markdown_render.rs â€” Markdown render
  • tui/src/render/* â€” highlight, renderable, line_utils

tui_app_server crate:

  • tui_app_server/src/app.rs â€” App state machine for remote sessions
  • tui_app_server/src/app_server_session.rs â€” AppServerClient wrapper
  • tui_app_server/src/model_catalog.rs â€” ModelCatalog

Previous article: [[00_co_overview]] Next article: [[02_co_prompt]]

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Corpus snapshot (121_openai-codex/codex-rs/) Current installed Codex
Crate count ~113 workspace members documented in-body Grows with releases â€” re-count Cargo.toml members
tui vs tui_app_server Dual Ratatui implementations cited API parity maintained; crate splits may shift
Host delegation lc/sp use this TUI surface Unchanged pattern

Re-verify: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/tui/src/app.rs, tui/src/chat_widget.rs on upgrade.

change history

  • 2026-03-26: Added ChatWidget dissection (9,707 lines state machine/60+ fields/120+ events/terminal detection keybindings/rate limit alert state/Guardian review tracking), ChatComposer input pipeline (9,874 lines/Config preset/popup state/paste burst/voice state/InputResult), invalidating active_cell_revision cache 5 reverse engineered sections.
  • 2026-03-26: TUI was analyzed and organized as a protocol-aware local client runtime.
  • 2025-07-19: Added details on 6 types of overlay/popup components, analyzed streaming pipeline (StreamState â†’ AdaptiveChunkingPolicy â†’ commit_tick) code snippet inclusion, compared tui_app_server parallel implementation, added EventBroker structure, expanded mermaid diagram.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 001_tui-design/01_co_tui.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

tui/src/model_catalog.rs (ëکگëٹ” tui_app_server ë‚´ ëڈ™ى‌¼ يŒŒى‌¼)ëٹ” ëھ¨ëچ¸ ëھ©ë،‌ê³¼ collaboration mode presetى‌„ ê´€ë¦¬ي•œë‹¤.

ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ ىƒپى„¸

ى•„ي‚¤ي…چى²ک ê°œىڑ”

graph LR
    D["LLM delta"] --> SC["MarkdownStreamCollector"]
    SC -->|"newline-gated<br/>commit"| Q["StreamState.queued_lines<br/>(VecDeque&lt;QueuedLine&gt;)"]
    Q --> P["AdaptiveChunkingPolicy<br/>Smooth / CatchUp"]
    P -->|DrainPlan| CT["commit_tick<br/>run_commit_tick()"]
    CT --> HC["HistoryCell emission<br/>AgentMessageCell"]

    style P fill:#ffd,stroke:#333

StreamState (streaming/mod.rs)

ëھ¨ë“  ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ىƒپيƒœى‌ک ê¸°ى´ˆ êµ¬ى،°ى²´ë‹¤. newline-gated markdown collectionê³¼ FIFO يپگë¥¼ ى†Œىœ ي•œë‹¤.

// tui/src/streaming/mod.rs
struct QueuedLine {
    line: Line<'static>,
    enqueued_at: Instant,
}

pub(crate) struct StreamState {
    pub collector: MarkdownStreamCollector,
    queued_lines: VecDeque<QueuedLine>,
    pub has_seen_delta: bool,
}

enqueued_at يƒ€ى‍„ىٹ¤يƒ¬ي”„ë،œ يپگ ى••ë ¥ى‌ک "ë‚کى‌´"ë¥¼ ى¶”ى پي•œë‹¤. step()ى‌€ ي•œ ى¤„, drain_n(max)ى‌€ ىµœëŒ€ max ى¤„, drain_all()ى‌€ ى „ى²´ë¥¼ يپگ ى•‍ى—گى„œ ê؛¼ë‚¸ë‹¤.

StreamController / PlanStreamController (streaming/controller.rs)

ë‘گ ى»¨يٹ¸ë،¤ëں¬ê°€ StreamStateë¥¼ ê°گى‹¸ê³  HistoryCell ë°©ى¶œ ê·œى¹™ى‌„ ى •ي•œë‹¤.

// tui/src/streaming/controller.rs
pub(crate) struct StreamController {
    state: StreamState,
    finishing_after_drain: bool,
    header_emitted: bool,
}

pub(crate) struct PlanStreamController {
    state: StreamState,
    header_emitted: bool,
    top_padding_emitted: bool,
}
  • push(delta): ëچ¸يƒ€ë¥¼ collectorى—گ ى¶”ê°€. ê°œي–‰ يڈ¬ي•¨ ى‹œ ى™„ى„±ëگœ ى¤„ى‌„ يپگى—گ ë„£ëٹ”ë‹¤
  • on_commit_tick(): ي•œ ى¤„ ë“œë ˆى‌¸ ي›„ AgentMessageCellë،œ ë°©ى¶œ
  • on_commit_tick_batch(max): adaptive catch-upىڑ© ë‹¤ى¤‘ ى¤„ ë“œë ˆى‌¸
  • finalize(): ى‍”ى—¬ ëچ°ى‌´ي„°ë¥¼ ى „ë¶€ ي”Œëں¬ى‹œي•´ى„œ ىµœى¢… ى…€ë،œ ë°©ى¶œ

PlanStreamControllerëٹ” "â€¢ Proposed Plan" ي—¤ëچ”ى™€ proposed_plan_style() ى پىڑ©, يŒ¨ë”© ê´€ë¦¬ë¥¼ ى¶”ê°€ي•œë‹¤.

Adaptive Chunking Policy (streaming/chunking.rs)

2ë‹¨ ê¸°ى–´ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œىœ¼ë،œ ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ي‘œى‹œ ى†چëڈ„ë¥¼ ى œى–´ي•œë‹¤.

// tui/src/streaming/chunking.rs
pub(crate) enum ChunkingMode {
    Smooth,   // ê¸°ë³¸: tickë‹¹ 1ى¤„ ë“œë ˆى‌¸
    CatchUp,  // يپگ ى••ë ¥ ë†’ى‌„ ë•Œ: ى „ى²´ backlog ë“œë ˆى‌¸
}

pub(crate) enum DrainPlan {
    Single,         // 1ى¤„ ë°©ى¶œ

// tui/src/streaming/commit_tick.rs
pub(crate) fn run_commit_tick(
    policy: &mut AdaptiveChunkingPolicy,
    stream_controller: Option<&mut StreamController>,
    plan_stream_controller: Option<&mut PlanStreamController>,
    scope: CommitTickScope,
    now: Instant,

// tui/src/tui/event_stream.rs
pub struct EventBroker<S: EventSource = CrosstermEventSource> {
    state: Mutex<EventBrokerState<S>>,
    resume_events_tx: watch::Sender<()>,
}

enum EventBrokerState<S: EventSource> {
    Paused,     // crossterm EventStream dropped
    Start,      // ë‹¤ى‌Œ poll ى‹œ ىƒˆ ى†Œىٹ¤ ىƒ‌ى„±
    Running(S), // ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ى†Œىٹ¤ ê°€ëڈ™ ى¤‘
}

ي„°ë¯¸ë„گ ê°گى§€ ê¸°ë°ک ي‚¤ë°”ى‌¸ë”© ى پى‌‘

// chatwidget.rs â€” queued_message_edit_binding_for_terminal (211â€“235ي–‰)
fn queued_message_edit_binding_for_terminal(terminal_info: TerminalInfo) -> KeyBinding {
    if matches!(terminal_info.multiplexer, Some(Multiplexer::Tmux { .. })) {
        return key_hint::shift(KeyCode::Left);  // tmuxëٹ” Alt+Upى‌„ ى‚¼ي‚¨ë‹¤
    }
    match terminal_info.name {
        AppleTerminal | WarpTerminal | VsCode => key_hint::shift(KeyCode::Left),
        Ghostty | Iterm2 | WezTerm | Kitty | Alacritty | Konsole
        | GnomeTerminal | Vte | WindowsTerminal | Dumb | Unknown
            => key_hint::alt(KeyCode::Up),
    }
}

matchê°€ exhaustiveى‌´ë¯€ë،œ ىƒˆë،œىڑ´ TerminalName ë³€يک•ى‌„ ى¶”ê°€ي•کë©´ ى»´يŒŒى‌¼ ى—گëں¬ê°€ ë°œىƒ‌ي•´ ë°”ى‌¸ë”© ê²°ى •ى‌„ ê°•ى œي•œë‹¤.

Rate Limit ê²½ê³  ىƒپيƒœ ë¨¸ى‹ 

// chatwidget.rs â€” RateLimitWarningState (414â€“477ي–‰)
const RATE_LIMIT_WARNING_THRESHOLDS: [f64; 3] = [75.0, 90.0, 95.0];

struct RateLimitWarningState {
    secondary_index: usize,  // ى£¼ê°„ ى œي•œ ى‍„ê³„ê°’ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
    primary_index: usize,    // 5ى‹œê°„ ى œي•œ ى‍„ê³„ê°’ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
}

impl RateLimitWarningState {
    fn take_warnings(&mut self, secondary_pct: Option<f64>, primary_pct: Option<f64>) -> Vec<String> {
        // 100%ى—گ ëڈ„ë‹¬ي•کë©´ ê²½ê³  ى—†ى‌Œ (ë³„ëڈ„ UIë،œ ى „ي™ک)
        // ê°پ ë ˆë²¨(75/90/95)ى‌€ ي•œ ë²ˆë§Œ ê²½ê³ , index ى „ى§„ىœ¼ë،œ ى‍¬ë°œ ë°©ى§€
    }
}

75% â†’ 90% â†’ 95% ىˆœىœ¼ë،œ ë‹¨ى،° ى „ى§„ي•کë©° ê°™ى‌€ ى‍„ê³„ê°’ ê²½ê³ ê°€ ë°کë³µëگکى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤. 100% ëڈ„ë‹¬ ى‹œ ê²½ê³  ëŒ€ى‹  ë³„ëڈ„ rate limit ى „ىڑ© UIê°€ ي™œى„±ي™”ëگœë‹¤.

// chatwidget.rs â€” PendingGuardianReviewStatus (598â€“664ي–‰)
struct PendingGuardianReviewStatus {
    entries: Vec<PendingGuardianReviewStatusEntry>,
}

impl PendingGuardianReviewStatus {
    fn start_or_update(&mut self, id: String, detail: String);
    fn finish(&mut self, id: &str) -> bool;
    fn status_indicator_state(&self) -> Option<StatusIndicatorState> {
        // 1ê°œ ë¦¬ë·°: "Reviewing approval request" + ë‹¨ى‌¼ ىƒپى„¸
        // Nê°œ ë¦¬ë·°: "Reviewing N approval requests" + ê¸€ë¨¸ë¦¬ ëھ©ë،‌ (ىµœëŒ€ 3ê°œ + "+N more")
    }
}

ëھ¨ë“  ي‚¤ ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ëٹ” handle_key_event()ë¥¼ ê±°ى¹کë©°, ي™œى„± يŒ‌ى—…ى‌´ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ يŒ‌ى—… ي•¸ë“¤ëں¬ê°€ ë¨¼ى € ى†Œë¹„ي•œë‹¤. ي‚¤ ى²کë¦¬ ي›„ sync_popups()ê°€ يک¸ى¶œëگکى–´ ë²„يچ¼/ى»¤ى„œ ىƒپيƒœى—گ ë§‍ê²Œ يŒ‌ى—…ى‌„ ى‍گëڈ™ ى—´ê±°ë‚ک ë‹«ëٹ”ë‹¤.

Non-Bracketed Paste Burst ê°گى§€

Windows ë“± ى‌¼ë¶€ ي„°ë¯¸ë„گى‌€ ë¶™ى—¬ë„£ê¸°ë¥¼ bracketed pasteë،œ ë³´ë‚´ى§€ ى•ٹê³  ë¹ ë¥¸ KeyCode::Char ى‹œي€€ىٹ¤ë،œ ë³´ë‚¸ë‹¤. PasteBurst ىƒپيƒœ ë¨¸ى‹ ى‌´ ى‌´ë¥¼ ê°گى§€ي•œë‹¤:

ASCII ى‍…ë ¥:
  ى²« ë¹ ë¥¸ ë¬¸ى‍گ â†’ ي™€ë“œ (ê¹œë¹،ى‍„ ى–µى œ)
  â†’ ي›„ى†چ ë¹ ë¥¸ ى‍…ë ¥ ي™•ى‌¸ â†’ paste-likeë،œ يŒگى • â†’ handle_paste()ë،œ ë‌¼ىڑ°يŒ…

Non-ASCII (IME):
  ى²« ë¬¸ى‍گ â†’ ى¦‰ى‹œ ى‚½ى‍… (IME ى‍…ë ¥ ëˆ„ë‌½ ë°©ى§€)
  â†’ ي›„ى†چ ê°گى§€ëٹ” ى—¬ى „ي‍ˆ ëڈ™ى‍‘ â†’ retro-capture ê²½ë،œ

LARGE_PASTE_CHAR_THRESHOLD = 1000ى‍گ ى´ˆê³¼ ى‹œ ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¥¼ ى§پى ‘ ى‚½ى‍…ي•کى§€ ى•ٹê³  [Pasted N chars] ي”Œë ˆى‌´ىٹ¤ي™€ëچ”ë،œ ëŒ€ى²´ي•کê³ , ى œى¶œ ى‹œ ي™•ى‍¥ي•œë‹¤.

// chatwidget.rs â€” active_cell_revision (684â€“692ي–‰)
/// ëھ¨ë…¸ي† ë‹‰ ى¹´ىڑ´ي„°. active cellى‌ک transcript ى¶œë ¥ى‌´ ë³€ي•  ىˆک ى‍ˆى‌„ ë•Œ bump.
/// ى‌کëڈ„ى پىœ¼ë،œ wrappingى‌„ ي—ˆىڑ©. ê·¹ي‍ˆ ë“œë¬¸ 1يڑŒ ى؛گى‹œ ى¶©ëڈŒ ê°€ëٹ¥ (ë¬´ي•´).

Axis 02: Prompt & context

002_prompt-context/02_co_prompt.md

Overview

Axis 02: Prompt & context â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • ى‹¤ى œ ى،°ë¦½ ىˆœى„œë¥¼ ë³´ë©´ codex.rsëٹ” ë¨¼ى € sandboxى™€ approval policyë¥¼ developer instructionsë،œ ë°”ê¾¸ê³ , ê·¸ ىœ„ى—گ يک„ى‍¬ turnى‌ک developer_instructions, memory tool ى§€ى‹œë¬¸, collaboration mode ى§€ى‹œë¬¸, realtime ê´€ë ¨ ë©”ى‹œى§€, personality ë©”ى‹œى§€ë¥¼ ى–¹ëٹ”ë‹¤. ë°کë©´ AGENTS.mdى™€ SKILL.mdëٹ” ى‌´ develop

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/context_manager/updates.rs:161
pub(crate) fn build_developer_update_item(text_sections: Vec<String>) -> Option<ResponseItem> {
    build_text_message("developer", text_sections)
}

fn build_text_message(role: &str, text_sections: Vec<String>) -> Option<ResponseItem> {
    let content = text_sections
        .into_iter()
        .map(|text| ContentItem::InputText { text })
        .collect();
    Some(ResponseItem::Message {
        id: None,
        role: role.to_string(),
        content,
        end_turn: None,
        phase: None,
    })
}

README / docs

// core/src/contextual_user_message.rs:113
pub(crate) fn is_memory_excluded_contextual_user_fragment(content_item: &ContentItem) -> bool {
    let ContentItem::InputText { text } = content_item else {
        return false;
    };
    AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.matches_text(text) || SKILL_FRAGMENT.matches_text(text)
}

README / docs

// core/src/project_doc.rs:35-37
pub const DEFAULT_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.md";
pub const LOCAL_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.override.md";

// core/src/project_doc.rs:77-120
pub(crate) async fn get_user_instructions(config: &Config) -> Option<String> {
    let project_docs = read_project_docs(config).await;
    let mut output = String::new();
    if let Some(instructions) = config.user_instructions.clone() {
        output.push_str(&instructions);
    }
    match project_docs {
        Ok(Some(docs)) => {
            if !output.is_empty() {
                output.push_str("\n\n--- project-doc ---\n\n");
            }
            output.push_str(&docs);
        }
        // ...
    };
    // JS REPL instructions ى¶”ê°€ (ي”¼ى²ک ي”Œë‍کê·¸)
    // Hierarchical agents message ى¶”ê°€ (ChildAgentsMd ي”¼ى²ک)
}

README / docs

// core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs:19-27
impl UserInstructions {
    pub(crate) fn serialize_to_text(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "{prefix}{directory}\n\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\n{contents}\n{suffix}",
            prefix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.start_marker(),  // "# AGENTS.md instructions for "
            directory = self.directory,
            contents = self.text,
            suffix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.end_marker(),    // "</INSTRUCTIONS>"
        )
    }
}

README / docs

# AGENTS.md instructions for /path/to/project

<INSTRUCTIONS>
(Config::instructions ë‚´ىڑ©)

--- project-doc ---

(ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ë£¨يٹ¸ AGENTS.md ë‚´ىڑ©)

(ي•کىœ„ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ AGENTS.md ë‚´ىڑ©)
</INSTRUCTIONS>

README / docs

// core/src/skills/render.rs:5-48
pub fn render_skills_section(skills: &[SkillMetadata]) -> Option<String> {
    let mut lines: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
    lines.push("## Skills".to_string());
    // ...
    for skill in skills {
        let path_str = skill.path_to_skills_md.to_string_lossy();
        lines.push(format!("- {name}: {description} (file: {path_str})"));
    }
    lines.push("### How to use skills".to_string());
    // يٹ¸ë¦¬ê±° ê·œى¹™, progressive disclosure ë“±
    Some(format!("{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_OPEN_TAG}\n{body}\n{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_CLOSE_TAG}"))
}

README / docs

// core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs:46-53
impl From<SkillInstructions> for ResponseItem {
    fn from(si: SkillInstructions) -> Self {
        SKILL_FRAGMENT.into_message(SKILL_FRAGMENT.wrap(format!(
            "<name>{}</name>\n<path>{}</path>\n{}",
            si.name, si.path, si.contents
        )))
    }
}

</skill>

// core/src/environment_context.rs:163-198
pub fn serialize_to_xml(self) -> String {
    let mut lines = Vec::new();
    if let Some(cwd) = self.cwd {
        lines.push(format!("  <cwd>{}</cwd>", cwd.to_string_lossy()));
    }
    lines.push(format!("  <shell>{}</shell>", self.shell.name()));
    if let Some(current_date) = self.current_date { /* ... */ }
    if let Some(timezone) = self.timezone { /* ... */ }
    match self.network { /* allowed/denied domains */ }
    if let Some(subagents) = self.subagents { /* ... */ }
    ENVIRONMENT_CONTEXT_FRAGMENT.wrap(lines.join("\n"))
}

README / docs

<environment_context>
  <cwd>/Users/dev/project</cwd>
  <shell>zsh</shell>
  <current_date>2026-03-26</current_date>
  <timezone>Asia/Seoul</timezone>
  <network enabled="true">
    <allowed>api.github.com</allowed>
    <denied>evil.example.com</denied>
  </network>
</environment_context>

// core/src/review_prompts.rs:13-16
const UNCOMMITTED_PROMPT: &str = "Review the current code changes (staged, unstaged, and \
    untracked files) and provide prioritized findings.";

const BASE_BRANCH_PROMPT: &str = "Review the code changes against the base branch \
    '{baseBranch}'. The merge base commit for this comparison is {mergeBaseSha}. \
    Run `git diff {mergeBaseSha}` to inspect the changes relative to {baseBranch}. \
    Provide prioritized, actionable findings.";

const COMMIT_PROMPT_WITH_TITLE: &str = "Review the code changes introduced by commit \
    {sha} (\"{title}\"). Provide prioritized, actionable findings.";

README / docs

// core/src/guardian/prompt.rs:437-439
pub(crate) fn guardian_policy_prompt() -> String {
    let prompt = include_str!("policy.md").trim_end();
    format!("{prompt}\n\n{}\n", guardian_output_contract_prompt())
}

// codex.rs:3597-3604
if separate_guardian_developer_message
    && let Some(developer_instructions) = turn_context.developer_instructions.as_deref()
{
    items.push(
        build_developer_update_item(vec![developer_instructions.to_string()])
    );
}

README / docs

<!-- core/templates/memories/read_path.md (ىڑ”ى•½) -->
## Memory
You have access to a memory folder with guidance from prior runs.
Memory layout:
- {{ base_path }}/memory_summary.md
- {{ base_path }}/MEMORY.md (searchable registry)
- {{ base_path }}/skills/<skill-name>/
- {{ base_path }}/rollout_summaries/

========= MEMORY_SUMMARY BEGINS =========
{{ memory_summary }}
========= MEMORY_SUMMARY ENDS =========

- rollout_path: {{ rollout_path }}
- rollout_cwd: {{ rollout_cwd }}

{{ rollout_contents }}

README / docs

// core/src/compact.rs:31-33
pub const SUMMARIZATION_PROMPT: &str = include_str!("../templates/compact/prompt.md");
pub const SUMMARY_PREFIX: &str = include_str!("../templates/compact/summary_prefix.md");
const COMPACT_USER_MESSAGE_MAX_TOKENS: usize = 20_000;

README / docs

<!-- core/templates/compact/prompt.md -->
You are performing a CONTEXT CHECKPOINT COMPACTION.
Create a handoff summary for another LLM that will resume the task.
Include:
- Current progress and key decisions made
- Important context, constraints, or user preferences
- What remains to be done (clear next steps)
- Any critical data, examples, or references needed to continue

README / docs

// core/src/codex.rs:871-876
pub(crate) fn model_context_window(&self) -> Option<i64> {
    let effective_context_window_percent = self.model_info.effective_context_window_percent;
    self.model_info.context_window.map(|context_window| {
        context_window.saturating_mul(effective_context_window_percent) / 100
    })
}

        E2["ى„¤ى • ë³€ê²½ â†’ build_settings_update_items()<br/>â†’ developer/user update items"]
