Main analysis

Prompt and Context Injection Pipeline

Analysis Snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” core/src/instructions/, core/src/context_manager/, AGENTS.md injection paths cited in-body.

The first misconception to dispel when looking at Codex's prompt system is the idea that all instructions are attached in one place at once. The actual code uses at least four separate channels. Basic base instructions, dynamically assembled developer instructions, contextual user fragments such as AGENTS.md and skills, and additional hints added by plugins or collaboration modes are entered in different formats.

This distinction is very important in our fork. We need to duplicate which directives go into which role messages, not just "copy one prompt file." Even if the same text is inserted as a developer message or a user context fragment, the subsequent memory exclusion rules, history reconstruction, and visible item processing methods vary.

Looking at the actual assembly sequence, codex.rs first changes the sandbox and approval policy to developer instructions, and then places the current turn's developer_instructions, memory tool directive, collaboration mode directive, realtime-related message, and personality message on top of it. On the other hand, AGENTS.md and SKILL.md are not included in this developer bundle, but are separately wrapped in the user role text of ResponseItem::Message and recorded as conversation items.

ëکگ ي•کë‚کى‌ک ى¤‘ىڑ”ي•œ ى°¨ى‌´ëٹ” plugin mentionى‌´ë‹¤. skillê³¼ ë‹¤ë¥´ê²Œ plugin mentionى‌€ build_plugin_injections()ë¥¼ ي†µي•´ developer instructions ىھ½ىœ¼ë،œ ë“¤ى–´ê°„ë‹¤. ى¦‰ Codexëٹ” "يŒŒى‌¼يک• ى§€ى‹œë¬¸"ê³¼ "ê¸°ëٹ¥يک• ي‍Œيٹ¸"ë¥¼ ى‌کëڈ„ى پىœ¼ë،œ ë‹¤ë¥¸ ى±„ë„گë،œ ë³´ë‚´ê³  ى‍ˆë‹¤. ى‌´ ë¶„ë¦¬ëٹ” ىڑ°ë¦¬ ىھ½ى—گى„œ يڈ¬يپ¬ي•  ë•Œ ê·¸ëŒ€ë،œ ê°€ى ¸ê°ˆ ê°€ى¹کê°€ يپ¬ë‹¤.

graph TD
    A["base instructions<br/>(protocol/prompts/default.md)"] --> E["model input context"]
    B["developer instructions bundle<br/>(codex.rs â†’ build_initial_context)"] --> E
    C["contextual user items<br/>(AGENTS.md, skills, env context)"] --> E
    D["plugin/app/skill hints<br/>(developer channel)"] --> E
    F["guardian policy prompt<br/>(ë³„ëڈ„ developer message)"] --> E

    style A fill:#2d5016,color:#fff
    style B fill:#1a3a5c,color:#fff
    style C fill:#5c3a1a,color:#fff
    style D fill:#3a1a5c,color:#fff
    style F fill:#5c1a2d,color:#fff

sequenceDiagram
    participant TC as TurnContext
    participant BIC as build_initial_context()
    participant DS as developer_sections Vec
    participant CUS as contextual_user_sections Vec
    participant UPD as context_manager::updates
    participant OUT as ResponseItem ى¶œë ¥

    TC->>BIC: sandbox/approval/model ىƒپيƒœ
    BIC->>DS: â‘  model_switch_message (ى،°ê±´ë¶€)
    BIC->>DS: â‘، DeveloperInstructions::from_policy()
    BIC->>DS: â‘¢ turn_context.developer_instructions
    BIC->>DS: â‘£ build_memory_tool_developer_instructions()
    BIC->>DS: â‘¤ from_collaboration_mode()
    BIC->>DS: â‘¥ build_initial_realtime_item()
    BIC->>DS: â‘¦ personality_spec_message()
    BIC->>DS: â‘§ render_apps_section()
    BIC->>DS: â‘¨ render_skills_section()
    BIC->>DS: â‘© render_plugins_section()
    BIC->>DS: â‘ھ commit_message_trailer_instruction()
    BIC->>CUS: UserInstructions (AGENTS.md)
    BIC->>CUS: EnvironmentContext (XML)
    DS->>UPD: build_developer_update_item()
    CUS->>UPD: build_contextual_user_message()
    UPD->>OUT: developer role message
    UPD->>OUT: user role message
    BIC->>OUT: guardian policy (ë³„ëڈ„ developer message, ى،°ê±´ë¶€)

technical reference

prompt input channel

Channel source file Entry format meaning
base instructions codex-rs/protocol/src/prompts/base_instructions/default.md BaseInstructions Common working rules of the very bottom
developer bundle DeveloperInstructions::from_* and codex.rs assembly developer role text Runtime policy and mode-specific instructions
AGENTS fragment core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs user role contextual fragment Action rules by repo or dir
skill fragment core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs user role contextual fragment Selected SKILL.md body
environment context core/src/environment_context.rs user role XML fragment cwd, shell, date, network, subagents
plugin hint core/src/plugins/injection.rs developer role text MCP, app, prefix hints exposed by plugin
guardian policy core/src/guardian/prompt.rs + policy.md Separate developer message guardian subagent only policy
review prompt core/src/review_prompts.rs user message Code review request prompt
custom prompt core/src/custom_prompts.rs Enter slash command $CODEX_HOME/prompts/*.md
compact prompt core/templates/compact/prompt.md user message context compaction summary instructions
memory prompt core/templates/memories/read_path.md developer text section Memory read path guide

developer bundle assembly sequence (build_initial_context)

This is the actual order in which the build_initial_context() function in codex.rs assembles developer_sections: Vec<String>. See code lines 3434â€“3606.

order Assembly elements Code location Conditions
â‘  model switch message build_model_instructions_update_item() When the model is different from the previous turn
â‘، sandbox/approval policy DeveloperInstructions::from_policy() Always
â‘¢ developer_instructions in current turn turn_context.developer_instructions When not a guardian
â‘£ memory tool guide build_memory_tool_developer_instructions() MemoryTool feature + use_memories
â‘¤ collaboration mode DeveloperInstructions::from_collaboration_mode() When a mode has a directive
â‘¥ realtime initial message build_initial_realtime_item() When realtime status changes
â‘¦ personality message personality_spec_message() Personality feature + when not baked
â‘§ apps section render_apps_section() When apps_enabled
â‘¨ skills section render_skills_section() When implicit skills exist
â‘© plugins section render_plugins_section() When loaded plugins exist
â‘ھ commit attribution commit_message_trailer_instruction() CodexGitCommit feature

Once assembled, it is synthesized into a single developer role ResponseItem::Message** by calling build_developer_update_item(developer_sections). Each section is entered separately as ContentItem::InputText, so it can be distinguished from the model's perspective.

// codex-rs/core/src/context_manager/updates.rs:161
pub(crate) fn build_developer_update_item(text_sections: Vec<String>) -> Option<ResponseItem> {
    build_text_message("developer", text_sections)
}

fn build_text_message(role: &str, text_sections: Vec<String>) -> Option<ResponseItem> {
    let content = text_sections
        .into_iter()
        .map(|text| ContentItem::InputText { text })
        .collect();
    Some(ResponseItem::Message {
        id: None,
        role: role.to_string(),
        content,
        end_turn: None,
        phase: None,
    })
}

Separate developer instructions vs system instructions

Codex does not use the "system" role. Instead, separate the directive into two roles.

Category role Characteristics Example
developer instructions "developer" Policy/functional directive. Define the model's behavioral constraints sandbox policy, approval rules, personality, collaboration mode
contextual user fragment "user" Project-contextual directives. It is recorded in conversation history, but can be selectively excluded from memory AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, environment context

The key reason for this separation is the memory exclusion rule. In contextual_user_message.rs, AGENTS.md and skills are classified as prompt scaffolding and are excluded from the memory stage-1 input, but environment context and subagent notification are maintained:

// core/src/contextual_user_message.rs:113
pub(crate) fn is_memory_excluded_contextual_user_fragment(content_item: &ContentItem) -> bool {
    let ContentItem::InputText { text } = content_item else {
        return false;
    };
    AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.matches_text(text) || SKILL_FRAGMENT.matches_text(text)
}

AGENTS.md discovery and injection mechanism

Hierarchical navigation (project_doc.rs)

AGENTS.md is not simply a file in the current directory. It is collected hierarchically from the project root to cwd.

ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ë£¨يٹ¸ يƒگىƒ‰:
  cwdى—گى„œ ىœ„ë،œ ىک¬ë‌¼ê°€ë©° project_root_markers (.git ë“±) يƒگىƒ‰
  â†’ ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ë£¨يٹ¸ ê²°ى •

يŒŒى‌¼ ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„ (ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ë³„):
  1. AGENTS.override.md   â†گ ë،œى»¬ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ ىڑ°ى„ 
  2. AGENTS.md            â†گ ê¸°ë³¸
  3. project_doc_fallback_filenames ى„¤ى •ê°’

ىˆکى§‘ ىˆœى„œ: project_root â†’ ... â†’ cwd (ىƒپىœ„ى—گى„œ ي•کىœ„ë،œ)
ê²°ي•©: ëھ¨ë“  يŒŒى‌¼ ë‚´ىڑ©ى‌„ "\n\n"ىœ¼ë،œ ى—°ê²°
ë°”ى‌´يٹ¸ ى œي•œ: project_doc_max_bytes ى´ˆê³¼ ى‹œ truncation

// core/src/project_doc.rs:35-37
pub const DEFAULT_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.md";
pub const LOCAL_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.override.md";

The collected project doc is combined with Config::instructions to create user_instructions:

// core/src/project_doc.rs:77-120
pub(crate) async fn get_user_instructions(config: &Config) -> Option<String> {
    let project_docs = read_project_docs(config).await;
    let mut output = String::new();
    if let Some(instructions) = config.user_instructions.clone() {
        output.push_str(&instructions);
    }
    match project_docs {
        Ok(Some(docs)) => {
            if !output.is_empty() {
                output.push_str("\n\n--- project-doc ---\n\n");
            }
            output.push_str(&docs);
        }
        // ...
    };
    // JS REPL instructions ى¶”ê°€ (ي”¼ى²ک ي”Œë‍کê·¸)
    // Hierarchical agents message ى¶”ê°€ (ChildAgentsMd ي”¼ى²ک)
}

Wrapping type (UserInstructions)

Once user_instructions are ready, they are wrapped in contextual_user_sections:

// core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs:19-27
impl UserInstructions {
    pub(crate) fn serialize_to_text(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "{prefix}{directory}\n\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\n{contents}\n{suffix}",
            prefix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.start_marker(),  // "# AGENTS.md instructions for "
            directory = self.directory,
            contents = self.text,
            suffix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.end_marker(),    // "</INSTRUCTIONS>"
        )
    }
}

Final output form:

# AGENTS.md instructions for /path/to/project

<INSTRUCTIONS>
(Config::instructions ë‚´ىڑ©)

--- project-doc ---

(ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ë£¨يٹ¸ AGENTS.md ë‚´ىڑ©)

(ي•کىœ„ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ AGENTS.md ë‚´ىڑ©)
</INSTRUCTIONS>

SKILL.md injection pipeline

Skill injection is divided into two stages: render_skills_section() in the developer bundle and build_skill_injections() separately.

Step 1: Skill list guide (developer channel)

render_skills_section() puts a list of available skills into developer instructions:

// core/src/skills/render.rs:5-48
pub fn render_skills_section(skills: &[SkillMetadata]) -> Option<String> {
    let mut lines: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
    lines.push("## Skills".to_string());
    // ...
    for skill in skills {
        let path_str = skill.path_to_skills_md.to_string_lossy();
        lines.push(format!("- {name}: {description} (file: {path_str})"));
    }
    lines.push("### How to use skills".to_string());
    // يٹ¸ë¦¬ê±° ê·œى¹™, progressive disclosure ë“±
    Some(format!("{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_OPEN_TAG}\n{body}\n{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_CLOSE_TAG}"))
}

Step 2: Inject skill body (user channel)

When the user explicitly mentions $skill-name, build_skill_injections() reads the SKILL.md file and wraps it in SkillInstructions:

// core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs:46-53
impl From<SkillInstructions> for ResponseItem {
    fn from(si: SkillInstructions) -> Self {
        SKILL_FRAGMENT.into_message(SKILL_FRAGMENT.wrap(format!(
            "<name>{}</name>\n<path>{}</path>\n{}",
            si.name, si.path, si.contents
        )))
    }
}

Final output form:

<skill>
<name>dev-frontend</name>
<path>/project/.codex/skills/dev-frontend/SKILL.md</path>
(SKILL.md ë³¸ë¬¸)
</skill>

Contextual User Fragment System

contextual_user_message.rs defines 6 types of fragments. Each is identified by a start/end marker.

Fragment start marker end marker excluding memory
AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT # AGENTS.md instructions for </INSTRUCTIONS> âœ… Excluded
SKILL_FRAGMENT <skill> </skill> âœ… Excluded
ENVIRONMENT_CONTEXT_FRAGMENT <environment_context> </environment_context> â‌Œ Maintain
USER_SHELL_COMMAND_FRAGMENT <user_shell_command> </user_shell_command> â‌Œ Maintain
TURN_ABORTED_FRAGMENT <turn_aborted> </turn_aborted> â‌Œ Maintain
SUBAGENT_NOTIFICATION_FRAGMENT <subagent_notification> </subagent_notification> â‌Œ Maintain

Even in the guardian transcript, these fragments are filtered out with is_contextual_user_message_content() and excluded. In other words, the guardian only sees the actual conversation content, not the prompt scaffolding.

Environment Context Structure

EnvironmentContext is serialized to XML and put into the contextual user fragment:

// core/src/environment_context.rs:163-198
pub fn serialize_to_xml(self) -> String {
    let mut lines = Vec::new();
    if let Some(cwd) = self.cwd {
        lines.push(format!("  <cwd>{}</cwd>", cwd.to_string_lossy()));
    }
    lines.push(format!("  <shell>{}</shell>", self.shell.name()));
    if let Some(current_date) = self.current_date { /* ... */ }
    if let Some(timezone) = self.timezone { /* ... */ }
    match self.network { /* allowed/denied domains */ }
    if let Some(subagents) = self.subagents { /* ... */ }
    ENVIRONMENT_CONTEXT_FRAGMENT.wrap(lines.join("\n"))
}

Example output:

<environment_context>
  <cwd>/Users/dev/project</cwd>
  <shell>zsh</shell>
  <current_date>2026-03-26</current_date>
  <timezone>Asia/Seoul</timezone>
  <network enabled="true">
    <allowed>api.github.com</allowed>
    <denied>evil.example.com</denied>
  </network>
</environment_context>

Custom Prompts (slash commands)

.md files in the $CODEX_HOME/prompts/ directory are loaded with a custom prompt:

// core/src/custom_prompts.rs:83-145
fn parse_frontmatter(content: &str) -> (Option<String>, Option<String>, String) {
    // YAML-like frontmatter يŒŒى‹±:
    // ---
    // description: "ى§§ى‌€ ى„¤ëھ…"
    // argument-hint: "ي‍Œيٹ¸ ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸"
    // ---
    // (ë³¸ë¬¸)
}

Extracts description and argument-hint from frontmatter and uses the rest as the prompt body. The file name (excluding extension) becomes the slash command name.

Review Prompts

Use a fixed prompt template for each code review mode:

// core/src/review_prompts.rs:13-16
const UNCOMMITTED_PROMPT: &str = "Review the current code changes (staged, unstaged, and \
    untracked files) and provide prioritized findings.";

const BASE_BRANCH_PROMPT: &str = "Review the code changes against the base branch \
    '{baseBranch}'. The merge base commit for this comparison is {mergeBaseSha}. \
    Run `git diff {mergeBaseSha}` to inspect the changes relative to {baseBranch}. \
    Provide prioritized, actionable findings.";

const COMMIT_PROMPT_WITH_TITLE: &str = "Review the code changes introduced by commit \
    {sha} (\"{title}\"). Provide prioritized, actionable findings.";

Guardian Prompt Assembly

The guardian subagent has a separate prompt pipeline.

Policy prompt (guardian/prompt.rs)

Include policy.md at compile time with include_str! and append the JSON output schema to your code:

// core/src/guardian/prompt.rs:437-439
pub(crate) fn guardian_policy_prompt() -> String {
    let prompt = include_str!("policy.md").trim_end();
    format!("{prompt}\n\n{}\n", guardian_output_contract_prompt())
}

output contract:

{
  "risk_level": "low" | "medium" | "high",
  "risk_score": 0-100,
  "rationale": "string",
  "evidence": [{"message": "string", "why": "string"}]
}

Request Prompt (build_guardian_prompt_items)

Extract the transcript from the conversation history, but filter out the contextual user messages, truncate them within the token budget, and composite them with the planned action JSON:

>>> TRANSCRIPT START
[1] user: ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ىڑ”ى²­ ë‚´ىڑ©
[2] tool shell call: {"command": "..."}
[3] assistant: ى‌‘ë‹µ ë‚´ىڑ©
>>> TRANSCRIPT END

>>> APPROVAL REQUEST START
Planned action JSON:
{"type": "shell", "command": ["rm", "-rf", "..."]}
>>> APPROVAL REQUEST END

The token budget manages user messages and tool evidence separately:

GUARDIAN_MAX_MESSAGE_TRANSCRIPT_TOKENS â€” user/assistant ë©”ى‹œى§€ ىکˆى‚°
GUARDIAN_MAX_TOOL_TRANSCRIPT_TOKENS    â€” tool call/result ىکˆى‚°
GUARDIAN_MAX_MESSAGE_ENTRY_TOKENS      â€” ê°œë³„ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ي† يپ° ىƒپي•œ
GUARDIAN_MAX_TOOL_ENTRY_TOKENS         â€” ê°œë³„ tool ي•­ëھ© ي† يپ° ىƒپي•œ
GUARDIAN_RECENT_ENTRY_LIMIT            â€” ىµœê·¼ non-user ي•­ëھ© ê°œىˆک ى œي•œ

The guardian subagent's developer_instructions are emitted as a separate developer message in build_initial_context() (separate_guardian_developer_message flag):

// codex.rs:3597-3604
if separate_guardian_developer_message
    && let Some(developer_instructions) = turn_context.developer_instructions.as_deref()
{
    items.push(
        build_developer_update_item(vec![developer_instructions.to_string()])
    );
}

Memory Prompt Template

The memory system renders prompts with the Askama template engine.

read_path.md â€” Memory read guidance (developer channel)

Read memory_summary.md from build_memory_tool_developer_instructions() and insert it into developer instructions:

<!-- core/templates/memories/read_path.md (ىڑ”ى•½) -->
## Memory
You have access to a memory folder with guidance from prior runs.
Memory layout:
- {{ base_path }}/memory_summary.md
- {{ base_path }}/MEMORY.md (searchable registry)
- {{ base_path }}/skills/<skill-name>/
- {{ base_path }}/rollout_summaries/

========= MEMORY_SUMMARY BEGINS =========
{{ memory_summary }}
========= MEMORY_SUMMARY ENDS =========

Token limit: truncate memory_summary with MEMORY_TOOL_DEVELOPER_INSTRUCTIONS_SUMMARY_TOKEN_LIMIT.

stage_one_input.md â€” Rollout summary input

<!-- core/templates/memories/stage_one_input.md -->
rollout_context:
- rollout_path: {{ rollout_path }}
- rollout_cwd: {{ rollout_cwd }}

rendered conversation:
{{ rollout_contents }}

The rollout content is truncated only up to the number of tokens to which the CONTEXT_WINDOW_PERCENT ratio is applied in addition to the effective_context_window_percent of the model's context_window.

Context Compaction

Automatic compaction occurs when the context window runs out:

// core/src/compact.rs:31-33
pub const SUMMARIZATION_PROMPT: &str = include_str!("../templates/compact/prompt.md");
pub const SUMMARY_PREFIX: &str = include_str!("../templates/compact/summary_prefix.md");
const COMPACT_USER_MESSAGE_MAX_TOKENS: usize = 20_000;

compaction prompt:

<!-- core/templates/compact/prompt.md -->
You are performing a CONTEXT CHECKPOINT COMPACTION.
Create a handoff summary for another LLM that will resume the task.
Include:
- Current progress and key decisions made
- Important context, constraints, or user preferences
- What remains to be done (clear next steps)
- Any critical data, examples, or references needed to continue

Compaction has two modes:

  • Pre-turn/Manual: InitialContextInjection::DoNotInject â€” Replace the history after summarizing, and reinject the initial context in the next turn.
  • Mid-turn: InitialContextInjection::BeforeLastUserMessage â€” Insert initial context before the last user message, even after the summary.

Context Window Management

Calculate effective context window

// core/src/codex.rs:871-876
pub(crate) fn model_context_window(&self) -> Option<i64> {
    let effective_context_window_percent = self.model_info.effective_context_window_percent;
    self.model_info.context_window.map(|context_window| {
        context_window.saturating_mul(effective_context_window_percent) / 100
    })
}

The actual usable token is calculated as context_window * effective_context_window_percent / 100. The default values â€‹â€‹are context_window=272_000, effective_context_window_percent=95, so about 258,400 tokens.

Token Usage Tracking

At the end of each turn, update the current token usage with update_token_info_from_usage(). When the context window is exceeded, compaction is triggered with set_token_usage_full(). In remote compact(compact_remote.rs), the function call history is trimmed with trim_function_call_history_to_fit_context_window().

flowchart LR

    B -->|"ي•œëڈ„ ى´ˆê³¼"| D{"remote compact ê°€ëٹ¥?"}
    D -->|"OpenAI provider"| E["remote compact<br/>(trim_function_call_history)"]
    D -->|"ê¸°يƒ€"| F["inline compact<br/>(SUMMARIZATION_PROMPT)"]
    E --> C
    F --> C

Full prompt assembly pipeline diagram

        D2["build_contextual_user_message(contextual_user_sections)<br/>â†’ user role ResponseItem"]

        E1["skill mentions â†’ build_skill_injections()<br/>â†’ user role items"]

    A4 --> BUILD
    B12 --> D1
    C3 --> D2
    D2 --> D3
    D3 --> INJECT

Interpretation from folk perspective

  • Do not copy only default.md
  • developer bundle assembly function is the key
  • AGENTS.md and skill are user contextual fragments, not developer channels
  • Unlike skill, plugin hint is a developer channel
  • guardian separates policy prompts into separate developer messages
  • AGENTS.md is collected hierarchically and AGENTS.override.md takes precedence
  • You also need to decide whether to fork the history reconstruction and memory exclusion rules together.
  • You need to decide how to use the context compaction strategy (remote vs inline)

Rules we can keep as is

Rules Reason
Policy directives are placed in the developer channel Sandbox and approval policies become clear
Place the repo local directive as a contextual user fragment Easy to manage separately from history and memory
The skill text is wrapped in a separate wrapper You can track what skills were used
Plugin hint is set to developer channel Function hints and file directives can be separated
AGENTS.md Hierarchical Collections It is possible to separate directives by directory in a monorepo
Manage memory exclusion rules on a fragment basis Scaffolding and automatic separation of conversation content are key
Place the context compaction prompt as an external template Prompt modification is possible without changing code

The boundary between core and optional

  • core: BaseInstructions, permissions-based developer prompt, AGENTS.md wrapper (including hierarchical collection), explicit skill injection, environment context, contextual fragment marker system
  • optional: memory prompt, personality prompt, plugin hint injection, skill dependency installation, collaboration mode preset, guardian subagent, realtime conversation, JS REPL instructions, custom prompts
  • When forking, you must decide whether to take all optional layers from the beginning.

Core File List

file Role
codex-rs/protocol/src/models.rs DeveloperInstructions structure, from_policy(), personality_spec_message(), etc.
codex-rs/protocol/src/prompts/base_instructions/default.md base instructions template
codex-rs/core/src/codex.rs build_initial_context() â€” Full assembly orchestration
codex-rs/core/src/context_manager/updates.rs build_developer_update_item(), build_contextual_user_message(), change settings diff
codex-rs/core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs Wrapping UserInstructions, SkillInstructions
codex-rs/core/src/contextual_user_message.rs 6 types of fragment definition, memory exclusion decision
codex-rs/core/src/project_doc.rs AGENTS.md hierarchical discovery, get_user_instructions()
codex-rs/core/src/environment_context.rs EnvironmentContext XML serialization
codex-rs/core/src/skills/render.rs render_skills_section() â€” developer channel skills list
codex-rs/core/src/skills/injection.rs build_skill_injections() â€” user channel skill body
codex-rs/core/src/custom_prompts.rs Custom prompt discovery, frontmatter parsing
codex-rs/core/src/review_prompts.rs Code review prompt template
codex-rs/core/src/guardian/prompt.rs guardian policy prompt, transcript extraction, token budget management
codex-rs/core/src/guardian/policy.md guardian policy text (include_str!)
codex-rs/core/src/memories/prompts.rs Memory Askama Template Rendering
codex-rs/core/templates/memories/read_path.md memory read path developer prompt template
codex-rs/core/templates/memories/stage_one_input.md memory phase-1 input template
codex-rs/core/templates/memories/consolidation.md Memory phase-2 integration prompt
codex-rs/core/templates/compact/prompt.md context compaction summary prompt
codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs Conversation compression logic, token restrictions
codex-rs/core/src/plugins/injection.rs plugin hint injection
codex-rs/core/src/models_manager/collaboration_mode_presets.rs collaboration mode developer instructions

ى،°ë¦½ى‌´ ëپ‌ë‚کë©´ build_developer_update_item(developer_sections) يک¸ى¶œë،œ **ë‹¨ى‌¼ developer role ResponseItem::Message**ë،œ ي•©ى„±ëگœë‹¤. ê°پ sectionى‌€ ë³„ëڈ„ ContentItem::InputTextë،œ ë“¤ى–´ê°€ë¯€ë،œ ëھ¨ëچ¸ ى‍…ى‍¥ى—گى„œ êµ¬ë¶„ ê°€ëٹ¥ي•کë‹¤.

// codex-rs/core/src/context_manager/updates.rs:161
pub(crate) fn build_developer_update_item(text_sections: Vec<String>) -> Option<ResponseItem> {
    build_text_message("developer", text_sections)
}

fn build_text_message(role: &str, text_sections: Vec<String>) -> Option<ResponseItem> {
    let content = text_sections
        .into_iter()
        .map(|text| ContentItem::InputText { text })
        .collect();
    Some(ResponseItem::Message {
        id: None,
        role: role.to_string(),
        content,
        end_turn: None,
        phase: None,
    })
}

developer instructions vs system instructions ë¶„ë¦¬

Codexëٹ” "system" roleى‌„ ى“°ى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤. ëŒ€ى‹  ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ roleë،œ ى§€ى‹œë¬¸ى‌„ ë¶„ë¦¬ي•œë‹¤.

êµ¬ë¶„ role يٹ¹ى„± ىکˆى‹œ
developer instructions "developer" ى •ى±…ى„±آ·ê¸°ëٹ¥ى„± ى§€ى‹œë¬¸. ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌ک ي–‰ëڈ™ ى œى•½ى‌„ ى •ى‌ک sandbox policy, approval rules, personality, collaboration mode
contextual user fragment "user" ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ë§¥ë‌½يک• ى§€ى‹œë¬¸. ëŒ€ي™” ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ى—گ ê¸°ë،‌ëگکى§€ë§Œ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ى—گى„œ ى„ ë³„ ى œى™¸ ê°€ëٹ¥ AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, environment context

ى‌´ ë¶„ë¦¬ى‌ک ي•µى‹¬ ى‌´ىœ ëٹ” ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى œى™¸ ê·œى¹™ى‌´ë‹¤. contextual_user_message.rsى—گى„œ AGENTS.mdى™€ skillى‌€ prompt scaffoldingىœ¼ë،œ ë¶„ë¥کëگکى–´ memory stage-1 ى‍…ë ¥ى—گى„œ ë¹ ى§€ى§€ë§Œ, environment contextى™€ subagent notificationى‌€ ىœ ى§€ëگœë‹¤:

// core/src/contextual_user_message.rs:113
pub(crate) fn is_memory_excluded_contextual_user_fragment(content_item: &ContentItem) -> bool {
    let ContentItem::InputText { text } = content_item else {
        return false;
    };
    AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.matches_text(text) || SKILL_FRAGMENT.matches_text(text)
}

AGENTS.md ë°œê²¬ ë°ڈ ى£¼ى‍… ë©”ى»¤ë‹ˆى¦ک

ê³„ى¸µى پ يƒگىƒ‰ (project_doc.rs)

AGENTS.mdëٹ” ë‹¨ىˆœي‍ˆ يک„ى‍¬ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ يŒŒى‌¼ ي•کë‚کê°€ ى•„ë‹ˆë‹¤. ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ë£¨يٹ¸ë¶€ي„° cwdê¹Œى§€ ê³„ى¸µى پىœ¼ë،œ ىˆکى§‘ëگœë‹¤.

ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ë£¨يٹ¸ يƒگىƒ‰:
  cwdى—گى„œ ىœ„ë،œ ىک¬ë‌¼ê°€ë©° project_root_markers (.git ë“±) يƒگىƒ‰
  â†’ ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ë£¨يٹ¸ ê²°ى •

يŒŒى‌¼ ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„ (ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ë³„):
  1. AGENTS.override.md   â†گ ë،œى»¬ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ ىڑ°ى„ 
  2. AGENTS.md            â†گ ê¸°ë³¸
  3. project_doc_fallback_filenames ى„¤ى •ê°’

ىˆکى§‘ ىˆœى„œ: project_root â†’ ... â†’ cwd (ىƒپىœ„ى—گى„œ ي•کىœ„ë،œ)
ê²°ي•©: ëھ¨ë“  يŒŒى‌¼ ë‚´ىڑ©ى‌„ "\n\n"ىœ¼ë،œ ى—°ê²°
ë°”ى‌´يٹ¸ ى œي•œ: project_doc_max_bytes ى´ˆê³¼ ى‹œ truncation

// core/src/project_doc.rs:35-37
pub const DEFAULT_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.md";
pub const LOCAL_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.override.md";

ىˆکى§‘ëگœ project docى‌€ Config::instructionsى™€ ي•©ى³گى ¸ user_instructionsê°€ ëگœë‹¤:

// core/src/project_doc.rs:77-120
pub(crate) async fn get_user_instructions(config: &Config) -> Option<String> {
    let project_docs = read_project_docs(config).await;
    let mut output = String::new();
    if let Some(instructions) = config.user_instructions.clone() {
        output.push_str(&instructions);
    }
    match project_docs {
        Ok(Some(docs)) => {
            if !output.is_empty() {
                output.push_str("\n\n--- project-doc ---\n\n");
            }
            output.push_str(&docs);
        }
        // ...
    };
    // JS REPL instructions ى¶”ê°€ (ي”¼ى²ک ي”Œë‍کê·¸)
    // Hierarchical agents message ى¶”ê°€ (ChildAgentsMd ي”¼ى²ک)
}

ë‍کي•‘ يک•ى‹‌ (UserInstructions)

user_instructionsê°€ ى¤€ë¹„ëگکë©´ contextual_user_sectionsى—گ ë‍کي•‘ëگکى–´ ë“¤ى–´ê°„ë‹¤:

// core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs:19-27
impl UserInstructions {
    pub(crate) fn serialize_to_text(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "{prefix}{directory}\n\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\n{contents}\n{suffix}",
            prefix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.start_marker(),  // "# AGENTS.md instructions for "
            directory = self.directory,
            contents = self.text,
            suffix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.end_marker(),    // "</INSTRUCTIONS>"
        )
    }
}

ىµœى¢… ى¶œë ¥ يک•يƒœ:

# AGENTS.md instructions for /path/to/project

<INSTRUCTIONS>
(Config::instructions ë‚´ىڑ©)

--- project-doc ---

(ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ë£¨يٹ¸ AGENTS.md ë‚´ىڑ©)

(ي•کىœ„ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ AGENTS.md ë‚´ىڑ©)
</INSTRUCTIONS>

SKILL.md ى£¼ى‍… يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸

ىٹ¤ي‚¬ ى£¼ى‍…ى‌€ developer bundle ى•ˆى‌ک render_skills_section()ê³¼ ë³„ëڈ„ى‌ک build_skill_injections() ë‘گ ë‹¨ê³„ë،œ ë‚کë‰œë‹¤.

1ë‹¨ê³„: ىٹ¤ي‚¬ ëھ©ë،‌ ى•ˆë‚´ (developer channel)

render_skills_section()ى‌€ ى‚¬ىڑ© ê°€ëٹ¥ي•œ ىٹ¤ي‚¬ ëھ©ë،‌ى‌„ developer instructionsى—گ ë„£ëٹ”ë‹¤:

// core/src/skills/render.rs:5-48
pub fn render_skills_section(skills: &[SkillMetadata]) -> Option<String> {
    let mut lines: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
    lines.push("## Skills".to_string());
    // ...
    for skill in skills {
        let path_str = skill.path_to_skills_md.to_string_lossy();
        lines.push(format!("- {name}: {description} (file: {path_str})"));
    }
    lines.push("### How to use skills".to_string());
    // يٹ¸ë¦¬ê±° ê·œى¹™, progressive disclosure ë“±
    Some(format!("{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_OPEN_TAG}\n{body}\n{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_CLOSE_TAG}"))
}

2ë‹¨ê³„: ىٹ¤ي‚¬ ë³¸ë¬¸ ى£¼ى‍… (user channel)

ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گê°€ $skill-nameىœ¼ë،œ ëھ…ى‹œى پ ë©کى…کي•کë©´ build_skill_injections()ê°€ SKILL.md يŒŒى‌¼ى‌„ ى‌½ى–´ SkillInstructionsë،œ ë‍کي•‘ي•œë‹¤:

// core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs:46-53
impl From<SkillInstructions> for ResponseItem {
    fn from(si: SkillInstructions) -> Self {
        SKILL_FRAGMENT.into_message(SKILL_FRAGMENT.wrap(format!(
            "<name>{}</name>\n<path>{}</path>\n{}",
            si.name, si.path, si.contents
        )))
    }
}

ىµœى¢… ى¶œë ¥ يک•يƒœ:

<skill>
<name>dev-frontend</name>
<path>/project/.codex/skills/dev-frontend/SKILL.md</path>
(SKILL.md ë³¸ë¬¸)
</skill>

Contextual User Fragment ى²´ê³„

contextual_user_message.rsëٹ” 6ى¢…ى‌ک fragmentë¥¼ ى •ى‌کي•œë‹¤. ê°پê°پى‌€ ى‹œى‍‘/ى¢…ë£Œ ë§ˆى»¤ë،œ ى‹‌ë³„ëگœë‹¤.

Fragment ى‹œى‍‘ ë§ˆى»¤ ى¢…ë£Œ ë§ˆى»¤ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى œى™¸
AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT # AGENTS.md instructions for </INSTRUCTIONS> âœ… ى œى™¸
SKILL_FRAGMENT <skill> </skill> âœ… ى œى™¸
ENVIRONMENT_CONTEXT_FRAGMENT <environment_context> </environment_context> â‌Œ ىœ ى§€
USER_SHELL_COMMAND_FRAGMENT <user_shell_command> </user_shell_command> â‌Œ ىœ ى§€
TURN_ABORTED_FRAGMENT <turn_aborted> </turn_aborted> â‌Œ ىœ ى§€
SUBAGENT_NOTIFICATION_FRAGMENT <subagent_notification> </subagent_notification> â‌Œ ىœ ى§€

guardian transcriptى—گى„œëڈ„ ى‌´ fragmentë“¤ى‌€ is_contextual_user_message_content()ë،œ ي•„ي„°ë§پëگکى–´ ى œى™¸ëگœë‹¤. ى¦‰ guardianى‌€ ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸ scaffoldingى‌´ ى•„ë‹Œ ى‹¤ى œ ëŒ€ي™” ë‚´ىڑ©ë§Œ ë³¸ë‹¤.

Environment Context êµ¬ى،°

EnvironmentContextëٹ” XMLë،œ ى§پë ¬ي™”ëگکى–´ contextual user fragmentë،œ ë“¤ى–´ê°„ë‹¤:

// core/src/environment_context.rs:163-198
pub fn serialize_to_xml(self) -> String {
    let mut lines = Vec::new();
    if let Some(cwd) = self.cwd {
        lines.push(format!("  <cwd>{}</cwd>", cwd.to_string_lossy()));
    }
    lines.push(format!("  <shell>{}</shell>", self.shell.name()));
    if let Some(current_date) = self.current_date { /* ... */ }
    if let Some(timezone) = self.timezone { /* ... */ }
    match self.network { /* allowed/denied domains */ }
    if let Some(subagents) = self.subagents { /* ... */ }
    ENVIRONMENT_CONTEXT_FRAGMENT.wrap(lines.join("\n"))
}

ى¶œë ¥ ىکˆى‹œ:

<environment_context>
  <cwd>/Users/dev/project</cwd>
  <shell>zsh</shell>
  <current_date>2026-03-26</current_date>
  <timezone>Asia/Seoul</timezone>
  <network enabled="true">
    <allowed>api.github.com</allowed>
    <denied>evil.example.com</denied>
  </network>
</environment_context>

fn parse_frontmatter(content: &str) -> (Option<String>, Option<String>, String) {

Review Prompts

ى½”ë“œ ë¦¬ë·° ëھ¨ë“œë³„ë،œ ê³ ى •ëگœ ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸ ي…œي”Œë¦؟ى‌„ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•œë‹¤:

// core/src/review_prompts.rs:13-16
const UNCOMMITTED_PROMPT: &str = "Review the current code changes (staged, unstaged, and \
    untracked files) and provide prioritized findings.";

const BASE_BRANCH_PROMPT: &str = "Review the code changes against the base branch \
    '{baseBranch}'. The merge base commit for this comparison is {mergeBaseSha}. \
    Run `git diff {mergeBaseSha}` to inspect the changes relative to {baseBranch}. \
    Provide prioritized, actionable findings.";

const COMMIT_PROMPT_WITH_TITLE: &str = "Review the code changes introduced by commit \
    {sha} (\"{title}\"). Provide prioritized, actionable findings.";

Guardian Prompt ى،°ë¦½

guardian subagentëٹ” ë³„ëڈ„ ë¶„ë¦¬ëگœ ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌„ ê°€ى§„ë‹¤.

ى •ى±… ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸ (guardian/prompt.rs)

policy.mdë¥¼ include_str!ë،œ ى»´يŒŒى‌¼ ى‹œى گى—گ يڈ¬ي•¨ي•کê³  JSON output schemaë¥¼ ى½”ë“œى—گى„œ ëچ§ë¶™ى‌¸ë‹¤:

// core/src/guardian/prompt.rs:437-439
pub(crate) fn guardian_policy_prompt() -> String {
    let prompt = include_str!("policy.md").trim_end();
    format!("{prompt}\n\n{}\n", guardian_output_contract_prompt())
}

output contract:

{
  "risk_level": "low" | "medium" | "high",
  "risk_score": 0-100,
  "rationale": "string",
  "evidence": [{"message": "string", "why": "string"}]
}

ىڑ”ى²­ ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸ (build_guardian_prompt_items)

ëŒ€ي™” ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ى—گى„œ transcriptë¥¼ ى¶”ى¶œي•کëگک contextual user messageëٹ” ي•„ي„°ë§پي•کê³ , ي† يپ° ىکˆى‚° ë‚´ى—گى„œ ى‍کë‌¼ë‚¸ ë’¤ planned action JSONê³¼ ي•©ى„±ي•œë‹¤:

>>> TRANSCRIPT START
[1] user: ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ىڑ”ى²­ ë‚´ىڑ©
[2] tool shell call: {"command": "..."}
[3] assistant: ى‌‘ë‹µ ë‚´ىڑ©
>>> TRANSCRIPT END

GUARDIAN_MAX_MESSAGE_TRANSCRIPT_TOKENS â€” user/assistant ë©”ى‹œى§€ ىکˆى‚°
GUARDIAN_MAX_TOOL_TRANSCRIPT_TOKENS    â€” tool call/result ىکˆى‚°

// codex.rs:3597-3604
if separate_guardian_developer_message
    && let Some(developer_instructions) = turn_context.developer_instructions.as_deref()

ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œى‌€ Askama ي…œي”Œë¦؟ ى—”ى§„ىœ¼ë،œ ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸ë¥¼ ë Œëچ”ë§پي•œë‹¤.

<!-- core/templates/memories/read_path.md (ىڑ”ى•½) -->
## Memory
You have access to a memory folder with guidance from prior runs.
Memory layout:
- {{ base_path }}/memory_summary.md
- {{ base_path }}/MEMORY.md (searchable registry)
- {{ base_path }}/skills/<skill-name>/
- {{ base_path }}/rollout_summaries/

========= MEMORY_SUMMARY BEGINS =========
{{ memory_summary }}
========= MEMORY_SUMMARY ENDS =========

ي† يپ° ى œي•œ: MEMORY_TOOL_DEVELOPER_INSTRUCTIONS_SUMMARY_TOKEN_LIMITىœ¼ë،œ memory_summaryë¥¼ truncation.

<!-- core/templates/memories/stage_one_input.md -->
rollout_context:
- rollout_path: {{ rollout_path }}
- rollout_cwd: {{ rollout_cwd }}

rendered conversation:
{{ rollout_contents }}

ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ ë‚´ىڑ©ى‌€ ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌ک context_windowى‌ک effective_context_window_percentى—گى„œ ى¶”ê°€ë،œ CONTEXT_WINDOW_PERCENT ë¹„ىœ¨ى‌„ ى پىڑ©ي•œ ي† يپ° ىˆکê¹Œى§€ë§Œ truncationëگœë‹¤.

Context Compaction (ëŒ€ي™” ى••ى¶•)

context windowê°€ ë¶€ى،±ي•´ى§€ë©´ ى‍گëڈ™ compactionى‌´ ى‹¤ي–‰ëگœë‹¤:

// core/src/compact.rs:31-33
pub const SUMMARIZATION_PROMPT: &str = include_str!("../templates/compact/prompt.md");
pub const SUMMARY_PREFIX: &str = include_str!("../templates/compact/summary_prefix.md");

compaction ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸:

<!-- core/templates/compact/prompt.md -->
You are performing a CONTEXT CHECKPOINT COMPACTION.
Create a handoff summary for another LLM that will resume the task.
Include:
- Current progress and key decisions made
- Important context, constraints, or user preferences
- What remains to be done (clear next steps)
- Any critical data, examples, or references needed to continue

compactionى‌€ ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ ëھ¨ë“œê°€ ى‍ˆë‹¤:

  • Pre-turn/Manual: InitialContextInjection::DoNotInject â€” ىڑ”ى•½ ي›„ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ë¥¼ êµگى²´ي•کê³ , ë‹¤ى‌Œ ي„´ى—گى„œ initial contextë¥¼ ى‍¬ى£¼ى‍…
  • Mid-turn: InitialContextInjection::BeforeLastUserMessage â€” ىڑ”ى•½ ë’¤ى—گëڈ„ ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ user message ى•‍ى—گ initial contextë¥¼ ى‚½ى‍…

Context Window ê´€ë¦¬

ىœ يڑ¨ context window ê³„ى‚°

// core/src/codex.rs:871-876
pub(crate) fn model_context_window(&self) -> Option<i64> {
    let effective_context_window_percent = self.model_info.effective_context_window_percent;
    self.model_info.context_window.map(|context_window| {
        context_window.saturating_mul(effective_context_window_percent) / 100
    })
}

context_window * effective_context_window_percent / 100ىœ¼ë،œ ى‹¤ى œ ى‚¬ىڑ© ê°€ëٹ¥ ي† يپ°ى‌„ ى‚°ى¶œي•œë‹¤. ê¸°ë³¸ê°’ى‌€ context_window=272_000, effective_context_window_percent=95ى‌´ë¯€ë،œ ى•½ 258,400 ي† يپ°.

ي† يپ° ى‚¬ىڑ©ëں‰ ى¶”ى پ

ë§¤ ي„´ ى¢…ë£Œ ى‹œ update_token_info_from_usage()ë،œ يک„ى‍¬ ي† يپ° ى‚¬ىڑ©ëں‰ى‌„ ê°±ى‹ . context window ى´ˆê³¼ ى‹œ set_token_usage_full()ë،œ compactionى‌„ يٹ¸ë¦¬ê±°ي•œë‹¤. ى›گê²© compact(compact_remote.rs)ى—گى„œëٹ” trim_function_call_history_to_fit_context_window()ë،œ function call ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ë¥¼ ى‍کë‌¼ë‚¸ë‹¤.

// router.rs â€” build_tool_call ي•µى‹¬ ë¶„ê¸°
match item {
    ResponseItem::FunctionCall { name, namespace, arguments, call_id, .. } => {
        // MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ى‌¸ى§€ ë¨¼ى € ê²€ى‚¬
        if let Some((server, tool)) = session.parse_mcp_tool_name(&name, &namespace).await {
            // â†’ ToolPayload::Mcp { server, tool, raw_arguments }
        } else {
            // â†’ ToolPayload::Function { arguments }
        }
    }
    ResponseItem::ToolSearchCall { call_id, execution: "client", arguments, .. } => {
        // â†’ ToolPayload::ToolSearch { arguments }
    }
    ResponseItem::CustomToolCall { name, input, call_id, .. } => {
        // â†’ ToolPayload::Custom { input }
    }
    ResponseItem::LocalShellCall { call_id, action: LocalShellAction::Exec(exec), .. } => {
        // â†’ ToolPayload::LocalShell { params }
    }
    _ => Ok(None),  // ى²کë¦¬ ë¶ˆê°€ itemى‌€ ë¬´ى‹œ
}

// registry.rs â€” handler lookupê³¼ namespace ي‚¤ ىƒ‌ى„±
pub(crate) fn tool_handler_key(tool_name: &str, namespace: Option<&str>) -> String {
    if let Some(namespace) = namespace {
        format!("{namespace}:{tool_name}")
    } else {
        tool_name.to_string()
    }
}

README / docs

// parallel.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ ë³‘ë ¬ ى œى–´ ë،œى§پ
tokio::select! {
    _ = cancellation_token.cancelled() => {
        Ok(Self::aborted_response(&call, secs))
    },
    res = async {
        let _guard = if supports_parallel {
            Either::Left(lock.read().await)      // RwLock read
        } else {
            Either::Right(lock.write().await)     // RwLock write
        };
        router.dispatch_tool_call_with_code_mode_result(
            session, turn, tracker, call.clone(), source,
        ).await
    } => res,
}

README / docs

sequenceDiagram
    participant Model as ëھ¨ëچ¸
    participant Router as ToolRouter
    participant Runtime as ToolCallRuntime
    participant Registry as ToolRegistry
    participant Hook as Hook System
    participant Handler as handler

    Model->>Router: ResponseItem (FunctionCall ë“±)
    Router->>Router: build_tool_call() â†’ ToolCall
    Router->>Runtime: handle_tool_call(call, cancellation_token)
    Runtime->>Runtime: RwLock read/write ê²Œى‌´يŒ…
    Runtime->>Registry: dispatch_any(invocation)
    Registry->>Registry: handler lookup + kind ê²€ى¦‌
    Registry->>Hook: pre_tool_use hook (shellë§Œ)
    Hook-->>Registry: allow / block
    Registry->>Handler: handle(invocation)
    Handler-->>Registry: ToolOutput
    Registry->>Hook: after_tool_use hook
    Hook-->>Registry: continue / abort
    Registry-->>Runtime: AnyToolResult
    Runtime-->>Model: ResponseInputItem

README / docs

// registry.rs â€” ToolRegistryBuilder
pub struct ToolRegistryBuilder {
    handlers: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn AnyToolHandler>>,
    specs: Vec<ConfiguredToolSpec>,
}

impl ToolRegistryBuilder {
    pub fn register_handler<H>(&mut self, name: impl Into<String>, handler: Arc<H>)
    where H: ToolHandler + 'static {
        let name = name.into();
        let handler: Arc<dyn AnyToolHandler> = handler;
        if self.handlers.insert(name.clone(), handler.clone()).is_some() {
            warn!("overwriting handler for tool {name}");
        }
    }

    pub fn build(self) -> (Vec<ConfiguredToolSpec>, ToolRegistry) {
        let registry = ToolRegistry::new(self.handlers);
        (self.specs, registry)
    }
}

README / docs

// spec.rs â€” MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ ë“±ë،‌
if let Some(mcp_tools) = mcp_tools {
    let mut entries: Vec<(String, Tool)> = mcp_tools.into_iter().collect();
    entries.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.cmp(&b.0));
    for (name, tool) in entries {
        match mcp_tool_to_openai_tool(name.clone(), tool) {
            Ok(converted_tool) => {
                builder.push_spec(ToolSpec::Function(converted_tool));
                builder.register_handler(name, mcp_handler.clone());
            }
            Err(e) => { tracing::error!("Failed to convert MCP tool: {e:?}"); }
        }
    }
}

README / docs

// spec.rs â€” Dynamic tool ë“±ë،‌
for tool in dynamic_tools {
    match dynamic_tool_to_openai_tool(tool) {
        Ok(converted_tool) => {
            builder.push_spec(ToolSpec::Function(converted_tool));
            builder.register_handler(tool.name.clone(), dynamic_tool_handler.clone());
        }
        Err(e) => { tracing::error!("Failed to convert dynamic tool: {e:?}"); }
    }
}

README / docs

// registry.rs
#[async_trait]
pub trait ToolHandler: Send + Sync {
    type Output: ToolOutput + 'static;

    fn kind(&self) -> ToolKind;                      // Function | Mcp

    fn matches_kind(&self, payload: &ToolPayload) -> bool { /* ê¸°ë³¸ êµ¬يک„ */ }

    async fn is_mutating(&self, _invocation: &ToolInvocation) -> bool {
        false  // ê¸°ë³¸ê°’: ë¹„يŒŒê´´ى پ
    }

    async fn handle(&self, invocation: ToolInvocation) -> Result<Self::Output, FunctionCallError>;
}

// contributors.rs
pub trait ToolContributor: Send + Sync {
    fn tools(
        &self,
        session_store: &ExtensionData,
        thread_store: &ExtensionData,
    ) -> Vec<Arc<dyn ToolExecutor<ToolCall>>>;
}

Main analysis

Tool registry and execution loop

Analysis snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” core/src/tools/router.rs, tools/, apply-patch, parallel dispatch.

Codex's tool system is not a simple structure like "when the model says a function name, a handler is immediately executed." In reality, there is a router that changes ResponseItem to ToolCall, and the router finds an appropriate handler through the registry and processes pre-tool hooks, mutating decisions, metrics, cancellation, and parallel gating during execution.

The reason this structure is important is because even if we discard the CLI, the tool runtime can be reused almost as is. In Codex, tools are core engine functions, not UI functions. It is particularly important that shell, apply_patch, request_user_input, mcp, view_image, dynamic, multi_agents, and plan lie under the same registry layer.

1. Full dispatch flow

1.1 Overview

When a model issues a tool call, Codex core processes it in the following order:

  1. ToolRouter::build_tool_call() â€” Interprets the ResponseItem variant (FunctionCall / ToolSearchCall / CustomToolCall / LocalShellCall) and converts it to the ToolCall { tool_name, tool_namespace, call_id, payload } structure.
  2. ToolCallRuntime::handle_tool_call() â€” Schedules dispatch while managing parallel/serial gating and cancellation.
  3. ToolRouter::dispatch_tool_call_with_code_mode_result() â†’ ToolRegistry::dispatch_any() â€” This is a â€œpolicy-inclusive execution pipelineâ€‌ that executes handler lookup, payload kind verification, pre-tool-use hook execution, mutating decision, telemetry, and after-tool-use hook.
  4. ToolHandler::handle() â€” Individual handlers perform the actual logic.
graph TD
    A["model ResponseItem"] --> B["ToolRouter::build_tool_call()"]
    B --> C["ToolCall"]
    C --> D["ToolCallRuntime::handle_tool_call()"]
    D --> E{"parallel?"}
    E -- "read lock" --> F["ToolRouter::dispatch_tool_call_with_code_mode_result()"]
    E -- "write lock" --> F
    F --> G["ToolRegistry::dispatch_any()"]
    G --> H["pre-tool-use hook"]
    H --> I["ToolHandler::handle()"]
    I --> J["after-tool-use hook"]
    J --> K["AnyToolResult â†’ ResponseInputItem"]

1.2 ToolRouter::build_tool_call() â€” item interpretation details

build_tool_call() is a static method and determines the payload of ToolCall according to the ResponseItem enum transformation of the model response.

// router.rs â€” build_tool_call ي•µى‹¬ ë¶„ê¸°
match item {
    ResponseItem::FunctionCall { name, namespace, arguments, call_id, .. } => {
        // MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ى‌¸ى§€ ë¨¼ى € ê²€ى‚¬
        if let Some((server, tool)) = session.parse_mcp_tool_name(&name, &namespace).await {
            // â†’ ToolPayload::Mcp { server, tool, raw_arguments }
        } else {
            // â†’ ToolPayload::Function { arguments }
        }
    }
    ResponseItem::ToolSearchCall { call_id, execution: "client", arguments, .. } => {
        // â†’ ToolPayload::ToolSearch { arguments }
    }
    ResponseItem::CustomToolCall { name, input, call_id, .. } => {
        // â†’ ToolPayload::Custom { input }
    }
    ResponseItem::LocalShellCall { call_id, action: LocalShellAction::Exec(exec), .. } => {
        // â†’ ToolPayload::LocalShell { params }
    }
    _ => Ok(None),  // ى²کë¦¬ ë¶ˆê°€ itemى‌€ ë¬´ى‹œ
}

Note: Even if FunctionCall comes in, session.parse_mcp_tool_name() first checks the MCP namespace and switches to ToolPayload::Mcp. In other words, MCP tools come in FunctionCall form, but are separated at the payload stage.

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/router.rs

1.3 ToolRegistry::dispatch_any() â€” Policy execution pipeline

The reason dispatch_any() is not a simple dispatch is that it goes through all 7 steps:

1. tool_calls ى¹´ىڑ´ي„° ى¦‌ê°€ (active_turn.turn_state)
2. handler lookup â€” tool_handler_key(name, namespace)ë،œ HashMap ى،°يڑŒ
3. payload kind ê²€ى¦‌ â€” handler.matches_kind(payload)
4. pre-tool-use hook â€” shell/exec/local_shell ê³„ى—´ى‌´ë©´ ëھ…ë ¹ ë¬¸ى‍گى—´ ى¶”ى¶œ ي›„ hook ى‹¤ي–‰
5. is_mutating يŒگى • â€” mutatingى‌´ë©´ tool_call_gate.wait_ready() ëŒ€ê¸°
6. handler.handle_any(invocation) â€” ى‹¤ى œ ى‹¤ي–‰, telemetry ى‍گëڈ™ ê³„ى¸،
7. after-tool-use hook â€” ى„±ê³µ/ى‹¤يŒ¨/duration/mutating ى •ë³´ë¥¼ hookى—گ ى „ë‹¬

If there is no handler, unsupported_tool_call_message is returned to the model. If the payload kind does not match, the entire turn is stopped with a Fatal error.

// registry.rs â€” handler lookupê³¼ namespace ي‚¤ ىƒ‌ى„±
pub(crate) fn tool_handler_key(tool_name: &str, namespace: Option<&str>) -> String {
    if let Some(namespace) = namespace {
        format!("{namespace}:{tool_name}")
    } else {
        tool_name.to_string()
    }
}

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/registry.rs

1.4 ToolCallRuntime â€” Parallel control and cancellation

ToolCallRuntime uses Arc<RwLock<()>> to control parallel execution:

  • **Handler with supports_parallel_tool_calls = true â†’ lock.read() (multiple handlers can run simultaneously)
  • handler with supports_parallel_tool_calls = false â†’ lock.write() (exclusive execution)

Cancellation is handled with tokio::select!. When CancellationToken is cancelled, it returns AbortedToolOutput immediately without waiting for the handler.

// parallel.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ ë³‘ë ¬ ى œى–´ ë،œى§پ
tokio::select! {
    _ = cancellation_token.cancelled() => {
        Ok(Self::aborted_response(&call, secs))
    },
    res = async {
        let _guard = if supports_parallel {
            Either::Left(lock.read().await)      // RwLock read
        } else {
            Either::Right(lock.write().await)     // RwLock write
        };
        router.dispatch_tool_call_with_code_mode_result(
            session, turn, tracker, call.clone(), source,
        ).await
    } => res,
}

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/parallel.rs

sequenceDiagram
    participant Model as ëھ¨ëچ¸
    participant Router as ToolRouter
    participant Runtime as ToolCallRuntime
    participant Registry as ToolRegistry
    participant Hook as Hook System
    participant Handler as handler

    Model->>Router: ResponseItem (FunctionCall ë“±)
    Router->>Router: build_tool_call() â†’ ToolCall
    Router->>Runtime: handle_tool_call(call, cancellation_token)
    Runtime->>Runtime: RwLock read/write ê²Œى‌´يŒ…
    Runtime->>Registry: dispatch_any(invocation)
    Registry->>Registry: handler lookup + kind ê²€ى¦‌
    Registry->>Hook: pre_tool_use hook (shellë§Œ)
    Hook-->>Registry: allow / block
    Registry->>Handler: handle(invocation)
    Handler-->>Registry: ToolOutput
    Registry->>Hook: after_tool_use hook
    Hook-->>Registry: continue / abort
    Registry-->>Runtime: AnyToolResult
    Runtime-->>Model: ResponseInputItem

2. Handler full catalog

2.1 handlers/ directory module listing

Full file structure under 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/:

module file Role

The entire mapping registered with ToolRegistryBuilder::register_handler() in the build_specs_with_discoverable_tools() function:

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/spec.rs (build_specs_with_discoverable_tools() function)

2.3 Classification of handler series

Series handlers Characteristics
execute shell ShellHandler, ShellCommandHandler, UnifiedExecHandler Run local process, contains intercept_apply_patch(), is_mutating = !is_known_safe_command()
File Navigation ReadFileHandler, ListDirHandler, GrepFilesHandler experimental tools, parallel support, read-only
Code Patch ApplyPatchHandler freeform/function two formats, serial execution only
External Tools McpHandler, McpResourceHandler, ViewImageHandler External system integration
Dialogue/Control PlanHandler, RequestUserInputHandler, RequestPermissionsHandler User Interaction and Planning Management
Tool Discovery ToolSearchHandler, ToolSuggestHandler app_tools/discoverable_tools Search and Recommendations
Dynamic Tools DynamicToolHandler Client injects at runtime
Sub Agent v1 5 types + v2 5 types + BatchJobHandler Multi-agent collaboration
Code Mode CodeModeExecuteHandler, CodeModeWaitHandler Run nested tool
REPL JsReplHandler, JsReplResetHandler JavaScript execution environment
Artifact ArtifactsHandler Artifact creation/management
Test TestSyncHandler Experimental synchronization tool

3. Dynamic tool registration mechanism

3.1 ToolRegistryBuilder pattern

Codex requires that the tool configuration can be changed at runtime, so it uses the ToolRegistryBuilder â†’ ToolRegistry builder pattern. The key is that register_handler() puts the name-handler mapping into HashMap<String, Arc<dyn AnyToolHandler>>, and build() converts it into an immutable ToolRegistry.

// registry.rs â€” ToolRegistryBuilder
pub struct ToolRegistryBuilder {
    handlers: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn AnyToolHandler>>,
    specs: Vec<ConfiguredToolSpec>,
}

impl ToolRegistryBuilder {
    pub fn register_handler<H>(&mut self, name: impl Into<String>, handler: Arc<H>)
    where H: ToolHandler + 'static {
        let name = name.into();
        let handler: Arc<dyn AnyToolHandler> = handler;
        if self.handlers.insert(name.clone(), handler.clone()).is_some() {
            warn!("overwriting handler for tool {name}");
        }
    }

    pub fn build(self) -> (Vec<ConfiguredToolSpec>, ToolRegistry) {
        let registry = ToolRegistry::new(self.handlers);
        (self.specs, registry)
    }
}

There is a register_many() method left as a comment, which can be used for batch registration of dynamic tools in the future.

3.2 MCP tool dynamic registration

The MCP tool iterates through the entire mcp_tools: HashMap<String, Tool> in build_specs_with_discoverable_tools() and registers each as mcp_tool_to_openai_tool() â†’ register_handler(name, mcp_handler.clone()). The same McpHandler instance is shared by all MCP tools.

// spec.rs â€” MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ ë“±ë،‌
if let Some(mcp_tools) = mcp_tools {
    let mut entries: Vec<(String, Tool)> = mcp_tools.into_iter().collect();
    entries.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.cmp(&b.0));
    for (name, tool) in entries {
        match mcp_tool_to_openai_tool(name.clone(), tool) {
            Ok(converted_tool) => {
                builder.push_spec(ToolSpec::Function(converted_tool));
                builder.register_handler(name, mcp_handler.clone());
            }
            Err(e) => { tracing::error!("Failed to convert MCP tool: {e:?}"); }
        }
    }
}

3.3 Dynamic Tool Registration

Dynamic tools follow a similar pattern. Iterate over the DynamicToolSpec array and register each with dynamic_tool_to_openai_tool() â†’ register_handler(tool.name, dynamic_tool_handler.clone()).

// spec.rs â€” Dynamic tool ë“±ë،‌
for tool in dynamic_tools {
    match dynamic_tool_to_openai_tool(tool) {
        Ok(converted_tool) => {
            builder.push_spec(ToolSpec::Function(converted_tool));
            builder.register_handler(tool.name.clone(), dynamic_tool_handler.clone());
        }
        Err(e) => { tracing::error!("Failed to convert dynamic tool: {e:?}"); }
    }
}

3.4 Differences between dynamic tools and MCP tools

Item dynamic tool MCP tool
definition location Client registers as app-server Provided by MCP Server
core payload ToolPayload::Function â†’ DynamicToolHandler ToolPayload::Mcp
inside handler Send DynamicToolCallRequest event to app-server through oneshot channel, wait for response RPC to MCP server with handle_mcp_tool_call()
Event DynamicToolCallRequest/Response McpToolCallBegin/End
Execution entity app-server â†’ client MCP Server Process
History item ThreadItem::DynamicToolCall ThreadItem::McpToolCall
is_mutating always true default false

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/dynamic.rs, mcp.rs

4. ToolHandler trait and ToolPayload type

4.1 ToolHandler trait

Core traits that every handler must implement:

// registry.rs
#[async_trait]
pub trait ToolHandler: Send + Sync {
    type Output: ToolOutput + 'static;

    fn kind(&self) -> ToolKind;                      // Function | Mcp

    fn matches_kind(&self, payload: &ToolPayload) -> bool { /* ê¸°ë³¸ êµ¬يک„ */ }

    async fn is_mutating(&self, _invocation: &ToolInvocation) -> bool {
        false  // ê¸°ë³¸ê°’: ë¹„يŒŒê´´ى پ
    }

    async fn handle(&self, invocation: ToolInvocation) -> Result<Self::Output, FunctionCallError>;
}

ToolKind is Function | There are only two values: Mcp. The default implementation of matches_kind() allows only the combinations (Function, ToolPayload::Function), (Function, ToolPayload::ToolSearch), and (Mcp, ToolPayload::Mcp).

4.2 ToolPayload enum

// context.rs
pub enum ToolPayload {
    Function { arguments: String },           // JSON ë¬¸ى‍گى—´
    ToolSearch { arguments: SearchToolCallParams },
    Custom { input: String },
    LocalShell { params: ShellToolCallParams },
    Mcp { server: String, tool: String, raw_arguments: String },
}

4.3 ToolInvocation structure

All context bundles passed to the handler:

// context.rs
pub struct ToolInvocation {
    pub session: Arc<Session>,
    pub turn: Arc<TurnContext>,
    pub tracker: SharedTurnDiffTracker,
    pub call_id: String,
    pub tool_name: String,
    pub tool_namespace: Option<String>,
    pub payload: ToolPayload,
}

5. Orchestrator â€” Authorize/Sandbox/Retry

ToolOrchestrator is a separate approval-sandbox-execution pipeline used inside shell and unified_exec family handler. It is a separate layer from the dispatch_any() policy pipeline of ToolRegistry.

Orchestrator ى‹¤ي–‰ ى‹œي€€ىٹ¤:
1. Approval â€” ExecApprovalRequirement::NeedsApproval â†’ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ/Guardian ىٹ¹ى‌¸
2. Sandbox ى„ يƒ‌ â€” SandboxManager::select_initial()
3. 1ى°¨ ى‹œëڈ„ â€” ToolRuntime::run() + network approval
4. ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„ â€” sandbox ى—گىٹ¤ى»¬ë ˆى‌´ى…ک ي›„ ى‍¬ى‹¤ي–‰ (approve ى؛گى‹œ ى‍¬ى‚¬ىڑ©)

// orchestrator.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ êµ¬ى،°
pub(crate) struct ToolOrchestrator {
    sandbox: SandboxManager,
}

impl ToolOrchestrator {
    pub async fn run<Rq, Out, T>(
        &mut self, tool: &mut T, req: &Rq,
        tool_ctx: &ToolCtx, turn_ctx: &TurnContext,
        approval_policy: AskForApproval,
    ) -> Result<OrchestratorRunResult<Out>, ToolError> {
        // 1) Approval ë‹¨ê³„
        // 2) SandboxAttempt ى„ يƒ‌
        // 3) run_attempt() â€” ى‹¤ي–‰ + network approval
        // 4) sandbox ى‹¤يŒ¨ ى‹œ ى—گىٹ¤ى»¬ë ˆى‌´ى…ک ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„
    }
}

The concrete runtime implementing the ToolRuntime trait is located in the runtimes/ directory:

runtime file Use
Shell runtime runtimes/shell.rs + runtimes/shell/ Used by ShellHandler, ShellCommandHandler
Unified exec runtime runtimes/unified_exec.rs UnifiedExecHandler uses
Apply patch runtime runtimes/apply_patch.rs ApplyPatchHandler uses

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/orchestrator.rs, runtimes/mod.rs

6. Network Approval Mechanism

6.1 Structure

NetworkApprovalService manages approval/rejection of network requests that occur during tool execution. Within the sandbox, implement an inline approval flow that asks the user for requests blocked by a network proxy.

// network_approval.rs
pub(crate) struct NetworkApprovalService {
    active_calls: Mutex<IndexMap<String, Arc<ActiveNetworkApprovalCall>>>,
    call_outcomes: Mutex<HashMap<String, NetworkApprovalOutcome>>,
    pending_host_approvals: Mutex<HashMap<HostApprovalKey, Arc<PendingHostApproval>>>,
    session_approved_hosts: Mutex<HashSet<HostApprovalKey>>,
    session_denied_hosts: Mutex<HashSet<HostApprovalKey>>,
}

6.2 Approval Mode

pub(crate) enum NetworkApprovalMode {
    Immediate,  // ى‹¤ي–‰ ى§پي›„ ى¦‰ى‹œ ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ê²°ê³¼ ي™•ى‌¸
    Deferred,   // ى‹¤ي–‰ ى™„ë£Œ ي›„ ë‚کى¤‘ى—گ ي™•ى‌¸ (exit code ê¸°ë°ک يŒگë‹¨)
}

6.3 Inline policy decision flow

When handle_inline_policy_request() is called:

  1. Check session_denied_hosts â†’ If the host has already been denied, immediately call NetworkDecision::deny
  2. Check session_approved_hosts â†’ If the host has already been approved, immediately NetworkDecision::Allow
  3. Create pending approval â†’ Duplicate requests for the same host are joined to a single approval
  4. Guardian or user approval â†’ Immediate rejection if AskForApproval::Never
  5. Result Caching â†’ If AllowForSession, applies to the entire session.
// HostApprovalKey â€” ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ى؛گى‹œ ي‚¤
struct HostApprovalKey {
    host: String,           // ى†Œë¬¸ى‍گ ى •ê·œي™”
    protocol: &'static str, // "http" | "https" | "socks5-tcp" | "socks5-udp"
    port: u16,
}

6.4 Integration with Orchestrator

ToolOrchestrator::run_attempt() calls begin_network_approval() before execution, and after execution it calls finish_immediate_network_approval() or finish_deferred_network_approval() depending on the mode.

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/network_approval.rs

7. Unified Exec Framework

7.1 Overview

UnifiedExecHandler is a PTY-based long-running process management tool. Two subfunctions are processed in one handler:

  • exec_command â€” Creates a new process, allocates a PTY, returns intermediate output after yield_time_ms
  • write_stdin â€” Send stdin input to existing session, collect output
// handlers/unified_exec.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ يŒŒë‌¼ë¯¸ي„°
struct ExecCommandArgs {
    cmd: String,
    workdir: Option<String>,
    shell: Option<String>,        // ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌´ ى…¸ ê²½ë،œ ى§€ى • ê°€ëٹ¥
    login: Option<bool>,          // -lc ëھ¨ë“œ
    tty: bool,                    // default false â†’ PTY ي• ë‹¹ ى—¬ë¶€
    yield_time_ms: u64,           // default 10_000ms
    max_output_tokens: Option<usize>,
    sandbox_permissions: SandboxPermissions,
    additional_permissions: Option<PermissionProfile>,
    justification: Option<String>,
    prefix_rule: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

7.2 Execution flow

1. resolve_workdir_base_path() â€” ى‍‘ى—… ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬ ê²°ى •
2. parse_arguments_with_base_path() â€” ى‌¸ى‍گ يŒŒى‹± (ê²½ë،œ ى •ê·œي™” يڈ¬ي•¨)
3. maybe_emit_implicit_skill_invocation() â€” ىٹ¤ي‚¬ telemetry
4. manager.allocate_process_id() â€” ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ID ي• ë‹¹
5. get_command() â€” ShellModeى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ command ë²،ي„° ىƒ‌ى„±
6. intercept_apply_patch() â€” apply_patch ëھ…ë ¹ ê°€ë،œى±„ê¸°
7. apply_granted_turn_permissions() â€” ي„´ ë ˆë²¨ ê¶Œي•œ ى پىڑ©
8. normalize_and_validate_additional_permissions() â€” ى¶”ê°€ ê¶Œي•œ ê²€ى¦‌
9. manager.exec_command() â€” UnifiedExecProcessManagerى—گ ىœ„ى‍„

7.3 Shell mode

pub enum UnifiedExecShellMode {
    Direct,             // ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ê¸°ë³¸ ى…¸ ى‚¬ىڑ©
    ZshFork(ZshForkConfig),  // codex ى „ىڑ© zsh fork ى‚¬ىڑ©
}

In Direct mode, the model can specify a different shell with the shell parameter. In ZshFork mode, a separate zsh binary and execve wrapper are used.

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/unified_exec.rs

8. Tool selection based on ToolsConfig and Feature Flag

ToolsConfig receives ToolsConfigParams (model information + feature flags + sandbox policy) and determines which tool to activate. Main feature flag:

Feature Flag Tools to control
Feature::ShellTool All shell families
Feature::UnifiedExec exec_command / write_stdin
Feature::ShellZshFork ShellCommand backend (ZshFork)
Feature::ApplyPatchFreeform apply_patch freeform format
Feature::CodeMode codex / codex_wait
Feature::CodeModeOnly code_mode_only mode
Feature::JsRepl js_repl / js_repl_reset
Feature::Collab spawn_agent, wait_agent, etc.
Feature::MultiAgentV2 v2 multiagent tool
Feature::SpawnCsv spawn_agents_on_csv
Feature::ToolSearch tool_search
Feature::ToolSuggest tool_suggest
Feature::ExecPermissionApprovals sandbox_permissions extension
Feature::RequestPermissionsTool request_permissions
Feature::Artifact artifacts
Feature::ImageGeneration image_generation
Feature::DefaultModeRequestUserInput request_user_input (default mode)

Shell tool type determination logic (ConfigShellToolType):

ShellZshFork ي™œى„± â†’ ShellCommand (ZshFork ë°±ى—”ë“œ)
UnifiedExec ي™œى„± + ConPTY ى§€ى›گ â†’ UnifiedExec
ê·¸ ى™¸ â†’ ëھ¨ëچ¸ ê¸°ë³¸ê°’ ëکگëٹ” ShellCommand fallback

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/spec.rs

9. tools/ module overall structure

Complete module configuration in directory 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/:

module file responsibility
mod.rs Top level re-export, output formatting telemetry constants, format_exec_output_*()
router.rs ToolRouter item â†’ ToolCall conversion, dispatch delegation
registry.rs ToolRegistry, ToolRegistryBuilder handler mapping, dispatch_any() policy pipeline
parallel.rs ToolCallRuntime Parallel/series gating, cancellation
spec.rs ToolsConfig, build_specs_with_discoverable_tools() Create tool spec, register handler
context.rs ToolInvocation, ToolPayload, ToolOutput trait calling context type
orchestrator.rs ToolOrchestrator Approve â†’ Sandbox â†’ Execute â†’ Retry
network_approval.rs NetworkApprovalService Network Request Approval Management
sandboxing.rs ToolRuntime trait, ApprovalStore, SandboxAttempt Approval/Sandbox Share trait
events.rs ToolEmitter, ToolEventCtx issue tool execution event
code_mode.rs CodeModeExecuteHandler, CodeModeWaitHandler code mode wrapper
code_mode_description.rs code mode specification augmentation
discoverable.rs DiscoverableTool, DiscoverableToolType connector/plugin discovery
runtimes/ shell.rs, unified_exec.rs, apply_patch.rs concrete ToolRuntime implementation
handlers/ 22+ modules Individual tool handler implementation
js_repl/ JavaScript REPL Infrastructure

10. Relationship between apply_patch and shell

Although apply_patch seems like a completely separate tool, actual shell family handlers (ShellHandler, ShellCommandHandler, UnifiedExecHandler) also intercept apply_patch in the command string through intercept_apply_patch(). That is, Codex attempts to handle patches on both the surfaces of "normal shell calls" and "dedicated patch tools." When forking, you must first decide whether you need a dual surface.

11. Connection between implicit skill invocation and tool execution

The shell and unified_exec handlers call maybe_emit_implicit_skill_invocation() immediately before execution. However, this function currently does not inject the skill body back into the prompt, but only leaves telemetry that the skill was implicitly used by looking at the command path. Just looking at the name, it looks like automatic skill activation, but the actual implementation is closer to usage tracking.

12. Fork seam / checklist

  • View ToolRouter and ToolRegistry as separate core layers
  • handler is viewed as an engine function, not a product UI
  • Distinguish between surface differences between dynamic tools and MCP tools.
  • Must determine whether apply_patch is a double entry point.
  • spec + router + registry + parallel runtime should be viewed as a set
  • shell + unified_exec + apply_patch is a bundle of local execution cores
  • mcp + mcp_tool_call + protocol events must be taken together
  • dynamic handler + app-server bridge is required for client-driven tool surface
  • Since ToolOrchestrator bundles approval-sandbox-retry, it is taken separately from the handler.
  • The core of NetworkApprovalService is the per-session host cache.
  • You need to decide whether to keep the pre-tool-use hook or simplify it.
  • You must decide whether to use the parallel execution lock policy as is or redesign it.
  • tool_suggest, legacy approval API, and multi_agents_v2 can be postponed to 2nd priority.
  • UnifiedExecShellMode::ZshFork is only for macOS/Zsh, so a fallback to Direct is required in Linux environments.

List of core files

Core Infrastructure

  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/mod.rs â€” Module root, output formatting

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Tool registry Paths cited in-body Tool names/APIs evolve per release â€” re-verify on upgrade
Execution pipeline Documented in this checkout Host/binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

Change history

  • 2026-03-26: The actual execution paths of tool router, registry, runtime, dynamic tool, and MCP tool were organized.
  • 2026-03-26: Summary and detailed version were combined.
  • 2025-07-17: Added full handler catalog (22+ modules, registration name correspondence table). Detailed documentation of the ToolRouter â†’ ToolRegistry â†’ handler dispatch flow with code snippets. Elaboration of dynamic tool registration mechanism (ToolRegistryBuilder pattern, MCP/Dynamic registration method). Added new section NetworkApprovalService (inline policy decisions, per-host caching, Orchestrator integration). Added detailed analysis of Unified Exec framework (exec_command/write_stdin, ShellMode, execution flow). Added ToolOrchestrator (authorization-sandbox-retry) section. Organized tool selection logic based on ToolsConfig and Feature Flag. The overall structure of the tools/ module was also added. Fork seam checklist update.
  • 2026-05-30: ToolExposure::Deferred, Extension API ToolContributor, Image Generation extension, Goal Steering reflect tool-level integration.

Latest update (2026-05-30)

This section summarizes the four major changes affecting the tool runtime in the most recent 412 commits (since 2025-07) in the Codex CLI repository.

A. ToolExposure::Deferred and deferred_mcp_tools

The tool exposure policy has been expanded from the existing Direct/Hidden dichotomy to 4-level enum.

value Meaning
Direct Included in the initial model-visible list, also used as a code mode nested tool
Deferred Excluded from the initial list, only exposed when searching with tool_search
DirectModelOnly Expose directly to the model, but not on code-mode nested surfaces
Hidden dispatch only â€” spec not exposed in model

The core motivation for introducing Deferred is to exclude all MCP tools from the initial prompt and only discover them via tool_search if the number of MCP tools exceeds DIRECT_MCP_TOOL_EXPOSURE_THRESHOLD (currently 100) or the ToolSearchAlwaysDeferMcpTools feature flag is active. build_mcp_tool_exposure() returns a McpToolExposure { direct_tools, deferred_tools } structure, which is delivered to the router through ToolRouterParams::deferred_mcp_tools.

In spec_plan.rs, the deferred MCP tool is created with McpHandler::new() and registered with planned_tools.add_with_exposure(handler, ToolExposure::Deferred). Afterwards, search_info() of all runtimes where append_tool_search_executor() is ToolExposure::Deferred is collected and injected into ToolSearchHandler.

ًں“پ codex-rs/tools/src/tool_executor.rs (enum definition) ًں“پ codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_exposure.rs (build_mcp_tool_exposure()) ًں“پ codex-rs/core/src/tools/spec_plan.rs (deferred registration + tool_search integration)

B. Extension API â€” ToolContributor and TurnItemEmitter

The new Extension API (codex-rs/ext/extension-api/) provides a ToolContributor trait that allows extensions to dynamically register native tools.

// contributors.rs
pub trait ToolContributor: Send + Sync {
    fn tools(
        &self,
        session_store: &ExtensionData,
        thread_store: &ExtensionData,
    ) -> Vec<Arc<dyn ToolExecutor<ToolCall>>>;
}

If you register tool_contributor() in ExtensionRegistryBuilder, tool_contributors() will be called every turn to recalculate the list of active tools. On the core side, ExtensionToolAdapter wraps ToolExecutor<ExtensionToolCall> with CoreToolRuntime and joins the existing ToolRegistry pipeline (pre/post hook, telemetry, parallel gating).

TurnItemEmitter is a channel that can push TurnItem (e.g. WebSearchItem) to the session while the extension tool is running. CoreTurnItemEmitter calls Session::emit_turn_item_started/completed() to deliver the event to the client. The extension does not extend session lifetime because the extension tool only has Weak<Session> and Weak<TurnContext>.

ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/extension-api/src/contributors.rs (ToolContributor, ToolLifecycleContributor) ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/extension-api/src/registry.rs (ExtensionRegistryBuilder) ًں“پ codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/extension_tools.rs (ExtensionToolAdapter, CoreTurnItemEmitter)

C. Image Generation Extension â€” First ToolContributor Implementation Example

codex-rs/ext/image-generation/ is the first practical application of the Extension API. ImageGenerationExtension implements three contributors simultaneously:

  • ThreadLifecycleContributor â€” Inserts ImageGenerationExtensionConfig(enabled + provider) into the thread store when thread starts.
  • ConfigContributor â€” Enabled status updated when config changes
  • ToolContributor â€” Feature::ImageGenExt + OpenAI backend + Returns ImageGenerationTool when Codex authentication conditions are met

ImageGenerationTool returns ToolExposure::DirectModelOnly, which excludes it from code-mode nested surfaces. It is registered as the imagegen tool in the namespace image_gen and uses ToolSpec::Namespace(ResponsesApiNamespace { ... }). When executed, a generate/edit request is sent to the gpt-image-2 model through CodexImagesBackend, and the generated image is returned as base64 and persisted on the local disk (generated_images/).

ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/image-generation/src/extension.rs (contributor registration) ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/image-generation/src/tool.rs (ImageGenerationTool, ToolExposure::DirectModelOnly)

D. Tool-level integration of Goal Steering

As Goal Steering was refactored into an Extension API (codex-rs/ext/goal/), the interface with the tool runtime was redefined into two layers: ToolLifecycleContributor and ToolContributor.

ToolLifecycleContributor::on_tool_finish() â€” When tool execution is complete, account_active_goal_progress() is called to reflect the token usage in the goal. Completion of the update_goal tool itself is excluded to avoid double counting. When budget exceedance is detected, InternalModelContextFragment is created with budget_limit_steering_item() and injected into the ongoing turn through GoalRuntimeHandle::inject_active_turn_steering(). This path goes through ThreadManager::get_thread() â†’ inject_if_running(), and is quietly skipped if there are no active turns.

ToolContributor::tools() â€” GoalToolExecutor returns three tools: get_goal, create_goal, and update_goal. These tools directly access the persisted goal DB through codex_state::StateRuntime.

TurnLifecycleContributor â€” If there is an active goal at the start of the turn, the goal_id is marked in the accounting snapshot of the turn, and the accumulated token is flushed to the DB when the turn ends/aborts. If UsageLimitExceeded occurs in on_turn_error(), the active goal switches to the UsageLimited state.

Goal Steering's key impact on tool runtime: Every tool call completion event triggers goal accounting, and when the budget is exceeded, a steering context is inserted between tool responses, causing the model to start wrapping up. This mechanism operates independently of the existing dispatch_any() pipeline and is connected only through the Extension API's lifecycle hook.

ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/goal/src/extension.rs (implementation of ToolLifecycleContributor, ToolContributor) ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/goal/src/runtime.rs (inject_active_turn_steering(), account_active_goal_progress()) ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/goal/src/steering.rs (budget_limit_steering_item())

    A["model ResponseItem"] --> B["ToolRouter::build_tool_call()"]

1.2 ToolRouter::build_tool_call() â€” item ي•´ى„‌ ىƒپى„¸

build_tool_call()ى‌€ static methodë،œى„œ, ëھ¨ëچ¸ ى‌‘ë‹µى‌ک ResponseItem enum ë³€يک•ى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ ToolCallى‌ک payloadë¥¼ ê²°ى •ي•œë‹¤.

// router.rs â€” build_tool_call ي•µى‹¬ ë¶„ê¸°
match item {
    ResponseItem::FunctionCall { name, namespace, arguments, call_id, .. } => {
        // MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ى‌¸ى§€ ë¨¼ى € ê²€ى‚¬
        if let Some((server, tool)) = session.parse_mcp_tool_name(&name, &namespace).await {
            // â†’ ToolPayload::Mcp { server, tool, raw_arguments }
        } else {
            // â†’ ToolPayload::Function { arguments }
        }
    }
    ResponseItem::ToolSearchCall { call_id, execution: "client", arguments, .. } => {
        // â†’ ToolPayload::ToolSearch { arguments }
    }
    ResponseItem::CustomToolCall { name, input, call_id, .. } => {
        // â†’ ToolPayload::Custom { input }
    }
    ResponseItem::LocalShellCall { call_id, action: LocalShellAction::Exec(exec), .. } => {
        // â†’ ToolPayload::LocalShell { params }
    }
    _ => Ok(None),  // ى²کë¦¬ ë¶ˆê°€ itemى‌€ ë¬´ى‹œ
}

ى£¼ى‌کي•  ى گ: FunctionCallى‌´ ë“¤ى–´ى™€ëڈ„ session.parse_mcp_tool_name()ى‌´ ë¨¼ى € MCP ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ë¥¼ ي™•ى‌¸ي•´ ToolPayload::Mcpë،œ ى „ي™کي•œë‹¤. ى¦‰ MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ëٹ” FunctionCall يک•يƒœë،œ ë“¤ى–´ىک¤ى§€ë§Œ payload ë‹¨ê³„ى—گى„œ ë¶„ë¦¬ëگœë‹¤.

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/router.rs

1.3 ToolRegistry::dispatch_any() â€” ى •ى±… ى‹¤ي–‰ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸

dispatch_any()ê°€ ë‹¨ىˆœ dispatchê°€ ى•„ë‹Œ ى‌´ىœ ëٹ” ë‹¤ى‌Œ 7ë‹¨ê³„ë¥¼ ëھ¨ë‘گ ê±°ى¹کê¸° ë•Œë¬¸ى‌´ë‹¤:

1. tool_calls ى¹´ىڑ´ي„° ى¦‌ê°€ (active_turn.turn_state)
2. handler lookup â€” tool_handler_key(name, namespace)ë،œ HashMap ى،°يڑŒ
3. payload kind ê²€ى¦‌ â€” handler.matches_kind(payload)
4. pre-tool-use hook â€” shell/exec/local_shell ê³„ى—´ى‌´ë©´ ëھ…ë ¹ ë¬¸ى‍گى—´ ى¶”ى¶œ ي›„ hook ى‹¤ي–‰
5. is_mutating يŒگى • â€” mutatingى‌´ë©´ tool_call_gate.wait_ready() ëŒ€ê¸°
6. handler.handle_any(invocation) â€” ى‹¤ى œ ى‹¤ي–‰, telemetry ى‍گëڈ™ ê³„ى¸،
7. after-tool-use hook â€” ى„±ê³µ/ى‹¤يŒ¨/duration/mutating ى •ë³´ë¥¼ hookى—گ ى „ë‹¬

handlerê°€ ى—†ىœ¼ë©´ unsupported_tool_call_messageë¥¼ ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گ ëگکëڈŒë¦°ë‹¤. payload kindê°€ ë¶ˆى‌¼ى¹کي•کë©´ Fatal ى—گëں¬ë،œ ى „ى²´ turnى‌„ ى¤‘ë‹¨ي•œë‹¤.

// registry.rs â€” handler lookupê³¼ namespace ي‚¤ ىƒ‌ى„±
pub(crate) fn tool_handler_key(tool_name: &str, namespace: Option<&str>) -> String {
    if let Some(namespace) = namespace {
        format!("{namespace}:{tool_name}")
    } else {
        tool_name.to_string()
    }
}

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/registry.rs

1.4 ToolCallRuntime â€” ë³‘ë ¬ ى œى–´ى™€ cancellation

ToolCallRuntimeى‌€ Arc<RwLock<()>>ë¥¼ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•´ ë³‘ë ¬ ى‹¤ي–‰ى‌„ ى œى–´ي•œë‹¤:

  • supports_parallel_tool_calls = trueى‌¸ handler â†’ lock.read() (ى—¬ëں¬ handler ëڈ™ى‹œ ى‹¤ي–‰ ê°€ëٹ¥)
  • supports_parallel_tool_calls = falseى‌¸ handler â†’ lock.write() (ëڈ…ى گ ى‹¤ي–‰)

cancellationى‌€ tokio::select!ë،œ ى²کë¦¬ي•œë‹¤. CancellationTokenى‌´ ى·¨ى†Œëگکë©´ handlerë¥¼ ê¸°ë‹¤ë¦¬ى§€ ى•ٹê³  ى¦‰ى‹œ AbortedToolOutputى‌„ ë°کي™کي•œë‹¤.

// parallel.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ ë³‘ë ¬ ى œى–´ ë،œى§پ
tokio::select! {
    _ = cancellation_token.cancelled() => {
        Ok(Self::aborted_response(&call, secs))
    },
    res = async {
        let _guard = if supports_parallel {
            Either::Left(lock.read().await)      // RwLock read
        } else {
            Either::Right(lock.write().await)     // RwLock write
        };
        router.dispatch_tool_call_with_code_mode_result(
            session, turn, tracker, call.clone(), source,
        ).await
    } => res,
}

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/parallel.rs

sequenceDiagram
    participant Model as ëھ¨ëچ¸
    participant Router as ToolRouter
    participant Runtime as ToolCallRuntime
    participant Registry as ToolRegistry
    participant Hook as Hook System
    participant Handler as handler

    Model->>Router: ResponseItem (FunctionCall ë“±)
    Router->>Router: build_tool_call() â†’ ToolCall
    Router->>Runtime: handle_tool_call(call, cancellation_token)
    Runtime->>Runtime: RwLock read/write ê²Œى‌´يŒ…
    Runtime->>Registry: dispatch_any(invocation)
    Registry->>Registry: handler lookup + kind ê²€ى¦‌
    Registry->>Hook: pre_tool_use hook (shellë§Œ)
    Hook-->>Registry: allow / block
    Registry->>Handler: handle(invocation)
    Handler-->>Registry: ToolOutput
    Registry->>Hook: after_tool_use hook
    Hook-->>Registry: continue / abort
    Registry-->>Runtime: AnyToolResult
    Runtime-->>Model: ResponseInputItem
