3. ëڈ™ى پ ëڈ„êµ¬ ë“±ë،‌ ë©”ى»¤ë‹ˆى¦ک

3.1 ToolRegistryBuilder يŒ¨ي„´

Codexëٹ” ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ى—گ tool êµ¬ى„±ى‌´ ë°”ë€” ىˆک ى‍ˆى–´ى•¼ ي•کë¯€ë،œ ToolRegistryBuilder â†’ ToolRegistry ë¹Œëچ” يŒ¨ي„´ى‌„ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•œë‹¤. ي•µى‹¬ى‌€ register_handler()ê°€ ى‌´ë¦„-handler ë§¤ي•‘ى‌„ HashMap<String, Arc<dyn AnyToolHandler>>ى—گ ë„£ê³ , build()ê°€ ى‌´ë¥¼ immutable ToolRegistryë،œ ë³€ي™کي•کëٹ” êµ¬ى،°ë‹¤.

// registry.rs â€” ToolRegistryBuilder
pub struct ToolRegistryBuilder {
    handlers: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn AnyToolHandler>>,
    specs: Vec<ConfiguredToolSpec>,
}

impl ToolRegistryBuilder {
    pub fn register_handler<H>(&mut self, name: impl Into<String>, handler: Arc<H>)
    where H: ToolHandler + 'static {
        let name = name.into();
        let handler: Arc<dyn AnyToolHandler> = handler;
        if self.handlers.insert(name.clone(), handler.clone()).is_some() {
            warn!("overwriting handler for tool {name}");
        }
    }

    pub fn build(self) -> (Vec<ConfiguredToolSpec>, ToolRegistry) {
        let registry = ToolRegistry::new(self.handlers);
        (self.specs, registry)
    }
}

ى£¼ى„‌ىœ¼ë،œ ë‚¨ê²¨ى§„ register_many() ë©”ى„œë“œê°€ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©°, ي–¥ي›„ dynamic toolى‌ک ى‌¼ê´„ ë“±ë،‌ى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©ëگ  ىˆک ى‍ˆë‹¤.

3.2 MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ ëڈ™ى پ ë“±ë،‌

MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ëٹ” build_specs_with_discoverable_tools() ى•ˆى—گى„œ mcp_tools: HashMap<String, Tool> ى „ى²´ë¥¼ ىˆœيڑŒي•کë©° ê°پê°پ mcp_tool_to_openai_tool() â†’ register_handler(name, mcp_handler.clone())ىœ¼ë،œ ë“±ë،‌ي•œë‹¤. ëڈ™ى‌¼ي•œ McpHandler ى‌¸ىٹ¤ي„´ىٹ¤ê°€ ëھ¨ë“  MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ى—گ ê³µىœ ëگœë‹¤.

// spec.rs â€” MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ ë“±ë،‌
if let Some(mcp_tools) = mcp_tools {
    let mut entries: Vec<(String, Tool)> = mcp_tools.into_iter().collect();
    entries.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.cmp(&b.0));
    for (name, tool) in entries {
        match mcp_tool_to_openai_tool(name.clone(), tool) {
            Ok(converted_tool) => {
                builder.push_spec(ToolSpec::Function(converted_tool));
                builder.register_handler(name, mcp_handler.clone());
            }
            Err(e) => { tracing::error!("Failed to convert MCP tool: {e:?}"); }
        }
    }
}

3.3 Dynamic Tool ë“±ë،‌

Dynamic toolëڈ„ ىœ ى‚¬ي•œ يŒ¨ي„´ى‌´ë‹¤. DynamicToolSpec ë°°ى—´ى‌„ ىˆœيڑŒي•کë©° ê°پê°پ dynamic_tool_to_openai_tool() â†’ register_handler(tool.name, dynamic_tool_handler.clone())ë،œ ë“±ë،‌ي•œë‹¤.

// spec.rs â€” Dynamic tool ë“±ë،‌
for tool in dynamic_tools {
    match dynamic_tool_to_openai_tool(tool) {
        Ok(converted_tool) => {
            builder.push_spec(ToolSpec::Function(converted_tool));
            builder.register_handler(tool.name.clone(), dynamic_tool_handler.clone());
        }
        Err(e) => { tracing::error!("Failed to convert dynamic tool: {e:?}"); }
    }
}

3.4 ëڈ™ى پ ëڈ„êµ¬ى™€ MCP ëڈ„êµ¬ى‌ک ى°¨ى‌´

ي•­ëھ© dynamic tool MCP tool
ى •ى‌ک ىœ„ى¹ک يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ê°€ app-serverë،œ ë“±ë،‌ MCP ى„œë²„ê°€ ى œê³µ
core payload ToolPayload::Function â†’ DynamicToolHandler ToolPayload::Mcp
handler ë‚´ë¶€ oneshot ى±„ë„گë،œ app-serverى—گ DynamicToolCallRequest ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ë°œى†،, ى‌‘ë‹µ ëŒ€ê¸° handle_mcp_tool_call()ë،œ MCP ى„œë²„ى—گ RPC
ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ DynamicToolCallRequest/Response McpToolCallBegin/End
ى‹¤ي–‰ ى£¼ى²´ app-server â†’ يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ MCP ى„œë²„ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤
ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ item ThreadItem::DynamicToolCall ThreadItem::McpToolCall
is_mutating ي•­ىƒپ true ê¸°ë³¸ false

ًں“پ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/dynamic.rs, mcp.rs

4. ToolHandler traitê³¼ ToolPayload يƒ€ى‍…

4.1 ToolHandler trait

ëھ¨ë“  handlerê°€ êµ¬يک„ي•´ى•¼ ي•کëٹ” ي•µى‹¬ trait:

// registry.rs
#[async_trait]
pub trait ToolHandler: Send + Sync {
    type Output: ToolOutput + 'static;

    fn kind(&self) -> ToolKind;                      // Function | Mcp

    fn matches_kind(&self, payload: &ToolPayload) -> bool { /* ê¸°ë³¸ êµ¬يک„ */ }

    async fn is_mutating(&self, _invocation: &ToolInvocation) -> bool {
        false  // ê¸°ë³¸ê°’: ë¹„يŒŒê´´ى پ
    }

    async fn handle(&self, invocation: ToolInvocation) -> Result<Self::Output, FunctionCallError>;
}

ToolKindëٹ” Function | Mcp ë‘گ ê°’ë§Œ ى‍ˆë‹¤. matches_kind()ى‌ک ê¸°ë³¸ êµ¬يک„ى‌€ (Function, ToolPayload::Function), (Function, ToolPayload::ToolSearch), (Mcp, ToolPayload::Mcp) ى،°ي•©ë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©ي•œë‹¤.

4.2 ToolPayload enum

// context.rs
pub enum ToolPayload {
    Function { arguments: String },           // JSON ë¬¸ى‍گى—´
    ToolSearch { arguments: SearchToolCallParams },
    Custom { input: String },
    LocalShell { params: ShellToolCallParams },
    Mcp { server: String, tool: String, raw_arguments: String },
}

handlerى—گê²Œ ى „ë‹¬ëگکëٹ” ëھ¨ë“  ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ë²ˆë“¤:

// context.rs
pub struct ToolInvocation {

5. Orchestrator â€” ىٹ¹ى‌¸/ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤/ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„

ToolOrchestratorëٹ” shellê³¼ unified_exec ê³„ى—´ handler ë‚´ë¶€ى—گى„œ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•کëٹ” ë³„ëڈ„ ىٹ¹ى‌¸-ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤-ى‹¤ي–‰ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌´ë‹¤. ToolRegistryى‌ک dispatch_any() ى •ى±… يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ê³¼ëٹ” ë³„ê°œ ê³„ى¸µى‌´ë‹¤.

Orchestrator ى‹¤ي–‰ ى‹œي€€ىٹ¤:
1. Approval â€” ExecApprovalRequirement::NeedsApproval â†’ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ/Guardian ىٹ¹ى‌¸

// orchestrator.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ êµ¬ى،°
pub(crate) struct ToolOrchestrator {
    sandbox: SandboxManager,
}

impl ToolOrchestrator {
    pub async fn run<Rq, Out, T>(
        &mut self, tool: &mut T, req: &Rq,
        tool_ctx: &ToolCtx, turn_ctx: &TurnContext,
        approval_policy: AskForApproval,
    ) -> Result<OrchestratorRunResult<Out>, ToolError> {
        // 1) Approval ë‹¨ê³„
        // 2) SandboxAttempt ى„ يƒ‌
        // 3) run_attempt() â€” ى‹¤ي–‰ + network approval
        // 4) sandbox ى‹¤يŒ¨ ى‹œ ى—گىٹ¤ى»¬ë ˆى‌´ى…ک ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„
    }
}

ToolRuntime traitë¥¼ êµ¬يک„ي•کëٹ” concrete ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ى‌€ runtimes/ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬ى—گ ى‍ˆë‹¤:

ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ يŒŒى‌¼ ىڑ©ëڈ„

// network_approval.rs
pub(crate) struct NetworkApprovalService {
    active_calls: Mutex<IndexMap<String, Arc<ActiveNetworkApprovalCall>>>,
    call_outcomes: Mutex<HashMap<String, NetworkApprovalOutcome>>,
    pending_host_approvals: Mutex<HashMap<HostApprovalKey, Arc<PendingHostApproval>>>,
    session_approved_hosts: Mutex<HashSet<HostApprovalKey>>,
    session_denied_hosts: Mutex<HashSet<HostApprovalKey>>,
}

7. Unified Exec ي”„ë ˆى‍„ى›Œيپ¬

7.1 ê°œىڑ”

UnifiedExecHandlerëٹ” PTY ê¸°ë°ک ى‍¥ê¸° ى‹¤ي–‰ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ê´€ë¦¬ ëڈ„êµ¬ë‹¤. ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ ي•کىœ„ ي•¨ىˆکë¥¼ ي•کë‚کى‌ک handlerى—گى„œ ى²کë¦¬ي•œë‹¤:

// handlers/unified_exec.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ يŒŒë‌¼ë¯¸ي„°
struct ExecCommandArgs {
    cmd: String,
    workdir: Option<String>,
    shell: Option<String>,        // ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌´ ى…¸ ê²½ë،œ ى§€ى • ê°€ëٹ¥
    login: Option<bool>,          // -lc ëھ¨ë“œ
    tty: bool,                    // default false â†’ PTY ي• ë‹¹ ى—¬ë¶€
    yield_time_ms: u64,           // default 10_000ms
    max_output_tokens: Option<usize>,
    sandbox_permissions: SandboxPermissions,
    additional_permissions: Option<PermissionProfile>,
    justification: Option<String>,
    prefix_rule: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

8. ToolsConfigى™€ Feature Flag ê¸°ë°ک ëڈ„êµ¬ ى„ يƒ‌

ToolsConfigëٹ” ToolsConfigParams(ëھ¨ëچ¸ ى •ë³´ + feature flags + sandbox ى •ى±…)ë¥¼ ë°›ى•„ ى–´ë–¤ ëڈ„êµ¬ë¥¼ ي™œى„±ي™”ي• ى§€ ê²°ى •ي•œë‹¤. ى£¼ىڑ” feature flag:

Feature Flag ى œى–´ي•کëٹ” ëڈ„êµ¬
Feature::ShellTool shell ê³„ى—´ ى „ى²´
Feature::UnifiedExec exec_command / write_stdin
Feature::ShellZshFork ShellCommand ë°±ى—”ë“œ (ZshFork)
Feature::ApplyPatchFreeform apply_patch freeform يک•ى‹‌
Feature::CodeMode codex / codex_wait
Feature::CodeModeOnly code_mode_only ëھ¨ë“œ
Feature::JsRepl js_repl / js_repl_reset
Feature::Collab spawn_agent, wait_agent ë“±
Feature::MultiAgentV2 v2 ë©€ي‹°ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ëڈ„êµ¬
Feature::SpawnCsv spawn_agents_on_csv
Feature::ToolSearch tool_search
Feature::ToolSuggest tool_suggest
Feature::ExecPermissionApprovals sandbox_permissions ي™•ى‍¥

// contributors.rs
pub trait ToolContributor: Send + Sync {

        &self,
        session_store: &ExtensionData,
        thread_store: &ExtensionData,

}

TurnItemEmitterëٹ” ي™•ى‍¥ ëڈ„êµ¬ê°€ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى¤‘ TurnItem(ىکˆ: WebSearchItem)ى‌„ ى„¸ى…کى—گ pushي•  ىˆک ى‍ˆëٹ” ى±„ë„گى‌´ë‹¤. CoreTurnItemEmitterê°€ Session::emit_turn_item_started/completed()ë¥¼ يک¸ى¶œي•´ يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ى—گ ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ë¥¼ ى „ë‹¬ي•œë‹¤. ي™•ى‍¥ ëڈ„êµ¬ê°€ Weak<Session>ê³¼ Weak<TurnContext>ë§Œ ê°–ê¸° ë•Œë¬¸ى—گ ي™•ى‍¥ى‌´ ى„¸ى…ک ىˆکëھ…ى‌„ ى—°ى‍¥ي•کى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤.

D. Goal Steeringى‌ک ëڈ„êµ¬-ë ˆë²¨ ي†µي•©

Goal Steeringى‌´ Extension APIë،œ ë¦¬يŒ©ي„°ë§پëگکë©´ى„œ(codex-rs/ext/goal/), ëڈ„êµ¬ ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ê³¼ى‌ک ى ‘ى گى‌´ ToolLifecycleContributorى™€ ToolContributor ë‘گ ê³„ى¸µىœ¼ë،œ ى‍¬ى •ë¦½ëگکى—ˆë‹¤.

ToolLifecycleContributor::on_tool_finish() â€” ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى™„ë£Œ ى‹œ account_active_goal_progress()ë¥¼ يک¸ى¶œي•´ ي† يپ° ى‚¬ىڑ©ëں‰ى‌„ goalى—گ ë°کىکپي•œë‹¤. update_goal ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‍گى²´ى‌ک ى™„ë£Œëٹ” ى‌´ى¤‘ ê³„ى‚°ى‌„ ي”¼ي•کê¸° ىœ„ي•´ ى œى™¸ي•œë‹¤. budget ى´ˆê³¼ê°€ ê°گى§€ëگکë©´ budget_limit_steering_item()ىœ¼ë،œ InternalModelContextFragmentë¥¼ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•کê³  GoalRuntimeHandle::inject_active_turn_steering()ى‌„ ي†µي•´ ى§„ي–‰ ى¤‘ى‌¸ ي„´ى—گ ى£¼ى‍…ي•œë‹¤. ى‌´ ê²½ë،œëٹ” ThreadManager::get_thread() â†’ inject_if_running()ى‌„ ê±°ى¹کë©°, ي™œى„± ي„´ى‌´ ى—†ىœ¼ë©´ ى،°ىڑ©ي‍ˆ ê±´ë„ˆë›´ë‹¤.

ToolContributor::tools() â€” GoalToolExecutorê°€ get_goal, create_goal, update_goal ى„¸ ëڈ„êµ¬ë¥¼ ë°کي™کي•œë‹¤. ى‌´ ëڈ„êµ¬ë“¤ى‌€ codex_state::StateRuntimeى‌„ ي†µي•´ persistëگœ goal DBى—گ ى§پى ‘ ى ‘ê·¼ي•œë‹¤.

TurnLifecycleContributor â€” turn ى‹œى‍‘ ى‹œ ي™œى„± goalى‌´ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ ي•´ë‹¹ ي„´ى‌ک accounting snapshotى—گ goal_idë¥¼ ë§ˆي‚¹ي•کê³ , turn ى¢…ë£Œ/abort ى‹œ ëˆ„ى پ ي† يپ°ى‌„ DBى—گ flushي•œë‹¤. on_turn_error()ى—گى„œ UsageLimitExceededê°€ ë°œىƒ‌ي•کë©´ ي™œى„± goalى‌„ UsageLimited ىƒپيƒœë،œ ى „ي™کي•œë‹¤.

Goal Steeringى‌´ ëڈ„êµ¬ ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ى—گ ë¯¸ى¹کëٹ” ي•µى‹¬ ىکپي–¥: ëھ¨ë“  ëڈ„êµ¬ يک¸ى¶œ ى™„ë£Œ ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ê°€ goal accountingى‌„ يٹ¸ë¦¬ê±°ي•کê³ , budget ى´ˆê³¼ ى‹œ ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‌‘ë‹µ ى‚¬ى‌´ى—گ steering contextê°€ ى‚½ى‍…ëگکى–´ ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌´ wrap-upى‌„ ى‹œى‍‘ي•کëڈ„ë،‌ ىœ ëڈ„ي•œë‹¤. ى‌´ ë©”ى»¤ë‹ˆى¦کى‌€ ê¸°ى،´ dispatch_any() يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ê³¼ ëڈ…ë¦½ى پىœ¼ë،œ ى‍‘ëڈ™ي•کë©°, Extension APIى‌ک lifecycle hookى‌„ ي†µي•´ى„œë§Œ ى—°ê²°ëگœë‹¤.

ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/goal/src/extension.rs (ToolLifecycleContributor, ToolContributor êµ¬يک„) ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/goal/src/runtime.rs (inject_active_turn_steering(), account_active_goal_progress()) ًں“پ codex-rs/ext/goal/src/steering.rs (budget_limit_steering_item())

Axis 04: Skill system

AXIS

004_skill-system/04_co_skill.md

Overview

Axis 04: Skill system â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • SkillsManagerëٹ” cwdى™€ config stackى‌„ ë°”يƒ•ىœ¼ë،œ skill rootë¥¼ ê³„ى‚°ي•کê³ , ë،œë”© ê²°ê³¼ë¥¼ ى‌´ى¤‘ ى؛گى‹œë،œ ë³´ê´€ي•œë‹¤.

// manager.rs â€” ىƒ‌ى„±ى‍گ ي•µى‹¬ë¶€
if !bundled_skills_enabled {
    uninstall_system_skills(&manager.codex_home);
} else if let Err(err) = install_system_skills(&manager.codex_home) {
    tracing::error!("failed to install system skills: {err}");

README / docs

// manager.rs
fn build_skill_outcome(&self, roots: Vec<SkillRoot>, skill_config_rules: &SkillConfigRules) -> SkillLoadOutcome {
    let outcome = filter_skill_load_outcome_for_product(
        load_skills_from_roots(roots),
        self.restriction_product,
    );
    let disabled_paths = resolve_disabled_skill_paths(&outcome.skills, skill_config_rules);
    finalize_skill_outcome(outcome, disabled_paths)
}

README / docs

// loader.rs â€” root ي•´ى„‌ ي•µى‹¬ ë،œى§پ (ى¶•ى•½)
match &layer.name {
    ConfigLayerSource::Project { .. } => {
        roots.push(SkillRoot {
            path: config_folder.as_path().join("skills"),
            scope: SkillScope::Repo,
        });
    }
    ConfigLayerSource::User { .. } => {
        // 1) $CODEX_HOME/skills (deprecated, backward compat)
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: config_folder.join("skills"), scope: SkillScope::User });
        // 2) $HOME/.agents/skills (user-installed)
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: home_dir.join(".agents/skills"), scope: SkillScope::User });
        // 3) $CODEX_HOME/skills/.system (embedded system skills cache)
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: system_cache_root_dir(config_folder), scope: SkillScope::System });
    }
    ConfigLayerSource::System { .. } => {
        // /etc/codex/skills â†’ Admin scope
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: config_folder.join("skills"), scope: SkillScope::Admin });
    }
    _ => {}
}

README / docs

// loader.rs
fn repo_agents_skill_roots(config_layer_stack: &ConfigLayerStack, cwd: &Path) -> Vec<SkillRoot> {
    let project_root = find_project_root(cwd, &project_root_markers);
    let dirs = dirs_between_project_root_and_cwd(cwd, &project_root);
    for dir in dirs {
        let agents_skills = dir.join(".agents").join("skills");
        if agents_skills.is_dir() {
            roots.push(SkillRoot { path: agents_skills, scope: SkillScope::Repo });
        }
    }
    roots
}

graph LR
    subgraph "Skill Root يƒگىƒ‰ ىˆœى„œ"
        P["Project config<br/>.codex/skills/"] -->|Repo| R["roots"]

        U1["$CODEX_HOME/skills/"] -->|User| R
        U2["$HOME/.agents/skills/"] -->|User| R
        PL["plugin skill roots"] -->|User| R
        S["$CODEX_HOME/skills/.system/"] -->|System| R
        AD["/etc/codex/skills/"] -->|Admin| R
    end
    R --> DEDUP["dedupe_skill_roots_by_path()"]

// loader.rs
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct SkillFrontmatter {
    name: Option<String>,
    description: Option<String>,
    metadata: SkillFrontmatterMetadata,  // short-description ë“±
}

fn parse_skill_file(path: &Path, scope: SkillScope) -> Result<SkillMetadata, SkillParseError> {
    let contents = fs::read_to_string(path)?;
    let frontmatter = extract_frontmatter(&contents)?;      // --- ... --- êµ¬ê°„ ى¶”ى¶œ
    let parsed: SkillFrontmatter = serde_yaml::from_str(&frontmatter)?;

    // nameى‌´ ى—†ىœ¼ë©´ ë¶€ëھ¨ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌„ ê¸°ë³¸ê°’ىœ¼ë،œ ى‚¬ىڑ©
    let base_name = parsed.name
        .map(sanitize_single_line)
        .unwrap_or_else(|| default_skill_name(path));
    let name = namespaced_skill_name(path, &base_name);     // plugin namespace ى ‘ë‘گى‚¬

    let LoadedSkillMetadata { interface, dependencies, policy, permission_profile, managed_network_override }
        = load_skill_metadata(path);  // agents/openai.yaml ë،œë“œ

    Ok(SkillMetadata { name, description, interface, dependencies, policy, ... })
}

    dependencies: Option<Dependencies>, // MCP ى„œë²„, env_var ë“±

}

    icon_large: Option<PathBuf>,

    default_prompt: Option<String>,
}

}

// loader.rs
fn resolve_asset_path(skill_dir: &Path, field: &str, path: Option<PathBuf>) -> Option<PathBuf> {
    // ى ˆëŒ€ ê²½ë،œ ê±°ë¶€, ".." ى»´يڈ¬ë„Œيٹ¸ ê±°ë¶€, "assets/" ى ‘ë‘گى‚¬ ي•„ىˆک
    let mut components = normalized.components();
    match components.next() {
        Some(Component::Normal(c)) if c == "assets" => {} // OK
        _ => { warn!("icon path must be under assets/"); return None; }
    }
    Some(skill_dir.join(normalized))
}

// loader.rs
outcome.skills.sort_by(|a, b| {
    scope_rank(a.scope).cmp(&scope_rank(b.scope))
        .then_with(|| a.name.cmp(&b.name))
        .then_with(|| a.path_to_skills_md.cmp(&b.path_to_skills_md))
});

const SYSTEM_SKILLS_DIR: Dir = include_dir::include_dir!("$CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/src/assets/samples");

README / docs

// skills/src/lib.rs
pub fn install_system_skills(codex_home: &Path) -> Result<(), SystemSkillsError> {
    let expected_fingerprint = embedded_system_skills_fingerprint(); // salt + ى „ى²´ يŒŒى‌¼ hash
    if dest.is_dir() && read_marker(&marker_path) == expected_fingerprint {
        return Ok(());  // ى‌´ë¯¸ ىµœى‹ 
    }
    // ê¸°ى،´ ى‚­ى œ ي›„ ى‍¬ى„¤ى¹ک
    fs::remove_dir_all(&dest)?;
    write_embedded_dir(&SYSTEM_SKILLS_DIR, &dest)?;
    fs::write(marker_path, format!("{expected_fingerprint}\n"))?;
    Ok(())
}

README / docs

pub(crate) fn resolve_disabled_skill_paths(
    skills: &[SkillMetadata],
    rules: &SkillConfigRules,
) -> HashSet<PathBuf> {
    // Path ى…€ë ‰ي„° â†’ ي•´ë‹¹ ê²½ë،œ ى§پى ‘ ى¶”ê°€/ى œê±°
    // Name ى…€ë ‰ي„° â†’ ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌ک ëھ¨ë“  skill ê²½ë،œى—گ ى پىڑ©
    // layer ىˆœى„œ ë³´ى،´: ë‚کى¤‘ ى…€ë ‰ي„°ê°€ ى•‍ى‌ک ى…€ë ‰ي„°ë¥¼ override
}

// injection.rs â€” ë‘گ ë‹¨ê³„ ى„ يƒ‌
pub(crate) fn collect_explicit_skill_mentions(
    inputs: &[UserInput],
    skills: &[SkillMetadata],
    disabled_paths: &HashSet<PathBuf>,
    connector_slug_counts: &HashMap<String, usize>,
) -> Vec<SkillMetadata> {
    // 1ë‹¨ê³„: structured UserInput::Skill â†’ path ê¸°ë°ک ى •ي™• ë§¤ى¹­ (ê°€ى‍¥ ê°•ي•œ selection)
    for input in inputs {
        if let UserInput::Skill { name, path } = input {
            blocked_plain_names.insert(name.clone());  // 2ë‹¨ê³„ى—گى„œ ى¤‘ë³µ ë°©ى§€
            if let Some(skill) = skills.iter().find(|s| s.path_to_skills_md == *path) {
                selected.push(skill.clone());
            }
        }
    }

    // 2ë‹¨ê³„: ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ $skill-name ëکگëٹ” [$skill-name](resource-path) â†’ ى‌´ë¦„/ê²½ë،œ ë§¤ى¹­
    for input in inputs {
        if let UserInput::Text { text, .. } = input {
            let mentions = extract_tool_mentions(text);
            select_skills_from_mentions(&context, &mentions, &mut selected);
        }
    }
    selected
}

README / docs

// injection.rs â€” mention يک•يƒœ
const SKILL_PATH_PREFIX: &str = "skill://";

// 1) ë§پيپ¬يک•: [$pdf](skill://path/to/SKILL.md) â†’ pathë،œ ى •ي™• ë§¤ى¹­
// 2) ë‹¨ىˆœيک•: $pdf â†’ nameىœ¼ë،œ ë§¤ى¹­ (ë‹¨, ى‌´ë¦„ى‌´ uniqueي•œ ê²½ىڑ°ë§Œ)
//    ambiguous name(ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ skillى‌´ 2ê°œ+)ى‌´ë©´ ىٹ¤ي‚µ
//    connector slugى™€ ى¶©ëڈŒي•´ëڈ„ ىٹ¤ي‚µ

// ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک ىک¤يƒگ ë°©ى§€
fn is_common_env_var(name: &str) -> bool {
    matches!(name.to_uppercase().as_str(), "PATH" | "HOME" | "USER" | "SHELL" | ...)
}

// mention ى‌´ë¦„ى—گ ي—ˆىڑ©ëگکëٹ” ë¬¸ى‍گ
fn is_mention_name_char(byte: u8) -> bool {
    matches!(byte, b'a'..=b'z' | b'A'..=b'Z' | b'0'..=b'9' | b'_' | b'-' | b':')
}

README / docs

// injection.rs
pub(crate) async fn build_skill_injections(
    mentioned_skills: &[SkillMetadata],
    otel: Option<&SessionTelemetry>,
    analytics_client: &AnalyticsEventsClient,
    tracking: TrackEventsContext,
) -> SkillInjections {
    for skill in mentioned_skills {
        match fs::read_to_string(&skill.path_to_skills_md).await {
            Ok(contents) => {
                // otel counter: codex.skill.injected (status=ok)
                // analytics: SkillInvocation { invocation_type: Explicit }
                result.items.push(ResponseItem::from(SkillInstructions {
                    name: skill.name.clone(),
                    path: skill.path_to_skills_md.to_string_lossy().into_owned(),
                    contents,       // SKILL.md ى „ى²´ ë³¸ë¬¸
                }));
            }
            Err(err) => {
                // otel counter: codex.skill.injected (status=error)
                result.warnings.push(format!("Failed to load skill {}: {err:#}", skill.name));
            }
        }
    }
    result
}

README / docs

// invocation_utils.rs â€” ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
pub(crate) fn build_implicit_skill_path_indexes(skills: Vec<SkillMetadata>) -> (
    HashMap<PathBuf, SkillMetadata>,  // scripts_dir â†’ skill (ىکˆ: .../pdf/scripts/ â†’ pdf)
    HashMap<PathBuf, SkillMetadata>,  // doc_path â†’ skill   (ىکˆ: .../pdf/SKILL.md â†’ pdf)
) {
    for skill in skills {
        // 1) SKILL.md ê²½ë،œ ى‍گى²´
        by_skill_doc_path.insert(normalize_path(&skill.path_to_skills_md), skill.clone());
        // 2) SKILL.md ë¶€ëھ¨ى‌ک scripts/ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬
        if let Some(skill_dir) = skill.path_to_skills_md.parent() {
            by_scripts_dir.insert(normalize_path(&skill_dir.join("scripts")), skill);
        }
    }
}

README / docs

// invocation_utils.rs

// 1) ىٹ¤يپ¬ë¦½يٹ¸ ى‹¤ي–‰ ê°گى§€: python scripts/rotate.py â†’ scripts/ ê²½ë،œë،œ skill ى—°ê²°
fn detect_skill_script_run(outcome: &SkillLoadOutcome, tokens: &[String], workdir: &Path) -> Option<SkillMetadata> {
    let script_token = script_run_token(tokens)?;  // python/bash/node ë“±ى‌ک runner + .py/.sh/.js ë“±ى‌ک ي™•ى‍¥ى‍گ
    let script_path = workdir.join(script_token);
    for ancestor in script_path.ancestors() {
        if let Some(candidate) = outcome.implicit_skills_by_scripts_dir.get(ancestor) {
            return Some(candidate.clone());
        }
    }
    None
}

// 2) ë¬¸ى„œ ى‌½ê¸° ê°گى§€: cat SKILL.md â†’ doc_pathë،œ skill ى—°ê²°
fn detect_skill_doc_read(outcome: &SkillLoadOutcome, tokens: &[String], workdir: &Path) -> Option<SkillMetadata> {
    // cat/sed/head/tail/less/more/bat/awk ى¤‘ ي•کë‚کى‌¼ ë•Œë§Œ ى§„ى‍…
    if !command_reads_file(tokens) { return None; }
    for token in tokens.iter().skip(1) {
        if let Some(candidate) = outcome.implicit_skills_by_doc_path.get(&normalize_path(token)) {
            return Some(candidate.clone());
        }
    }
    None
}

README / docs

// invocation_utils.rs
pub(crate) async fn maybe_emit_implicit_skill_invocation(sess: &Session, ...) {
    let candidate = detect_implicit_skill_invocation_for_command(...)?;
    // ى¤‘ë³µ ë°©ى§€: seen_key = "scope:path:name"
    if !turn_context.turn_skills.implicit_invocation_seen_skills.insert(seen_key) { return; }
    // otel counter: codex.skill.injected (invoke_type=implicit)
    // analytics: SkillInvocation { invocation_type: Implicit }
}

README / docs

// render.rs
pub fn render_skills_section(skills: &[SkillMetadata]) -> Option<String> {
    let mut lines = Vec::new();
    lines.push("## Skills");
    lines.push("A skill is a set of local instructions...");
    lines.push("### Available skills");
    for skill in skills {
        lines.push(format!("- {name}: {description} (file: {path})"));
    }
    lines.push("### How to use skills");
    // trigger rules, progressive disclosure, coordination, safety ë“± ىƒپى„¸ ê°€ى‌´ë“œ
    Some(format!("{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_OPEN_TAG}\n{body}\n{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_CLOSE_TAG}"))
}

README / docs

// env_var_dependencies.rs
pub(crate) fn collect_env_var_dependencies(mentioned_skills: &[SkillMetadata]) -> Vec<SkillDependencyInfo> {
    for skill in mentioned_skills {
        for tool in &skill.dependencies?.tools {
            if tool.r#type == "env_var" && !tool.value.is_empty() {
                dependencies.push(SkillDependencyInfo {
                    skill_name: skill.name.clone(),
                    name: tool.value.clone(),       // ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک ى‌´ë¦„
                    description: tool.description.clone(),
                });
            }
        }
    }
}

    WorkspaceShared,   // workspace ê³µىœ  ىٹ¤ي‚¬

    Personal,          // ê°œى‌¸ ىٹ¤ي‚¬
    Example,           // ىکˆى‹œ ىٹ¤ي‚¬
}

pub enum RemoteSkillProductSurface {
    Chatgpt, Codex, Api, Atlas,
}

README / docs

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ
    participant Core as Codex Core
    participant SM as SkillsManager
    participant Loader as loader.rs
    participant FS as يŒŒى‌¼ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ
    participant Inj as injection.rs
    participant OTel as Telemetry

    User->>Core: "$pdfë،œ ë³€ي™کي•´ى¤ک" (UserInput::Text)
    Core->>SM: skills_for_config(config)
    SM->>SM: cache hit ي™•ى‌¸
    alt cache miss
        SM->>Loader: skill_roots() â†’ root ëھ©ë،‌
        SM->>Loader: load_skills_from_roots(roots)
        Loader->>FS: BFS يƒگىƒ‰ (SKILL.md ê²€ىƒ‰)
        FS-->>Loader: SKILL.md + agents/openai.yaml
        Loader-->>SM: SkillLoadOutcome
        SM->>SM: filter_for_product + disabled_paths
        SM->>SM: build_implicit_indexes
        SM->>SM: cacheى—گ ى €ى‍¥
    end
    SM-->>Core: SkillLoadOutcome

    Core->>Inj: collect_explicit_skill_mentions(inputs, skills)
    Note over Inj: "$pdf" يŒŒى‹± â†’ name ë§¤ى¹­<br/>unique nameى‌´ë©´ ى„ يƒ‌
    Inj-->>Core: mentioned_skills: [pdf]

    Core->>Inj: build_skill_injections(mentioned_skills)
    Inj->>FS: fs::read_to_string(SKILL.md)
    FS-->>Inj: SKILL.md ë³¸ë¬¸
    Inj->>OTel: codex.skill.injected (status=ok, invoke_type=explicit)
    Inj-->>Core: SkillInjections { items: [SkillInstructions] }

    Core->>Core: م€ˆskillم€‰ wrapperë،œ user messageى—گ ى£¼ى‍…

Main analysis

#Skill loadingآ·triggerآ·injection analysis

Analysis snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” core-skills/, skills/, SkillTool integration.

Codex's skill system has more layers than meets the eye. Usually, it is easy to understand as "read SKILL.md and put it in the prompt," but the actual implementation is separated into root selection, metadata load, enabled or disabled judgment, explicit mention selection, dependency installation, body injection, and implicit invocation telemetry.

The reason we need to look at this structure properly is because we need to decide how to fork the skill system. Now, Codex skills are simple text directives, but at the same time have interface metadata and tool dependencies that can be exposed to the UI. In other words, it's less of a "documentation piece" and more of a "small plugin documentation".

1. SkillsManager â€” Cache and entry point

SkillsManager calculates the skill root based on cwd and config stack, and stores the loading results as double cache.

// manager.rs
pub struct SkillsManager {
    codex_home: PathBuf,
    plugins_manager: Arc<PluginsManager>,
    restriction_product: Option<Product>,
    cache_by_cwd: RwLock<HashMap<PathBuf, SkillLoadOutcome>>,       // cwd ê¸°ë°ک
    cache_by_config: RwLock<HashMap<ConfigSkillsCacheKey, SkillLoadOutcome>>,  // config ê¸°ë°ک
}

The reason there are two caches: cache_by_cwd is for the app-server's skills_for_cwd() path, and cache_by_config prevents role-local/session-local overrides from leaking between different sessions when querying with an already configured Config.

Immediately install or remove system skills depending on the bundled_skills_enabled flag in the constructor:

// manager.rs â€” ىƒ‌ى„±ى‍گ ي•µى‹¬ë¶€
if !bundled_skills_enabled {
    uninstall_system_skills(&manager.codex_home);
} else if let Err(err) = install_system_skills(&manager.codex_home) {
    tracing::error!("failed to install system skills: {err}");
}

The actual skill build pipeline is concentrated in build_skill_outcome():

// manager.rs
fn build_skill_outcome(&self, roots: Vec<SkillRoot>, skill_config_rules: &SkillConfigRules) -> SkillLoadOutcome {
    let outcome = filter_skill_load_outcome_for_product(
        load_skills_from_roots(roots),
        self.restriction_product,
    );
    let disabled_paths = resolve_disabled_skill_paths(&outcome.skills, skill_config_rules);
    finalize_skill_outcome(outcome, disabled_paths)
}

finalize_skill_outcome() applies the disabled path and then builds two indexes (implicit_skills_by_scripts_dir, implicit_skills_by_doc_path) for implicit invocation.

2. Skill Root Analysis â€” Where to find skills

root has user, repo, system, and admin scopes, and system skills are installed separately from the codex_skills package. Therefore, if we maintain system skills in our fork, we should also bring the concept of "installation and cache directories" rather than simple file bundles.

skill_roots_from_layer_stack_inner() iterates through the config layer and determines the root by scope:

// loader.rs â€” root ي•´ى„‌ ي•µى‹¬ ë،œى§پ (ى¶•ى•½)
match &layer.name {
    ConfigLayerSource::Project { .. } => {
        roots.push(SkillRoot {
            path: config_folder.as_path().join("skills"),
            scope: SkillScope::Repo,
        });
    }
    ConfigLayerSource::User { .. } => {
        // 1) $CODEX_HOME/skills (deprecated, backward compat)
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: config_folder.join("skills"), scope: SkillScope::User });
        // 2) $HOME/.agents/skills (user-installed)
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: home_dir.join(".agents/skills"), scope: SkillScope::User });
        // 3) $CODEX_HOME/skills/.system (embedded system skills cache)
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: system_cache_root_dir(config_folder), scope: SkillScope::System });
    }
    ConfigLayerSource::System { .. } => {
        // /etc/codex/skills â†’ Admin scope
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: config_folder.join("skills"), scope: SkillScope::Admin });
    }
    _ => {}
}

Additionally, repo_agents_skill_roots() adds .agents/skills/ in each directory to the repo scope, going up from cwd to the project root:

// loader.rs
fn repo_agents_skill_roots(config_layer_stack: &ConfigLayerStack, cwd: &Path) -> Vec<SkillRoot> {
    let project_root = find_project_root(cwd, &project_root_markers);
    let dirs = dirs_between_project_root_and_cwd(cwd, &project_root);
    for dir in dirs {
        let agents_skills = dir.join(".agents").join("skills");
        if agents_skills.is_dir() {
            roots.push(SkillRoot { path: agents_skills, scope: SkillScope::Repo });
        }
    }
    roots
}

graph LR
    subgraph "Skill Root يƒگىƒ‰ ىˆœى„œ"
        P["Project config<br/>.codex/skills/"] -->|Repo| R["roots"]
        A1[".agents/skills/<br/>(cwd â†’ project root)"] -->|Repo| R
        U1["$CODEX_HOME/skills/"] -->|User| R
        U2["$HOME/.agents/skills/"] -->|User| R
        PL["plugin skill roots"] -->|User| R
        S["$CODEX_HOME/skills/.system/"] -->|System| R
        AD["/etc/codex/skills/"] -->|Admin| R
    end
    R --> DEDUP["dedupe_skill_roots_by_path()"]

3. SKILL.md format and parsing

3.1 Directory structure

skill-name/
â”œâ”€â”€ SKILL.md                 â†گ ي•„ىˆک (frontmatter + ë³¸ë¬¸)
â”œâ”€â”€ agents/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ openai.yaml          â†گ UI ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„° (interface, dependencies, policy, permissions)
â”œâ”€â”€ scripts/                 â†گ ى‹¤ي–‰ ê°€ëٹ¥ي•œ ى½”ë“œ
â”œâ”€â”€ references/              â†گ ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ى—گ ë،œë“œي•  ى°¸ى،° ë¬¸ى„œ
â””â”€â”€ assets/                  â†گ ى¶œë ¥ى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•  يŒŒى‌¼ (icon, template ë“±)

3.2 SKILL.md frontmatter parsing

parse_skill_file() parses the YAML frontmatter and generates SkillMetadata:

// loader.rs
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct SkillFrontmatter {
    name: Option<String>,
    description: Option<String>,
    metadata: SkillFrontmatterMetadata,  // short-description ë“±
}

fn parse_skill_file(path: &Path, scope: SkillScope) -> Result<SkillMetadata, SkillParseError> {
    let contents = fs::read_to_string(path)?;
    let frontmatter = extract_frontmatter(&contents)?;      // --- ... --- êµ¬ê°„ ى¶”ى¶œ
    let parsed: SkillFrontmatter = serde_yaml::from_str(&frontmatter)?;

    // nameى‌´ ى—†ىœ¼ë©´ ë¶€ëھ¨ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌„ ê¸°ë³¸ê°’ىœ¼ë،œ ى‚¬ىڑ©
    let base_name = parsed.name
        .map(sanitize_single_line)
        .unwrap_or_else(|| default_skill_name(path));
    let name = namespaced_skill_name(path, &base_name);     // plugin namespace ى ‘ë‘گى‚¬

    let LoadedSkillMetadata { interface, dependencies, policy, permission_profile, managed_network_override }
        = load_skill_metadata(path);  // agents/openai.yaml ë،œë“œ

    Ok(SkillMetadata { name, description, interface, dependencies, policy, ... })
}

Validation: name up to 64 characters, description up to 1024 characters. If the value is empty, an error is returned.

3.3 agents/openai.yaml structure

// loader.rs â€” ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„° يŒŒى‌¼ êµ¬ى،°
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct SkillMetadataFile {
    interface: Option<Interface>,       // display_name, icon, brand_color, default_prompt
    dependencies: Option<Dependencies>, // MCP ى„œë²„, env_var ë“±
    policy: Option<Policy>,             // allow_implicit_invocation, products
    permissions: Option<SkillPermissionProfile>,  // network, file_system, macos seatbelt
}

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct Interface {
    display_name: Option<String>,
    short_description: Option<String>,
    icon_small: Option<PathBuf>,    // assets/ ي•کىœ„ ىƒپëŒ€ ê²½ë،œë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©
    icon_large: Option<PathBuf>,
    brand_color: Option<String>,
    default_prompt: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct Policy {
    allow_implicit_invocation: Option<bool>,  // ê¸°ë³¸ê°’ true
    products: Vec<Product>,                   // ë¹ˆ ë°°ى—´ى‌´ë©´ ëھ¨ë“  productى—گى„œ ى‚¬ىڑ© ê°€ëٹ¥
}

The icon path must be a relative path under assets/. Rejected when using ..:

// loader.rs
fn resolve_asset_path(skill_dir: &Path, field: &str, path: Option<PathBuf>) -> Option<PathBuf> {
    // ى ˆëŒ€ ê²½ë،œ ê±°ë¶€, ".." ى»´يڈ¬ë„Œيٹ¸ ê±°ë¶€, "assets/" ى ‘ë‘گى‚¬ ي•„ىˆک
    let mut components = normalized.components();
    match components.next() {
        Some(Component::Normal(c)) if c == "assets" => {} // OK
        _ => { warn!("icon path must be under assets/"); return None; }
    }
    Some(skill_dir.join(normalized))
}

3.4 Progressive Disclosure Design

Skills are loaded progressively in three stages:

steps Content size
Level 1 â€” Metadata name + description (always present in context) ~100 words
Level 2 â€” SKILL.md body Load only when skill is triggered <5k words recommended
Level 3 â€” Bundled resources Codex loads individual files only when needed Unlimited (scripts can only be executed)

4. Skill navigation and loading

discover_skills_under_root() searches under root with BFS:

// loader.rs â€” يƒگىƒ‰ ى œي•œ ىƒپىˆک
const SKILLS_FILENAME: &str = "SKILL.md";
const MAX_SCAN_DEPTH: usize = 6;           // rootë،œë¶€ي„° ىµœëŒ€ 6ë‹¨ê³„
const MAX_SKILLS_DIRS_PER_ROOT: usize = 2000;  // rootë‹¹ ىµœëŒ€ 2000 ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬

// symlink ë”°ë‌¼ê°€ê¸°: Repo/User/Adminى—گى„œë§Œ, Systemى—گى„œëٹ” ي•کى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ
let follow_symlinks = matches!(scope, SkillScope::Repo | SkillScope::User | SkillScope::Admin);

Loading results are sorted by scope priority (Repo=0 < User=1 < System=2 < Admin=3) and name, and duplicates of the same path are removed:

// loader.rs
outcome.skills.sort_by(|a, b| {
    scope_rank(a.scope).cmp(&scope_rank(b.scope))
        .then_with(|| a.name.cmp(&b.name))
        .then_with(|| a.path_to_skills_md.cmp(&b.path_to_skills_md))
});

5. System Skill Installation â€” Bundle Embedding

The system skill is embedded at compile time as include_dir! in the codex_skills crate:

// codex-rs/skills/src/lib.rs
const SYSTEM_SKILLS_DIR: Dir = include_dir::include_dir!("$CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/src/assets/samples");

install_system_skills() unpacks files embedded in $CODEX_HOME/skills/.system/, but skips them if there are no changes to the fingerprint marker:

// skills/src/lib.rs
pub fn install_system_skills(codex_home: &Path) -> Result<(), SystemSkillsError> {
    let expected_fingerprint = embedded_system_skills_fingerprint(); // salt + ى „ى²´ يŒŒى‌¼ hash
    if dest.is_dir() && read_marker(&marker_path) == expected_fingerprint {
        return Ok(());  // ى‌´ë¯¸ ىµœى‹ 
    }
    // ê¸°ى،´ ى‚­ى œ ي›„ ى‍¬ى„¤ى¹ک
    fs::remove_dir_all(&dest)?;
    write_embedded_dir(&SYSTEM_SKILLS_DIR, &dest)?;
    fs::write(marker_path, format!("{expected_fingerprint}\n"))?;
    Ok(())
}

List of currently bundled system skills: imagegen, openai-docs, plugin-creator, skill-creator, skill-installer.

6. Config Rules â€” Enable/Disable Skills

SkillConfigRules controls enabled/disabled for each skill in the User/SessionFlags config layer:

// config_rules.rs
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub(crate) struct SkillConfigRule {
    pub selector: SkillConfigRuleSelector,  // Name("pdf") ëکگëٹ” Path("/path/to/SKILL.md")
    pub enabled: bool,
}

// ى‹¤ى œ ى پىڑ©
pub(crate) fn resolve_disabled_skill_paths(
    skills: &[SkillMetadata],
    rules: &SkillConfigRules,
) -> HashSet<PathBuf> {
    // Path ى…€ë ‰ي„° â†’ ي•´ë‹¹ ê²½ë،œ ى§پى ‘ ى¶”ê°€/ى œê±°
    // Name ى…€ë ‰ي„° â†’ ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌ک ëھ¨ë“  skill ê²½ë،œى—گ ى پىڑ©
    // layer ىˆœى„œ ë³´ى،´: ë‚کى¤‘ ى…€ë ‰ي„°ê°€ ى•‍ى‌ک ى…€ë ‰ي„°ë¥¼ override
}

This system allows users to turn off specific skills with [[skills.config]] entries in codex.toml.

7. Explicit Trigger â€” Explicit skill selection

Skill triggers are divided into explicit trigger and implicit trigger. Only explicit triggers actually lead to build_skill_injections(), which reads the SKILL.md body and injects it into a contextual user fragment.

7.1 collect_explicit_skill_mentions() â€” Selection algorithm

// injection.rs â€” ë‘گ ë‹¨ê³„ ى„ يƒ‌
pub(crate) fn collect_explicit_skill_mentions(
    inputs: &[UserInput],
    skills: &[SkillMetadata],
    disabled_paths: &HashSet<PathBuf>,
    connector_slug_counts: &HashMap<String, usize>,
) -> Vec<SkillMetadata> {
    // 1ë‹¨ê³„: structured UserInput::Skill â†’ path ê¸°ë°ک ى •ي™• ë§¤ى¹­ (ê°€ى‍¥ ê°•ي•œ selection)
    for input in inputs {
        if let UserInput::Skill { name, path } = input {
            blocked_plain_names.insert(name.clone());  // 2ë‹¨ê³„ى—گى„œ ى¤‘ë³µ ë°©ى§€
            if let Some(skill) = skills.iter().find(|s| s.path_to_skills_md == *path) {
                selected.push(skill.clone());
            }
        }
    }

    // 2ë‹¨ê³„: ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ $skill-name ëکگëٹ” [$skill-name](resource-path) â†’ ى‌´ë¦„/ê²½ë،œ ë§¤ى¹­
    for input in inputs {
        if let UserInput::Text { text, .. } = input {
            let mentions = extract_tool_mentions(text);
            select_skills_from_mentions(&context, &mentions, &mut selected);
        }
    }
    selected
}

7.2 Parsing text mentions

extract_tool_mentions() parses both $skill-name and [$skill-name](resource-path):

// injection.rs â€” mention يک•يƒœ
const SKILL_PATH_PREFIX: &str = "skill://";

// 1) ë§پيپ¬يک•: [$pdf](skill://path/to/SKILL.md) â†’ pathë،œ ى •ي™• ë§¤ى¹­
// 2) ë‹¨ىˆœيک•: $pdf â†’ nameىœ¼ë،œ ë§¤ى¹­ (ë‹¨, ى‌´ë¦„ى‌´ uniqueي•œ ê²½ىڑ°ë§Œ)
//    ambiguous name(ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ skillى‌´ 2ê°œ+)ى‌´ë©´ ىٹ¤ي‚µ
//    connector slugى™€ ى¶©ëڈŒي•´ëڈ„ ىٹ¤ي‚µ

// ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک ىک¤يƒگ ë°©ى§€
fn is_common_env_var(name: &str) -> bool {
    matches!(name.to_uppercase().as_str(), "PATH" | "HOME" | "USER" | "SHELL" | ...)
}

// mention ى‌´ë¦„ى—گ ي—ˆىڑ©ëگکëٹ” ë¬¸ى‍گ
fn is_mention_name_char(byte: u8) -> bool {
    matches!(byte, b'a'..=b'z' | b'A'..=b'Z' | b'0'..=b'9' | b'_' | b'-' | b':')
}

7.3 build_skill_injections() â€” Actual body injections

// injection.rs
pub(crate) async fn build_skill_injections(
    mentioned_skills: &[SkillMetadata],
    otel: Option<&SessionTelemetry>,
    analytics_client: &AnalyticsEventsClient,
    tracking: TrackEventsContext,
) -> SkillInjections {
    for skill in mentioned_skills {
        match fs::read_to_string(&skill.path_to_skills_md).await {
            Ok(contents) => {
                // otel counter: codex.skill.injected (status=ok)
                // analytics: SkillInvocation { invocation_type: Explicit }
                result.items.push(ResponseItem::from(SkillInstructions {
                    name: skill.name.clone(),
                    path: skill.path_to_skills_md.to_string_lossy().into_owned(),
                    contents,       // SKILL.md ى „ى²´ ë³¸ë¬¸
                }));
            }
            Err(err) => {
                // otel counter: codex.skill.injected (status=error)
                result.warnings.push(format!("Failed to load skill {}: {err:#}", skill.name));
            }
        }
    }
    result
}

The injected skill is wrapped in a <skill> tag and entered into the user message fragment.

8. Implicit Trigger â€” Implicit detection and telemetry

Even if there is a policy field like allow_implicit_invocation, looking at the code now, the implicit path is closer to "detection and recording" and not "automatically inserting actual directives"**. Just by looking at the name, you should not misunderstand it as an automatic skill auto-run.

8.1 Implicit detection index

// invocation_utils.rs â€” ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
pub(crate) fn build_implicit_skill_path_indexes(skills: Vec<SkillMetadata>) -> (
    HashMap<PathBuf, SkillMetadata>,  // scripts_dir â†’ skill (ىکˆ: .../pdf/scripts/ â†’ pdf)
    HashMap<PathBuf, SkillMetadata>,  // doc_path â†’ skill   (ىکˆ: .../pdf/SKILL.md â†’ pdf)
) {
    for skill in skills {
        // 1) SKILL.md ê²½ë،œ ى‍گى²´
        by_skill_doc_path.insert(normalize_path(&skill.path_to_skills_md), skill.clone());
        // 2) SKILL.md ë¶€ëھ¨ى‌ک scripts/ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬
        if let Some(skill_dir) = skill.path_to_skills_md.parent() {
            by_scripts_dir.insert(normalize_path(&skill_dir.join("scripts")), skill);
        }
    }
}

8.2 Command-based detection

We tokenize the commands the agent executes and check two things:

// invocation_utils.rs

// 1) ىٹ¤يپ¬ë¦½يٹ¸ ى‹¤ي–‰ ê°گى§€: python scripts/rotate.py â†’ scripts/ ê²½ë،œë،œ skill ى—°ê²°
fn detect_skill_script_run(outcome: &SkillLoadOutcome, tokens: &[String], workdir: &Path) -> Option<SkillMetadata> {
    let script_token = script_run_token(tokens)?;  // python/bash/node ë“±ى‌ک runner + .py/.sh/.js ë“±ى‌ک ي™•ى‍¥ى‍گ
    let script_path = workdir.join(script_token);
    for ancestor in script_path.ancestors() {
        if let Some(candidate) = outcome.implicit_skills_by_scripts_dir.get(ancestor) {
            return Some(candidate.clone());
        }
    }
    None
}

// 2) ë¬¸ى„œ ى‌½ê¸° ê°گى§€: cat SKILL.md â†’ doc_pathë،œ skill ى—°ê²°
fn detect_skill_doc_read(outcome: &SkillLoadOutcome, tokens: &[String], workdir: &Path) -> Option<SkillMetadata> {
    // cat/sed/head/tail/less/more/bat/awk ى¤‘ ي•کë‚کى‌¼ ë•Œë§Œ ى§„ى‍…
    if !command_reads_file(tokens) { return None; }
    for token in tokens.iter().skip(1) {
        if let Some(candidate) = outcome.implicit_skills_by_doc_path.get(&normalize_path(token)) {
            return Some(candidate.clone());
        }
    }
    None
}

Detected results only publish telemetry (no body injection):

// invocation_utils.rs
pub(crate) async fn maybe_emit_implicit_skill_invocation(sess: &Session, ...) {
    let candidate = detect_implicit_skill_invocation_for_command(...)?;
    // ى¤‘ë³µ ë°©ى§€: seen_key = "scope:path:name"
    if !turn_context.turn_skills.implicit_invocation_seen_skills.insert(seen_key) { return; }
    // otel counter: codex.skill.injected (invoke_type=implicit)
    // analytics: SkillInvocation { invocation_type: Implicit }
}

9. Skill list rendering

render_skills_section() wraps the available skills in a <skills_instructions> tag and puts them into the system prompt:

// render.rs
pub fn render_skills_section(skills: &[SkillMetadata]) -> Option<String> {
    let mut lines = Vec::new();
    lines.push("## Skills");
    lines.push("A skill is a set of local instructions...");
    lines.push("### Available skills");
    for skill in skills {
        lines.push(format!("- {name}: {description} (file: {path})"));
    }
    lines.push("### How to use skills");
    // trigger rules, progressive disclosure, coordination, safety ë“± ىƒپى„¸ ê°€ى‌´ë“œ
    Some(format!("{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_OPEN_TAG}\n{body}\n{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_CLOSE_TAG}"))
}

Key usage instructions included in this render:

  • Must be used when the user mentions a skill using $SkillName or plain text
  • Open SKILL.md, but read only as much as necessary with progressive disclosure.
  • If scripts/ is present, execution/patch takes precedence over code rewriting.
  • If multiple skills are applied, select the minimum set and specify the order

10. Env Var Dependencies â€” Environment variable dependencies

Independent of the MCP server installation, skills can have dependencies of type env_var:

// env_var_dependencies.rs
pub(crate) fn collect_env_var_dependencies(mentioned_skills: &[SkillMetadata]) -> Vec<SkillDependencyInfo> {
    for skill in mentioned_skills {
        for tool in &skill.dependencies?.tools {
            if tool.r#type == "env_var" && !tool.value.is_empty() {
                dependencies.push(SkillDependencyInfo {
                    skill_name: skill.name.clone(),
                    name: tool.value.clone(),       // ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک ى‌´ë¦„
                    description: tool.description.clone(),
                });
            }
        }
    }
}

Solution order: â‘  session cache â†’ â‘، env::var() â†’ â‘¢ Ask the user through UI (request_user_input). Values â€‹â€‹are stored only in session memory and are not written to disk.

11. Remote Skills â€” Server-based skills

remote.rs implements a client that queries and downloads remote skills through the ChatGPT API. Currently not yet connected to an active product surface (note: "intentionally kept around for future wiring").

11.1 Remote skill scope

// remote.rs
pub enum RemoteSkillScope {
    WorkspaceShared,   // workspace ê³µىœ  ىٹ¤ي‚¬
    AllShared,         // ى „ى²´ ê³µىœ  ىٹ¤ي‚¬
    Personal,          // ê°œى‌¸ ىٹ¤ي‚¬
    Example,           // ىکˆى‹œ ىٹ¤ي‚¬
}

pub enum RemoteSkillProductSurface {
    Chatgpt, Codex, Api, Atlas,
}

11.2 Views and Downloads

// remote.rs â€” ى،°يڑŒ
pub async fn list_remote_skills(config, auth, scope, product_surface, enabled)
    -> Result<Vec<RemoteSkillSummary>>
{
    // GET {chatgpt_base_url}/hazelnuts?product_surface=codex&scope=workspace-shared
    // ChatGPT auth ي•„ىˆک (API key ë¶ˆê°€)
}

// remote.rs â€” ë‹¤ىڑ´ë،œë“œ (zip يک•يƒœë،œ ë‚´ë ¤ë°›ى•„ $CODEX_HOME/skills/{id}/ ى—گ ي’€ê¸°)
pub async fn export_remote_skill(config, auth, skill_id) -> Result<RemoteSkillDownloadResult> {
    // GET {chatgpt_base_url}/hazelnuts/{skill_id}/export
    // zip magic bytes ê²€ى¦‌ â†’ safe_joinىœ¼ë،œ path traversal ë°©ى§€ â†’ ى••ى¶• ي•´ى œ
}

safe_join() prevents path traversal attacks by verifying that all path components of a zip entry are Normal.

12. MCP Dependency installation path

Skills also have tool dependencies. You can define the MCP server, transport, command, url, etc. with SkillToolDependency, and if the feature flag is turned on, there is even a path to modify the global config after asking with request_user_input whether to install the MCP server required for the selected skill.

// model.rs
pub struct SkillToolDependency {
    pub r#type: String,          // "mcp_server", "env_var" ë“±
    pub value: String,           // ى„œë²„ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌´ë‚ک ë³€ىˆک ى‌´ë¦„
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub transport: Option<String>, // "stdio", "sse" ë“±
    pub command: Option<String>,   // ى‹¤ي–‰ ëھ…ë ¹ى–´
    pub url: Option<String>,       // ى„œë²„ URL
}
steps Description file
1 Collect tool dependency required for selected skill skills/model.rs
2 Comparison with currently installed MCP server mcp/skill_dependencies.rs
3 If necessary, ask whether to install with request_user_input mcp/skill_dependencies.rs
4 Modify global config to register MCP server mcp/skill_dependencies.rs
5 If OAuth is required, try logging in mcp/skill_dependencies.rs

This route is quite aggressive. Therefore, even if we maintain the skill in our fork, a separate policy decision must be made as to whether the skill can even modify the global config.

Comprehensive architecture diagram

graph TD

    subgraph "ë،œë”©"

    subgraph "ي•„ي„°ë§پ"

        RDP --> FIN["finalize_skill_outcome()"]

    subgraph "UI ي‘œë©´"

sequenceDiagram
    participant User as ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ
    participant Core as Codex Core
    participant SM as SkillsManager

    participant FS as يŒŒى‌¼ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ

    participant OTel as Telemetry

    User->>Core: "$pdfë،œ ë³€ي™کي•´ى¤ک" (UserInput::Text)

    alt cache miss
        SM->>Loader: skill_roots() â†’ root ëھ©ë،‌

    Note over Inj: "$pdf" يŒŒى‹± â†’ name ë§¤ى¹­<br/>unique nameى‌´ë©´ ى„ يƒ‌
    Inj-->>Core: mentioned_skills: [pdf]

    Core->>Inj: build_skill_injections(mentioned_skills)
    Inj->>FS: fs::read_to_string(SKILL.md)
    FS-->>Inj: SKILL.md ë³¸ë¬¸

    Core->>Core: م€ˆskillم€‰ wrapperë،œ user messageى—گ ى£¼ى‍…

Technical Reference

Core data structures

struct Meaning representative file
SkillMetadata Name, description, interface, dependencies, policy, permissions, path, scope skills/model.rs
SkillPolicy Implicit invocation allowed and product restrictions skills/model.rs
SkillInterface display name, icon, brand color, default prompt skills/model.rs
SkillDependencies List of external tool dependencies (tools: Vec<SkillToolDependency>) skills/model.rs
SkillToolDependency Individual dependencies consisting of type, value, transport, command, and url skills/model.rs
SkillLoadOutcome Loaded skill, error, disabled path, implicit index skills/model.rs
SkillConfigRules List of enable/disable rules extracted from config layer skills/config_rules.rs
SkillInjections items to be injected (ResponseItem) and warnings skills/injection.rs
SkillManagedNetworkOverride network allowed/denied domains by skill skills/model.rs
RemoteSkillSummary Remote skill id, name, description skills/remote.rs

skill root and scope

scope path Remarks
Repo .codex/skills/, .agents/skills/ (cwdâ†’project root each step) Local skill created by user
User $CODEX_HOME/skills/, $HOME/.agents/skills/, plugin roots universal personal skill
System $CODEX_HOME/skills/.system/ Bundle skill embedded in binary
Admin /etc/codex/skills/ organizational policy skill

lifecycle

steps Description
root calculation skill_roots() combines config layer + plugin + .agents paths
Navigation discover_skills_under_root() finds SKILL.md file with BFS (depthâ‰¤6, dirsâ‰¤2000)
parsing parse_skill_file() reads frontmatter + agents/openai.yaml
product filter filter_skill_load_outcome_for_product() applies product restrictions
config rules resolve_disabled_skill_paths() applies disabling based on user settings
implicit index build_implicit_skill_path_indexes() builds scripts_dir/doc_path index
explicit selection collect_explicit_skill_mentions() selects the skill for the current turn using structured input and $skill-name
body injection build_skill_injections() wraps the actual SKILL.md body in <skill> tags
Show list render_skills_section() creates available skills catalog as <skills_instructions>
Resolve dependency env_var â†’ session cache/env/UI question, MCP â†’ global config modification

trigger type

trigger Actual Action code path
structured UserInput::Skill Explicit selection based on path, strongest explicit selection skills/injection.rs
text $skill-name Plain name â†’ Select only when unique match skills/injection.rs
Link type [$name](skill://path) Accurate matching based on path skills/injection.rs
plugin mention skill Generate developer hint rather than body plugins/injection.rs
shell script execution detection Issue telemetry when running a scripts/ sub (not inject body) skills/invocation_utils.rs
file read detection When reading SKILL.md with cat/head, etc., telemetry is issued (not body injection) skills/invocation_utils.rs

Actual injection format

Item Format implementation
skill text user message fragment with <skill> wrapper instructions/user_instructions.rs
Introduction to skill list <skills_instructions> wrapper, inserted into system prompt skills/render.rs
app-server skill query skills/list RPC app-server/src/codex_message_processor.rs
skill enable/disable skills/config/write RPC app-server/src/codex_message_processor.rs

Differences we especially need to remember

Name Real Meaning
explicit skill mention Leads to actual body injection
implicit skill invocation Command-related telemetry, not automatic body injection, is the key
bundled system skills Embed in binary as include_dir! â†’ cache in $CODEX_HOME/skills/.system/
plugin skills It is handled together with the plugin capability hint rather than the skill itself
remote skills ChatGPT API-based remote skill (not yet connected, future wiring)
env_var dependency Stored only in session-local memory, not written to disk
MCP dependency Global config modification is possible, security policy review required

Fork Checklist

  • skill loader and injector are different
  • Only [x] explicit trigger leads to actual body injection
  • implicit invocation is currently closer to the telemetry hook
  • skill is closer to a package with UI metadata and dependencies
  • Remember that there is a system skill installation path (include_dir! + fingerprint marker)
  • MCP dependency installation function is powerful, but requires separate policy review
  • config rules allow skill enable/disable to be controlled on a per user/session basis
  • remote skills API exists, but is not yet connected
  • env_var dependency is session-only, and MCP dependency modifies global config.
  • A separate decision must be made whether to maintain the global MCP config modification function.
  • We need to decide whether to use default_prompt and icon metadata as is in our product UI.
  • It must be decided whether to maintain product restriction and disabled path policies.
  • We need to decide whether to fork the remote skills client to fit our infrastructure or remove it altogether.
  • We need to decide whether to integrate skill permission profile (network domain override, file_system, macos seatbelt) into our sandbox.

List of core files

file Role
codex-rs/core/src/skills/manager.rs SkillsManager â€” Dual cache, root calculation, outcome build
codex-rs/core/src/skills/loader.rs root interpretation, BFS navigation, parsing SKILL.md/openai.yaml
codex-rs/core/src/skills/model.rs Core data structures (SkillMetadata, Policy, Interface, etc.)
codex-rs/core/src/skills/injection.rs Collect explicit mention, inject SKILL.md body
codex-rs/core/src/skills/invocation_utils.rs Detect implicit invocation (telemetry only)
codex-rs/core/src/skills/render.rs Rendering a list of available skills
codex-rs/core/src/skills/system.rs system skill install/uninstall delegate
codex-rs/core/src/skills/config_rules.rs config layer based enable/disable rules
codex-rs/core/src/skills/env_var_dependencies.rs env_var dependency collection and UI questions
codex-rs/core/src/skills/remote.rs Remote skill inquiry/download (ChatGPT API)
codex-rs/skills/src/lib.rs system skill embedding, fingerprint, installation logic
codex-rs/core/src/mcp/skill_dependencies.rs MCP server dependency installation
codex-rs/app-server/src/codex_message_processor.rs RPC: skills/list, skills/config/write

Area Snapshot Current

Reorganize core file lists into role-based tables.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 004_skill-system/04_co_skill.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

ىٹ¤ي‚¬ ë،œë”© آ· يٹ¸ë¦¬ê±° آ· ى£¼ى‍… ë¶„ى„‌

ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” core-skills/, skills/, SkillTool integration.

Codexى‌ک skill ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œى‌€ ê²‰ë³´ê¸°ë³´ë‹¤ ى¸µى‌´ ë§ژë‹¤. ë³´ي†µى‌€ "SKILL.mdë¥¼ ى‌½ى–´ى„œ ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸ى—گ ë„£ëٹ”ë‹¤" ى •ëڈ„ë،œ ى‌´ي•´ي•کê¸° ى‰¬ىڑ´ëچ°, ى‹¤ى œ êµ¬يک„ى‌€ root ى„ يƒ‌, metadata ë،œë“œ, enabled or disabled يŒگى •, explicit mention ى„ يƒ‌, dependency ى„¤ى¹ک, ë³¸ë¬¸ ى£¼ى‍…, implicit invocation telemetryê¹Œى§€ ë”°ë،œ ë¶„ë¦¬ëگکى–´ ى‍ˆë‹¤.

ى‌´ êµ¬ى،°ë¥¼ ى œëŒ€ë،œ ë´گى•¼ ي•کëٹ” ى‌´ىœ ëٹ” ىڑ°ë¦¬ê°€ ىٹ¤ي‚¬ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œى‌„ ى–´ë–»ê²Œ يڈ¬يپ¬ي• ى§€ ê²°ى •ي•´ى•¼ ي•کê¸° ë•Œë¬¸ى‌´ë‹¤. ى§€ê¸ˆ Codex skillى‌€ ë‹¨ىˆœ ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ى§€ى‹œë¬¸ى‌´ë©´ى„œëڈ„, ëڈ™ى‹œى—گ UI ë…¸ى¶œ ê°€ëٹ¥ي•œ ى‌¸ي„°يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„°ى™€ ëڈ„êµ¬ ى‌کى،´ى„±ى‌„ ê°€ى§„ë‹¤. ى¦‰ "ë¬¸ى„œ ى،°ê°پ"ى‌´ ى•„ë‹ˆë‌¼ "ى‍‘ى‌€ ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ ى„¤ëھ…ى„œ"ى—گ ëچ” ê°€ê¹‌ë‹¤.

1. SkillsManager â€” ى؛گى‹œى™€ ى§„ى‍…ى گ

SkillsManagerëٹ” cwdى™€ config stackى‌„ ë°”يƒ•ىœ¼ë،œ skill rootë¥¼ ê³„ى‚°ي•کê³ , ë،œë”© ê²°ê³¼ë¥¼ ى‌´ى¤‘ ى؛گى‹œë،œ ë³´ê´€ي•œë‹¤.

// manager.rs
pub struct SkillsManager {
    codex_home: PathBuf,
    plugins_manager: Arc<PluginsManager>,
    restriction_product: Option<Product>,
    cache_by_cwd: RwLock<HashMap<PathBuf, SkillLoadOutcome>>,       // cwd ê¸°ë°ک
    cache_by_config: RwLock<HashMap<ConfigSkillsCacheKey, SkillLoadOutcome>>,  // config ê¸°ë°ک
}

ى؛گى‹œê°€ ë‘گ ê°œى‌¸ ى‌´ىœ : cache_by_cwdëٹ” app-serverى‌ک skills_for_cwd() ê²½ë،œىڑ©ى‌´ê³ , cache_by_configëٹ” ى‌´ë¯¸ êµ¬ى„±ëگœ Configë،œ ى،°يڑŒي•  ë•Œ role-local/session-local ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œê°€ ë‹¤ë¥¸ ى„¸ى…ک ى‚¬ى‌´ى—گى„œ ëˆ„ى¶œëگکى§€ ى•ٹëڈ„ë،‌ ي•œë‹¤.

ىƒ‌ى„±ى‍گى—گى„œ bundled_skills_enabled ي”Œë‍کê·¸ى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ system skillى‌„ ى¦‰ى‹œ ى„¤ى¹کي•کê±°ë‚ک ى œê±°ي•œë‹¤:

// manager.rs â€” ىƒ‌ى„±ى‍گ ي•µى‹¬ë¶€
if !bundled_skills_enabled {
    uninstall_system_skills(&manager.codex_home);
} else if let Err(err) = install_system_skills(&manager.codex_home) {
    tracing::error!("failed to install system skills: {err}");
}

ى‹¤ى œ skill ë¹Œë“œ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌€ build_skill_outcome()ى—گ ى§‘ى•½ëگœë‹¤:

// manager.rs
fn build_skill_outcome(&self, roots: Vec<SkillRoot>, skill_config_rules: &SkillConfigRules) -> SkillLoadOutcome {
    let outcome = filter_skill_load_outcome_for_product(
        load_skills_from_roots(roots),
        self.restriction_product,
    );
    let disabled_paths = resolve_disabled_skill_paths(&outcome.skills, skill_config_rules);
    finalize_skill_outcome(outcome, disabled_paths)
}

finalize_skill_outcome()ى‌€ disabled pathë¥¼ ى پىڑ©ي•œ ë’¤, implicit invocationىڑ© ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤ ë‘گ ê°œ(implicit_skills_by_scripts_dir, implicit_skills_by_doc_path)ë¥¼ êµ¬ى¶•ي•œë‹¤.

2. Skill Root ي•´ى„‌ â€” ى–´ë””ى„œ ىٹ¤ي‚¬ى‌„ ى°¾ëٹ”ê°€

rootëٹ” user, repo, system, admin scopeë¥¼ ë‚کëˆ  ê°–ê³ , system skillى‌€ codex_skills يŒ¨ي‚¤ى§€ى—گى„œ ë³„ëڈ„ ى„¤ى¹کëگœë‹¤. ë”°ë‌¼ى„œ ىڑ°ë¦¬ يڈ¬يپ¬ى—گى„œ system skillى‌„ ىœ ى§€ي•œë‹¤ë©´, ë‹¨ىˆœ يŒŒى‌¼ ë²ˆë“¤ë³´ë‹¤ "ى„¤ى¹کى™€ ى؛گى‹œ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬" ê°œë…گê¹Œى§€ ê°™ى‌´ ê°€ى ¸ى™€ى•¼ ي•œë‹¤.

skill_roots_from_layer_stack_inner()ê°€ config layerë¥¼ ىˆœيڑŒي•کë©° scopeë³„ rootë¥¼ ê²°ى •ي•œë‹¤:

// loader.rs â€” root ي•´ى„‌ ي•µى‹¬ ë،œى§پ (ى¶•ى•½)
match &layer.name {
    ConfigLayerSource::Project { .. } => {
        roots.push(SkillRoot {
            path: config_folder.as_path().join("skills"),
            scope: SkillScope::Repo,
        });
    }
    ConfigLayerSource::User { .. } => {
        // 1) $CODEX_HOME/skills (deprecated, backward compat)
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: config_folder.join("skills"), scope: SkillScope::User });
        // 2) $HOME/.agents/skills (user-installed)
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: home_dir.join(".agents/skills"), scope: SkillScope::User });
        // 3) $CODEX_HOME/skills/.system (embedded system skills cache)
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: system_cache_root_dir(config_folder), scope: SkillScope::System });
    }
    ConfigLayerSource::System { .. } => {
        // /etc/codex/skills â†’ Admin scope
        roots.push(SkillRoot { path: config_folder.join("skills"), scope: SkillScope::Admin });
    }
    _ => {}
}

ى¶”ê°€ë،œ repo_agents_skill_roots()ëٹ” cwdى—گى„œ project rootê¹Œى§€ ىک¬ë‌¼ê°€ë©´ى„œ ê°پ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬ى‌ک .agents/skills/ ë¥¼ repo scopeë،œ ى¶”ê°€ي•œë‹¤:

// loader.rs
fn repo_agents_skill_roots(config_layer_stack: &ConfigLayerStack, cwd: &Path) -> Vec<SkillRoot> {
    let project_root = find_project_root(cwd, &project_root_markers);
    let dirs = dirs_between_project_root_and_cwd(cwd, &project_root);
    for dir in dirs {
        let agents_skills = dir.join(".agents").join("skills");
        if agents_skills.is_dir() {
            roots.push(SkillRoot { path: agents_skills, scope: SkillScope::Repo });
        }
    }
    roots
}

graph LR
    subgraph "Skill Root يƒگىƒ‰ ىˆœى„œ"
        P["Project config<br/>.codex/skills/"] -->|Repo| R["roots"]
        A1[".agents/skills/<br/>(cwd â†’ project root)"] -->|Repo| R
        U1["$CODEX_HOME/skills/"] -->|User| R
        U2["$HOME/.agents/skills/"] -->|User| R
        PL["plugin skill roots"] -->|User| R
        S["$CODEX_HOME/skills/.system/"] -->|System| R
        AD["/etc/codex/skills/"] -->|Admin| R
    end
    R --> DEDUP["dedupe_skill_roots_by_path()"]

3. SKILL.md يڈ¬ë§·ê³¼ يŒŒى‹±

3.1 ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬ êµ¬ى،°

skill-name/
â”œâ”€â”€ SKILL.md                 â†گ ي•„ىˆک (frontmatter + ë³¸ë¬¸)
â”œâ”€â”€ agents/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ openai.yaml          â†گ UI ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„° (interface, dependencies, policy, permissions)
â”œâ”€â”€ scripts/                 â†گ ى‹¤ي–‰ ê°€ëٹ¥ي•œ ى½”ë“œ
â”œâ”€â”€ references/              â†گ ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ى—گ ë،œë“œي•  ى°¸ى،° ë¬¸ى„œ
â””â”€â”€ assets/                  â†گ ى¶œë ¥ى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•  يŒŒى‌¼ (icon, template ë“±)

3.2 SKILL.md frontmatter يŒŒى‹±

parse_skill_file()ى‌´ YAML frontmatterë¥¼ يŒŒى‹±ي•´ SkillMetadataë¥¼ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•œë‹¤:

// loader.rs
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct SkillFrontmatter {
    name: Option<String>,
    description: Option<String>,
    metadata: SkillFrontmatterMetadata,  // short-description ë“±
}

fn parse_skill_file(path: &Path, scope: SkillScope) -> Result<SkillMetadata, SkillParseError> {
    let contents = fs::read_to_string(path)?;
    let frontmatter = extract_frontmatter(&contents)?;      // --- ... --- êµ¬ê°„ ى¶”ى¶œ
    let parsed: SkillFrontmatter = serde_yaml::from_str(&frontmatter)?;

    // nameى‌´ ى—†ىœ¼ë©´ ë¶€ëھ¨ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌„ ê¸°ë³¸ê°’ىœ¼ë،œ ى‚¬ىڑ©
    let base_name = parsed.name
        .map(sanitize_single_line)
        .unwrap_or_else(|| default_skill_name(path));
    let name = namespaced_skill_name(path, &base_name);     // plugin namespace ى ‘ë‘گى‚¬

    let LoadedSkillMetadata { interface, dependencies, policy, permission_profile, managed_network_override }
        = load_skill_metadata(path);  // agents/openai.yaml ë،œë“œ

    Ok(SkillMetadata { name, description, interface, dependencies, policy, ... })
}

ىœ يڑ¨ى„± ê²€ى¦‌: name ىµœëŒ€ 64ى‍گ, description ىµœëŒ€ 1024ى‍گ. ë¹ˆ ê°’ى‌´ë©´ ى—گëں¬ë¥¼ ë°کي™کي•œë‹¤.

3.3 agents/openai.yaml êµ¬ى،°

// loader.rs â€” ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„° يŒŒى‌¼ êµ¬ى،°
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct SkillMetadataFile {
    interface: Option<Interface>,       // display_name, icon, brand_color, default_prompt
    dependencies: Option<Dependencies>, // MCP ى„œë²„, env_var ë“±
    policy: Option<Policy>,             // allow_implicit_invocation, products
    permissions: Option<SkillPermissionProfile>,  // network, file_system, macos seatbelt
}

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct Interface {
    display_name: Option<String>,
    short_description: Option<String>,
    icon_small: Option<PathBuf>,    // assets/ ي•کىœ„ ىƒپëŒ€ ê²½ë،œë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©
    icon_large: Option<PathBuf>,
    brand_color: Option<String>,
    default_prompt: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct Policy {
    allow_implicit_invocation: Option<bool>,  // ê¸°ë³¸ê°’ true
    products: Vec<Product>,                   // ë¹ˆ ë°°ى—´ى‌´ë©´ ëھ¨ë“  productى—گى„œ ى‚¬ىڑ© ê°€ëٹ¥
}

icon ê²½ë،œëٹ” ë°کë“œى‹œ assets/ ي•کىœ„ ىƒپëŒ€ ê²½ë،œى—¬ى•¼ ي•œë‹¤. .. ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‹œ ê±°ë¶€ëگœë‹¤:

// loader.rs
fn resolve_asset_path(skill_dir: &Path, field: &str, path: Option<PathBuf>) -> Option<PathBuf> {
    // ى ˆëŒ€ ê²½ë،œ ê±°ë¶€, ".." ى»´يڈ¬ë„Œيٹ¸ ê±°ë¶€, "assets/" ى ‘ë‘گى‚¬ ي•„ىˆک
    let mut components = normalized.components();
    match components.next() {
        Some(Component::Normal(c)) if c == "assets" => {} // OK
        _ => { warn!("icon path must be under assets/"); return None; }
    }
    Some(skill_dir.join(normalized))
}

3.4 Progressive Disclosure ى„¤ê³„

ىٹ¤ي‚¬ى‌€ ى„¸ ë‹¨ê³„ë،œ ى گى§„ى پ ë،œë”©ëگœë‹¤:

ë‹¨ê³„ ë‚´ىڑ© يپ¬ê¸°
Level 1 â€” Metadata name + description (ي•­ىƒپ ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ى—گ ى،´ى‍¬) ~100 words
Level 2 â€” SKILL.md body skillى‌´ triggerëگ  ë•Œë§Œ ë،œë“œ <5k words ê¶Œى‍¥
Level 3 â€” Bundled resources Codexê°€ ي•„ىڑ”ي•  ë•Œë§Œ ê°œë³„ يŒŒى‌¼ ë،œë“œ ë¬´ى œي•œ (scriptsëٹ” ى‹¤ي–‰ë§Œىœ¼ë،œ ê°€ëٹ¥)

4. Skill يƒگىƒ‰ê³¼ ë،œë”©

discover_skills_under_root()ى‌€ BFSë،œ root ى•„ë‍کë¥¼ يƒگىƒ‰ي•œë‹¤:

// loader.rs â€” يƒگىƒ‰ ى œي•œ ىƒپىˆک
const SKILLS_FILENAME: &str = "SKILL.md";
const MAX_SCAN_DEPTH: usize = 6;           // rootë،œë¶€ي„° ىµœëŒ€ 6ë‹¨ê³„
const MAX_SKILLS_DIRS_PER_ROOT: usize = 2000;  // rootë‹¹ ىµœëŒ€ 2000 ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬

// symlink ë”°ë‌¼ê°€ê¸°: Repo/User/Adminى—گى„œë§Œ, Systemى—گى„œëٹ” ي•کى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ
let follow_symlinks = matches!(scope, SkillScope::Repo | SkillScope::User | SkillScope::Admin);

ë،œë”© ê²°ê³¼ëٹ” scope ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„(Repo=0 < User=1 < System=2 < Admin=3)ى™€ ى‌´ë¦„ ىˆœىœ¼ë،œ ى •ë ¬ëگکê³ , ê°™ى‌€ ê²½ë،œى‌ک ى¤‘ë³µى‌€ ى œê±°ëگœë‹¤:

// loader.rs
outcome.skills.sort_by(|a, b| {
    scope_rank(a.scope).cmp(&scope_rank(b.scope))
        .then_with(|| a.name.cmp(&b.name))
        .then_with(|| a.path_to_skills_md.cmp(&b.path_to_skills_md))
});

5. System Skill ى„¤ى¹ک â€” ë²ˆë“¤ ى‍„ë² ë”©

system skillى‌€ codex_skills يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸ى—گ include_dir!ë،œ ى»´يŒŒى‌¼يƒ€ى‍„ ى‍„ë² ë”©ëگœë‹¤:

// codex-rs/skills/src/lib.rs
const SYSTEM_SKILLS_DIR: Dir = include_dir::include_dir!("$CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/src/assets/samples");

install_system_skills()ëٹ” $CODEX_HOME/skills/.system/ى—گ ى‍„ë² ë”©ëگœ يŒŒى‌¼ë“¤ى‌„ ي’€ى–´ë†“ëگک, fingerprint markerë،œ ë³€ê²½ى‌´ ى—†ىœ¼ë©´ ىٹ¤ي‚µي•œë‹¤:

// skills/src/lib.rs
pub fn install_system_skills(codex_home: &Path) -> Result<(), SystemSkillsError> {
    let expected_fingerprint = embedded_system_skills_fingerprint(); // salt + ى „ى²´ يŒŒى‌¼ hash
    if dest.is_dir() && read_marker(&marker_path) == expected_fingerprint {
        return Ok(());  // ى‌´ë¯¸ ىµœى‹ 
    }
    // ê¸°ى،´ ى‚­ى œ ي›„ ى‍¬ى„¤ى¹ک
    fs::remove_dir_all(&dest)?;
    write_embedded_dir(&SYSTEM_SKILLS_DIR, &dest)?;
    fs::write(marker_path, format!("{expected_fingerprint}\n"))?;
    Ok(())
}

يک„ى‍¬ ë²ˆë“¤ëگœ system skill ëھ©ë،‌: imagegen, openai-docs, plugin-creator, skill-creator, skill-installer.

6. Config Rules â€” ىٹ¤ي‚¬ ي™œى„±ي™”/ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™”

SkillConfigRulesëٹ” User/SessionFlags config layerى—گى„œ ىٹ¤ي‚¬ë³„ enabled/disabledë¥¼ ى œى–´ي•œë‹¤:

// config_rules.rs
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub(crate) struct SkillConfigRule {
    pub selector: SkillConfigRuleSelector,  // Name("pdf") ëکگëٹ” Path("/path/to/SKILL.md")
    pub enabled: bool,
}

// ى‹¤ى œ ى پىڑ©
pub(crate) fn resolve_disabled_skill_paths(
    skills: &[SkillMetadata],
    rules: &SkillConfigRules,
) -> HashSet<PathBuf> {
    // Path ى…€ë ‰ي„° â†’ ي•´ë‹¹ ê²½ë،œ ى§پى ‘ ى¶”ê°€/ى œê±°
    // Name ى…€ë ‰ي„° â†’ ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌ک ëھ¨ë“  skill ê²½ë،œى—گ ى پىڑ©
    // layer ىˆœى„œ ë³´ى،´: ë‚کى¤‘ ى…€ë ‰ي„°ê°€ ى•‍ى‌ک ى…€ë ‰ي„°ë¥¼ override
}

ى‌´ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ëچ•ë¶„ى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گê°€ codex.tomlى—گى„œ [[skills.config]] ي•­ëھ©ىœ¼ë،œ يٹ¹ى • ىٹ¤ي‚¬ى‌„ ëپŒ ىˆک ى‍ˆë‹¤.

7. Explicit Trigger â€” ëھ…ى‹œى پ ىٹ¤ي‚¬ ى„ يƒ‌

ىٹ¤ي‚¬ triggerëٹ” ëھ…ى‹œى پ triggerى™€ ى•”ى‹œى پ triggerë،œ ë‚کë‰œë‹¤. ëھ…ى‹œى پ triggerë§Œى‌´ ى‹¤ى œ build_skill_injections()ë،œ ى‌´ى–´ى ¸ SKILL.md ë³¸ë¬¸ى‌„ ى‌½ê³  contextual user fragmentë،œ ى£¼ى‍…ي•œë‹¤.

7.1 collect_explicit_skill_mentions() â€” ى„ يƒ‌ ى•Œê³ ë¦¬ى¦ک

// injection.rs â€” ë‘گ ë‹¨ê³„ ى„ يƒ‌
pub(crate) fn collect_explicit_skill_mentions(
    inputs: &[UserInput],
    skills: &[SkillMetadata],
    disabled_paths: &HashSet<PathBuf>,
    connector_slug_counts: &HashMap<String, usize>,
) -> Vec<SkillMetadata> {
    // 1ë‹¨ê³„: structured UserInput::Skill â†’ path ê¸°ë°ک ى •ي™• ë§¤ى¹­ (ê°€ى‍¥ ê°•ي•œ selection)
    for input in inputs {
        if let UserInput::Skill { name, path } = input {
            blocked_plain_names.insert(name.clone());  // 2ë‹¨ê³„ى—گى„œ ى¤‘ë³µ ë°©ى§€
            if let Some(skill) = skills.iter().find(|s| s.path_to_skills_md == *path) {
                selected.push(skill.clone());
            }
        }
    }

    // 2ë‹¨ê³„: ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ $skill-name ëکگëٹ” [$skill-name](resource-path) â†’ ى‌´ë¦„/ê²½ë،œ ë§¤ى¹­
    for input in inputs {
        if let UserInput::Text { text, .. } = input {
            let mentions = extract_tool_mentions(text);
            select_skills_from_mentions(&context, &mentions, &mut selected);
        }
    }
    selected
}

7.2 ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ mention يŒŒى‹±

extract_tool_mentions()ê°€ $skill-nameê³¼ [$skill-name](resource-path) ë‘گ يک•يƒœë¥¼ يŒŒى‹±ي•œë‹¤:

// injection.rs â€” mention يک•يƒœ
const SKILL_PATH_PREFIX: &str = "skill://";

// 1) ë§پيپ¬يک•: [$pdf](skill://path/to/SKILL.md) â†’ pathë،œ ى •ي™• ë§¤ى¹­
// 2) ë‹¨ىˆœيک•: $pdf â†’ nameىœ¼ë،œ ë§¤ى¹­ (ë‹¨, ى‌´ë¦„ى‌´ uniqueي•œ ê²½ىڑ°ë§Œ)
//    ambiguous name(ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ skillى‌´ 2ê°œ+)ى‌´ë©´ ىٹ¤ي‚µ
//    connector slugى™€ ى¶©ëڈŒي•´ëڈ„ ىٹ¤ي‚µ

// ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک ىک¤يƒگ ë°©ى§€
fn is_common_env_var(name: &str) -> bool {
    matches!(name.to_uppercase().as_str(), "PATH" | "HOME" | "USER" | "SHELL" | ...)
}

// mention ى‌´ë¦„ى—گ ي—ˆىڑ©ëگکëٹ” ë¬¸ى‍گ
fn is_mention_name_char(byte: u8) -> bool {
    matches!(byte, b'a'..=b'z' | b'A'..=b'Z' | b'0'..=b'9' | b'_' | b'-' | b':')
}

7.3 build_skill_injections() â€” ى‹¤ى œ ë³¸ë¬¸ ى£¼ى‍…

// injection.rs
pub(crate) async fn build_skill_injections(
    mentioned_skills: &[SkillMetadata],
    otel: Option<&SessionTelemetry>,
    analytics_client: &AnalyticsEventsClient,
    tracking: TrackEventsContext,
) -> SkillInjections {
    for skill in mentioned_skills {
        match fs::read_to_string(&skill.path_to_skills_md).await {
            Ok(contents) => {
                // otel counter: codex.skill.injected (status=ok)
                // analytics: SkillInvocation { invocation_type: Explicit }
                result.items.push(ResponseItem::from(SkillInstructions {
                    name: skill.name.clone(),
                    path: skill.path_to_skills_md.to_string_lossy().into_owned(),
                    contents,       // SKILL.md ى „ى²´ ë³¸ë¬¸
                }));
            }
            Err(err) => {
                // otel counter: codex.skill.injected (status=error)
                result.warnings.push(format!("Failed to load skill {}: {err:#}", skill.name));
            }
        }
    }
    result
}

ى£¼ى‍…ëگœ skillى‌€ <skill> يƒœê·¸ë،œ ê°گى‹¸ى ¸ user message fragmentë،œ ë“¤ى–´ê°„ë‹¤.

8. Implicit Trigger â€” ى•”ى‹œى پ ê°گى§€ى™€ telemetry

allow_implicit_invocation ê°™ى‌€ ى •ى±… ي•„ë“œê°€ ى‍ˆى–´ëڈ„, ى§€ê¸ˆ ى½”ë“œë§Œ ë†“ê³  ë³´ë©´ implicit pathëٹ” "يƒگى§€ى™€ ê¸°ë،‌"ى—گ ëچ” ê°€ê¹‌ê³  "ى‹¤ى œ ى§€ى‹œë¬¸ ى‍گëڈ™ يˆ¬ى‍…"ى‌€ ى•„ë‹ˆë‹¤. ى‌´ë¦„ë§Œ ë³´ê³  ى‍گëڈ™ skill auto-runى²کëں¼ ىک¤ي•´ي•کë©´ ى•ˆ ëگœë‹¤.

8.1 ى•”ى‹œى پ ê°گى§€ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤

// invocation_utils.rs â€” ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤
pub(crate) fn build_implicit_skill_path_indexes(skills: Vec<SkillMetadata>) -> (
    HashMap<PathBuf, SkillMetadata>,  // scripts_dir â†’ skill (ىکˆ: .../pdf/scripts/ â†’ pdf)
    HashMap<PathBuf, SkillMetadata>,  // doc_path â†’ skill   (ىکˆ: .../pdf/SKILL.md â†’ pdf)
) {
    for skill in skills {
        // 1) SKILL.md ê²½ë،œ ى‍گى²´
        by_skill_doc_path.insert(normalize_path(&skill.path_to_skills_md), skill.clone());
        // 2) SKILL.md ë¶€ëھ¨ى‌ک scripts/ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬
        if let Some(skill_dir) = skill.path_to_skills_md.parent() {
            by_scripts_dir.insert(normalize_path(&skill_dir.join("scripts")), skill);
        }
    }
}

8.2 command ê¸°ë°ک ê°گى§€

ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ê°€ ى‹¤ي–‰ي•کëٹ” ëھ…ë ¹ى–´ë¥¼ ي† يپ°ي™”ي•´ى„œ ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ë¥¼ ى²´يپ¬ي•œë‹¤:

// invocation_utils.rs

// 1) ىٹ¤يپ¬ë¦½يٹ¸ ى‹¤ي–‰ ê°گى§€: python scripts/rotate.py â†’ scripts/ ê²½ë،œë،œ skill ى—°ê²°
fn detect_skill_script_run(outcome: &SkillLoadOutcome, tokens: &[String], workdir: &Path) -> Option<SkillMetadata> {
    let script_token = script_run_token(tokens)?;  // python/bash/node ë“±ى‌ک runner + .py/.sh/.js ë“±ى‌ک ي™•ى‍¥ى‍گ
    let script_path = workdir.join(script_token);
    for ancestor in script_path.ancestors() {
        if let Some(candidate) = outcome.implicit_skills_by_scripts_dir.get(ancestor) {
            return Some(candidate.clone());
        }
    }
    None
}

// 2) ë¬¸ى„œ ى‌½ê¸° ê°گى§€: cat SKILL.md â†’ doc_pathë،œ skill ى—°ê²°
fn detect_skill_doc_read(outcome: &SkillLoadOutcome, tokens: &[String], workdir: &Path) -> Option<SkillMetadata> {
    // cat/sed/head/tail/less/more/bat/awk ى¤‘ ي•کë‚کى‌¼ ë•Œë§Œ ى§„ى‍…
    if !command_reads_file(tokens) { return None; }
    for token in tokens.iter().skip(1) {
        if let Some(candidate) = outcome.implicit_skills_by_doc_path.get(&normalize_path(token)) {
            return Some(candidate.clone());
        }
    }
    None
}

ê°گى§€ëگœ ê²°ê³¼ëٹ” telemetry ë°œي–‰ë§Œ ي•œë‹¤ (ë³¸ë¬¸ ى£¼ى‍… ى•„ë‹ک):

// invocation_utils.rs
pub(crate) async fn maybe_emit_implicit_skill_invocation(sess: &Session, ...) {
    let candidate = detect_implicit_skill_invocation_for_command(...)?;
    // ى¤‘ë³µ ë°©ى§€: seen_key = "scope:path:name"
    if !turn_context.turn_skills.implicit_invocation_seen_skills.insert(seen_key) { return; }
    // otel counter: codex.skill.injected (invoke_type=implicit)
    // analytics: SkillInvocation { invocation_type: Implicit }
}

9. Skill ëھ©ë،‌ ë Œëچ”ë§پ

render_skills_section()ى‌€ available skillsë¥¼ <skills_instructions> يƒœê·¸ë،œ ê°گى‹¸ system promptى—گ ë„£ëٹ”ë‹¤:

// render.rs
pub fn render_skills_section(skills: &[SkillMetadata]) -> Option<String> {
    let mut lines = Vec::new();
    lines.push("## Skills");
    lines.push("A skill is a set of local instructions...");
    lines.push("### Available skills");
    for skill in skills {
        lines.push(format!("- {name}: {description} (file: {path})"));
    }
    lines.push("### How to use skills");
    // trigger rules, progressive disclosure, coordination, safety ë“± ىƒپى„¸ ê°€ى‌´ë“œ
    Some(format!("{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_OPEN_TAG}\n{body}\n{SKILLS_INSTRUCTIONS_CLOSE_TAG}"))
}

ى‌´ ë Œëچ”ë§پ ê²°ê³¼ى—گ يڈ¬ي•¨ëگکëٹ” ى‚¬ىڑ© ى§€ى¹¨ ي•µى‹¬:

  • ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گê°€ $SkillNameى‌´ë‚ک يڈ‰ë¬¸ىœ¼ë،œ ىٹ¤ي‚¬ى‌„ ى–¸ê¸‰ي•کë©´ ë°کë“œى‹œ ى‚¬ىڑ©
  • SKILL.mdë¥¼ ى—´ëگک, progressive disclosureë،œ ي•„ىڑ”ي•œ ë§Œيپ¼ë§Œ ى‌½ê¸°
  • scripts/ê°€ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ ى½”ë“œ ى‍¬ى‍‘ى„±ë³´ë‹¤ ى‹¤ي–‰/يŒ¨ى¹ک ىڑ°ى„ 
  • ى—¬ëں¬ ىٹ¤ي‚¬ى‌´ ى پىڑ©ëگکë©´ ىµœى†Œ ى§‘ي•©ى‌„ ê³ ë¥´ê³  ىˆœى„œë¥¼ ëھ…ى‹œ

10. Env Var Dependencies â€” ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک ى‌کى،´ى„±

MCP ى„œë²„ ى„¤ى¹کى™€ ë³„ê°œë،œ, skillى‌€ env_var يƒ€ى‍… dependencyë¥¼ ê°€ى§ˆ ىˆک ى‍ˆë‹¤:

// env_var_dependencies.rs
pub(crate) fn collect_env_var_dependencies(mentioned_skills: &[SkillMetadata]) -> Vec<SkillDependencyInfo> {
    for skill in mentioned_skills {
        for tool in &skill.dependencies?.tools {
            if tool.r#type == "env_var" && !tool.value.is_empty() {
                dependencies.push(SkillDependencyInfo {
                    skill_name: skill.name.clone(),
                    name: tool.value.clone(),       // ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک ى‌´ë¦„
                    description: tool.description.clone(),
                });
            }
        }
    }
}

ي•´ê²° ىˆœى„œ: â‘  session cache â†’ â‘، env::var() â†’ â‘¢ UIë،œ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گى—گê²Œ ى§ˆë¬¸ (request_user_input). ê°’ى‌€ ى„¸ى…ک ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ى—گë§Œ ى €ى‍¥ëگکê³  ë””ىٹ¤يپ¬ى—گëٹ” ى“°ى‌´ى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤.

11. Remote Skills â€” ى„œë²„ ê¸°ë°ک ىٹ¤ي‚¬

remote.rsëٹ” ChatGPT APIë¥¼ ي†µي•´ ى›گê²© ىٹ¤ي‚¬ى‌„ ى،°يڑŒي•کê³  ë‹¤ىڑ´ë،œë“œي•کëٹ” يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ë¥¼ êµ¬يک„ي•œë‹¤. يک„ى‍¬ëٹ” ى•„ى§پ active product surfaceى—گ ى—°ê²°ëگکى§€ ى•ٹى•کë‹¤ (ى£¼ى„‌: "intentionally kept around for future wiring").

graph TD

Axis 05: Subagents

AXIS

005_subagents-collab/05_co_subagent.md

Overview

Axis 05: Subagents â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” subagent/collaboration dispatch.

  • Codexى‌ک ى„œë¸Œى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ëٹ” ىƒ‌ê°پë³´ë‹¤ ى œي’ˆ ê¸°ëٹ¥ى²کëں¼ ë…¸ى¶œëگکى–´ ى‍ˆى§€ë§Œ, ë‚´ë¶€ë¥¼ ë³´ë©´ ى‚¬ى‹¤ىƒپ "يک„ى‍¬ turnى‌„ ê¸°ë°کىœ¼ë،œ ىƒˆ threadë¥¼ spawnي•کëٹ” يک‘ى—… يˆ´ ى„¸يٹ¸"ë‹¤. ى¦‰ ëڈ…ë¦½ëگœ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ي”„ë ˆى‍„ى›Œيپ¬ê°€ ë”°ë،œ ى‍ˆëٹ” ê²ƒى‌´ ى•„ë‹ˆë‌¼, ê¸°ى،´ Sessionê³¼ TurnContextë¥¼ ي™•ى‍¥ي•´ى„œ ي•کىœ„ conversationى‌„ ë§Œë“œëٹ” ë°©ى‹‌ى‌´ë‹¤.

// core/src/agent/control.rs
#[derive(Clone, Default)]
pub(crate) struct AgentControl {
    /// Weak handle back to the global thread registry/state.
    /// This is `Weak` to avoid reference cycles and shadow persistence of the form
    /// `ThreadManagerState -> CodexThread -> Session -> SessionServices -> ThreadManagerState`.
    manager: Weak<ThreadManagerState>,
    state: Arc<AgentRegistry>,
}

    C --> D{"SessionSource ىœ يک•?"}
    D -->|ThreadSpawn| E["prepare_thread_spawn<br/>ë‹‰ë„¤ى‍„آ·ê²½ë،œ ىکˆى•½"]
    D -->|ê¸°يƒ€| F["ê¸°ë³¸ ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„°"]

    F --> G
    G --> H{"fork ىکµى…ک?"}

    I --> K["reservation.commit<br/>ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„° ي™•ى •"]
