// core/src/agent/control.rs â€” spawn_agent_internal (ى¶•ى•½)
async fn spawn_agent_internal(
    &self,
    config: Config,
    items: Vec<UserInput>,
    session_source: Option<SessionSource>,
    options: SpawnAgentOptions,
) -> CodexResult<LiveAgent> {
    let state = self.upgrade()?;                                   // Weak â†’ Arc
    let mut reservation = self.state.reserve_spawn_slot(           // â‘  ىٹ¬ë،¯ ي™•ë³´
        config.agent_max_threads
    )?;
    let inherited_shell_snapshot = self                             // â‘، ë¶€ëھ¨ shell ىƒپى†چ
        .inherited_shell_snapshot_for_source(&state, session_source.as_ref())
        .await;
    let inherited_exec_policy = self                               // â‘¢ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى •ى±… ىƒپى†چ
        .inherited_exec_policy_for_source(&state, session_source.as_ref(), &config)
        .await;

    // â‘£ ThreadSpawnى‌´ë©´ ë‹‰ë„¤ى‍„آ·ê²½ë،œ ىکˆى•½
    let (session_source, mut agent_metadata) = match session_source {
        Some(SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn {
            parent_thread_id, depth, agent_path, agent_role, ..
        })) => {
            self.prepare_thread_spawn(
                &mut reservation, &config,
                parent_thread_id, depth, agent_path, agent_role,
                None, // preferred_agent_nickname
            )?
        }
        other => (other, AgentMetadata::default()),
    };

    // â‘¤ ى‹¤ى œ thread ىƒ‌ى„± (fork vs ى‌¼ë°ک)
    let new_thread = match session_source {
        Some(session_source) if options.fork_parent_spawn_call_id.is_some() => {
            // ë¶€ëھ¨ rolloutى‌„ JSONL ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·ىœ¼ë،œ يڈ¬يپ¬
            state.fork_thread_with_source(config, initial_history, self.clone(),
                session_source, false, inherited_shell_snapshot,
                inherited_exec_policy).await?
        }
        Some(session_source) => {
            state.spawn_new_thread_with_source(config, self.clone(),
                session_source, false, None,
                inherited_shell_snapshot, inherited_exec_policy).await?
        }
        None => state.spawn_new_thread(config, self.clone()).await?,
    };

    agent_metadata.agent_id = Some(new_thread.thread_id);
    reservation.commit(agent_metadata.clone());                    // â‘¥ ىکˆى•½ ي™•ى •
    state.notify_thread_created(new_thread.thread_id);             // â‘¦ يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ ى•Œë¦¼
    self.send_input(new_thread.thread_id, items).await?;           // â‘§ ى´ˆê¸° ى‍…ë ¥
    self.maybe_start_completion_watcher(                           // â‘¨ ë¹„ëڈ™ê¸° ى™„ë£Œ ê°گى‹œ
        new_thread.thread_id, notification_source,
        child_reference, agent_metadata.agent_path.clone(),
    );

    Ok(LiveAgent { thread_id: new_thread.thread_id, metadata: agent_metadata,
        status: self.get_status(new_thread.thread_id).await })
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/control.rs
pub(crate) async fn send_input(
    &self,
    agent_id: ThreadId,
    items: Vec<UserInput>,
) -> CodexResult<String> {
    let last_task_message = render_input_preview(&items);
    let state = self.upgrade()?;
    let result = self.handle_thread_request_result(
        agent_id, &state,
        state.send_op(agent_id, Op::UserInput {
            items,
            final_output_json_schema: None,
        }).await,
    ).await;
    if result.is_ok() {
        self.state.update_last_task_message(agent_id, last_task_message);
    }
    result
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/control.rs
pub(crate) async fn close_agent(&self, agent_id: ThreadId) -> CodexResult<String> {
    // â‘  state_dbى—گ Closed ىƒپيƒœ ê¸°ë،‌ (ى‍¬ى‹œى‍‘ ى‹œ resume ى•ˆ ي•¨)
    let state = self.upgrade()?;
    if let Ok(thread) = state.get_thread(agent_id).await
        && let Some(state_db_ctx) = thread.state_db()
    {
        let _ = state_db_ctx.set_thread_spawn_edge_status(
            agent_id, DirectionalThreadSpawnEdgeStatus::Closed
        ).await;
    }
    // â‘، ى‍گى‹  + ëھ¨ë“  ي•کىœ„ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ shutdown
    self.shutdown_agent_tree(agent_id).await
}

async fn shutdown_agent_tree(&self, agent_id: ThreadId) -> CodexResult<String> {
    let descendant_ids = self.live_thread_spawn_descendants(agent_id).await?;
    let result = self.shutdown_live_agent(agent_id).await;
    for descendant_id in descendant_ids {
        let _ = self.shutdown_live_agent(descendant_id).await;
    }
    result
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/control.rs (ى¶•ى•½)
fn maybe_start_completion_watcher(&self, child_thread_id: ThreadId, ...) {
    let Some(SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn {
        parent_thread_id, ..
    })) = session_source else { return; };

    let control = self.clone();
    tokio::spawn(async move {
        // watch::Receiverë،œ ىƒپيƒœ ë³€ي™”ë¥¼ êµ¬ëڈ…
        let status = match control.subscribe_status(child_thread_id).await {
            Ok(mut status_rx) => {
                let mut status = status_rx.borrow().clone();
                while !is_final(&status) {            // Running/PendingInitى‌´ë©´ ê³„ى†چ ëŒ€ê¸°
                    if status_rx.changed().await.is_err() { break; }
                    status = status_rx.borrow().clone();
                }
                status
            }
            Err(_) => control.get_status(child_thread_id).await,
        };

        if !is_final(&status) { return; }
        // ë¶€ëھ¨ threadى—گ ى™„ë£Œ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ى£¼ى‍…
        let message = format_subagent_notification_message(&child_reference, &status);
        // V2ى—گى„œëٹ” InterAgentCommunication, V1ى—گى„œëٹ” inject_user_message_without_turn
        // ...
    });
}

README / docs

sequenceDiagram
    participant Parent as ë¶€ëھ¨ thread
    participant Control as AgentControl
    participant Registry as AgentRegistry
    participant Child as child thread
    participant Watcher as completion watcher
    participant Delegate as codex delegate

    Parent->>Control: spawn_agent(config, items)
    Control->>Registry: reserve_spawn_slot(max_threads)
    Registry-->>Control: SpawnReservation
    Control->>Registry: reserve_agent_nickname / reserve_agent_path
    Control->>Child: spawn_new_thread_with_source
    Control->>Registry: reservation.commit(metadata)
    Control->>Child: send_input(ى´ˆê¸° ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸)
    Control->>Watcher: tokio::spawn(completion_watcher)

    Parent->>Control: send_input(ى¶”ê°€ ى§€ى‹œ)
    Control->>Child: Op::UserInput ى „ë‹¬

    Child->>Delegate: approval ىڑ”ى²­
    Delegate->>Parent: ë¶€ëھ¨ ى •ى±…ىœ¼ë،œ ى¤‘ê³„
    Parent-->>Delegate: ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ى‌‘ë‹µ
    Delegate-->>Child: ى‌‘ë‹µ ى „ë‹¬

    Child-->>Watcher: status â†’ Completed
    Watcher->>Parent: inject notification message

    Parent->>Control: close_agent
    Control->>Child: shutdown_live_agent
    Control->>Registry: release_spawned_thread

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
#[derive(Default)]
pub(crate) struct AgentRegistry {
    active_agents: Mutex<ActiveAgents>,
    total_count: AtomicUsize,
}

#[derive(Default)]
struct ActiveAgents {
    agent_tree: HashMap<String, AgentMetadata>,
    used_agent_nicknames: HashSet<String>,
    nickname_reset_count: usize,
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]
pub(crate) struct AgentMetadata {
    pub(crate) agent_id: Option<ThreadId>,
    pub(crate) agent_path: Option<AgentPath>,
    pub(crate) agent_nickname: Option<String>,
    pub(crate) agent_role: Option<String>,
    pub(crate) last_task_message: Option<String>,
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
pub(crate) fn reserve_spawn_slot(
    self: &Arc<Self>,
    max_threads: Option<usize>,
) -> Result<SpawnReservation> {
    if let Some(max_threads) = max_threads {
        if !self.try_increment_spawned(max_threads) {
            return Err(CodexErr::AgentLimitReached { max_threads });
        }
    } else {
        self.total_count.fetch_add(1, Ordering::AcqRel);
    }
    Ok(SpawnReservation { state: Arc::clone(self), active: true, .. })
}

fn try_increment_spawned(&self, max_threads: usize) -> bool {
    let mut current = self.total_count.load(Ordering::Acquire);
    loop {
        if current >= max_threads { return false; }
        match self.total_count.compare_exchange_weak(
            current, current + 1, Ordering::AcqRel, Ordering::Acquire,
        ) {
            Ok(_) => return true,
            Err(updated) => current = updated,
        }
    }
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
pub(crate) struct SpawnReservation {
    state: Arc<AgentRegistry>,
    active: bool,
    reserved_agent_nickname: Option<String>,
    reserved_agent_path: Option<AgentPath>,
}

impl SpawnReservation {
    pub(crate) fn commit(mut self, agent_metadata: AgentMetadata) {
        self.reserved_agent_nickname = None;
        self.reserved_agent_path = None;
        self.state.register_spawned_thread(agent_metadata);
        self.active = false;   // Drop ى‹œ ى¹´ىڑ´ي„° ê°گى†Œ ë°©ى§€
    }
}

impl Drop for SpawnReservation {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if self.active {       // commit ى•ˆ ëگگىœ¼ë©´ â†’ ىٹ¬ë،¯ ë°کي™ک
            if let Some(agent_path) = self.reserved_agent_path.take() {
                self.state.release_reserved_agent_path(&agent_path);
            }
            self.total_count.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::AcqRel);
        }
    }
}

README / docs

stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Reserved : reserve_spawn_slot
    Reserved --> NicknameReserved : reserve_agent_nickname
    NicknameReserved --> PathReserved : reserve_agent_path
    PathReserved --> Committed : commit(metadata)
    Committed --> [*]

    Reserved --> RolledBack : Drop (spawn ى‹¤يŒ¨)
    NicknameReserved --> RolledBack : Drop
    PathReserved --> RolledBack : Drop
    RolledBack --> [*] : total_count--, path ي•´ى œ

README / docs

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
fn format_agent_nickname(name: &str, nickname_reset_count: usize) -> String {
    match nickname_reset_count {
        0 => name.to_string(),
        reset_count => {
            let value = reset_count + 1;
            let suffix = match value % 100 {
                11..=13 => "th",
                _ => match value % 10 {
                    1 => "st", 2 => "nd", 3 => "rd", _ => "th",
                },
            };
            format!("{name} the {value}{suffix}")
        }
    }
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/agent_resolver.rs
pub(crate) async fn resolve_agent_target(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    turn: &Arc<TurnContext>,
    target: &str,
) -> Result<ThreadId, FunctionCallError> {
    register_session_root(session, turn);
    // â‘  ى§پى ‘ ThreadId ë¬¸ى‍گى—´ى‌´ë©´ ى¦‰ى‹œ ë°کي™ک
    if let Ok(thread_id) = ThreadId::from_string(target) {
        return Ok(thread_id);
    }
    // â‘، AgentPath ê¸°ë°ک ي•´ى„‌ (ىکˆ: "workers/auth-fix")
    session.services.agent_control
        .resolve_agent_reference(session.conversation_id, &turn.session_source, target)
        .await
        .map_err(|err| FunctionCallError::RespondToModel(err.to_string()))
}

README / docs

pub(crate) async fn resolve_agent_targets(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    turn: &Arc<TurnContext>,
    targets: Vec<String>,
) -> Result<Vec<ThreadId>, FunctionCallError> {
    if targets.is_empty() {
        return Err(FunctionCallError::RespondToModel(
            "agent targets must be non-empty".to_string(),
        ));
    }
    let mut resolved = Vec::with_capacity(targets.len());
    for target in &targets {
        resolved.push(resolve_agent_target(session, turn, target).await?);
    }
    Ok(resolved)
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/role.rs â€” built_in::configs() (ى¶•ى•½)
BTreeMap::from([
    ("default".to_string(), AgentRoleConfig {
        description: Some("Default agent.".to_string()),
        config_file: None,
        nickname_candidates: None,
    }),
    ("explorer".to_string(), AgentRoleConfig {
        description: Some(r#"Use `explorer` for specific codebase questions.
Explorers are fast and authoritative. ..."#.to_string()),
        config_file: Some("explorer.toml".into()),
        nickname_candidates: None,
    }),
    ("worker".to_string(), AgentRoleConfig {
        description: Some(r#"Use for execution and production work. ..."#.to_string()),
        config_file: None,
        nickname_candidates: None,
    }),
])

README / docs

// core/src/agent/role.rs â€” built_in::config_file_contents
pub(super) fn config_file_contents(path: &Path) -> Option<&'static str> {
    const EXPLORER: &str = include_str!("builtins/explorer.toml");
    const AWAITER: &str = include_str!("builtins/awaiter.toml");
    match path.to_str()? {
        "explorer.toml" => Some(EXPLORER),
        "awaiter.toml" => Some(AWAITER),
        _ => None,
    }
}

README / docs

# core/src/agent/builtins/awaiter.toml
background_terminal_max_timeout = 3600000
model_reasoning_effort = "low"
developer_instructions="""You are an awaiter.
Your role is to await the completion of a specific command or task
and report its status only when it is finished.
...
"""

// core/src/agent/role.rs
pub(crate) async fn apply_role_to_config(
    config: &mut Config,
    role_name: Option<&str>,
) -> Result<(), String> {
    let role_name = role_name.unwrap_or(DEFAULT_ROLE_NAME);
    let role = resolve_role_config(config, role_name)
        .cloned()
        .ok_or_else(|| format!("unknown agent_type '{role_name}'"))?;
    apply_role_to_config_inner(config, role_name, &role).await
        .map_err(|err| AGENT_TYPE_UNAVAILABLE_ERROR.to_string())
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/role.rs
fn preservation_policy(config: &Config, role_layer_toml: &TomlValue) -> (bool, bool) {
    let role_selects_provider = role_layer_toml.get("model_provider").is_some();
    let role_selects_profile = role_layer_toml.get("profile").is_some();
    // roleى‌´ providerë‚ک profileى‌„ ëھ…ى‹œي•کى§€ ى•ٹىœ¼ë©´ â†’ ë¶€ëھ¨ ê²ƒى‌„ ë³´ى،´
    let preserve_current_profile = !role_selects_provider && !role_selects_profile;
    let preserve_current_provider =
        preserve_current_profile && !role_updates_active_profile_provider;
    (preserve_current_profile, preserve_current_provider)
}

pub(crate) fn build(user_defined_agent_roles: &BTreeMap<String, AgentRoleConfig>) -> String {
    let built_in_roles = built_in::configs();
    // ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى •ى‌ک ى—­ي• ى‌´ ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌ک ë‚´ى‍¥ ى—­ي• ى‌„ ëچ®ى–´ى”€
    let mut seen = BTreeSet::new();
    let mut formatted_roles = Vec::new();

    for (name, declaration) in built_in_roles { /* ... */ }

README / docs

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
fn session_depth(session_source: &SessionSource) -> i32 {
    match session_source {
        SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn { depth, .. }) => *depth,
        _ => 0,
    }
}

pub(crate) fn next_thread_spawn_depth(session_source: &SessionSource) -> i32 {
    session_depth(session_source).saturating_add(1)
}

pub(crate) fn exceeds_thread_spawn_depth_limit(depth: i32, max_depth: i32) -> bool {
    depth > max_depth
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/control.rs â€” resume_single_agent_from_rollout ë‚´ë¶€
if let SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn { depth, .. }) = &session_source
    && *depth >= config.agent_max_depth
{
    let _ = config.features.disable(Feature::SpawnCsv);
    let _ = config.features.disable(Feature::Collab);
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/status.rs
pub(crate) fn agent_status_from_event(msg: &EventMsg) -> Option<AgentStatus> {
    match msg {
        EventMsg::TurnStarted(_)  => Some(AgentStatus::Running),
        EventMsg::TurnComplete(ev) => Some(AgentStatus::Completed(ev.last_agent_message.clone())),
        EventMsg::TurnAborted(ev) => match ev.reason {
            TurnAbortReason::Interrupted => Some(AgentStatus::Interrupted),
            _ => Some(AgentStatus::Errored(format!("{:?}", ev.reason))),
        },
        EventMsg::Error(ev) => Some(AgentStatus::Errored(ev.message.clone())),
        EventMsg::ShutdownComplete => Some(AgentStatus::Shutdown),
        _ => None,
    }
}

pub(crate) fn is_final(status: &AgentStatus) -> bool {
    !matches!(status, AgentStatus::PendingInit | AgentStatus::Running | AgentStatus::Interrupted)
}

README / docs

stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> PendingInit : thread ىƒ‌ى„±
    PendingInit --> Running : TurnStarted
    Running --> Completed : TurnComplete
    Running --> Interrupted : TurnAborted(Interrupted)
    Running --> Errored : TurnAborted / Error
    Interrupted --> Running : resume_agent
    Running --> Shutdown : ShutdownComplete

    note right of Completed : is_final = true
    note right of Errored : is_final = true
    note right of Shutdown : is_final = true

README / docs

// core/src/agent/control.rs
async fn inherited_shell_snapshot_for_source(
    &self, state: &Arc<ThreadManagerState>,
    session_source: Option<&SessionSource>,
) -> Option<Arc<ShellSnapshot>> {
    let Some(SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn {
        parent_thread_id, ..
    })) = session_source else { return None; };
    let parent_thread = state.get_thread(*parent_thread_id).await.ok()?;
    parent_thread.codex.session.user_shell().shell_snapshot()
}

async fn inherited_exec_policy_for_source(
    &self, state: &Arc<ThreadManagerState>,
    session_source: Option<&SessionSource>,
    child_config: &Config,
) -> Option<Arc<ExecPolicyManager>> {
    // ...
    let parent_config = parent_thread.codex.session.get_config().await;
    if !child_uses_parent_exec_policy(&parent_config, child_config) {
        return None;   // ى •ى±…ى‌´ ë‹¤ë¥´ë©´ ىƒپى†چ ى•ˆ ي•¨
    }
    Some(Arc::clone(&parent_thread.codex.session.services.exec_policy))
}

README / docs

// core/src/agent/control.rs â€” resume_agent_from_rollout (ى¶•ى•½)
pub(crate) async fn resume_agent_from_rollout(&self, config: Config,
    thread_id: ThreadId, session_source: SessionSource,
) -> CodexResult<ThreadId> {
    let resumed_thread_id = self.resume_single_agent_from_rollout(
        config.clone(), thread_id, session_source
    ).await?;

    let mut resume_queue = VecDeque::from([(thread_id, root_depth)]);
    while let Some((parent_thread_id, parent_depth)) = resume_queue.pop_front() {
        let child_ids = state_db_ctx.list_thread_spawn_children_with_status(
            parent_thread_id, DirectionalThreadSpawnEdgeStatus::Open
        ).await?;
        for child_thread_id in child_ids {
            let child_depth = parent_depth + 1;
            // ى‌´ë¯¸ liveى‌´ë©´ ê±´ë„ˆë›°ê³ , ى•„ë‹ˆë©´ rolloutى—گى„œ ë³µى›گ
            if state.get_thread(child_thread_id).await.is_ok() {
                // already live
            } else {
                self.resume_single_agent_from_rollout(
                    config.clone(), child_thread_id, child_session_source
                ).await?;
            }
            resume_queue.push_back((child_thread_id, child_depth));
        }
    }
    Ok(resumed_thread_id)
}

graph TD
    R["resume_agent_from_rollout(root)"] --> A["resume_single(root)"]
    A --> Q["BFS queue: root"]
    Q --> B["state_db: list children of root<br/>(Openë§Œ)"]
    B --> C1["resume_single(child-1)"]
    B --> C2["resume_single(child-2)"]
    C1 --> Q2["queueى—گ child-1 ى¶”ê°€"]
    C2 --> Q3["queueى—گ child-2 ى¶”ê°€"]
    Q2 --> D["child-1ى‌ک children ë³µى›گ..."]
    Q3 --> E["child-2ى‌ک children ë³µى›گ..."]

README / docs

// core/src/agent/control.rs
pub(crate) async fn send_inter_agent_communication(
    &self, agent_id: ThreadId,
    communication: InterAgentCommunication,
) -> CodexResult<String> {
    let last_task_message = communication.content.clone();
    let state = self.upgrade()?;
    let result = self.handle_thread_request_result(
        agent_id, &state,
        state.send_op(agent_id, Op::InterAgentCommunication { communication }).await,
    ).await;
    if result.is_ok() {
        self.state.update_last_task_message(agent_id, last_task_message);
    }
    result
}

README / docs

// completion watcher ë‚´ë¶€ (V2 ê²½ë،œ)
if child_agent_path.is_some() && thread.enabled(Feature::MultiAgentV2) {
    let communication = InterAgentCommunication::new(
        child_agent_path, parent_agent_path,
        Vec::new(), message, /*trigger_turn*/ false,
    );
    let _ = control.send_inter_agent_communication(parent_thread_id, communication).await;
    return;
}
// V1 fallback: ë¶€ëھ¨ threadى—گ user message ى§پى ‘ ى£¼ى‍…
parent_thread.inject_user_message_without_turn(message).await;

Main analysis

Subagent and collaboration tool analysis

Analysis snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” subagent/collaboration dispatch.

Codex's subagent is exposed more like a product function than you might think, but if you look inside, it is actually "a set of collaboration tools that spawn a new thread based on the current turn." In other words, there is no independent agent framework, but a method of creating a sub-conversation by extending the existing Session and TurnContext.

The reason this is important is because to determine whether to maintain a subagent in our fork, we need to look at the inheritance structure, not the UI. When spawn_agent creates a new child, it copies the parent's effective config and overwrites the current model, provider, reasoning effort, approval policy, sandbox, cwd, and developer instructions. Add a role config on top of it, and record the parent and depth with SessionSource::SubAgent::ThreadSpawn metadata.

In other words, a subagent is a â€œseparate runtime with similar settings to the parentâ€‌ and is not a completely independent worker. This makes it easy to inherit consistent policies, but on the other hand, when producing a product, you have to decide which settings to inherit and which to break. In particular, it is very important that it goes all the way down to developer_instructions.

Codex exposes this collaboration aspect as a tool. The core has spawn_agent, send_input, wait_agent, resume_agent, and close_agent handlers, and the app-server protocol records them separately as CollabAgentToolCall. In other words, the subagent is not a hidden function inside the model, but has an explicit tool surface.

Additionally, codex_delegate.rs relays approval, request_user_input, and MCP tool events between parents and children. So the child can receive approval and responses through the parent session without directly asking the user. From a product design perspective, this acts as a "subagent orchestration bus".

Lastly, mode and role are also important. The collaboration mode preset provides developer instructions in templates such as plan.md and default.md, and files such as awaiter.toml, a built-in role, are minimal examples of creating sub-agents for specific purposes. In other words, Codex treats subagent as a bundle of prompt plus config plus state inheritance rather than a simple API.

1. AgentControl â€” Subagent control plane

AgentControl is the hub of the multi-agent system. One instance is created per root thread tree, and this instance is shared by all subagents derived from the same root. Inside there is a Weak reference to ThreadManagerState and an Arc in AgentRegistry.

// core/src/agent/control.rs
#[derive(Clone, Default)]
pub(crate) struct AgentControl {
    /// Weak handle back to the global thread registry/state.
    /// This is `Weak` to avoid reference cycles and shadow persistence of the form
    /// `ThreadManagerState -> CodexThread -> Session -> SessionServices -> ThreadManagerState`.
    manager: Weak<ThreadManagerState>,
    state: Arc<AgentRegistry>,
}

Design points:

  • Prevent circular references with Weak references. The path ThreadManagerState â†’ CodexThread â†’ Session â†’ SessionServices â†’ ThreadManagerState is broken through Weak.
  • Since one AgentControl owns the entire root tree, all children's spawn slots, nicknames, and paths are managed in one AgentRegistry.

2. spawn â†’ delegate â†’ wait overall flow

graph TD
    A["ë¶€ëھ¨ turn: spawn_agent يک¸ى¶œ"] --> B["AgentControl::spawn_agent_internal"]
    B --> C["AgentRegistry::reserve_spawn_slot<br/>(max_threads CAS ى²´يپ¬)"]
    C --> D{"SessionSource ىœ يک•?"}
    D -->|ThreadSpawn| E["prepare_thread_spawn<br/>ë‹‰ë„¤ى‍„آ·ê²½ë،œ ىکˆى•½"]
    D -->|ê¸°يƒ€| F["ê¸°ë³¸ ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„°"]
    E --> G["shell snapshot ىƒپى†چ<br/>exec policy ىƒپى†چ"]
    F --> G
    G --> H{"fork ىکµى…ک?"}
    H -->|Yes| I["ë¶€ëھ¨ rollout JSONL ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·<br/>fork_thread_with_source"]
    H -->|No| J["spawn_new_thread_with_source"]
    I --> K["reservation.commit<br/>ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„° ي™•ى •"]
    J --> K
    K --> L["notify_thread_created<br/>(يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ êµ¬ëڈ… يٹ¸ë¦¬ê±°)"]
    L --> M["send_input: ى´ˆê¸° ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸ ى „ى†،"]
    M --> N["maybe_start_completion_watcher<br/>(tokio::spawn ë¹„ëڈ™ê¸° ê°گى‹œ)"]

    style C fill:#ffd,stroke:#f90
    style E fill:#dfd,stroke:#090
    style N fill:#ddf,stroke:#009

2.1 spawn step detailed code

The core of spawn is spawn_agent_internal. Below is an excerpt of the main flow:

// core/src/agent/control.rs â€” spawn_agent_internal (ى¶•ى•½)
async fn spawn_agent_internal(
    &self,
    config: Config,
    items: Vec<UserInput>,
    session_source: Option<SessionSource>,
    options: SpawnAgentOptions,
) -> CodexResult<LiveAgent> {
    let state = self.upgrade()?;                                   // Weak â†’ Arc
    let mut reservation = self.state.reserve_spawn_slot(           // â‘  ىٹ¬ë،¯ ي™•ë³´
        config.agent_max_threads
    )?;
    let inherited_shell_snapshot = self                             // â‘، ë¶€ëھ¨ shell ىƒپى†چ
        .inherited_shell_snapshot_for_source(&state, session_source.as_ref())
        .await;
    let inherited_exec_policy = self                               // â‘¢ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى •ى±… ىƒپى†چ
        .inherited_exec_policy_for_source(&state, session_source.as_ref(), &config)
        .await;

    // â‘£ ThreadSpawnى‌´ë©´ ë‹‰ë„¤ى‍„آ·ê²½ë،œ ىکˆى•½
    let (session_source, mut agent_metadata) = match session_source {
        Some(SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn {
            parent_thread_id, depth, agent_path, agent_role, ..
        })) => {
            self.prepare_thread_spawn(
                &mut reservation, &config,
                parent_thread_id, depth, agent_path, agent_role,
                None, // preferred_agent_nickname
            )?
        }
        other => (other, AgentMetadata::default()),
    };

    // â‘¤ ى‹¤ى œ thread ىƒ‌ى„± (fork vs ى‌¼ë°ک)
    let new_thread = match session_source {
        Some(session_source) if options.fork_parent_spawn_call_id.is_some() => {
            // ë¶€ëھ¨ rolloutى‌„ JSONL ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·ىœ¼ë،œ يڈ¬يپ¬
            state.fork_thread_with_source(config, initial_history, self.clone(),
                session_source, false, inherited_shell_snapshot,
                inherited_exec_policy).await?
        }
        Some(session_source) => {
            state.spawn_new_thread_with_source(config, self.clone(),
                session_source, false, None,
                inherited_shell_snapshot, inherited_exec_policy).await?
        }
        None => state.spawn_new_thread(config, self.clone()).await?,
    };

    agent_metadata.agent_id = Some(new_thread.thread_id);
    reservation.commit(agent_metadata.clone());                    // â‘¥ ىکˆى•½ ي™•ى •
    state.notify_thread_created(new_thread.thread_id);             // â‘¦ يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ ى•Œë¦¼
    self.send_input(new_thread.thread_id, items).await?;           // â‘§ ى´ˆê¸° ى‍…ë ¥
    self.maybe_start_completion_watcher(                           // â‘¨ ë¹„ëڈ™ê¸° ى™„ë£Œ ê°گى‹œ
        new_thread.thread_id, notification_source,
        child_reference, agent_metadata.agent_path.clone(),
    );

    Ok(LiveAgent { thread_id: new_thread.thread_id, metadata: agent_metadata,
        status: self.get_status(new_thread.thread_id).await })
}

2.2 send_input â€” Send message

// core/src/agent/control.rs
pub(crate) async fn send_input(
    &self,
    agent_id: ThreadId,
    items: Vec<UserInput>,
) -> CodexResult<String> {
    let last_task_message = render_input_preview(&items);
    let state = self.upgrade()?;
    let result = self.handle_thread_request_result(
        agent_id, &state,
        state.send_op(agent_id, Op::UserInput {
            items,
            final_output_json_schema: None,
        }).await,
    ).await;
    if result.is_ok() {
        self.state.update_last_task_message(agent_id, last_task_message);
    }
    result
}

send_op issues Op::UserInput to the target thread through ThreadManagerState. If successful, the registry's last_task_message is updated and the current task content is displayed when list_agents is searched.

2.3 close_agent â€” Close and subtree cleanup

close_agent does not simply kill a single thread, but recursively traverses the entire spawn tree and cleans up all live descendants:

// core/src/agent/control.rs
pub(crate) async fn close_agent(&self, agent_id: ThreadId) -> CodexResult<String> {
    // â‘  state_dbى—گ Closed ىƒپيƒœ ê¸°ë،‌ (ى‍¬ى‹œى‍‘ ى‹œ resume ى•ˆ ي•¨)
    let state = self.upgrade()?;
    if let Ok(thread) = state.get_thread(agent_id).await
        && let Some(state_db_ctx) = thread.state_db()
    {
        let _ = state_db_ctx.set_thread_spawn_edge_status(
            agent_id, DirectionalThreadSpawnEdgeStatus::Closed
        ).await;
    }
    // â‘، ى‍گى‹  + ëھ¨ë“  ي•کىœ„ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ shutdown
    self.shutdown_agent_tree(agent_id).await
}

async fn shutdown_agent_tree(&self, agent_id: ThreadId) -> CodexResult<String> {
    let descendant_ids = self.live_thread_spawn_descendants(agent_id).await?;
    let result = self.shutdown_live_agent(agent_id).await;
    for descendant_id in descendant_ids {
        let _ = self.shutdown_live_agent(descendant_id).await;
    }
    result
}

2.4 Completion Watcher â€” completion watcher

Even if the parent does not explicitly call wait_agent, the parent is notified when the child has finished. maybe_start_completion_watcher handles this by launching a background task with tokio::spawn:

// core/src/agent/control.rs (ى¶•ى•½)
fn maybe_start_completion_watcher(&self, child_thread_id: ThreadId, ...) {
    let Some(SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn {
        parent_thread_id, ..
    })) = session_source else { return; };

    let control = self.clone();
    tokio::spawn(async move {
        // watch::Receiverë،œ ىƒپيƒœ ë³€ي™”ë¥¼ êµ¬ëڈ…
        let status = match control.subscribe_status(child_thread_id).await {
            Ok(mut status_rx) => {
                let mut status = status_rx.borrow().clone();
                while !is_final(&status) {            // Running/PendingInitى‌´ë©´ ê³„ى†چ ëŒ€ê¸°
                    if status_rx.changed().await.is_err() { break; }
                    status = status_rx.borrow().clone();
                }
                status
            }
            Err(_) => control.get_status(child_thread_id).await,
        };

        if !is_final(&status) { return; }
        // ë¶€ëھ¨ threadى—گ ى™„ë£Œ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ى£¼ى‍…
        let message = format_subagent_notification_message(&child_reference, &status);
        // V2ى—گى„œëٹ” InterAgentCommunication, V1ى—گى„œëٹ” inject_user_message_without_turn
        // ...
    });
}

sequenceDiagram
    participant Parent as ë¶€ëھ¨ thread
    participant Control as AgentControl
    participant Registry as AgentRegistry
    participant Child as child thread
    participant Watcher as completion watcher
    participant Delegate as codex delegate

    Parent->>Control: spawn_agent(config, items)
    Control->>Registry: reserve_spawn_slot(max_threads)
    Registry-->>Control: SpawnReservation
    Control->>Registry: reserve_agent_nickname / reserve_agent_path
    Control->>Child: spawn_new_thread_with_source
    Control->>Registry: reservation.commit(metadata)
    Control->>Child: send_input(ى´ˆê¸° ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸)
    Control->>Watcher: tokio::spawn(completion_watcher)

    Parent->>Control: send_input(ى¶”ê°€ ى§€ى‹œ)
    Control->>Child: Op::UserInput ى „ë‹¬

    Child->>Delegate: approval ىڑ”ى²­
    Delegate->>Parent: ë¶€ëھ¨ ى •ى±…ىœ¼ë،œ ى¤‘ê³„
    Parent-->>Delegate: ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ى‌‘ë‹µ
    Delegate-->>Child: ى‌‘ë‹µ ى „ë‹¬

    Child-->>Watcher: status â†’ Completed
    Watcher->>Parent: inject notification message

    Parent->>Control: close_agent
    Control->>Child: shutdown_live_agent
    Control->>Registry: release_spawned_thread

3. AgentRegistry â€” Slot, nickname, tree management

AgentRegistry centrally manages all spawn resources within one root session tree.

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
#[derive(Default)]
pub(crate) struct AgentRegistry {
    active_agents: Mutex<ActiveAgents>,
    total_count: AtomicUsize,
}

#[derive(Default)]
struct ActiveAgents {
    agent_tree: HashMap<String, AgentMetadata>,
    used_agent_nicknames: HashSet<String>,
    nickname_reset_count: usize,
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]
pub(crate) struct AgentMetadata {
    pub(crate) agent_id: Option<ThreadId>,
    pub(crate) agent_path: Option<AgentPath>,
    pub(crate) agent_nickname: Option<String>,
    pub(crate) agent_role: Option<String>,
    pub(crate) last_task_message: Option<String>,
}

3.1 Slot Reservation â€” CAS-based concurrency control

reserve_spawn_slot, which is called first when spawning, ensures a limit on the number of threads with an atomic compare-and-swap:

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
pub(crate) fn reserve_spawn_slot(
    self: &Arc<Self>,
    max_threads: Option<usize>,
) -> Result<SpawnReservation> {
    if let Some(max_threads) = max_threads {
        if !self.try_increment_spawned(max_threads) {
            return Err(CodexErr::AgentLimitReached { max_threads });
        }
    } else {
        self.total_count.fetch_add(1, Ordering::AcqRel);
    }
    Ok(SpawnReservation { state: Arc::clone(self), active: true, .. })
}

fn try_increment_spawned(&self, max_threads: usize) -> bool {
    let mut current = self.total_count.load(Ordering::Acquire);
    loop {
        if current >= max_threads { return false; }
        match self.total_count.compare_exchange_weak(
            current, current + 1, Ordering::AcqRel, Ordering::Acquire,
        ) {
            Ok(_) => return true,
            Err(updated) => current = updated,
        }
    }
}

3.2 SpawnReservation â€” RAII reservation/rollback

SpawnReservation is a RAII pattern that is automatically cleaned up by Drop if spawn fails:

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
pub(crate) struct SpawnReservation {
    state: Arc<AgentRegistry>,
    active: bool,
    reserved_agent_nickname: Option<String>,
    reserved_agent_path: Option<AgentPath>,
}

impl SpawnReservation {
    pub(crate) fn commit(mut self, agent_metadata: AgentMetadata) {
        self.reserved_agent_nickname = None;
        self.reserved_agent_path = None;
        self.state.register_spawned_thread(agent_metadata);
        self.active = false;   // Drop ى‹œ ى¹´ىڑ´ي„° ê°گى†Œ ë°©ى§€
    }
}

impl Drop for SpawnReservation {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if self.active {       // commit ى•ˆ ëگگىœ¼ë©´ â†’ ىٹ¬ë،¯ ë°کي™ک
            if let Some(agent_path) = self.reserved_agent_path.take() {
                self.state.release_reserved_agent_path(&agent_path);
            }
            self.total_count.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::AcqRel);
        }
    }
}

stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Reserved : reserve_spawn_slot
    Reserved --> NicknameReserved : reserve_agent_nickname
    NicknameReserved --> PathReserved : reserve_agent_path
    PathReserved --> Committed : commit(metadata)
    Committed --> [*]

    Reserved --> RolledBack : Drop (spawn ى‹¤يŒ¨)
    NicknameReserved --> RolledBack : Drop
    PathReserved --> RolledBack : Drop
    RolledBack --> [*] : total_count--, path ي•´ى œ

3.3 Nickname pool and reset

Agent nicknames are randomly drawn from agent_names.txt. When the pool is exhausted, a counter is raised and an ordinal suffix such as "Atlas the 2nd" is added:

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
fn format_agent_nickname(name: &str, nickname_reset_count: usize) -> String {
    match nickname_reset_count {
        0 => name.to_string(),
        reset_count => {
            let value = reset_count + 1;
            let suffix = match value % 100 {
                11..=13 => "th",
                _ => match value % 10 {
                    1 => "st", 2 => "nd", 3 => "rd", _ => "th",
                },
            };
            format!("{name} the {value}{suffix}")
        }
    }
}

4. agent_resolver â€” Path-based agent resolution

Tools such as send_input and wait_agent specify the agent as a model string. agent_resolver converts this to ThreadId:

// core/src/agent/agent_resolver.rs
pub(crate) async fn resolve_agent_target(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    turn: &Arc<TurnContext>,
    target: &str,
) -> Result<ThreadId, FunctionCallError> {
    register_session_root(session, turn);
    // â‘  ى§پى ‘ ThreadId ë¬¸ى‍گى—´ى‌´ë©´ ى¦‰ى‹œ ë°کي™ک
    if let Ok(thread_id) = ThreadId::from_string(target) {
        return Ok(thread_id);
    }
    // â‘، AgentPath ê¸°ë°ک ي•´ى„‌ (ىکˆ: "workers/auth-fix")
    session.services.agent_control
        .resolve_agent_reference(session.conversation_id, &turn.session_source, target)
        .await
        .map_err(|err| FunctionCallError::RespondToModel(err.to_string()))
}

Multiple objects are also interpreted sequentially:

pub(crate) async fn resolve_agent_targets(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    turn: &Arc<TurnContext>,
    targets: Vec<String>,
) -> Result<Vec<ThreadId>, FunctionCallError> {
    if targets.is_empty() {
        return Err(FunctionCallError::RespondToModel(
            "agent targets must be non-empty".to_string(),
        ));
    }
    let mut resolved = Vec::with_capacity(targets.len());
    for target in &targets {
        resolved.push(resolve_agent_target(session, turn, target).await?);
    }
    Ok(resolved)
}

resolve_agent_reference searches ThreadId in agent_tree after resolving the relative path of AgentPath. The root itself is excluded from analysis.

5. Role system â€” role-based config layer

5.1 Built-in role definitions

Codex provides three built-in roles:

Role Description config_file Features
default default agent None Leave parent settings
explorer Codebase navigation only explorer.toml Fast and authoritative, parallel spawn recommended
worker Execution/implementation work None Specifies file ownership, recognizes other workers 
// core/src/agent/role.rs â€” built_in::configs() (ى¶•ى•½)
BTreeMap::from([
    ("default".to_string(), AgentRoleConfig {
        description: Some("Default agent.".to_string()),
        config_file: None,
        nickname_candidates: None,
    }),
    ("explorer".to_string(), AgentRoleConfig {
        description: Some(r#"Use `explorer` for specific codebase questions.
Explorers are fast and authoritative. ..."#.to_string()),
        config_file: Some("explorer.toml".into()),
        nickname_candidates: None,
    }),
    ("worker".to_string(), AgentRoleConfig {
        description: Some(r#"Use for execution and production work. ..."#.to_string()),
        config_file: None,
        nickname_candidates: None,
    }),
])

The awaiter role is currently commented out (// Awaiter is temp removed). awaiter.toml is still built-in, but has been removed from the role list.

5.2 explorer.toml example

The explorer.toml file is a built-in file, but is embedded in the binary as include_str!:

// core/src/agent/role.rs â€” built_in::config_file_contents
pub(super) fn config_file_contents(path: &Path) -> Option<&'static str> {
    const EXPLORER: &str = include_str!("builtins/explorer.toml");
    const AWAITER: &str = include_str!("builtins/awaiter.toml");
    match path.to_str()? {
        "explorer.toml" => Some(EXPLORER),
        "awaiter.toml" => Some(AWAITER),
        _ => None,
    }
}

5.3 awaiter.toml â€” Example minimal role config

# core/src/agent/builtins/awaiter.toml
background_terminal_max_timeout = 3600000
model_reasoning_effort = "low"
developer_instructions="""You are an awaiter.
Your role is to await the completion of a specific command or task
and report its status only when it is finished.
...
"""

This file shows the minimal structure of a role config: a role is defined with only model configuration + developer instructions.

5.4 apply_role_to_config â€” Role application mechanism

Role application is a method of inserting role TOML with â€œsession-flag priorityâ€‌ into the existing config layer stack. In this case, the parent's profile and provider are explicitly preserved:

// core/src/agent/role.rs
pub(crate) async fn apply_role_to_config(
    config: &mut Config,
    role_name: Option<&str>,
) -> Result<(), String> {
    let role_name = role_name.unwrap_or(DEFAULT_ROLE_NAME);
    let role = resolve_role_config(config, role_name)
        .cloned()
        .ok_or_else(|| format!("unknown agent_type '{role_name}'"))?;
    apply_role_to_config_inner(config, role_name, &role).await
        .map_err(|err| AGENT_TYPE_UNAVAILABLE_ERROR.to_string())
}

The core logic of the retention policy:

// core/src/agent/role.rs
fn preservation_policy(config: &Config, role_layer_toml: &TomlValue) -> (bool, bool) {
    let role_selects_provider = role_layer_toml.get("model_provider").is_some();
    let role_selects_profile = role_layer_toml.get("profile").is_some();
    // roleى‌´ providerë‚ک profileى‌„ ëھ…ى‹œي•کى§€ ى•ٹىœ¼ë©´ â†’ ë¶€ëھ¨ ê²ƒى‌„ ë³´ى،´
    let preserve_current_profile = !role_selects_provider && !role_selects_profile;
    let preserve_current_provider =
        preserve_current_profile && !role_updates_active_profile_provider;
    (preserve_current_profile, preserve_current_provider)
}

graph LR
    A["ê¸°ى،´ config layer stack"] --> B["role TOML ë،œë“œ<br/>(built-in or يŒŒى‌¼)"]
    B --> C{"roleى‌´ provider/<br/>profile ëھ…ى‹œ?"}
    C -->|No| D["ë¶€ëھ¨ profileآ·provider ë³´ى،´"]
    C -->|Yes| E["roleى‌ک ى„¤ى •ىœ¼ë،œ êµگى²´"]
    D --> F["SessionFlags ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„ë،œ<br/>layer ى‚½ى‍…"]
    E --> F
    F --> G["Config::load_config_with_layer_stack<br/>ىµœى¢… config ىƒ‌ى„±"]

5.5 Automatically generate spawn tool description

Descriptive text for the spawn_agent tool is automatically generated from built-in and custom roles:

// core/src/agent/role.rs â€” spawn_tool_spec::build
pub(crate) fn build(user_defined_agent_roles: &BTreeMap<String, AgentRoleConfig>) -> String {
    let built_in_roles = built_in::configs();
    // ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى •ى‌ک ى—­ي• ى‌´ ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌ک ë‚´ى‍¥ ى—­ي• ى‌„ ëچ®ى–´ى”€
    let mut seen = BTreeSet::new();
    let mut formatted_roles = Vec::new();
    for (name, declaration) in user_defined_roles { /* ... */ }
    for (name, declaration) in built_in_roles { /* ... */ }
    format!("Optional type name...\nAvailable roles:\n{}", formatted_roles.join("\n"))
}

6. Depth limits and state machines

6.1 Spawn depth calculation

// core/src/agent/registry.rs
fn session_depth(session_source: &SessionSource) -> i32 {
    match session_source {
        SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn { depth, .. }) => *depth,
        _ => 0,
    }
}

pub(crate) fn next_thread_spawn_depth(session_source: &SessionSource) -> i32 {
    session_depth(session_source).saturating_add(1)
}

pub(crate) fn exceeds_thread_spawn_depth_limit(depth: i32, max_depth: i32) -> bool {
    depth > max_depth
}

If the depth limit is exceeded, Feature::SpawnCsv and Feature::Collab are disabled and the agent can no longer spawn subagents:

// core/src/agent/control.rs â€” resume_single_agent_from_rollout ë‚´ë¶€
if let SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn { depth, .. }) = &session_source
    && *depth >= config.agent_max_depth
{
    let _ = config.features.disable(Feature::SpawnCsv);
    let _ = config.features.disable(Feature::Collab);
}

6.2 AgentStatus state machine

// core/src/agent/status.rs
pub(crate) fn agent_status_from_event(msg: &EventMsg) -> Option<AgentStatus> {
    match msg {
        EventMsg::TurnStarted(_)  => Some(AgentStatus::Running),
        EventMsg::TurnComplete(ev) => Some(AgentStatus::Completed(ev.last_agent_message.clone())),
        EventMsg::TurnAborted(ev) => match ev.reason {
            TurnAbortReason::Interrupted => Some(AgentStatus::Interrupted),
            _ => Some(AgentStatus::Errored(format!("{:?}", ev.reason))),
        },
        EventMsg::Error(ev) => Some(AgentStatus::Errored(ev.message.clone())),
        EventMsg::ShutdownComplete => Some(AgentStatus::Shutdown),
        _ => None,
    }
}

pub(crate) fn is_final(status: &AgentStatus) -> bool {
    !matches!(status, AgentStatus::PendingInit | AgentStatus::Running | AgentStatus::Interrupted)
}

stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> PendingInit : thread ىƒ‌ى„±
    PendingInit --> Running : TurnStarted
    Running --> Completed : TurnComplete
    Running --> Interrupted : TurnAborted(Interrupted)
    Running --> Errored : TurnAborted / Error
    Interrupted --> Running : resume_agent
    Running --> Shutdown : ShutdownComplete

    note right of Completed : is_final = true
    note right of Errored : is_final = true
    note right of Shutdown : is_final = true

7. Thread inheritance details

7.1 shell snapshot inheritance

The child inherits the parent's shell environment (environment variables, cwd, etc.):

// core/src/agent/control.rs
async fn inherited_shell_snapshot_for_source(
    &self, state: &Arc<ThreadManagerState>,
    session_source: Option<&SessionSource>,
) -> Option<Arc<ShellSnapshot>> {
    let Some(SessionSource::SubAgent(SubAgentSource::ThreadSpawn {
        parent_thread_id, ..
    })) = session_source else { return None; };
    let parent_thread = state.get_thread(*parent_thread_id).await.ok()?;
    parent_thread.codex.session.user_shell().shell_snapshot()
}

7.2 exec policy inheritance

The execution policy is conditional inheritance. Share the parent's ExecPolicyManager only if the parent and child's configs are compatible:

async fn inherited_exec_policy_for_source(
    &self, state: &Arc<ThreadManagerState>,
    session_source: Option<&SessionSource>,
    child_config: &Config,
) -> Option<Arc<ExecPolicyManager>> {
    // ...
    let parent_config = parent_thread.codex.session.get_config().await;
    if !child_uses_parent_exec_policy(&parent_config, child_config) {
        return None;   // ى •ى±…ى‌´ ë‹¤ë¥´ë©´ ىƒپى†چ ى•ˆ ي•¨
    }
    Some(Arc::clone(&parent_thread.codex.session.services.exec_policy))
}

7.3 resume â€” Restore entire agent tree

resume_agent_from_rollout traverses not only a single agent but also the entire child tree recorded in state_db with BFS and recursively restores it:

// core/src/agent/control.rs â€” resume_agent_from_rollout (ى¶•ى•½)
pub(crate) async fn resume_agent_from_rollout(&self, config: Config,
    thread_id: ThreadId, session_source: SessionSource,
) -> CodexResult<ThreadId> {
    let resumed_thread_id = self.resume_single_agent_from_rollout(
        config.clone(), thread_id, session_source
    ).await?;

    let mut resume_queue = VecDeque::from([(thread_id, root_depth)]);
    while let Some((parent_thread_id, parent_depth)) = resume_queue.pop_front() {
        let child_ids = state_db_ctx.list_thread_spawn_children_with_status(
            parent_thread_id, DirectionalThreadSpawnEdgeStatus::Open
        ).await?;
        for child_thread_id in child_ids {
            let child_depth = parent_depth + 1;
            // ى‌´ë¯¸ liveى‌´ë©´ ê±´ë„ˆë›°ê³ , ى•„ë‹ˆë©´ rolloutى—گى„œ ë³µى›گ
            if state.get_thread(child_thread_id).await.is_ok() {
                // already live
            } else {
                self.resume_single_agent_from_rollout(
                    config.clone(), child_thread_id, child_session_source
                ).await?;
            }
            resume_queue.push_back((child_thread_id, child_depth));
        }
    }
    Ok(resumed_thread_id)
}

graph TD
    R["resume_agent_from_rollout(root)"] --> A["resume_single(root)"]
    A --> Q["BFS queue: root"]
    Q --> B["state_db: list children of root<br/>(Openë§Œ)"]
    B --> C1["resume_single(child-1)"]
    B --> C2["resume_single(child-2)"]
    C1 --> Q2["queueى—گ child-1 ى¶”ê°€"]
    C2 --> Q3["queueى—گ child-2 ى¶”ê°€"]
    Q2 --> D["child-1ى‌ک children ë³µى›گ..."]
    Q3 --> E["child-2ى‌ک children ë³µى›گ..."]

8. Inter-Agent Communication (V2)

In V2, direct message exchange between agents is possible:

// core/src/agent/control.rs
pub(crate) async fn send_inter_agent_communication(
    &self, agent_id: ThreadId,
    communication: InterAgentCommunication,
) -> CodexResult<String> {
    let last_task_message = communication.content.clone();
    let state = self.upgrade()?;
    let result = self.handle_thread_request_result(
        agent_id, &state,
        state.send_op(agent_id, Op::InterAgentCommunication { communication }).await,
    ).await;
    if result.is_ok() {
        self.state.update_last_task_message(agent_id, last_task_message);
    }
    result
}

The completion watcher also uses InterAgentCommunication instead of inject_user_message_without_turn in V2 to notify parents:

// completion watcher ë‚´ë¶€ (V2 ê²½ë،œ)
if child_agent_path.is_some() && thread.enabled(Feature::MultiAgentV2) {
    let communication = InterAgentCommunication::new(
        child_agent_path, parent_agent_path,
        Vec::new(), message, /*trigger_turn*/ false,
    );
    let _ = control.send_inter_agent_communication(parent_thread_id, communication).await;
    return;
}
// V1 fallback: ë¶€ëھ¨ threadى—گ user message ى§پى ‘ ى£¼ى‍…
parent_thread.inject_user_message_without_turn(message).await;

Technical Reference

Collaboration Tool Surface

tools Role representative file
spawn_agent child thread creation 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/spawn.rs
send_input send input to child 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/send_input.rs
wait_agent Await the termination or status of one or more children 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/wait.rs
resume_agent Resume terminated child (based on rollout) 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/resume_agent.rs
close_agent child exit 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/close_agent.rs

What spawn_agent actually inherits

Item Inheritance Evidence
model inheritance build_agent_shared_config()
provider inheritance config.model_provider = turn.provider.clone()
reasoning effort inheritance config.model_reasoning_effort = turn.reasoning_effort
developer instructions inheritance config.developer_instructions = turn.developer_instructions.clone()
compact prompt inheritance config.compact_prompt = turn.compact_prompt.clone()
approval policy inheritance runtime override step
sandbox policy inheritance runtime override step
cwd inheritance runtime override step
shell snapshot inheritance inherited_shell_snapshot_for_source()
exec policy conditional inheritance After checking child_uses_parent_exec_policy()
role setting Additional Applications apply_role_to_config()

The core implementation is in 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents_common.rs.

subagent metadata

metadata Meaning
parent_thread_id From which thread it is derived
depth Current spawn depth
agent_path Path based on task name (e.g. workers/auth-fix)
agent_nickname Display name (e.g. Atlas, Atlas the 2nd)
agent_role applied role (default, explorer, worker)
last_task_message Most recently sent job message

app-server reflects this metadata in thread summary and history items. with_thread_spawn_agent_metadata() overwrites the nickname and role on the thread source and exposes them to the UI and search results.

runtime messaging

Action Meaning
send_input Interaction tool with event publishing and child status recording
wait_agent watch based state collector
close_agent Record the state just before termination and organize the entire subtree
resume_agent BFS restoration maintaining spawn lineage
interrupt_agent Interrupt execution by issuing Op::Interrupt

What the delegate layer does

Features Description file
child spawn Launches an actual Codex child thread core/src/codex_delegate.rs
event forwarding Relaying child events to parent session core/src/codex_delegate.rs
approval routing Resolving child approval with parent policy core/src/codex_delegate.rs
request_user_input routing Child passes questions to parents core/src/codex_delegate.rs
MCP tool join Reattach approval question and MCP metadata core/src/codex_delegate.rs
one-shot mode Enter initial input and automatically close upon completion core/src/codex_delegate.rs

Representation in app-server and history

Event history item
CollabAgentSpawnBegin/End CollabAgentToolCall with SpawnAgent
CollabAgentInteractionBegin/End CollabAgentToolCall with SendInput
CollabWaitingBegin/End CollabAgentToolCall with Wait
CollabCloseBegin/End CollabAgentToolCall with CloseAgent
CollabResumeBegin/End CollabAgentToolCall with ResumeAgent

mode and role prompt assets

assets Meaning
core/templates/collaboration_mode/default.md Basic Collaboration Mode Rules
core/templates/collaboration_mode/plan.md Developer directives for plan mode
core/src/models_manager/collaboration_mode_presets.rs Bundle mod templates into actual presets
core/src/agent/builtins/awaiter.toml Minimal example of a built-in role (currently inactive)
core/src/agent/builtins/explorer.toml explorer role config

This combination shows that â€œa subagent is not just an API, but a profile of prompt and configâ€‌.

Fork Checklist

  • Subagent is viewed as a child thread rather than an independent worker
  • Clearly record which settings are automatically inherited
  • app-server history view together
  • View the delegate layer as an approval bus
  • Analyzed AgentRegistryâ€™s CAS-based slot management and RAII reservation patterns.
  • Analyzed the AgentStatus state machine and completion watcher structure.
  • Analyzed depth limitations and feature deactivation mechanisms.
  • Analyzed the Inter-Agent Communication (V2) path.
  • You need to decide whether to keep the role system as is or simplify it.
  • You must decide whether to include the multi_agents_v2 series or postpone it to a later stage.
  • You must decide whether to maintain the relative path resolution of agent_resolver in the fork.

List of core files

  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/agent/mod.rs â€” Module public API
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/agent/control.rs â€” AgentControl control plane
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/agent/registry.rs â€” AgentRegistry slot tree management
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/agent/role.rs â€” Apply role system and config layer
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/agent/status.rs â€” State machine and event mapping
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/agent/agent_resolver.rs â€” Pathâ†’ThreadId resolution
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/agent/builtins/explorer.toml â€” explorer role config
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/agent/builtins/awaiter.toml â€” awaiter role config (inactive)
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents_common.rs
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/spawn.rs
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/send_input.rs
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/wait.rs
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/close_agent.rs
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/resume_agent.rs
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/codex_delegate.rs
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/models_manager/collaboration_mode_presets.rs
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/thread_history.rs
  • 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2.rs

Korean corpus (reference)
