Main analysis

App Server آ· Client Surface آ· Fork Extraction Strategy

Analysis snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” app/server/client surfaces.

1. App Server and Client Surface

Codex's rich client surface does not end with CLI alone. The actual center is the triangle of app-server, app-server-protocol, and app-server-client, and TypeScript SDK and Python SDK adhere to different philosophies above and outside of it.

app-server is the actual runtime that exposes the Codex core as a long-running server. app-server-protocol is a wire schema-specific layer, and app-server-client is a client facade that does not distinguish between in-process and remote. These three layers are the core of the rich client surface. On the other hand, the difference is that TypeScript SDK is a CLI wrapper, and Python SDK is an app-server wrapper.

In other words, if our product plans to create a rich surface such as a browser or desktop, it is correct to set the fork reference point from app-server-protocol, not CLI. This is because protocol boundaries are more important than transport.

graph TD
    A["core runtime"] --> B["app-server"]
    B --> C["app-server-protocol"]
    C --> D["in-process client"]
    C --> E["remote client (WebSocket)"]
    B --> F["Python SDK (app-server wrapper)"]
    G["CLI (codex exec)"] --> H["TypeScript SDK (CLI wrapper)"]
    
    subgraph transport
        I["stdio://"]
        J["ws://IP:PORT"]
    end
    
    B --> I
    B --> J
    E --> J

Exposed surface layer 4

floor Meaning file
app-server Long-running Codex server runtime app-server/src/lib.rs
app-server-protocol JSON-RPC wire schema (v1 legacy + v2 current) app-server-protocol/src/protocol/common.rs, v2.rs
app-server-client in-process and remote facade app-server-client/src/lib.rs, remote.rs
SDK TS is a CLI wrapper, Python is an app-server wrapper sdk/typescript/, sdk/python/

2. Protocol message details

2.1 Initialization handshake

The client must complete the handshake in the following order: initialize â†’ initialized. The initialize response includes the server version, supported capabilities, and user agent.

// Client â†’ Server: initialize ىڑ”ى²­
{
  "id": 1,
  "method": "initialize",
  "params": {
    "clientInfo": {
      "name": "codex_vscode",
      "title": "Codex VS Code Extension",
      "version": "0.1.0"
    },
    "capabilities": {
      "experimentalApi": true,
      "optOutNotificationMethods": ["item/agentMessage/delta"]
    }
  }
}

// Server â†’ Client: initialize ى‌‘ë‹µ
{ "id": 1, "result": { "userAgent": "codex/1.0.0", "serverInfo": { ... } } }

// Client â†’ Server: initialized ى•Œë¦¼ (id ى—†ى‌Œ)
{ "method": "initialized", "params": {} }

optOutNotificationMethods allows the client to proactively filter out events that are not needed. This is an important contract not only for bandwidth optimization but also for SDK design.

2.2 Client â†’ Server request (ClientRequest) full map

The client_request_definitions! macro declaratively creates the entire ClientRequest enum. Below is a breakdown by category.

Category method wire name params / response
Thread lifecycle thread/start ThreadStartParams â†’ ThreadStartResponse
thread/resume ThreadResumeParams â†’ ThreadResumeResponse
thread/fork ThreadForkParams â†’ ThreadForkResponse
thread/archive / thread/unarchive Simple threadId based
thread/list ThreadListParams â†’ ThreadListResponse
thread/loaded/list List of threads currently in memory
thread/read ThreadReadParams â†’ ThreadReadResponse (includeTurns option)
thread/name/set thread name setting
thread/metadata/update update thread metadata
thread/rollback rollback per turn
thread/compact/start Start compressing context
thread/shellCommand Execute shell in thread context
thread/unsubscribe Unsubscribe from thread events
Turn lifecycle turn/start TurnStartParams â†’ TurnStartResponse
turn/steer Inject additional input into ongoing turn
turn/interrupt Abort a turn in progress
Review review/start ReviewStartParams â†’ ReviewStartResponse
Skills / Plugins skills/list, skills/config/write skill list and settings
plugin/list, plugin/read, plugin/install, plugin/uninstall Plugin CRUD
Model model/list List of available models
Filesystem fs/readFile, fs/writeFile, fs/createDirectory, fs/getMetadata, fs/readDirectory, fs/remove, fs/copy, fs/watch, fs/unwatch Remote FS Access
Config config/read, config/value/write, config/batchWrite Read/Write server settings
Account account/read, account/login/start, account/login/cancel, account/logout, account/rateLimits/read Authentication flow
Command exec command/exec, command/exec/write, command/exec/terminate, command/exec/resize Run standalone commands (PTY supported)
MCP config/mcpServer/reload, mcpServerStatus/list, mcpServer/oauth/login MCP Server Management
Experimental thread/realtime/*, experimentalFeature/*, collaborationMode/list Experimental features

2.3 Server â†’ Client Notification (ServerNotification) Full Map

The server_notification_definitions! macro generates all server push events.

sequenceDiagram
    participant C as Client
    participant S as App Server
    
    C->>S: thread/start
    S-->>C: thread/started (notification)
    C->>S: turn/start {threadId, input}
    S-->>C: turn/started
    S-->>C: item/started (AgentMessage)
    S-->>C: item/agentMessage/delta (ë°کë³µ)
    S-->>C: item/commandExecution/requestApproval (ServerRequest)
    C->>S: resolve {id, result: {decision: "accept"}}
    S-->>C: item/started (CommandExecution)
    S-->>C: item/commandExecution/outputDelta (ë°کë³µ)
    S-->>C: item/completed (CommandExecution)
    S-->>C: item/completed (AgentMessage)
    S-->>C: turn/completed
    S-->>C: thread/tokenUsage/updated

Key notification categories:

Category wire name Use
Thread status thread/started, thread/status/changed, thread/closed, thread/archived, thread/unarchived, thread/name/updated thread life cycle
Turn status turn/started, turn/completed, turn/diff/updated, turn/plan/updated turn life cycle, diff/plan streaming
Item status item/started, item/completed item life cycle
Streaming Delta item/agentMessage/delta, item/commandExecution/outputDelta, item/fileChange/outputDelta, item/reasoning/summaryTextDelta, item/reasoning/textDelta, item/plan/delta Real-time chunks
Approval review item/autoApprovalReview/started, item/autoApprovalReview/completed Guardian automatic approval
Hook hook/started, hook/completed hook execution status
Skills/Config skills/changed, configWarning, deprecationNotice Setting change notification
Account account/updated, account/rateLimits/updated, account/login/completed Certification Status
MCP item/mcpToolCall/progress, mcpServer/oauthLogin/completed, mcpServer/startupStatus/updated MCP Server Status
FS fs/changed file system watch
Command exec command/exec/outputDelta standalone command output
Model model/rerouted Model automatic switching notification
Context thread/compacted, thread/tokenUsage/updated context compression, token usage
Realtime (exp) thread/realtime/started, thread/realtime/itemAdded, thread/realtime/transcriptUpdated, thread/realtime/outputAudio/delta, thread/realtime/error, thread/realtime/closed Voice real-time

2.4 Server â†’ Client reverse request (ServerRequest)

This is a reverse JSON-RPC request where the server asks the client to make a decision. The client must respond with result; if ignored, the turn stops.

method Use Example response
item/commandExecution/requestApproval Request permission to execute command { "decision": "accept" }
item/fileChange/requestApproval File Change Approval Request { "decision": "accept" }
item/tool/requestUserInput Request for user input (elicitation) free form
item/tool/call dynamic tool callback tool return result
item/permissions/requestApproval Grant additional permissions permission grant
mcpServer/elicitation/request MCP server elicitation elicitation response
account/chatgptAuthTokens/refresh Authentication token renewal new token 
// Server â†’ Client: ëھ…ë ¹ ى‹¤ي–‰ ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ىڑ”ى²­
{
  "id": "req-42",
  "method": "item/commandExecution/requestApproval",
  "params": {
    "threadId": "abc-123",
    "turnId": "turn-1",
    "itemId": "item-5",
    "command": "rm -rf node_modules",
    "cwd": "/workspace/project"
  }
}

// Client â†’ Server: ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ى‌‘ë‹µ
{ "id": "req-42", "result": { "decision": "accept" } }

2.5 Core data model

Thread structure

// app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2.rs
pub struct Thread {
    pub id: String,
    pub preview: String,         // ë³´ي†µ ى²« ë²ˆى§¸ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€
    pub ephemeral: bool,         // ë””ىٹ¤يپ¬ى—گ ى €ى‍¥ي•کى§€ ى•ٹëٹ” thread
    pub model_provider: String,  // "openai" ë“±
    pub created_at: i64,         // Unix timestamp
    pub updated_at: i64,
    pub status: ThreadStatus,
    pub cwd: PathBuf,
    pub cli_version: String,
    pub source: SessionSource,   // CLI, VSCode, exec, app-server ë“±
    pub agent_nickname: Option<String>,  // sub-agent ë‹‰ë„¤ى‍„
    pub agent_role: Option<String>,
    pub git_info: Option<GitInfo>,
    pub name: Option<String>,
    pub turns: Vec<Turn>,        // resume/fork/read ë•Œë§Œ ى±„ى›Œى§گ
}

Turn structure

pub struct Turn {
    pub id: String,
    pub items: Vec<ThreadItem>,  // resume/fork ى‌‘ë‹µى—گى„œë§Œ ى±„ى›Œى§گ
    pub status: TurnStatus,      // InProgress | Completed | Failed | Interrupted
    pub error: Option<TurnError>,
}

ThreadItem classification (13 types)

pub enum ThreadItem {
    UserMessage { id, content: Vec<UserInput> },
    HookPrompt { id, fragments },
    AgentMessage { id, text, phase, memory_citation },
    Plan { id, text },                    // experimental
    Reasoning { id, summary, content },
    CommandExecution { id, command, cwd, process_id, source,
                       status, command_actions, aggregated_output,
                       exit_code, duration_ms },
    FileChange { id, changes: Vec<FileUpdateChange>, status },
    McpToolCall { id, server, tool, status, arguments,
                  result, error, duration_ms },
    DynamicToolCall { id, tool, arguments, status,
                      content_items, success, duration_ms },
    CollabAgentToolCall { id, tool, status, sender_thread_id,
                          receiver_thread_ids, prompt, model,
                          reasoning_effort, agents_states },
    WebSearch { id, query, action },
    ImageView { id, path },
    ImageGeneration { id, status, revised_prompt, result, saved_path },
    EnteredReviewMode { id, review },
    ExitedReviewMode { id, review },
    ContextCompaction { id },
}

TurnStartParams â€” Turn unit override

pub struct TurnStartParams {
    pub thread_id: String,
    pub input: Vec<UserInput>,
    pub cwd: Option<PathBuf>,                // ى‍‘ى—… ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ
    pub approval_policy: Option<AskForApproval>,  // ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ى •ى±…
    pub approvals_reviewer: Option<ApprovalsReviewer>,
    pub sandbox_policy: Option<SandboxPolicy>,
    pub model: Option<String>,               // ëھ¨ëچ¸ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ
    pub service_tier: Option<Option<ServiceTier>>,
    pub effort: Option<ReasoningEffort>,     // reasoning effort
    pub summary: Option<ReasoningSummary>,
    pub personality: Option<Personality>,
    pub output_schema: Option<JsonValue>,    // structured output
    pub collaboration_mode: Option<CollaborationMode>,  // experimental
}

3. Transport layer

The AppServerTransport enum supports two transports.

// app-server/src/transport.rs
pub enum AppServerTransport {
    Stdio,
    WebSocket { bind_address: SocketAddr },
}

impl AppServerTransport {
    pub const DEFAULT_LISTEN_URL: &'static str = "stdio://";

    pub fn from_listen_url(listen_url: &str) -> Result<Self, ...> {
        // "stdio://"  â†’ Stdio
        // "ws://IP:PORT" â†’ WebSocket { bind_address }
    }
}

WebSocket transport is axum-based and exposes /readyz and /healthz endpoints. Channel capacity is fixed at CHANNEL_CAPACITY = 128.

graph LR
    subgraph "stdio transport"
        A["stdin (JSON line)"] --> B["MessageProcessor"]
        B --> C["stdout (JSON line)"]
    end
    
    subgraph "WebSocket transport"
        D["ws:// connect"] --> E["axum Router"]
        E --> F["MessageProcessor"]
        F --> G["ws:// frame"]
        E --> H["/readyz"]
        E --> I["/healthz"]
    end
    
    subgraph "in-process transport"
        J["InProcessClientHandle"] --> K["mpsc channels"]
        K --> L["MessageProcessor"]
        L --> M["broadcast events"]
    end

4. Client API details

4.1 In-Process Client (Rust)

It is a facade for consumers within the same process, such as TUI and exec. It is connected to MessageProcessor and the bounded mpsc channel, and there is no separate process boundary.

// app-server-client/src/lib.rs

/// ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ىˆکى‹  يƒ€ى‍…
pub enum AppServerEvent {
    Lagged { skipped: usize },      // backpressure ى´ˆê³¼ ى‹œ
    ServerNotification(ServerNotification),
    ServerRequest(ServerRequest),
    Disconnected { message: String },
}

/// ي•µى‹¬ client facade
pub struct InProcessAppServerClient { /* ... */ }

impl InProcessAppServerClient {
    /// ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ ى‹œى‍‘ + initialize/initialized ي•¸ë“œى…°ى‌´يپ¬ ى‍گëڈ™ ىˆکي–‰
    pub async fn start(args: InProcessClientStartArgs) -> IoResult<Self>;

    /// typed JSON-RPC ىڑ”ى²­ â€” ى‌‘ë‹µى‌„ ى œë„¤ë¦­ Të،œ ë””ى½”ë”©
    pub async fn request_typed<T: DeserializeOwned>(
        &self, request: ClientRequest
    ) -> Result<T, TypedRequestError>;

    /// raw JSON-RPC ىڑ”ى²­
    pub async fn request(&self, request: ClientRequest) -> IoResult<RequestResult>;

    /// ى•Œë¦¼ ى „ى†،
    pub async fn notify(&self, notification: ClientNotification) -> IoResult<()>;

    /// ServerRequestى—گ ëŒ€ي•œ ى‌‘ë‹µ (approval ë“±)
    pub async fn resolve_server_request(
        &self, request_id: RequestId, result: JsonRpcResult
    ) -> IoResult<()>;

    /// ServerRequest ê±°ë¶€
    pub async fn reject_server_request(
        &self, request_id: RequestId, error: JSONRPCErrorError
    ) -> IoResult<()>;

    /// ë‹¤ى‌Œ ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ىˆکى‹  (backpressure-aware)
    pub async fn next_event(&mut self) -> Option<InProcessServerEvent>;

    /// graceful shutdown (5ى´ˆ يƒ€ى‍„ى•„ى›ƒ ي›„ abort)
    pub async fn shutdown(self) -> IoResult<()>;
}

backpressure design: Uses try_send and returns WouldBlock when the channel is full. However, delivery of terminal events such as TurnCompleted is guaranteed.

4.2 Remote Client (WebSocket)

WebSocket-based remote access client. It is designed so that upper layers such as TUI are not aware of transport by providing the same AppServerEvent surface as InProcessAppServerClient.

// app-server-client/src/remote.rs

pub struct RemoteAppServerConnectArgs {
    pub websocket_url: String,
    pub client_name: String,
    pub client_version: String,
    pub experimental_api: bool,
    pub opt_out_notification_methods: Vec<String>,
    pub channel_capacity: usize,
}

pub struct RemoteAppServerClient { /* ... */ }

impl RemoteAppServerClient {
    /// WebSocket ى—°ê²° + initialize ي•¸ë“œى…°ى‌´يپ¬ (10ى´ˆ يƒ€ى‍„ى•„ى›ƒ)
    pub async fn connect(args: RemoteAppServerConnectArgs) -> IoResult<Self>;
    pub async fn request(&self, ...) -> IoResult<RequestResult>;
    pub async fn request_typed<T>(&self, ...) -> Result<T, TypedRequestError>;
    pub async fn notify(&self, ...) -> IoResult<()>;
    pub async fn next_event(&mut self) -> Option<AppServerEvent>;
    pub async fn shutdown(self) -> IoResult<()>;
}

4.3 Integrated Client enum

/// transport ë¬´ê´€ ي†µي•© surface
pub enum AppServerClient {
    InProcess(InProcessAppServerClient),
    Remote(RemoteAppServerClient),
}

impl AppServerClient {
    pub async fn request(&self, ...) -> IoResult<RequestResult>;
    pub async fn request_typed<T>(&self, ...) -> Result<T, TypedRequestError>;
    pub async fn notify(&self, ...) -> IoResult<()>;
    pub async fn resolve_server_request(&self, ...) -> IoResult<()>;
    pub async fn reject_server_request(&self, ...) -> IoResult<()>;
    pub async fn next_event(&mut self) -> Option<AppServerEvent>;
    pub async fn shutdown(self) -> IoResult<()>;
}

classDiagram
    class AppServerClient {
        <<enum>>
        InProcess(InProcessAppServerClient)
        Remote(RemoteAppServerClient)
        +request()
        +request_typed~T~()
        +notify()
        +resolve_server_request()
        +reject_server_request()
        +next_event()
        +shutdown()
    }
    
    class InProcessAppServerClient {
        +start(args) IoResult~Self~
        +request_handle() Handle
    }
    
    class RemoteAppServerClient {
        +connect(args) IoResult~Self~
    }
    
    AppServerClient --> InProcessAppServerClient
    AppServerClient --> RemoteAppServerClient

5. Session management and Thread state machine

5.1 ThreadState internal structure

// app-server/src/thread_state.rs
pub struct ThreadState {
    pub pending_interrupts: Vec<...>,
    pub pending_rollbacks: Option<ConnectionRequestId>,
    pub turn_summary: TurnSummary,     // file_change, cmd ى‹¤ي–‰ ىƒپيƒœ ى¶”ى پ
    pub cancel_tx: Option<oneshot::Sender<()>>,
    pub experimental_raw_events: bool,
    pub listener_generation: u64,      // listener êµگى²´ ى‹œ increment
    listener_command_tx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedSender<ThreadListenerCommand>>,
    current_turn_history: ThreadHistoryBuilder,
    listener_thread: Option<Weak<CodexThread>>,
}

5.2 Thread life cycle state machine

stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Created: thread/start
    Created --> Running: turn/start
    Running --> Running: turn/steer (same-turn steering)
    Running --> Interrupted: turn/interrupt
    Running --> Completed: turn/completed
    Interrupted --> Running: turn/start (new turn)
    Completed --> Running: turn/start (new turn)
    Completed --> Compacting: thread/compact/start
    Compacting --> Completed: compaction done
    Completed --> Archived: thread/archive
    Archived --> Completed: thread/unarchive
    Completed --> Forked: thread/fork
    Completed --> RolledBack: thread/rollback
    RolledBack --> Running: turn/start
    
    Created --> Resumed: thread/resume
    Resumed --> Running: turn/start

5.3 ThreadHistoryBuilder â€” rollout replay

It is a core builder that reorganizes past events into Turn[] when thread resumes.

// app-server-protocol/src/protocol/thread_history.rs
pub struct ThreadHistoryBuilder {
    turns: Vec<Turn>,
    current_turn: Option<PendingTurn>,
    next_item_index: i64,
    current_rollout_index: usize,
    next_rollout_index: usize,
}

impl ThreadHistoryBuilder {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    pub fn handle_event(&mut self, event: &EventMsg);  // ê³µىœ  reducer
    pub fn handle_rollout_item(&mut self, item: &RolloutItem);
    pub fn finish(self) -> Vec<Turn>;
    pub fn active_turn_snapshot(&self) -> Option<Turn>;
    pub fn has_active_turn(&self) -> bool;
    pub fn reset(&mut self);
}

/// rollout يŒŒى‌¼ى—گى„œ Turn[] ى‍¬êµ¬ى„±
pub fn build_turns_from_rollout_items(items: &[RolloutItem]) -> Vec<Turn>;

handle_event is a shared reducer that processes all variants of EventMsg, and uses the same logic for resume/rejoin/fork.

6. SDK Surface Comparison

6.1 TypeScript SDK â€” CLI wrapper method

TypeScript SDK is a thin wrapper that runs codex exec as a subprocess and parses the stdout JSONL stream. Do not use app-server protocol directly.

// sdk/typescript/src/codex.ts
export class Codex {
  constructor(options: CodexOptions = {});
  startThread(options?: ThreadOptions): Thread;
  resumeThread(id: string, options?: ThreadOptions): Thread;
}

// sdk/typescript/src/thread.ts
export class Thread {
  get id(): string | null;
  async run(input: Input, turnOptions?: TurnOptions): Promise<Turn>;
  async runStreamed(input: Input, turnOptions?: TurnOptions): Promise<StreamedTurn>;
}

// ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ يƒ€ى‍… (exec JSONL ê¸°ë°ک)
type ThreadEvent =
  | ThreadStartedEvent    // { type: "thread.started", thread_id }
  | TurnStartedEvent      // { type: "turn.started" }
  | TurnCompletedEvent    // { type: "turn.completed", usage }
  | TurnFailedEvent       // { type: "turn.failed", error }
  | ItemStartedEvent      // { type: "item.started", item }
  | ItemUpdatedEvent      // { type: "item.updated", item }
  | ItemCompletedEvent    // { type: "item.completed", item }
  | ThreadErrorEvent;     // { type: "error", message }

// Item يƒ€ى‍… (7ê°€ى§€ â€” app-serverى‌ک 13ê°€ى§€ë³´ë‹¤ ë‹¨ىˆœي™”)
type ThreadItem =
  | AgentMessageItem | ReasoningItem | CommandExecutionItem
  | FileChangeItem | McpToolCallItem | WebSearchItem
  | TodoListItem | ErrorItem;

Constraint: The event name is dot-case (item.completed) and is different from app-server's slash-case (item/completed). Only 7 out of 13 item types are exposed. In fact, a subset of the app-server protocol is wrapped in a separate contract.

6.2 Python SDK â€” app-server wrapper method

The Python SDK launches app-server as a stdio subprocess and directly implements the JSON-RPC protocol. Typed methods are provided for all methods in the v2 protocol.

# sdk/python/src/codex_app_server/client.py
class AppServerClient:
    """Synchronous typed JSON-RPC client for codex app-server over stdio."""

    def __init__(self, config=None, approval_handler=None): ...
    def start(self) -> None: ...
    def close(self) -> None: ...
    def initialize(self) -> InitializeResponse: ...

    # Thread lifecycle
    def thread_start(self, params=None) -> ThreadStartResponse: ...
    def thread_resume(self, thread_id, params=None) -> ThreadResumeResponse: ...
    def thread_list(self, params=None) -> ThreadListResponse: ...
    def thread_read(self, thread_id, include_turns=False) -> ThreadReadResponse: ...
    def thread_fork(self, thread_id, params=None) -> ThreadForkResponse: ...
    def thread_archive(self, thread_id) -> ThreadArchiveResponse: ...
    def thread_unarchive(self, thread_id) -> ThreadUnarchiveResponse: ...
    def thread_set_name(self, thread_id, name) -> ThreadSetNameResponse: ...
    def thread_compact(self, thread_id) -> ThreadCompactStartResponse: ...

    # Turn lifecycle
    def turn_start(self, thread_id, input_items, params=None) -> TurnStartResponse: ...
    def turn_interrupt(self, thread_id, turn_id) -> TurnInterruptResponse: ...
    def turn_steer(self, thread_id, expected_turn_id, input_items) -> TurnSteerResponse: ...

    # Model
    def model_list(self, include_hidden=False) -> ModelListResponse: ...

    # يژ¸ى‌ک ë©”ى„œë“œ
    def wait_for_turn_completed(self, turn_id) -> TurnCompletedNotification: ...
    def stream_until_methods(self, methods) -> list[Notification]: ...
    def stream_text(self, thread_id, text, params=None) -> Iterator[AgentMessageDeltaNotification]: ...

    # ServerRequest ى‍گëڈ™ ى²کë¦¬
    def _handle_server_request(self, msg) -> JsonObject: ...

# sdk/python/src/codex_app_server/api.py â€” ê³ ىˆکى¤€ Codex API
class Codex:
    """Minimal typed SDK surface for app-server v2."""
    def __init__(self, config=None): ...
    def thread_start(self, ...) -> ThreadStartResponse: ...
    def run(self, thread_id, input, ...) -> RunResult: ...
    def run_streamed(self, thread_id, input, ...) -> Iterator[Notification]: ...
    def close(self): ...

ServerRequest automatic processing: Python SDK automatically acceptsâ€™ approval requests in _handle_server_request`. This is an opinionated default at the SDK level.

def _default_approval_handler(self, method, params):
    if method == "item/commandExecution/requestApproval":
        return {"decision": "accept"}
    if method == "item/fileChange/requestApproval":
        return {"decision": "accept"}
    return {}

6.3 SDK Comparison Summary

graph TB
    subgraph "TypeScript SDK"
        TS_Codex["Codex"] --> TS_Thread["Thread"]
        TS_Thread --> TS_Exec["CodexExec (subprocess)"]
        TS_Exec --> TS_JSONL["stdout JSONL stream"]
        TS_JSONL --> TS_Events["ThreadEvent (dot-case)"]
    end
    
    subgraph "Python SDK"
        PY_Codex["Codex"] --> PY_Client["AppServerClient"]
        PY_Client --> PY_Proc["subprocess (stdio://)"]
        PY_Proc --> PY_JSONRPC["JSON-RPC v2 protocol"]
        PY_JSONRPC --> PY_Notif["Notification (slash-case)"]
    end
    
    subgraph "Rust Client"
        RS_Client["AppServerClient"] --> RS_InProc["InProcess (mpsc channels)"]
        RS_Client --> RS_Remote["Remote (WebSocket)"]
        RS_InProc --> RS_MP["MessageProcessor"]
        RS_Remote --> RS_MP
    end
dimension TypeScript SDK Python SDK Rust Client
transport subprocess stdout JSONL subprocess stdio JSON-RPC in-process channels or WebSocket
protocol level exec event (subset) app-server v2 full app-server v2 full
thread management default start/resume full lifecycle full lifecycle
approval processing impossible (handled by exec) approval_handler callback resolve/reject_server_request
event name dot-case (item.completed) slash-case (item/completed) enum variant
structured output outputSchema file based TurnStartParams.output_schema TurnStartParams.output_schema
concurrency single thread per turn turn consumer lock async mpsc

7. Message processor internal structure

7.1 MessageProcessor layer

graph TD
    A["TransportEvent (incoming)"] --> B["MessageProcessor"]
    B --> C["CodexMessageProcessor"]
    C --> D["ThreadManager (core)"]
    C --> E["ConfigApi"]
    C --> F["FsApi"]
    C --> G["FsWatchManager"]
    C --> H["ExternalAgentConfigApi"]
    B --> I["OutgoingMessageSender"]
    I --> J["OutboundConnectionState"]
    J --> K["transport writer"]

MessageProcessor is the top-level dispatcher that routes incoming JSON-RPC, and CodexMessageProcessor is responsible for the actual thread/turn/skill logic. Between these two, ConnectionSessionState manages per-connection state.

7.2 app-server module configuration

module Role
lib.rs Bootstrap server, load config, start transport
transport.rs stdio/WebSocket connection management, axum router
message_processor.rs JSON-RPC dispatch, connection state
codex_message_processor.rs thread/turn/skill business logic
bespoke_event_handling.rs core EventMsg â†’ ServerNotification conversion
thread_state.rs per-thread runtime status
thread_status.rs tracking thread state changes
in_process.rs in-process runtime based on in-memory channel
config_api.rs config read/write handler
fs_api.rs filesystem RPC handler
fs_watch.rs filesystem change monitoring
command_exec.rs Run standalone commands (PTY supported)
dynamic_tools.rs dynamic tool callback management

8. What a rich client must handle

  • item/agentMessage/delta â€” Streaming text chunk
  • item/tool/call â€” dynamic tool callback (passed as ServerRequest)
  • item/tool/requestUserInput â€” elicitation request
  • item/commandExecution/requestApproval â€” Approve command execution
  • item/fileChange/requestApproval â€” File change approval
  • item/permissions/requestApproval â€” Request additional permissions
  • collab agent status (CollabAgentToolCall item)
  • skills/changed â€” Notification of skill list change
  • turn/completed â€” delivery guaranteed (also not dropped on backpressure)
  • thread/tokenUsage/updated â€” Track token usage
  • model/rerouted â€” Notify model auto-switching

Reuse seam

seam Reason
app-server-protocol Can be immediately reused as a contract layer between UI and server â€” includes automatic JSON Schema generation
app-server-client In-process and remote can be attached to the same model
app-server message processor Thread/turn/item lifecycle is well organized
ThreadHistoryBuilder Turn[] reconfiguration logic is separated in rollout file
connectors app directory, cache TTL, normalized AppInfo creation are separate

Combined Risk

risk Description
app-server bootstrap binding core state, auth, config loader, transport, arg0 dispatch are intertwined
SDK philosophy differences TS is a CLI wrapper (exec JSONL), Python is an app-server wrapper (JSON-RPC) â€” the event names are also different
connectors depend ChatGPT account and directory endpoint premise
number of rich events The entire operation cannot be replicated by only receiving delta â€” ServerRequest backcall processing required
v1/v2 dual maintenance deprecated v1 API still exists in code, incurring cleanup costs

9. Our style fork extraction strategy

What matters now is not how much analysis you did, but what you keep and what you throw away. openai/codex has many functions and a large surface area. If we fork it and push it into the product, our product will be dragged into the Codex structure. Conversely, if we precisely cut out only the necessary layers, we can quickly create already proven prompt assembly, tool runtime, and subagent orchestrator as our assets.

The judgment I have made after looking at the code so far is clear. What we need to get to first is not the CLI, but the middle layer of the core. In particular, instructions, contextual_user_message, tools/router, tools/registry, tools/handlers, skills, codex_delegate, and app-server-protocol are key. Conversely, in the early stages, there is almost no need for TUI, desktop UX, plugin marketplace, release script, or OS-specific packaging layers.

The extraction strategy is less of a â€œprocess of reducing a large reservoir into smaller productsâ€‌ and more of a process of â€œrebinding already separated layers into our own boundaries.â€‌

graph TD
    A["prompt contract"] --> B["tool runtime"]
    B --> C["skill system"]
    B --> D["subagent runtime"]
    C --> E["dependency policy"]
    D --> F["app server control plane"]
    F --> G["our product API"]

graph LR
    A["leave behind"] --> B["TUI and packaging"]
    A --> C["desktop UX"]
    A --> D["marketplace UI"]
    E["extract first"] --> F["instructions and contextual fragments"]
    E --> G["router and registry and handlers"]
    E --> H["skills loader and injector"]
    E --> I["subagent control and protocol"]

Priority extraction target

Priority Bundle Reason
1 prompt contract It is the baseline of model behavior
2 tool router and registry It is the center of engine performance
3 Core handlers shell, patch, mcp, dynamic, multi_agents are actual features
4 skills loader and injector Can be reused with our own instruction package system
5 subagent and app-server protocol Collaboration and API surfaces can come together

Things that can be discarded in the early stages

area Reason
TUI rendering Not directly related to our product UI
desktop app experience Independent of product core
plugin marketplace All It can be replaced with capability catalog later
release and install scripts Fork is not the core
Distribution packaging layer by OS Separate from product backend extraction

File axis we need to take

axis First File
prompt codex-rs/protocol/src/prompts/base_instructions/default.md, codex-rs/core/src/codex.rs, codex-rs/core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs, codex-rs/core/src/contextual_user_message.rs
tools codex-rs/core/src/tools/router.rs, codex-rs/core/src/tools/registry.rs, codex-rs/core/src/tools/parallel.rs, codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/*
skills codex-rs/core/src/skills/manager.rs, codex-rs/core/src/skills/loader.rs, codex-rs/core/src/skills/injection.rs, codex-rs/core/src/skills/render.rs
subagents codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/multi_agents/*, codex-rs/core/src/codex_delegate.rs, codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/thread_history.rs

Recommendation extraction steps

  • Step 1: Separate only the prompt contract into a separate package.
  • Step 2: Cut the tool router, registry, and core handler into separate runtime crates or modules.
  • Step 3: Attach skill loader and injector, but lock global config modification function.
  • Step 4: Attach a subagent control surface that includes only spawn/send/wait first.
  • Step 5: Connect the app-server protocol with our API and unify the history items.

Fork method recommended

method Recommendation Reason
Long-term maintenance fork of entire repository 35 I keep getting pulled into the upstream UI and product layers
vendoring only the folders you need 55 Fast but difficult to track updates
Repackaging core functionality into our boundaries 75 Most annoying, but best in the long run

Final Judgment

What we need is not â€œhow to use the Codex,â€‌ but â€œhow to dismantle the Codex.â€‌ Looking at the structure we've seen so far, the first thing we need to touch on is the prompt contract and tool runtime, and skill and subagent are the second layer on top of that. If this order is followed, the fork is not simply copied but can be reassembled under control.

List of core files (app-server)

  • codex-rs/app-server/README.md
  • codex-rs/app-server/src/lib.rs â€” Server bootstrapping, config loading
  • codex-rs/app-server/src/transport.rs â€” stdio/WebSocket transport
  • codex-rs/app-server/src/message_processor.rs â€” JSON-RPC dispatch
  • codex-rs/app-server/src/codex_message_processor.rs â€” thread/turn business logic
  • codex-rs/app-server/src/in_process.rs â€” in-process runtime
  • codex-rs/app-server/src/thread_state.rs â€” per-thread state
  • codex-rs/app-server/src/bespoke_event_handling.rs â€” EventMsg â†’ Notification conversion
  • codex-rs/app-server/src/command_exec.rs â€” Standalone command execution
  • codex-rs/app-server/src/dynamic_tools.rs â€” dynamic tool callback
  • codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/common.rs â€” ClientRequest, ServerNotification, ServerRequest macro definitions
  • codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2.rs â€” All v2 data types
  • codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/thread_history.rs â€” ThreadHistoryBuilder
  • codex-rs/app-server-client/src/lib.rs â€” InProcess/Remote integration facade
  • codex-rs/app-server-client/src/remote.rs â€” WebSocket client
  • codex-rs/connectors/src/lib.rs
  • sdk/typescript/src/codex.ts, thread.ts, events.ts, items.ts
  • sdk/python/src/codex_app_server/client.py, api.py, async_client.py

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release â€” re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

Change history

  • 2025-07-18: Added detailed protocol message full map (ClientRequest 50+, ServerNotification 40+, ServerRequest 7). Document the core data model (Thread, Turn, ThreadItem, TurnStartParams) with code snippets. Transport layer (stdio/WebSocket/in-process) Mermaid diagram added. Signature details for 3 types of Client API (InProcess, Remote, integrated enum). Session management and Thread state machine diagram added. ThreadHistoryBuilder rollout replay structure documentation. Added 3 types of SDKs (TypeScript/Python/Rust) comparison table and code snippet. MessageProcessor internal structure and module configuration organized.
  • 2026-03-26: It has been summarized that the center of the rich client facade is not CLI but the app-server plus protocol plus client facade.
  • 2026-03-26: We removed the existing MVP judgment document and organized our style of fork extraction priorities and step-by-step strategy.
  • 2026-03-26: The app-server surface document and fork extraction strategy document were integrated.

Korean corpus (reference)

2.3 Server â†’ Client ى•Œë¦¼ (ServerNotification) ى „ى²´ ë§µ

server_notification_definitions! ë§¤يپ¬ë،œê°€ ëھ¨ë“  ى„œë²„ push ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ë¥¼ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•œë‹¤.

sequenceDiagram
    participant C as Client
    participant S as App Server
    
    C->>S: thread/start
    S-->>C: thread/started (notification)
    C->>S: turn/start {threadId, input}
    S-->>C: turn/started
    S-->>C: item/started (AgentMessage)
    S-->>C: item/agentMessage/delta (ë°کë³µ)
    S-->>C: item/commandExecution/requestApproval (ServerRequest)
    C->>S: resolve {id, result: {decision: "accept"}}
    S-->>C: item/started (CommandExecution)
    S-->>C: item/commandExecution/outputDelta (ë°کë³µ)
    S-->>C: item/completed (CommandExecution)
    S-->>C: item/completed (AgentMessage)
    S-->>C: turn/completed
    S-->>C: thread/tokenUsage/updated

ي•µى‹¬ ى•Œë¦¼ ë¶„ë¥ک:

ë¶„ë¥ک wire name ىڑ©ëڈ„
Thread ىƒپيƒœ thread/started, thread/status/changed, thread/closed, thread/archived, thread/unarchived, thread/name/updated thread ىƒ‌ëھ…ى£¼ê¸°
Turn ىƒپيƒœ turn/started, turn/completed, turn/diff/updated, turn/plan/updated turn ىƒ‌ëھ…ى£¼ê¸°, diff/plan ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ
Item ىƒپيƒœ item/started, item/completed item ىƒ‌ëھ…ى£¼ê¸°
ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ëچ¸يƒ€ item/agentMessage/delta, item/commandExecution/outputDelta, item/fileChange/outputDelta, item/reasoning/summaryTextDelta, item/reasoning/textDelta, item/plan/delta ى‹¤ى‹œê°„ ى²­يپ¬
Approval review item/autoApprovalReview/started, item/autoApprovalReview/completed Guardian ى‍گëڈ™ ىٹ¹ى‌¸
Hook hook/started, hook/completed hook ى‹¤ي–‰ ىƒپيƒœ
Skills/Config skills/changed, configWarning, deprecationNotice ى„¤ى • ë³€ê²½ ى•Œë¦¼
Account account/updated, account/rateLimits/updated, account/login/completed ى‌¸ى¦‌ ىƒپيƒœ
MCP item/mcpToolCall/progress, mcpServer/oauthLogin/completed, mcpServer/startupStatus/updated MCP ى„œë²„ ىƒپيƒœ
FS fs/changed يŒŒى‌¼ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ watch
Command exec command/exec/outputDelta standalone ëھ…ë ¹ ى¶œë ¥
Model model/rerouted ëھ¨ëچ¸ ى‍گëڈ™ ى „ي™ک ى•Œë¦¼
Context thread/compacted, thread/tokenUsage/updated ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ى••ى¶•, ي† يپ° ى‚¬ىڑ©ëں‰
Realtime (exp) thread/realtime/started, thread/realtime/itemAdded, thread/realtime/transcriptUpdated, thread/realtime/outputAudio/delta, thread/realtime/error, thread/realtime/closed ى‌Œى„± ى‹¤ى‹œê°„

2.4 Server â†’ Client ى—­ىڑ”ى²­ (ServerRequest)

ى„œë²„ê°€ يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ى—گê²Œ ê²°ى •ى‌„ ىڑ”êµ¬ي•کëٹ” ى—­ë°©ي–¥ JSON-RPC ىڑ”ى²­ى‌´ë‹¤. يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ëٹ” ë°کë“œى‹œ resultë،œ ى‌‘ë‹µي•´ى•¼ ي•کë©°, ë¬´ى‹œي•کë©´ turnى‌´ ë©ˆى¶کë‹¤.

method ىڑ©ëڈ„ ى‌‘ë‹µ ىکˆى‹œ
item/commandExecution/requestApproval ëھ…ë ¹ ى‹¤ي–‰ ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ىڑ”ى²­ { "decision": "accept" }
item/fileChange/requestApproval يŒŒى‌¼ ë³€ê²½ ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ىڑ”ى²­ { "decision": "accept" }
item/tool/requestUserInput ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى‍…ë ¥ ىڑ”ى²­ (elicitation) ى‍گىœ  يک•ى‹‌
item/tool/call dynamic tool ى—­يک¸ى¶œ tool ê²°ê³¼ ë°کي™ک
item/permissions/requestApproval ى¶”ê°€ ê¶Œي•œ ىٹ¹ى‌¸ permission grant
mcpServer/elicitation/request MCP ى„œë²„ elicitation elicitation ى‌‘ë‹µ
account/chatgptAuthTokens/refresh ى‌¸ى¦‌ ي† يپ° ê°±ى‹  ىƒˆ ي† يپ° 
// Server â†’ Client: ëھ…ë ¹ ى‹¤ي–‰ ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ىڑ”ى²­
{
  "id": "req-42",
  "method": "item/commandExecution/requestApproval",
  "params": {
    "threadId": "abc-123",
    "turnId": "turn-1",
    "itemId": "item-5",
    "command": "rm -rf node_modules",
    "cwd": "/workspace/project"
  }
}

// Client â†’ Server: ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ى‌‘ë‹µ
{ "id": "req-42", "result": { "decision": "accept" } }

2.5 ي•µى‹¬ ëچ°ى‌´ي„° ëھ¨ëچ¸

Thread êµ¬ى،°

// app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2.rs
pub struct Thread {
    pub id: String,
    pub preview: String,         // ë³´ي†µ ى²« ë²ˆى§¸ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€
    pub ephemeral: bool,         // ë””ىٹ¤يپ¬ى—گ ى €ى‍¥ي•کى§€ ى•ٹëٹ” thread
    pub model_provider: String,  // "openai" ë“±
    pub created_at: i64,         // Unix timestamp
    pub updated_at: i64,
    pub status: ThreadStatus,
    pub cwd: PathBuf,
    pub cli_version: String,
    pub source: SessionSource,   // CLI, VSCode, exec, app-server ë“±
    pub agent_nickname: Option<String>,  // sub-agent ë‹‰ë„¤ى‍„
    pub agent_role: Option<String>,
    pub git_info: Option<GitInfo>,
    pub name: Option<String>,
    pub turns: Vec<Turn>,        // resume/fork/read ë•Œë§Œ ى±„ى›Œى§گ
}

Turn êµ¬ى،°

pub struct Turn {
    pub id: String,
    pub items: Vec<ThreadItem>,  // resume/fork ى‌‘ë‹µى—گى„œë§Œ ى±„ى›Œى§گ
    pub status: TurnStatus,      // InProgress | Completed | Failed | Interrupted
    pub error: Option<TurnError>,
}

ThreadItem ë¶„ë¥ک (13ê°€ى§€)

pub enum ThreadItem {
    UserMessage { id, content: Vec<UserInput> },
    HookPrompt { id, fragments },
    AgentMessage { id, text, phase, memory_citation },
    Plan { id, text },                    // experimental
    Reasoning { id, summary, content },
    CommandExecution { id, command, cwd, process_id, source,
                       status, command_actions, aggregated_output,
                       exit_code, duration_ms },
    FileChange { id, changes: Vec<FileUpdateChange>, status },
    McpToolCall { id, server, tool, status, arguments,
                  result, error, duration_ms },
    DynamicToolCall { id, tool, arguments, status,
                      content_items, success, duration_ms },
    CollabAgentToolCall { id, tool, status, sender_thread_id,
                          receiver_thread_ids, prompt, model,
                          reasoning_effort, agents_states },
    WebSearch { id, query, action },
    ImageView { id, path },
    ImageGeneration { id, status, revised_prompt, result, saved_path },
    EnteredReviewMode { id, review },
    ExitedReviewMode { id, review },
    ContextCompaction { id },
}

TurnStartParams â€” turn ë‹¨ىœ„ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ

pub struct TurnStartParams {
    pub thread_id: String,
    pub input: Vec<UserInput>,
    pub cwd: Option<PathBuf>,                // ى‍‘ى—… ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ
    pub approval_policy: Option<AskForApproval>,  // ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ى •ى±…
    pub approvals_reviewer: Option<ApprovalsReviewer>,
    pub sandbox_policy: Option<SandboxPolicy>,
    pub model: Option<String>,               // ëھ¨ëچ¸ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ
    pub service_tier: Option<Option<ServiceTier>>,
    pub effort: Option<ReasoningEffort>,     // reasoning effort
    pub summary: Option<ReasoningSummary>,
    pub personality: Option<Personality>,
    pub output_schema: Option<JsonValue>,    // structured output
    pub collaboration_mode: Option<CollaborationMode>,  // experimental
}

3. Transport ê³„ى¸µ

AppServerTransport enumى‌´ ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ transportë¥¼ ى§€ى›گي•œë‹¤.

// app-server/src/transport.rs
pub enum AppServerTransport {
    Stdio,
    WebSocket { bind_address: SocketAddr },
}

impl AppServerTransport {
    pub const DEFAULT_LISTEN_URL: &'static str = "stdio://";

    pub fn from_listen_url(listen_url: &str) -> Result<Self, ...> {
        // "stdio://"  â†’ Stdio
        // "ws://IP:PORT" â†’ WebSocket { bind_address }
    }
}

WebSocket transportëٹ” axum ê¸°ë°کى‌´ë©°, /readyzى™€ /healthz ى—”ë“œيڈ¬ى‌¸يٹ¸ë¥¼ ي•¨ê»ک ë…¸ى¶œي•œë‹¤. ى±„ë„گ ىڑ©ëں‰ى‌€ CHANNEL_CAPACITY = 128ë،œ ê³ ى •ëگکى–´ ى‍ˆë‹¤.

graph LR
    subgraph "stdio transport"
        A["stdin (JSON line)"] --> B["MessageProcessor"]
        B --> C["stdout (JSON line)"]
    end
    
    subgraph "WebSocket transport"
        D["ws:// connect"] --> E["axum Router"]
        E --> F["MessageProcessor"]
        F --> G["ws:// frame"]
        E --> H["/readyz"]
        E --> I["/healthz"]
    end
    
    subgraph "in-process transport"
        J["InProcessClientHandle"] --> K["mpsc channels"]
        K --> L["MessageProcessor"]
        L --> M["broadcast events"]
    end

4. Client API ىƒپى„¸

4.1 In-Process Client (Rust)

TUIى™€ exec ê°™ى‌€ ê°™ى‌€ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ى•ˆى‌ک ى†Œë¹„ى‍گë¥¼ ىœ„ي•œ facadeë‹¤. MessageProcessorى™€ bounded mpsc ى±„ë„گë،œ ى—°ê²°ëگکë©°, ë³„ëڈ„ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ê²½ê³„ê°€ ى—†ë‹¤.

// app-server-client/src/lib.rs

/// ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ىˆکى‹  يƒ€ى‍…
pub enum AppServerEvent {
    Lagged { skipped: usize },      // backpressure ى´ˆê³¼ ى‹œ
    ServerNotification(ServerNotification),
    ServerRequest(ServerRequest),
    Disconnected { message: String },
}

/// ي•µى‹¬ client facade
pub struct InProcessAppServerClient { /* ... */ }

impl InProcessAppServerClient {
    /// ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ ى‹œى‍‘ + initialize/initialized ي•¸ë“œى…°ى‌´يپ¬ ى‍گëڈ™ ىˆکي–‰
    pub async fn start(args: InProcessClientStartArgs) -> IoResult<Self>;

    /// typed JSON-RPC ىڑ”ى²­ â€” ى‌‘ë‹µى‌„ ى œë„¤ë¦­ Të،œ ë””ى½”ë”©
    pub async fn request_typed<T: DeserializeOwned>(
        &self, request: ClientRequest
    ) -> Result<T, TypedRequestError>;

    /// raw JSON-RPC ىڑ”ى²­
    pub async fn request(&self, request: ClientRequest) -> IoResult<RequestResult>;

    /// ى•Œë¦¼ ى „ى†،
    pub async fn notify(&self, notification: ClientNotification) -> IoResult<()>;

    /// ServerRequestى—گ ëŒ€ي•œ ى‌‘ë‹µ (approval ë“±)
    pub async fn resolve_server_request(
        &self, request_id: RequestId, result: JsonRpcResult
    ) -> IoResult<()>;

    /// ServerRequest ê±°ë¶€
    pub async fn reject_server_request(
        &self, request_id: RequestId, error: JSONRPCErrorError
    ) -> IoResult<()>;

    /// ë‹¤ى‌Œ ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ىˆکى‹  (backpressure-aware)
    pub async fn next_event(&mut self) -> Option<InProcessServerEvent>;

    /// graceful shutdown (5ى´ˆ يƒ€ى‍„ى•„ى›ƒ ي›„ abort)
    pub async fn shutdown(self) -> IoResult<()>;
}

backpressure ى„¤ê³„: try_sendë¥¼ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•کë©°, ى±„ë„گى‌´ ê°€ë“‌ ى°¨ë©´ WouldBlockى‌„ ë°کي™کي•œë‹¤. ë‹¨, TurnCompletedى™€ ê°™ى‌€ terminal ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ëٹ” deliveryê°€ ë³´ى‍¥ëگœë‹¤.

4.2 Remote Client (WebSocket)

WebSocket ê¸°ë°ک ى›گê²© ى ‘ى†چ يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸. InProcessAppServerClientى™€ ëڈ™ى‌¼ي•œ AppServerEvent surfaceë¥¼ ى œê³µي•کى—¬ TUI ë“±ى‌ک ىƒپىœ„ ى¸µى‌´ transportë¥¼ ى‌کى‹‌ي•کى§€ ى•ٹëڈ„ë،‌ ى„¤ê³„ëگگë‹¤.

// app-server-client/src/remote.rs

pub struct RemoteAppServerConnectArgs {
    pub websocket_url: String,
    pub client_name: String,
    pub client_version: String,
    pub experimental_api: bool,
    pub opt_out_notification_methods: Vec<String>,
    pub channel_capacity: usize,
}

pub struct RemoteAppServerClient { /* ... */ }

impl RemoteAppServerClient {
    /// WebSocket ى—°ê²° + initialize ي•¸ë“œى…°ى‌´يپ¬ (10ى´ˆ يƒ€ى‍„ى•„ى›ƒ)
    pub async fn connect(args: RemoteAppServerConnectArgs) -> IoResult<Self>;
    pub async fn request(&self, ...) -> IoResult<RequestResult>;
    pub async fn request_typed<T>(&self, ...) -> Result<T, TypedRequestError>;
    pub async fn notify(&self, ...) -> IoResult<()>;
    pub async fn next_event(&mut self) -> Option<AppServerEvent>;
    pub async fn shutdown(self) -> IoResult<()>;
}

4.3 ي†µي•© Client enum

/// transport ë¬´ê´€ ي†µي•© surface
pub enum AppServerClient {
    InProcess(InProcessAppServerClient),
    Remote(RemoteAppServerClient),
}

impl AppServerClient {
    pub async fn request(&self, ...) -> IoResult<RequestResult>;
    pub async fn request_typed<T>(&self, ...) -> Result<T, TypedRequestError>;
    pub async fn notify(&self, ...) -> IoResult<()>;
    pub async fn resolve_server_request(&self, ...) -> IoResult<()>;
    pub async fn reject_server_request(&self, ...) -> IoResult<()>;
    pub async fn next_event(&mut self) -> Option<AppServerEvent>;
    pub async fn shutdown(self) -> IoResult<()>;
}

classDiagram
    class AppServerClient {
        <<enum>>
        InProcess(InProcessAppServerClient)
        Remote(RemoteAppServerClient)
        +request()
        +request_typed~T~()
        +notify()
        +resolve_server_request()
        +reject_server_request()
        +next_event()
        +shutdown()
    }
    
    class InProcessAppServerClient {
        +start(args) IoResult~Self~
        +request_handle() Handle
    }
    
    class RemoteAppServerClient {
        +connect(args) IoResult~Self~
    }
    
    AppServerClient --> InProcessAppServerClient
    AppServerClient --> RemoteAppServerClient

5. Session ê´€ë¦¬ى™€ Thread ىƒپيƒœ ë¨¸ى‹ 

5.1 ThreadState ë‚´ë¶€ êµ¬ى،°

// app-server/src/thread_state.rs
pub struct ThreadState {
    pub pending_interrupts: Vec<...>,
    pub pending_rollbacks: Option<ConnectionRequestId>,
    pub turn_summary: TurnSummary,     // file_change, cmd ى‹¤ي–‰ ىƒپيƒœ ى¶”ى پ
    pub cancel_tx: Option<oneshot::Sender<()>>,
    pub experimental_raw_events: bool,
    pub listener_generation: u64,      // listener êµگى²´ ى‹œ increment
    listener_command_tx: Option<mpsc::UnboundedSender<ThreadListenerCommand>>,
    current_turn_history: ThreadHistoryBuilder,
    listener_thread: Option<Weak<CodexThread>>,
}

5.2 Thread ىƒ‌ëھ…ى£¼ê¸° ىƒپيƒœ ë¨¸ى‹ 

stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Created: thread/start
    Created --> Running: turn/start
    Running --> Running: turn/steer (same-turn steering)
    Running --> Interrupted: turn/interrupt
    Running --> Completed: turn/completed
    Interrupted --> Running: turn/start (new turn)
    Completed --> Running: turn/start (new turn)
    Completed --> Compacting: thread/compact/start
    Compacting --> Completed: compaction done
    Completed --> Archived: thread/archive
    Archived --> Completed: thread/unarchive
    Completed --> Forked: thread/fork
    Completed --> RolledBack: thread/rollback
    RolledBack --> Running: turn/start
    
    Created --> Resumed: thread/resume
    Resumed --> Running: turn/start

5.3 ThreadHistoryBuilder â€” rollout replay

thread resume ى‹œ ê³¼ê±° ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ë¥¼ Turn[]ىœ¼ë،œ ى‍¬êµ¬ى„±ي•کëٹ” ي•µى‹¬ ë¹Œëچ”ë‹¤.

// app-server-protocol/src/protocol/thread_history.rs
pub struct ThreadHistoryBuilder {
    turns: Vec<Turn>,
    current_turn: Option<PendingTurn>,
    next_item_index: i64,
    current_rollout_index: usize,
    next_rollout_index: usize,
}

impl ThreadHistoryBuilder {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    pub fn handle_event(&mut self, event: &EventMsg);  // ê³µىœ  reducer
    pub fn handle_rollout_item(&mut self, item: &RolloutItem);
    pub fn finish(self) -> Vec<Turn>;
    pub fn active_turn_snapshot(&self) -> Option<Turn>;
    pub fn has_active_turn(&self) -> bool;
    pub fn reset(&mut self);
}

/// rollout يŒŒى‌¼ى—گى„œ Turn[] ى‍¬êµ¬ى„±
pub fn build_turns_from_rollout_items(items: &[RolloutItem]) -> Vec<Turn>;

handle_eventëٹ” EventMsgى‌ک ëھ¨ë“  variantë¥¼ ى²کë¦¬ي•کëٹ” ê³µىœ  reducerë،œ, resume/rejoin/fork ëھ¨ë‘گى—گى„œ ê°™ى‌€ ë،œى§پى‌„ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•œë‹¤.

6. SDK Surface ë¹„êµگ

6.1 TypeScript SDK â€” CLI wrapper ë°©ى‹‌

TypeScript SDKëٹ” codex execى‌„ subprocessë،œ ى‹¤ي–‰ي•کê³ , stdout JSONL ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¼ى‌„ يŒŒى‹±ي•کëٹ” thin wrapperë‹¤. app-server protocolى‌„ ى§پى ‘ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•کى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤.

// sdk/typescript/src/codex.ts
export class Codex {
  constructor(options: CodexOptions = {});
  startThread(options?: ThreadOptions): Thread;
  resumeThread(id: string, options?: ThreadOptions): Thread;
}

// sdk/typescript/src/thread.ts
export class Thread {
  get id(): string | null;
  async run(input: Input, turnOptions?: TurnOptions): Promise<Turn>;
  async runStreamed(input: Input, turnOptions?: TurnOptions): Promise<StreamedTurn>;
}

// ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ يƒ€ى‍… (exec JSONL ê¸°ë°ک)
type ThreadEvent =
  | ThreadStartedEvent    // { type: "thread.started", thread_id }
  | TurnStartedEvent      // { type: "turn.started" }
  | TurnCompletedEvent    // { type: "turn.completed", usage }
  | TurnFailedEvent       // { type: "turn.failed", error }
  | ItemStartedEvent      // { type: "item.started", item }
  | ItemUpdatedEvent      // { type: "item.updated", item }
  | ItemCompletedEvent    // { type: "item.completed", item }
  | ThreadErrorEvent;     // { type: "error", message }

// Item يƒ€ى‍… (7ê°€ى§€ â€” app-serverى‌ک 13ê°€ى§€ë³´ë‹¤ ë‹¨ىˆœي™”)
type ThreadItem =
  | AgentMessageItem | ReasoningItem | CommandExecutionItem
  | FileChangeItem | McpToolCallItem | WebSearchItem
  | TodoListItem | ErrorItem;

ى œى•½: ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ى‌´ë¦„ى‌´ dot-case (item.completed)ى‌´ê³  app-serverى‌ک slash-case (item/completed)ى™€ ë‹¤ë¥´ë‹¤. Item يƒ€ى‍…ëڈ„ 13ê°€ى§€ ى¤‘ 7ê°€ى§€ë§Œ ë…¸ى¶œëگœë‹¤. ى‚¬ى‹¤ىƒپ app-server protocolى‌ک ى„œë¸Œى…‹ى‌„ ë³„ëڈ„ contractىœ¼ë،œ ê°گى‹¼ ê²ƒى‌´ë‹¤.

6.2 Python SDK â€” app-server wrapper ë°©ى‹‌

Python SDKëٹ” app-serverë¥¼ stdio subprocessë،œ ë‌„ىڑ°ê³  JSON-RPC ي”„ë،œي† ى½œى‌„ ى§پى ‘ êµ¬يک„ي•œë‹¤. v2 protocolى‌ک ëھ¨ë“  methodى—گ typed ë©”ى„œë“œë¥¼ ى œê³µي•œë‹¤.

# sdk/python/src/codex_app_server/client.py
class AppServerClient:
    """Synchronous typed JSON-RPC client for codex app-server over stdio."""

    def __init__(self, config=None, approval_handler=None): ...
    def start(self) -> None: ...
    def close(self) -> None: ...
    def initialize(self) -> InitializeResponse: ...

    # Thread lifecycle
    def thread_start(self, params=None) -> ThreadStartResponse: ...
    def thread_resume(self, thread_id, params=None) -> ThreadResumeResponse: ...
    def thread_list(self, params=None) -> ThreadListResponse: ...
    def thread_read(self, thread_id, include_turns=False) -> ThreadReadResponse: ...
    def thread_fork(self, thread_id, params=None) -> ThreadForkResponse: ...
    def thread_archive(self, thread_id) -> ThreadArchiveResponse: ...
    def thread_unarchive(self, thread_id) -> ThreadUnarchiveResponse: ...
    def thread_set_name(self, thread_id, name) -> ThreadSetNameResponse: ...
    def thread_compact(self, thread_id) -> ThreadCompactStartResponse: ...

    # Turn lifecycle
    def turn_start(self, thread_id, input_items, params=None) -> TurnStartResponse: ...
    def turn_interrupt(self, thread_id, turn_id) -> TurnInterruptResponse: ...
    def turn_steer(self, thread_id, expected_turn_id, input_items) -> TurnSteerResponse: ...

    # Model
    def model_list(self, include_hidden=False) -> ModelListResponse: ...

    # يژ¸ى‌ک ë©”ى„œë“œ
    def wait_for_turn_completed(self, turn_id) -> TurnCompletedNotification: ...
    def stream_until_methods(self, methods) -> list[Notification]: ...
    def stream_text(self, thread_id, text, params=None) -> Iterator[AgentMessageDeltaNotification]: ...

    # ServerRequest ى‍گëڈ™ ى²کë¦¬
    def _handle_server_request(self, msg) -> JsonObject: ...

# sdk/python/src/codex_app_server/api.py â€” ê³ ىˆکى¤€ Codex API
class Codex:
    """Minimal typed SDK surface for app-server v2."""
    def __init__(self, config=None): ...
    def thread_start(self, ...) -> ThreadStartResponse: ...
    def run(self, thread_id, input, ...) -> RunResult: ...
    def run_streamed(self, thread_id, input, ...) -> Iterator[Notification]: ...
    def close(self): ...

ServerRequest ى‍گëڈ™ ى²کë¦¬: Python SDKëٹ” _handle_server_requestى—گى„œ approval ىڑ”ى²­ى‌„ ى‍گëڈ™ىœ¼ë،œ acceptي•œë‹¤. ى‌´ê²ƒى‌€ SDK ë ˆë²¨ى‌ک opinionated defaultë‹¤.

def _default_approval_handler(self, method, params):
    if method == "item/commandExecution/requestApproval":
        return {"decision": "accept"}
    if method == "item/fileChange/requestApproval":
        return {"decision": "accept"}
    return {}

6.3 SDK ë¹„êµگ ىڑ”ى•½

graph TB
    subgraph "TypeScript SDK"
        TS_Codex["Codex"] --> TS_Thread["Thread"]
        TS_Thread --> TS_Exec["CodexExec (subprocess)"]
        TS_Exec --> TS_JSONL["stdout JSONL stream"]
        TS_JSONL --> TS_Events["ThreadEvent (dot-case)"]
    end
    
    subgraph "Python SDK"
        PY_Codex["Codex"] --> PY_Client["AppServerClient"]
        PY_Client --> PY_Proc["subprocess (stdio://)"]
        PY_Proc --> PY_JSONRPC["JSON-RPC v2 protocol"]
        PY_JSONRPC --> PY_Notif["Notification (slash-case)"]
    end
    
    subgraph "Rust Client"
        RS_Client["AppServerClient"] --> RS_InProc["InProcess (mpsc channels)"]
        RS_Client --> RS_Remote["Remote (WebSocket)"]
        RS_InProc --> RS_MP["MessageProcessor"]
        RS_Remote --> RS_MP
    end
ى°¨ى›گ TypeScript SDK Python SDK Rust Client
transport subprocess stdout JSONL subprocess stdio JSON-RPC in-process channels ëکگëٹ” WebSocket
protocol ىˆکى¤€ exec ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ (ى„œë¸Œى…‹) app-server v2 ى „ى²´ app-server v2 ى „ى²´
thread ê´€ë¦¬ ê¸°ë³¸ start/resume ى „ى²´ lifecycle ى „ى²´ lifecycle
approval ى²کë¦¬ ë¶ˆê°€ëٹ¥ (execى‌´ ى²کë¦¬) approval_handler ى½œë°± resolve/reject_server_request
ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ى‌´ë¦„ dot-case (item.completed) slash-case (item/completed) enum variant
structured output outputSchema يŒŒى‌¼ ê¸°ë°ک TurnStartParams.output_schema TurnStartParams.output_schema
ëڈ™ى‹œى„± single thread per turn turn consumer lock async mpsc

7. ë©”ى‹œى§€ ي”„ë،œى„¸ى„œ ë‚´ë¶€ êµ¬ى،°

7.1 MessageProcessor ê³„ى¸µ

graph TD
    A["TransportEvent (incoming)"] --> B["MessageProcessor"]
    B --> C["CodexMessageProcessor"]
    C --> D["ThreadManager (core)"]
    C --> E["ConfigApi"]
    C --> F["FsApi"]
    C --> G["FsWatchManager"]
    C --> H["ExternalAgentConfigApi"]
    B --> I["OutgoingMessageSender"]
    I --> J["OutboundConnectionState"]
    J --> K["transport writer"]

Axis 07: Sandbox & policy

AXIS

007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_co_sandbox.md

Overview

Axis 07: Sandbox & policy â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” sandbox and execution policy.

  • Codexى‌ک ëھ…ë ¹ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى œى–´ëٹ” ë‹¨ىˆœي‍ˆ "ي—ˆىڑ©/ê±°ë¶€"ê°€ ى•„ë‹ˆë‹¤. ى‹¤ى œ êµ¬ى،°ëٹ” ى„¸ ê²¹ى‌´ë‹¤. ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤(OS-ë ˆë²¨ ê²©ë¦¬), exec-policy(Starlark ê¸°ë°ک ëھ…ë ¹ ê·œى¹™ ى—”ى§„), approval routing(ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ىٹ¹ى‌¸ê³¼ guardian ى‍گëڈ™ يŒگى •)ى‌´ ê²¹ê²¹ى‌´ ىŒ“ى—¬ ى‍ˆë‹¤.
  • ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ يƒ€ى‍…ى‌€ ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ë³„ë،œ ë‹¤ë¥´ë‹¤. macOSëٹ” seatbelt(sandbox-exec ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼), Linuxëٹ” bubblewrap + seccomp(ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ê²©ë¦¬ + syscall ي•„ي„°ë§پ), Windowsëٹ” restricted token + ى „ىڑ© ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ + ë°©ي™”ë²½ ê·œى¹™ ê¸°ë°کى‌´ë‹¤. codex-sandboxing يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸ê°€ SandboxTypeى‌„ ê²°ى •ي•کê³ , SandboxExecRequestë،œ ë³€ي™کي•´ `c
  • exec-policyëٹ” ëچ” ي‌¥ë¯¸ë،­ë‹¤. codex-execpolicy يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸ëٹ” Starlark(Python-like DSL)ë،œ ى‍‘ى„±ëگœ .rules يŒŒى‌¼ى‌„ يŒŒى‹±ي•´ Policyë¥¼ ë§Œë“ ë‹¤. ExecPolicyManagerëٹ” config layer stackى—گى„œ rules ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ë¥¼ precedence ىˆœى„œë،œ ى‌½ê³ , ëھ…ë ¹ى‌„ Decision::Allow | Prompt | Forbiddenىœ¼ë،œ ë¶„ë¥کي•œë‹¤. ى—¬ê¸°ى—گ
  • approval routingى‌€ AskForApproval ى •ى±…ى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ ë¶„ê¸°ي•œë‹¤. Never, OnFailure, OnRequest, UnlessTrusted, Granular ë‹¤ى„¯ ê°€ى§€ê°€ ى‍ˆê³ , Granularى‌€ sandbox approvalê³¼ rules approvalى‌„ ëڈ…ë¦½ى پىœ¼ë،œ ى œى–´ي•œë‹¤. ىٹ¹ى‌¸ى‌´ ي•„ىڑ”ي•œ ê²½ىڑ° guardian ى‍گëڈ™ يŒگى •ىœ¼ë،œ ë„کى–´ê°ˆ ىˆک ى‍ˆë‹¤(â†’ 12_guardian-approval

    B -->|Forbidden| E["reject"]
    D -->|user approves| C
    D -->|guardian auto-approves| C
    C --> F["OS sandbox: seatbelt / bubblewrap+seccomp / restricted token"]
    F --> G["process execution"]

README / docs

// codex-rs/sandboxing/src/manager.rs
pub fn get_platform_sandbox(windows_sandbox_enabled: bool) -> Option<SandboxType> {
    if cfg!(target_os = "macos") {
        Some(SandboxType::MacosSeatbelt)
    } else if cfg!(target_os = "linux") {
        Some(SandboxType::LinuxSeccomp)
    } else if cfg!(target_os = "windows") {
        if windows_sandbox_enabled {
            Some(SandboxType::WindowsRestrictedToken)
        } else { None }
    } else { None }
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/sandboxing/src/policy_transforms.rs
pub fn should_require_platform_sandbox(
    file_system_policy: &FileSystemSandboxPolicy,
    network_policy: NetworkSandboxPolicy,
    has_managed_network_requirements: bool,
) -> bool {
    if has_managed_network_requirements { return true; }
    if !network_policy.is_enabled() {
        return !matches!(file_system_policy.kind, FileSystemSandboxKind::ExternalSandbox);
    }
    match file_system_policy.kind {
        FileSystemSandboxKind::Restricted => !file_system_policy.has_full_disk_write_access(),
        FileSystemSandboxKind::Unrestricted | FileSystemSandboxKind::ExternalSandbox => false,
    }
}

README / docs

    A["SandboxTransformRequest"] --> B["EffectiveSandboxPermissions ê³„ى‚°"]

    C -->|MacosSeatbelt| D["sandbox-exec -p &lt;SBPL&gt; -- cmd"]
    C -->|LinuxSeccomp| E["codex-linux-sandbox --sandbox-policy ... -- cmd"]
    C -->|WindowsRestrictedToken| F["restricted tokenىœ¼ë،œ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ىƒ‌ى„±"]
    C -->|None| G["ى›گë³¸ ëھ…ë ¹ ê·¸ëŒ€ë،œ"]

  (sysctl-name "kern.osversion")

enum NetworkSeccompMode {
    Restricted,    // ëھ¨ë“  ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ syscall ى°¨ë‹¨, AF_UNIXë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©
    ProxyRouted,   // AF_INET/INET6ë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ© (ي”„ë،‌ى‹œ ë¸Œë¦؟ى§€ىڑ©), AF_UNIX ى°¨ë‹¨
}

// ë¬´ى،°ê±´ ى°¨ë‹¨
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_ptrace);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_io_uring_setup);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_io_uring_enter);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_io_uring_register);

// ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê´€ë ¨ ى°¨ë‹¨
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_connect);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_accept);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_accept4);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_bind);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_listen);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_sendto);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_sendmmsg);
// ... shutdown, getpeername, getsockname, recvmmsg, getsockopt, setsockopt

// socket()/socketpair()ëٹ” AF_UNIXë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©
let unix_only_rule = SeccompRule::new(vec![SeccompCondition::new(
    0,                        // ى²« ë²ˆى§¸ ى‌¸ى‍گ (domain)
    SeccompCmpArgLen::Dword,
    SeccompCmpOp::Ne,
    libc::AF_UNIX as u64,    // AF_UNIXê°€ ى•„ë‹ˆë©´ ى°¨ë‹¨
)?])?;
rules.insert(libc::SYS_socket, vec![unix_only_rule]);

README / docs

let filter = SeccompFilter::new(
    rules,
    SeccompAction::Allow,                     // ê¸°ë³¸: ي—ˆىڑ©
    SeccompAction::Errno(libc::EPERM as u32), // ê·œى¹™ ë§¤ى¹ک ى‹œ: EPERM ë°کي™ک
    TargetArch::x86_64,                       // ëکگëٹ” aarch64
)?;
let prog: BpfProgram = filter.try_into()?;
apply_filter(&prog)?;

README / docs

// codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/landlock.rs
fn install_filesystem_landlock_rules_on_current_thread(
    writable_roots: Vec<AbsolutePathBuf>,
) -> Result<()> {
    let abi = ABI::V5;
    let access_rw = AccessFs::from_all(abi);
    let access_ro = AccessFs::from_read(abi);
    let mut ruleset = Ruleset::default()
        .handle_access(access_rw)?
        .create()?
        .add_rules(landlock::path_beneath_rules(&["/"], access_ro))?    // ى „ى²´ ى‌½ê¸°
        .add_rules(landlock::path_beneath_rules(&["/dev/null"], access_rw))? // /dev/null ى“°ê¸°
        .set_no_new_privs(true);
    if !writable_roots.is_empty() {
        ruleset = ruleset.add_rules(
            landlock::path_beneath_rules(&writable_roots, access_rw)    // ي—ˆىڑ© ê²½ë،œ ى“°ê¸°
        )?;
    }
    ruleset.restrict_self()?;
    Ok(())
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/setup_orchestrator.rs
pub const OFFLINE_USERNAME: &str = "CodexSandboxOffline";
pub const ONLINE_USERNAME: &str = "CodexSandboxOnline";

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/sandbox_users.rs
pub fn provision_sandbox_users(/* ... */) -> Result<()> {
    ensure_sandbox_users_group(log)?;
    let offline_password = random_password();   // 24ى‍گ ë‍œëچ¤ ë¹„ë°€ë²ˆيک¸
    let online_password = random_password();
    ensure_sandbox_user(offline_username, &offline_password, log)?;
    ensure_sandbox_user(online_username, &online_password, log)?;
    write_secrets(codex_home, /* ... */)?;       // DPAPIë،œ ى•”يک¸ي™” ى €ى‍¥
    Ok(())
}

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/token.rs
unsafe fn create_token_with_caps_from(
    base_token: HANDLE,
    psid_capabilities: &[*mut c_void],
) -> Result<HANDLE> {
    let flags = DISABLE_MAX_PRIVILEGE   // ëھ¨ë“  يٹ¹ê¶Œ ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™”
              | LUA_TOKEN               // Limited User Account
              | WRITE_RESTRICTED;       // ى“°ê¸° ى œي•œ

    CreateRestrictedToken(
        base_token, flags,
        0, std::ptr::null(),            // SID ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™” ى—†ى‌Œ
        0, std::ptr::null(),            // يٹ¹ê¶Œ ى‚­ى œ ى—†ى‌Œ
        entries.len() as u32,           // ى œي•œ SID: capability + logon + everyone
        entries.as_mut_ptr(),
        &mut new_token,
    );
    set_default_dacl(new_token, &dacl_sids)?;   // يŒŒى‌´ي”„ IPCë¥¼ ىœ„ي•œ DACL
    enable_single_privilege(new_token, "SeChangeNotifyPrivilege")?;
    Ok(new_token)
}

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/firewall.rs
const OFFLINE_BLOCK_RULE_NAME: &str = "codex_sandbox_offline_block_outbound";

fn configure_rule(rule: &INetFwRule3, /* ... */) -> Result<()> {
    rule.SetDirection(NET_FW_RULE_DIR_OUT)?;   // ى•„ى›ƒë°”ىڑ´ë“œë§Œ
    rule.SetAction(NET_FW_ACTION_BLOCK)?;      // ى°¨ë‹¨
    rule.SetProtocol(NET_FW_IP_PROTOCOL_ANY.0)?;
    rule.SetLocalUserAuthorizedList(&BSTR::from(local_user_spec))?;
    // read-back ê²€ى¦‌: SIDê°€ ىک¬ë°”ë¥´ê²Œ ى پىڑ©ëگگëٹ”ى§€ ي™•ى‌¸
}

graph TB
    subgraph macOS
        M1["sandbox-exec (seatbelt)"]
        M2["SBPL deny-default ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼"]

    end
