        L1["bubblewrap"]
        L2["ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ê²©ë¦¬<br/>user/pid/net"]
        L3["seccomp BPF ي•„ي„°"]
        L4["Landlock يڈ´ë°±"]
        L1 --> L2
        L2 --> L3
        L3 -.->|ë ˆê±°ى‹œ| L4
    end
    subgraph Windows
        W1["setup orchestrator"]
        W2["ى „ىڑ© ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ىƒ‌ى„±"]
        W3["CreateRestrictedToken"]
        W4["ACL ê¸°ë°ک يŒŒى‌¼ ى ‘ê·¼"]
        W5["ë°©ي™”ë²½ ى•„ى›ƒë°”ىڑ´ë“œ ى°¨ë‹¨"]
        W1 --> W2 --> W3
        W3 --> W4
        W3 --> W5
    end

Main analysis

Codex sandbox and execution policy

Analysis snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” sandbox and execution policy.

Codex's command execution control is not simply "allow/deny". The actual structure is three layers. Sandbox (OS-level isolation), exec-policy (Starlark-based command rule engine), and approval routing (user approval and automatic guardian determination) are layered.

Sandbox types vary by platform. macOS is seatbelt (sandbox-exec profile), Linux is bubblewrap + seccomp (namespace isolation + syscall filtering), and Windows is restricted token + dedicated user + firewall rule-based. The codex-sandboxing crate determines the SandboxType, converts it to SandboxExecRequest, and passes it to ExecRequest in core/src/sandboxing/mod.rs. In this process, network policy (NetworkSandboxPolicy) and file system policy (FileSystemSandboxPolicy) are applied together.

exec-policy is more interesting. The codex-execpolicy crate creates a Policy by parsing a .rules file written in Starlark (Python-like DSL). ExecPolicyManager reads the rules directory from the config layer stack in precedence order and executes the command Decision::Allow | Prompt | Classified as â€کForbiddenâ€™. Here, is_known_safe_command()(read-only command whitelist) andcommand_might_be_dangerous()` (dangerous command heuristic) act as fallbacks.

Approval routing branches according to the AskForApproval policy. There are five types: Never, OnFailure, OnRequest, UnlessTrusted, and Granular. Granular independently controls sandbox approval and rules approval. If approval is required, it can proceed to automatic guardian determination (â†’ see 12_guardian-approval).

graph TD
    A["model tool call"] --> B["exec-policy rules check"]
    B -->|Allow| C["sandbox wrapping"]
    B -->|Prompt| D["approval routing"]
    B -->|Forbidden| E["reject"]
    D -->|user approves| C
    D -->|guardian auto-approves| C
    C --> F["OS sandbox: seatbelt / bubblewrap+seccomp / restricted token"]
    F --> G["process execution"]

1. Sandbox creation: SandboxManager pipeline

The key entry point to the sandbox is the SandboxManager in the codex-sandboxing crate. It operates in two steps.

1.1 Platform detection â€” get_platform_sandbox()

The compile-time cfg! macro detects the OS and returns the sandbox type for that platform:

// codex-rs/sandboxing/src/manager.rs
pub fn get_platform_sandbox(windows_sandbox_enabled: bool) -> Option<SandboxType> {
    if cfg!(target_os = "macos") {
        Some(SandboxType::MacosSeatbelt)
    } else if cfg!(target_os = "linux") {
        Some(SandboxType::LinuxSeccomp)
    } else if cfg!(target_os = "windows") {
        if windows_sandbox_enabled {
            Some(SandboxType::WindowsRestrictedToken)
        } else { None }
    } else { None }
}

1.2 Select sandbox type â€” select_initial()

Determines whether to activate the sandbox according to SandboxablePreference (Auto/Require/Forbid). In Auto mode, should_require_platform_sandbox() checks filesystem and network policies and activates the platform sandbox only if restrictions are needed:

// codex-rs/sandboxing/src/policy_transforms.rs
pub fn should_require_platform_sandbox(
    file_system_policy: &FileSystemSandboxPolicy,
    network_policy: NetworkSandboxPolicy,
    has_managed_network_requirements: bool,
) -> bool {
    if has_managed_network_requirements { return true; }
    if !network_policy.is_enabled() {
        return !matches!(file_system_policy.kind, FileSystemSandboxKind::ExternalSandbox);
    }
    match file_system_policy.kind {
        FileSystemSandboxKind::Restricted => !file_system_policy.has_full_disk_write_access(),
        FileSystemSandboxKind::Unrestricted | FileSystemSandboxKind::ExternalSandbox => false,
    }
}

1.3 Transform â€” SandboxManager::transform()

Receives SandboxTransformRequest and creates a final SandboxExecRequest. In this process, additional permissions (the tool's PermissionProfile) are merged with the existing policy and platform-specific wrapping instructions are created:

graph LR
    A["SandboxTransformRequest"] --> B["EffectiveSandboxPermissions ê³„ى‚°"]
    B --> C{"SandboxType?"}
    C -->|MacosSeatbelt| D["sandbox-exec -p &lt;SBPL&gt; -- cmd"]
    C -->|LinuxSeccomp| E["codex-linux-sandbox --sandbox-policy ... -- cmd"]
    C -->|WindowsRestrictedToken| F["restricted tokenىœ¼ë،œ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ىƒ‌ى„±"]
    C -->|None| G["ى›گë³¸ ëھ…ë ¹ ê·¸ëŒ€ë،œ"]
    D --> H["SandboxExecRequest"]
    E --> H
    F --> H
    G --> H

2. Sandbox implementation for each platform

2.1 macOS â€” Seatbelt (sandbox-exec)

On macOS, Apple's Seatbelt profile is applied through /usr/bin/sandbox-exec. Prevent PATH injection attacks by hardcoding the path:

// codex-rs/sandboxing/src/seatbelt.rs
pub const MACOS_PATH_TO_SEATBELT_EXECUTABLE: &str = "/usr/bin/sandbox-exec";

2.1.1 SBPL profile structure

Three .sbpl files are included at compile time with include_str!:

file Role
seatbelt_base_policy.sbpl Based on (deny default) â€” Allows minimal process forking, sysctl reading, PTY, IOKit, etc.
seatbelt_network_policy.sbpl Allow additional TLS certificate lookup, DNS settings reading, etc. when network is active
restricted_read_only_platform_defaults.sbpl platform default read path

The core of the default policy (inspired by Chrome sandbox policies):

; seatbelt_base_policy.sbpl â€” ë°œى·Œ
(version 1)
(deny default)                         ; ëھ¨ë“  ê²ƒى‌„ ê¸°ë³¸ ى°¨ë‹¨
(allow process-exec)                   ; ى‍گى‹‌ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ى‹¤ي–‰ ي—ˆىڑ©
(allow process-fork)                   ; fork ي—ˆىڑ©
(allow signal (target same-sandbox))   ; ê°™ى‌€ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ ë‚´ ى‹œê·¸ë„گë§Œ
(allow sysctl-read                     ; CPU ى •ë³´, ى»¤ë„گ ë²„ى „ ë“± ى œي•œى پ sysctl
  (sysctl-name "hw.ncpu")
  (sysctl-name "kern.osversion")
  ;; ... ى•½ 40ê°œ ي•­ëھ©
)
(allow pseudo-tty)                     ; PTY ي• ë‹¹ ي—ˆىڑ©

2.1.2 Dynamic file system policy

create_seatbelt_command_args_for_policies_with_extensions() combines SBPL policies at runtime. Core logic:

  • Read policy: Parameterize the allowed path in the form (allow file-read* (subpath (param "READABLE_ROOT_0")))
  • Write policy: (allow file-write* (regex #"^/")) when allowing full writing, WRITABLE_ROOT_N parameter when restricting
  • unreadable path exclusion: Exclude sensitive directories from writable root with (require-not (subpath ...)) combination.
// ى •ى±… ى،°ي•© ىˆœى„œ â€” seatbelt.rs ë°œى·Œ
let mut policy_sections = vec![
    MACOS_SEATBELT_BASE_POLICY.to_string(),   // deny default ê¸°ë°ک
    file_read_policy,                          // ى‌½ê¸° ي—ˆىڑ© ê²½ë،œ
    file_write_policy,                         // ى“°ê¸° ي—ˆىڑ© ê²½ë،œ
    network_policy,                            // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى •ى±…
];
if include_platform_defaults {
    policy_sections.push(MACOS_RESTRICTED_READ_ONLY_PLATFORM_DEFAULTS.to_string());
}

Final command: sandbox-exec -p <full_policy> -DREADABLE_ROOT_0=/path -DWRITABLE_ROOT_0=/path -- <command>

2.1.3 Network Policy

Network policy branches into three modes:

Conditions Action
Has proxy environment variable Allow loopback only for proxy ports + network policy
Network active, no proxy (allow network-outbound) + (allow network-inbound)
network inactive No network rules (default deny)

Unix domain sockets are controlled with AllowAll or a per-path allowlist:

; ê²½ë،œë³„ allowlist ىکˆى‹œ â€” ëڈ™ى پ ىƒ‌ى„±
(allow system-socket (socket-domain AF_UNIX))
(allow network-bind (local unix-socket (subpath (param "UNIX_SOCKET_PATH_0"))))
(allow network-outbound (remote unix-socket (subpath (param "UNIX_SOCKET_PATH_0"))))

2.2 Linux â€” Bubblewrap + Seccomp

The Linux sandbox overlaps two layers: bubblewrap (filesystem namespace isolation) and seccomp (syscall filtering). Landlock remains a legacy fallback.

graph TD
    A["codex-linux-sandbox ى§„ى‍…"] --> B{"--use-legacy-landlock?"}
    B -->|Yes| C["Landlock + seccomp ى§پى ‘ ى پىڑ©"]
    B -->|No| D["bubblewrap ë‍کي•‘"]
    D --> E["bwrap --new-session --die-with-parent ..."]
    E --> F["ë‚´ë¶€: --apply-seccomp-then-exec"]
    F --> G["PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS + seccomp ي•„ي„° ى„¤ى¹ک"]
    G --> H["execvp(command)"]
    C --> G

2.2.1 Bubblewrap file system isolation

create_bwrap_command_args() determines the filesystem mount structure:

// codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/bwrap.rs â€” ë§ˆىڑ´يٹ¸ ىˆœى„œ
// 1. ى „ى²´ ى‌½ê¸°: --ro-bind / /
// 2. ى œي•œ ى‌½ê¸°: --tmpfs / ë،œ ى‹œى‍‘, ê°œë³„ --ro-bind ى¶”ê°€
// 3. --dev /dev (ىµœى†Œ ë””ë°”ى‌´ىٹ¤ ë…¸ë“œ: null, zero, urandom ë“±)
// 4. --bind <root> <root> ë،œ ى“°ê¸° ê°€ëٹ¥ ê²½ë،œ ى‍¬ë§ˆىڑ´يٹ¸
// 5. --ro-bind <subpath> <subpath> ë،œ ë³´يک¸ ي•کىœ„ ê²½ë،œ ë³µى›گ

Namespace isolation flags:

// ê¸°ë³¸ bubblewrap ي”Œë‍کê·¸ â€” bwrap.rs
vec![
    "--new-session",         // ىƒˆ ى„¸ى…ک ë¦¬ëچ”
    "--die-with-parent",     // ë¶€ëھ¨ ى¢…ë£Œ ى‹œ ي•¨ê»ک ى¢…ë£Œ
    "--unshare-user",        // ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ë¶„ë¦¬
    "--unshare-pid",         // PID ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ë¶„ë¦¬
    "--unshare-net",         // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى°¨ë‹¨ ى‹œى—گë§Œ ى¶”ê°€
    "--proc", "/proc",       // ىƒˆ /proc ë§ˆىڑ´يٹ¸
]

Platform default read path (automatically added in restricted mode):

const LINUX_PLATFORM_DEFAULT_READ_ROOTS: &[&str] = &[
    "/bin", "/sbin", "/usr", "/etc", "/lib", "/lib64",
    "/nix/store", "/run/current-system/sw",
];

The Bubblewrap executor will try the system /usr/bin/bwrap first, and fall back to the vendored bwrap if it is an older version that does not support --argv0:

// codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/launcher.rs
fn preferred_bwrap_launcher_for_path(system_bwrap_path: &Path) -> BubblewrapLauncher {
    if !system_bwrap_supports_argv0(system_bwrap_path) {
        return BubblewrapLauncher::Vendored;
    }
    BubblewrapLauncher::System(/* ... */)
}

2.2.2 Seccomp syscall filtering

If network blocking is necessary, the seccomp BPF filter is installed. There are two modes:

// codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/landlock.rs
enum NetworkSeccompMode {
    Restricted,    // ëھ¨ë“  ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ syscall ى°¨ë‹¨, AF_UNIXë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©
    ProxyRouted,   // AF_INET/INET6ë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ© (ي”„ë،‌ى‹œ ë¸Œë¦؟ى§€ىڑ©), AF_UNIX ى°¨ë‹¨
}

Restricted mode â€” List of syscalls to block:

// ë¬´ى،°ê±´ ى°¨ë‹¨
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_ptrace);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_io_uring_setup);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_io_uring_enter);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_io_uring_register);

// ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê´€ë ¨ ى°¨ë‹¨
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_connect);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_accept);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_accept4);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_bind);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_listen);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_sendto);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_sendmmsg);
// ... shutdown, getpeername, getsockname, recvmmsg, getsockopt, setsockopt

// socket()/socketpair()ëٹ” AF_UNIXë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©
let unix_only_rule = SeccompRule::new(vec![SeccompCondition::new(
    0,                        // ى²« ë²ˆى§¸ ى‌¸ى‍گ (domain)
    SeccompCmpArgLen::Dword,
    SeccompCmpOp::Ne,
    libc::AF_UNIX as u64,    // AF_UNIXê°€ ى•„ë‹ˆë©´ ى°¨ë‹¨
)?])?;
rules.insert(libc::SYS_socket, vec![unix_only_rule]);

Create a seccomp filter:

let filter = SeccompFilter::new(
    rules,
    SeccompAction::Allow,                     // ê¸°ë³¸: ي—ˆىڑ©
    SeccompAction::Errno(libc::EPERM as u32), // ê·œى¹™ ë§¤ى¹ک ى‹œ: EPERM ë°کي™ک
    TargetArch::x86_64,                       // ëکگëٹ” aarch64
)?;
let prog: BpfProgram = filter.try_into()?;
apply_filter(&prog)?;

2.2.3 Landlock legacy fallback

Landlock ABI V5 based file system limitations. Currently bubblewrap is the default, but can be enabled with the --use-legacy-landlock flag:

// codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/landlock.rs
fn install_filesystem_landlock_rules_on_current_thread(
    writable_roots: Vec<AbsolutePathBuf>,
) -> Result<()> {
    let abi = ABI::V5;
    let access_rw = AccessFs::from_all(abi);
    let access_ro = AccessFs::from_read(abi);
    let mut ruleset = Ruleset::default()
        .handle_access(access_rw)?
        .create()?
        .add_rules(landlock::path_beneath_rules(&["/"], access_ro))?    // ى „ى²´ ى‌½ê¸°
        .add_rules(landlock::path_beneath_rules(&["/dev/null"], access_rw))? // /dev/null ى“°ê¸°
        .set_no_new_privs(true);
    if !writable_roots.is_empty() {
        ruleset = ruleset.add_rules(
            landlock::path_beneath_rules(&writable_roots, access_rw)    // ي—ˆىڑ© ê²½ë،œ ى“°ê¸°
        )?;
    }
    ruleset.restrict_self()?;
    Ok(())
}

2.3 Windows â€” Restricted Token + Firewall

Windows Sandbox is the most complex. Create a dedicated local user, isolate the file system with restricted tokens and ACLs, and block the network with Windows Firewall rules.

graph TD
    A["setup_orchestrator ى‹¤ي–‰"] --> B["ى „ىڑ© ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ىƒ‌ى„±"]
    B --> B1["CodexSandboxOffline â€” ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى°¨ë‹¨"]
    B --> B2["CodexSandboxOnline â€” ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ي—ˆىڑ©"]
    B1 --> C["ë°©ي™”ë²½ ê·œى¹™ ى„¤ى¹ک"]
    B2 --> C
    C --> D["ACL ى„¤ى • â€” ى‌½ê¸°/ى“°ê¸° ê²½ë،œ ى œى–´"]
    D --> E["CreateRestrictedToken"]
    E --> F["restricted tokenىœ¼ë،œ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ى‹¤ي–‰"]

2.3.1 Dedicated user provisioning

Create two local users and add them to the CodexSandboxUsers group:

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/setup_orchestrator.rs
pub const OFFLINE_USERNAME: &str = "CodexSandboxOffline";
pub const ONLINE_USERNAME: &str = "CodexSandboxOnline";

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/sandbox_users.rs
pub fn provision_sandbox_users(/* ... */) -> Result<()> {
    ensure_sandbox_users_group(log)?;
    let offline_password = random_password();   // 24ى‍گ ë‍œëچ¤ ë¹„ë°€ë²ˆيک¸
    let online_password = random_password();
    ensure_sandbox_user(offline_username, &offline_password, log)?;
    ensure_sandbox_user(online_username, &online_password, log)?;
    write_secrets(codex_home, /* ... */)?;       // DPAPIë،œ ى•”يک¸ي™” ى €ى‍¥
    Ok(())
}

Automatically exclude sensitive directories (when reading user profile):

const USERPROFILE_READ_ROOT_EXCLUSIONS: &[&str] = &[
    ".ssh", ".gnupg", ".aws", ".azure", ".kube",
    ".docker", ".config", ".npm", ".pki", ".terraform.d",
];

2.3.2 Restricted Token Generation

Create a least-privilege token with the CreateRestrictedToken Win32 API:

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/token.rs
unsafe fn create_token_with_caps_from(
    base_token: HANDLE,
    psid_capabilities: &[*mut c_void],
) -> Result<HANDLE> {
    let flags = DISABLE_MAX_PRIVILEGE   // ëھ¨ë“  يٹ¹ê¶Œ ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™”
              | LUA_TOKEN               // Limited User Account
              | WRITE_RESTRICTED;       // ى“°ê¸° ى œي•œ

    CreateRestrictedToken(
        base_token, flags,
        0, std::ptr::null(),            // SID ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™” ى—†ى‌Œ
        0, std::ptr::null(),            // يٹ¹ê¶Œ ى‚­ى œ ى—†ى‌Œ
        entries.len() as u32,           // ى œي•œ SID: capability + logon + everyone
        entries.as_mut_ptr(),
        &mut new_token,
    );
    set_default_dacl(new_token, &dacl_sids)?;   // يŒŒى‌´ي”„ IPCë¥¼ ىœ„ي•œ DACL
    enable_single_privilege(new_token, "SeChangeNotifyPrivilege")?;
    Ok(new_token)
}

2.3.3 Firewall Rules

Block outbound traffic from offline users using the Windows Firewall COM API:

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/firewall.rs
const OFFLINE_BLOCK_RULE_NAME: &str = "codex_sandbox_offline_block_outbound";

fn configure_rule(rule: &INetFwRule3, /* ... */) -> Result<()> {
    rule.SetDirection(NET_FW_RULE_DIR_OUT)?;   // ى•„ى›ƒë°”ىڑ´ë“œë§Œ
    rule.SetAction(NET_FW_ACTION_BLOCK)?;      // ى°¨ë‹¨
    rule.SetProtocol(NET_FW_IP_PROTOCOL_ANY.0)?;
    rule.SetLocalUserAuthorizedList(&BSTR::from(local_user_spec))?;
    // read-back ê²€ى¦‌: SIDê°€ ىک¬ë°”ë¥´ê²Œ ى پىڑ©ëگگëٹ”ى§€ ي™•ى‌¸
}

3. Policy transformation pipeline

EffectiveSandboxPermissions in policy_transforms.rs merges the base policy with tool-specific additional permissions:

graph LR
    A["SandboxPolicy<br/>(configى—گى„œ)"] --> C["EffectiveSandboxPermissions"]
    B["PermissionProfile<br/>(tool ىڑ”ى²­)"] --> C
    C --> D["effective_file_system_policy"]
    C --> E["effective_network_policy"]
    D --> F["ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ë³„ ë‍کي•‘"]
    E --> F

Merge rules:

  • File System: Add the read/write path requested by the tool to the existing policy (only in Restricted mode)
  • Network: Promote to NetworkSandboxPolicy::Enabled if the tool requests a network, otherwise demote it to Restricted.
  • macOS extension: intersection/merge seatbelt profile extension

4. Technical Reference

Sandbox type

Type OS isolation mechanism core files
MacosSeatbelt macOS sandbox-exec SBPL profile codex-rs/sandboxing/src/seatbelt.rs
LinuxSeccomp Linux bubblewrap (namespace) + seccomp (BPF) codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/
WindowsRestrictedToken Windows restricted token + ACL + firewall codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/
None any Run without sandbox â€”

exec-policy engine

Components Role file
Policy prefix/network rule set written in Starlark codex-rs/execpolicy/
ExecPolicyManager Policy loading, evaluation, runtime modification codex-rs/core/src/exec_policy.rs
BANNED_PREFIX_SUGGESTIONS List of prefixes too wide for automatic rule creation codex-rs/core/src/exec_policy.rs
is_known_safe_command read-only safety command whitelist codex-rs/core/src/is_safe_command.rs
command_might_be_dangerous Risk Command Heuristic codex-rs/core/src/is_dangerous_command.rs

approval branch

policy Meaning
Never No approval UI â†’ Allowed when sandbox is disabled, otherwise prohibited
OnFailure Run in sandbox, approve on failure
OnRequest If it is a restricted sandbox, only escalation is approved; if it is unrestricted, it is allowed
UnlessTrusted Allow if is_known_safe_command is true, accept otherwise
Granular Independent control of sandbox_approval and rules

5. Security comparison by platform

graph TB
    subgraph macOS
        M1["sandbox-exec (seatbelt)"]
        M2["SBPL deny-default ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼"]
        M3["يŒŒë‌¼ë¯¸ي„°ي™”ëگœ ê²½ë،œ ى •ى±…"]
        M1 --> M2 --> M3
    end
    subgraph Linux
        L1["bubblewrap"]
        L2["ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ê²©ë¦¬<br/>user/pid/net"]
        L3["seccomp BPF ي•„ي„°"]
        L4["Landlock يڈ´ë°±"]
        L1 --> L2
        L2 --> L3
        L3 -.->|ë ˆê±°ى‹œ| L4
    end
    subgraph Windows
        W1["setup orchestrator"]
        W2["ى „ىڑ© ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ىƒ‌ى„±"]
        W3["CreateRestrictedToken"]
        W4["ACL ê¸°ë°ک يŒŒى‌¼ ى ‘ê·¼"]
        W5["ë°©ي™”ë²½ ى•„ى›ƒë°”ىڑ´ë“œ ى°¨ë‹¨"]
        W1 --> W2 --> W3
        W3 --> W4
        W3 --> W5
    end
side macOS Linux Windows
File system isolation SBPL file-read*/file-write* rules bubblewrap bind mount (or Landlock) ACL + restricted token DACL
network isolation SBPL network-outbound deny seccomp connect/bind/listen block + --unshare-net Windows Firewall outbound blocking rules
Process Isolation same-sandbox signal restriction Separate PID namespace Separate user sessions
Prevent privilege escalation sandbox-exec limits itself PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS DISABLE_MAX_PRIVILEGE | LUA_TOKEN
proxy support Allow loopback per port + unix socket ProxyRouted seccomp mode + network bridge online/offline user separation

6. Fork perspective

  • The exec-policy engine is already separated into an independent crate (codex-execpolicy), making it easy to reuse.
  • is_known_safe_command and command_might_be_dangerous are worth taking
  • The sandbox type determination logic is isolated in the codex-sandboxing crate.
  • Routing approval is combined with guardian and must be viewed together.
  • Linux sandbox has a dual structure of bubblewrap â†’ seccomp, and each can be independently reused.
  • Windows Sandbox tightly combines three axes: user provisioning + token + firewall.
  • You need to decide whether to use the Starlark DSL as is or change to a simpler rule format.
  • Need to review the distribution trade-off of bubblewrap vendoring strategy (system bwrap vs. bundle bwrap)

7. Core file list

crate file Role
codex-sandboxing src/manager.rs SandboxManager, SandboxType, get_platform_sandbox()
src/seatbelt.rs Create macOS SBPL policy, proxy/socket policy
src/landlock.rs Create Linux sandbox CLI arguments
src/policy_transforms.rs Merge policies, EffectiveSandboxPermissions
src/seatbelt_base_policy.sbpl Seatbelt deny-default default policy
src/seatbelt_network_policy.sbpl Additional policies when network active
codex-linux-sandbox src/linux_run_main.rs Linux sandbox entry point, CLI parsing
src/bwrap.rs Bubblewrap Mount Configuration
src/landlock.rs Apply seccomp + Landlock
src/launcher.rs bwrap launcher (system vs vendored)
src/proxy_routing.rs Proxy bridge within a network namespace
codex-windows-sandbox-rs src/token.rs CreateRestrictedToken wrapper
src/firewall.rs Windows Firewall Rule Management
src/sandbox_users.rs Create/Manage Dedicated User
src/setup_orchestrator.rs Setup Orchestrator
src/policy.rs policy parsing
src/acl.rs ACL manipulation
codex-execpolicy src/ Starlark based exec-policy engine
codex-core src/exec_policy.rs ExecPolicyManager
src/is_safe_command.rs Safety Command Whitelist
src/is_dangerous_command.rs Risk Command Heuristic

Previous article: [[06_co_surface]]
Next document: [[08_co_config]]

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release â€” re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

Change history

  • 2026-03-26: Sandbox type, exec-policy engine, and approval routing were organized into a triple structure.
  • 2026-07-14: Added in-depth analysis based on source code â€” macOS seatbelt SBPL creation logic, Linux bubblewrap+seccomp dual layer (syscall filter details), Windows restricted token+firewall+dedicated user provisioning, policy transformation pipeline, security comparison table by platform, 5 Mermaid diagrams, and 20 code snippets added.

Latest update (2026-05-30)

It reflects key changes related to the sandbox in the most recent 412 commits (2026-05-15 ~ 2026-05-29).

Windows Sandbox major refactoring: PermissionProfile-centric shift

The Windows Sandbox execution path has been completely converted from the legacy SandboxPolicy base to the PermissionProfile + ResolvedWindowsSandboxPermissions system. It is a gradual migration in seven stages.

  1. Introduction of ResolvedWindowsSandboxPermissions (windows-sandbox-rs/src/resolved_permissions.rs, #22896): A new structure was created to interpret PermissionProfile for Windows Runtime. try_from_permission_profile() accepts only Managed profiles and includes the FileSystemSandboxKind::Restricted validation. `WindowsSandboxTokenMode::ReadOnlyCapability | Determination of WritableRootsCapability is also handled in this layer.
  2. Write Root Switch (#22923): ACL-based write path creation has been changed to consume ResolvedWindowsSandboxPermissions::writable_roots_for_cwd() directly. The deny glob rule that was missing in the legacy SandboxPolicy projection is reflected.
  3. Setup Switch (#23167): Gathering read/write roots in elevated setup has been changed to gather_read_roots() / gather_write_roots_for_permissions(), which derive directly from resolved permissions.
  4. elevated runner IPC (#22918, #23715): SpawnRequest has been updated to IPC protocol v2 to pass permission_profile + permission_profile_cwd fields.
  5. Remove SandboxPolicy runner plumbing (#23813): Delete windows-sandbox-rs/src/policy.rs. The restricted-token capture, session setup, world-writable audit, read-root grant, and command-runner session APIs all directly accept PermissionProfile. Complete removal of legacy policy_json_or_preset field from IPC protocol v2. 22 files, net -251 lines.
  6. Propagate workspace roots (#24108): Thread workspace_roots: Vec<AbsolutePathBuf> instead of a single permission_profile_cwd. The :workspace_roots symbolic item is materialized for all runtime roots with materialize_project_roots_with_workspace_roots(). Bump to IPC protocol v3. The windows_sandbox_workspace_roots explicit argument has been added to ExecRequest::new() to prevent implicit fallback.
  7. Capture cancellation fix (#24974): Fixed an issue where test root settings were broken after switching workspace roots.

Introduction of WFP Network Isolation

In addition to firewall rules based on the Windows Firewall COM API (INetFwRule3), a Windows Filtering Platform (WFP) kernel filter was added (#20101).

  • windows-sandbox-rs/src/wfp.rs: FwpmEngineOpen0 â†’ Install persistent provider/sublayer/filter within the transaction.
  • windows-sandbox-rs/src/wfp/filter_specs.rs: Define filter specifications declaratively. Register WFP filters such as ICMP connect/assign (v4/v6) and DNS port 53 (v4/v6) as FWPM_FILTER_FLAG_PERSISTENT.
  • windows-sandbox-rs/src/wfp_setup.rs: Calls install_wfp_filters_for_account() in the elevated setup helper, and issues non-fatal logging + OTel metrics (codex.windows_sandbox.wfp_setup_*) upon failure.
  • Firewall policy validation (#22353): Checks INetFwPolicy2::LocalPolicyModifyState and fails elevated setup if the local firewall policy is ignored on the managed host.

Guardian Circuit Breaker InterruptTurn Action

GuardianRejectionCircuitBreaker (core/src/guardian/mod.rs) triggers a turn interruption. It was a structure introduced outside the previous 412 scope, but within this scope, Guardian prompt cache key stabilization (#24891~#24895), review metrics (#24897), runtime permission profile update (#22931), and auto-review permission profile override modification (#23956) were applied.

Core flow: When calling record_denial(), returns InterruptTurn if the threshold of 3 consecutive rejections (MAX_CONSECUTIVE_GUARDIAN_DENIALS_PER_TURN) or 10 rejections out of the last 50 reviews (MAX_RECENT_AUTO_REVIEW_DENIALS_PER_TURN) is exceeded. record_guardian_denial() in review.rs receives this, issues a GuardianWarning event, and aborts the turn with abort_turn_if_active(turn_id, TurnAbortReason::Interrupted).

deny-read sandboxing preservation

Fixed a bug where the access = none(deny) read restriction in the permission profile was missing from the safe command bypass (#23943). Even commands allowed with is_known_safe_command() (cat, ls, etc.) remain sandboxed if the deny-read item (**/*.env, etc.) is enabled. The same logic applies to the execpolicy allow rule. A modification to the deny-read glob interpretation of cloud requirements (#23729) has also been included.

Sandbox cancellation when Windows network is rejected

If the Guardian or network proxy denies network access, execution is canceled through ExecExpiration, but the Windows Sandbox capture path was propagating only the timeout component (#19880). With this fix, network rejection will also result in an immediate shutdown.

Other sandbox-related changes

  • Windows deny-read parity (#18202): The access = none read restriction supported only on macOS/Linux also applies to Windows. However, the unelevated restricted-token backend cannot force read denial, so it is processed as fail-closed.
  • Specify write-root capability SID range (#21479): Limit the capability SID of legacy/elevated tokens to only the effective write root.
  • Linux sandbox shell cleanup preserved (#22729): Fix shell cleanup script to run on interrupt.
  • bundled bwrap installation context (#23634): Interpret the vendored bwrap binary path in the installation context.
  • Unix socket deny configuration (#24970): Correctly apply the deny rule to Unix socket permissions.
  • Windows sandbox log improvements: rolling file rotation (#24117) + attach log to feedback report (#24623).
  • Windows sandbox firewall validation (#22353): Handle setup failure when firewall policy is invalid on managed hosts.

Add/delete core files

file status Content
windows-sandbox-rs/src/policy.rs Delete Remove legacy SandboxPolicy parser/export
windows-sandbox-rs/src/resolved_permissions.rs New establishment/expansion ResolvedWindowsSandboxPermissions, resolve workspace roots
windows-sandbox-rs/src/wfp.rs New WFP engine, provider/sublayer/filter installation
windows-sandbox-rs/src/wfp/filter_specs.rs New Declarative WFP Filter Specification (ICMP, DNS 53)
windows-sandbox-rs/src/wfp_setup.rs New Install WFP + OTel metrics in elevated setup
windows-sandbox-rs/src/identity.rs Expand ResolvedWindowsSandboxPermissions based setup
core/src/guardian/review.rs change Guardian review metrics, prompt cache key stabilization

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_co_sandbox.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Codex ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ى™€ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى •ى±…

ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” sandbox and execution policy.

Codexى‌ک ëھ…ë ¹ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى œى–´ëٹ” ë‹¨ىˆœي‍ˆ "ي—ˆىڑ©/ê±°ë¶€"ê°€ ى•„ë‹ˆë‹¤. ى‹¤ى œ êµ¬ى،°ëٹ” ى„¸ ê²¹ى‌´ë‹¤. ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤(OS-ë ˆë²¨ ê²©ë¦¬), exec-policy(Starlark ê¸°ë°ک ëھ…ë ¹ ê·œى¹™ ى—”ى§„), approval routing(ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ىٹ¹ى‌¸ê³¼ guardian ى‍گëڈ™ يŒگى •)ى‌´ ê²¹ê²¹ى‌´ ىŒ“ى—¬ ى‍ˆë‹¤.

ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ يƒ€ى‍…ى‌€ ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ë³„ë،œ ë‹¤ë¥´ë‹¤. macOSëٹ” seatbelt(sandbox-exec ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼), Linuxëٹ” bubblewrap + seccomp(ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ê²©ë¦¬ + syscall ي•„ي„°ë§پ), Windowsëٹ” restricted token + ى „ىڑ© ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ + ë°©ي™”ë²½ ê·œى¹™ ê¸°ë°کى‌´ë‹¤. codex-sandboxing يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸ê°€ SandboxTypeى‌„ ê²°ى •ي•کê³ , SandboxExecRequestë،œ ë³€ي™کي•´ core/src/sandboxing/mod.rsى‌ک ExecRequestë،œ ë„کê¸´ë‹¤. ى‌´ ê³¼ى •ى—گى„œ ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى •ى±…(NetworkSandboxPolicy)ê³¼ يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ى •ى±…(FileSystemSandboxPolicy)ى‌´ ي•¨ê»ک ى پىڑ©ëگœë‹¤.

exec-policyëٹ” ëچ” ي‌¥ë¯¸ë،­ë‹¤. codex-execpolicy يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸ëٹ” Starlark(Python-like DSL)ë،œ ى‍‘ى„±ëگœ .rules يŒŒى‌¼ى‌„ يŒŒى‹±ي•´ Policyë¥¼ ë§Œë“ ë‹¤. ExecPolicyManagerëٹ” config layer stackى—گى„œ rules ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ë¥¼ precedence ىˆœى„œë،œ ى‌½ê³ , ëھ…ë ¹ى‌„ Decision::Allow | Prompt | Forbiddenىœ¼ë،œ ë¶„ë¥کي•œë‹¤. ى—¬ê¸°ى—گ is_known_safe_command()(ى‌½ê¸° ى „ىڑ© ëھ…ë ¹ ي™”ى‌´يٹ¸ë¦¬ىٹ¤يٹ¸)ى™€ command_might_be_dangerous()(ىœ„ي—ک ëھ…ë ¹ يœ´ë¦¬ىٹ¤ي‹±)ê°€ fallbackىœ¼ë،œ ى‍‘ëڈ™ي•œë‹¤.

approval routingى‌€ AskForApproval ى •ى±…ى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ ë¶„ê¸°ي•œë‹¤. Never, OnFailure, OnRequest, UnlessTrusted, Granular ë‹¤ى„¯ ê°€ى§€ê°€ ى‍ˆê³ , Granularى‌€ sandbox approvalê³¼ rules approvalى‌„ ëڈ…ë¦½ى پىœ¼ë،œ ى œى–´ي•œë‹¤. ىٹ¹ى‌¸ى‌´ ي•„ىڑ”ي•œ ê²½ىڑ° guardian ى‍گëڈ™ يŒگى •ىœ¼ë،œ ë„کى–´ê°ˆ ىˆک ى‍ˆë‹¤(â†’ 12_guardian-approval ى°¸ى،°).

graph TD
    A["model tool call"] --> B["exec-policy rules check"]
    B -->|Allow| C["sandbox wrapping"]
    B -->|Prompt| D["approval routing"]
    B -->|Forbidden| E["reject"]
    D -->|user approves| C
    D -->|guardian auto-approves| C
    C --> F["OS sandbox: seatbelt / bubblewrap+seccomp / restricted token"]
    F --> G["process execution"]

1. ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ ىƒ‌ى„±: SandboxManager يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸

ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ى‌ک ي•µى‹¬ ى§„ى‍…ى گى‌€ codex-sandboxing يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸ى‌ک SandboxManagerë‹¤. ë‘گ ë‹¨ê³„ë،œ ëڈ™ى‍‘ي•œë‹¤.

1.1 ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ ê°گى§€ â€” get_platform_sandbox()

ى»´يŒŒى‌¼ يƒ€ى‍„ cfg! ë§¤يپ¬ë،œë،œ OSë¥¼ ê°گى§€ي•کê³ , ي•´ë‹¹ ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ى‌ک ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ يƒ€ى‍…ى‌„ ë°کي™کي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/sandboxing/src/manager.rs
pub fn get_platform_sandbox(windows_sandbox_enabled: bool) -> Option<SandboxType> {
    if cfg!(target_os = "macos") {
        Some(SandboxType::MacosSeatbelt)
    } else if cfg!(target_os = "linux") {
        Some(SandboxType::LinuxSeccomp)
    } else if cfg!(target_os = "windows") {
        if windows_sandbox_enabled {
            Some(SandboxType::WindowsRestrictedToken)
        } else { None }
    } else { None }
}

1.2 ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ يƒ€ى‍… ى„ يƒ‌ â€” select_initial()

SandboxablePreference(Auto/Require/Forbid)ى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ ي™œى„±ي™” ى—¬ë¶€ë¥¼ ê²°ى •ي•œë‹¤. Auto ëھ¨ë“œى—گى„œëٹ” should_require_platform_sandbox()ê°€ يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œآ·ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى •ى±…ى‌„ ê²€ى‚¬ي•´, ى œي•œى‌´ ي•„ىڑ”ي•œ ê²½ىڑ°ى—گë§Œ ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ë¥¼ ي™œى„±ي™”ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/sandboxing/src/policy_transforms.rs
pub fn should_require_platform_sandbox(
    file_system_policy: &FileSystemSandboxPolicy,
    network_policy: NetworkSandboxPolicy,
    has_managed_network_requirements: bool,
) -> bool {
    if has_managed_network_requirements { return true; }
    if !network_policy.is_enabled() {
        return !matches!(file_system_policy.kind, FileSystemSandboxKind::ExternalSandbox);
    }
    match file_system_policy.kind {
        FileSystemSandboxKind::Restricted => !file_system_policy.has_full_disk_write_access(),
        FileSystemSandboxKind::Unrestricted | FileSystemSandboxKind::ExternalSandbox => false,
    }
}

1.3 ë³€ي™ک â€” SandboxManager::transform()

SandboxTransformRequestë¥¼ ë°›ى•„ ىµœى¢… SandboxExecRequestë¥¼ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•œë‹¤. ى‌´ ê³¼ى •ى—گى„œ ى¶”ê°€ ê¶Œي•œ(toolى‌ک PermissionProfile)ى‌´ ê¸°ى،´ ى •ى±…ê³¼ ë³‘ي•©ëگکê³ , ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ë³„ ë‍کي•‘ ëھ…ë ¹ى‌´ ىƒ‌ى„±ëگœë‹¤:

graph LR
    A["SandboxTransformRequest"] --> B["EffectiveSandboxPermissions ê³„ى‚°"]
    B --> C{"SandboxType?"}
    C -->|MacosSeatbelt| D["sandbox-exec -p &lt;SBPL&gt; -- cmd"]
    C -->|LinuxSeccomp| E["codex-linux-sandbox --sandbox-policy ... -- cmd"]
    C -->|WindowsRestrictedToken| F["restricted tokenىœ¼ë،œ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ىƒ‌ى„±"]
    C -->|None| G["ى›گë³¸ ëھ…ë ¹ ê·¸ëŒ€ë،œ"]
    D --> H["SandboxExecRequest"]
    E --> H
    F --> H
    G --> H

2. ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ë³„ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ êµ¬يک„

2.1 macOS â€” Seatbelt (sandbox-exec)

macOSى—گى„œëٹ” /usr/bin/sandbox-execى‌„ ي†µي•´ Appleى‌ک Seatbelt ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ى‌„ ى پىڑ©ي•œë‹¤. ê²½ë،œë¥¼ ي•کë“œى½”ë”©ي•´ PATH ى£¼ى‍… ê³µê²©ى‌„ ë°©ى–´ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/sandboxing/src/seatbelt.rs
pub const MACOS_PATH_TO_SEATBELT_EXECUTABLE: &str = "/usr/bin/sandbox-exec";

2.1.1 SBPL ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ êµ¬ى،°

ى„¸ ê°œى‌ک .sbpl يŒŒى‌¼ى‌´ include_str!ë،œ ى»´يŒŒى‌¼ يƒ€ى‍„ى—گ يڈ¬ي•¨ëگœë‹¤:

يŒŒى‌¼ ى—­ي• 
seatbelt_base_policy.sbpl (deny default) ê¸°ë°ک â€” ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ يڈ¬يپ¬, sysctl ى‌½ê¸°, PTY, IOKit ë“± ىµœى†Œ ي—ˆىڑ©
seatbelt_network_policy.sbpl ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ي™œى„± ى‹œ TLS ى‌¸ى¦‌ى„œ ى،°يڑŒ, DNS ى„¤ى • ى‌½ê¸° ë“± ى¶”ê°€ ي—ˆىڑ©
restricted_read_only_platform_defaults.sbpl ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ ê¸°ë³¸ ى‌½ê¸° ê²½ë،œ

ê¸°ë³¸ ى •ى±…ى‌ک ي•µى‹¬(Chrome ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ ى •ى±…ى—گى„œ ىکپê°گ):

; seatbelt_base_policy.sbpl â€” ë°œى·Œ
(version 1)
(deny default)                         ; ëھ¨ë“  ê²ƒى‌„ ê¸°ë³¸ ى°¨ë‹¨
(allow process-exec)                   ; ى‍گى‹‌ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ى‹¤ي–‰ ي—ˆىڑ©
(allow process-fork)                   ; fork ي—ˆىڑ©
(allow signal (target same-sandbox))   ; ê°™ى‌€ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ ë‚´ ى‹œê·¸ë„گë§Œ
(allow sysctl-read                     ; CPU ى •ë³´, ى»¤ë„گ ë²„ى „ ë“± ى œي•œى پ sysctl
  (sysctl-name "hw.ncpu")
  (sysctl-name "kern.osversion")
  ;; ... ى•½ 40ê°œ ي•­ëھ©
)
(allow pseudo-tty)                     ; PTY ي• ë‹¹ ي—ˆىڑ©

2.1.2 ëڈ™ى پ يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ى •ى±…

create_seatbelt_command_args_for_policies_with_extensions()ê°€ ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ى—گ SBPL ى •ى±…ى‌„ ى،°ي•©ي•œë‹¤. ي•µى‹¬ ë،œى§پ:

  • ى‌½ê¸° ى •ى±…: (allow file-read* (subpath (param "READABLE_ROOT_0"))) يک•يƒœë،œ ي—ˆىڑ© ê²½ë،œë¥¼ يŒŒë‌¼ë¯¸ي„°ي™”
  • ى“°ê¸° ى •ى±…: ى „ى²´ ى“°ê¸° ي—ˆىڑ© ى‹œ (allow file-write* (regex #"^/")), ى œي•œ ى‹œ WRITABLE_ROOT_N يŒŒë‌¼ë¯¸ي„°
  • unreadable ê²½ë،œ ى°¨ê°گ: (require-not (subpath ...)) ى،°ي•©ىœ¼ë،œ ë¯¼ê°گ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ë¥¼ ى“°ê¸° ê°€ëٹ¥ ë£¨يٹ¸ى—گى„œ ى œى™¸
// ى •ى±… ى،°ي•© ىˆœى„œ â€” seatbelt.rs ë°œى·Œ
let mut policy_sections = vec![
    MACOS_SEATBELT_BASE_POLICY.to_string(),   // deny default ê¸°ë°ک
    file_read_policy,                          // ى‌½ê¸° ي—ˆىڑ© ê²½ë،œ
    file_write_policy,                         // ى“°ê¸° ي—ˆىڑ© ê²½ë،œ
    network_policy,                            // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى •ى±…
];
if include_platform_defaults {
    policy_sections.push(MACOS_RESTRICTED_READ_ONLY_PLATFORM_DEFAULTS.to_string());
}

ىµœى¢… ëھ…ë ¹: sandbox-exec -p <full_policy> -DREADABLE_ROOT_0=/path -DWRITABLE_ROOT_0=/path -- <command>

2.1.3 ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى •ى±…

ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى •ى±…ى‌€ ى„¸ ê°€ى§€ ëھ¨ë“œë،œ ë¶„ê¸°ي•œë‹¤:

ى،°ê±´ ëڈ™ى‍‘
ي”„ë،‌ى‹œ ي™کê²½ ë³€ىˆک ى‍ˆى‌Œ ي”„ë،‌ى‹œ يڈ¬يٹ¸ë§Œ loopback ي—ˆىڑ© + ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى •ى±…
ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ي™œى„±, ي”„ë،‌ى‹œ ى—†ى‌Œ (allow network-outbound) + (allow network-inbound)
ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ë¹„ي™œى„± ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê·œى¹™ ى—†ى‌Œ (ê¸°ë³¸ deny)

Unix ëڈ„ë©”ى‌¸ ى†Œى¼“ى‌€ AllowAll ëکگëٹ” ê²½ë،œë³„ allowlistë،œ ى œى–´ي•œë‹¤:

; ê²½ë،œë³„ allowlist ىکˆى‹œ â€” ëڈ™ى پ ىƒ‌ى„±
(allow system-socket (socket-domain AF_UNIX))
(allow network-bind (local unix-socket (subpath (param "UNIX_SOCKET_PATH_0"))))
(allow network-outbound (remote unix-socket (subpath (param "UNIX_SOCKET_PATH_0"))))

2.2 Linux â€” Bubblewrap + Seccomp

Linux ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ëٹ” ë‘گ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ë¥¼ ê²¹ى¹œë‹¤: bubblewrap(يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ê²©ë¦¬)ê³¼ seccomp(syscall ي•„ي„°ë§پ). Landlockى‌€ ë ˆê±°ى‹œ يڈ´ë°±ىœ¼ë،œ ë‚¨ى•„ ى‍ˆë‹¤.

graph TD
    A["codex-linux-sandbox ى§„ى‍…"] --> B{"--use-legacy-landlock?"}
    B -->|Yes| C["Landlock + seccomp ى§پى ‘ ى پىڑ©"]
    B -->|No| D["bubblewrap ë‍کي•‘"]
    D --> E["bwrap --new-session --die-with-parent ..."]
    E --> F["ë‚´ë¶€: --apply-seccomp-then-exec"]
    F --> G["PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS + seccomp ي•„ي„° ى„¤ى¹ک"]
    G --> H["execvp(command)"]
    C --> G

2.2.1 Bubblewrap يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ê²©ë¦¬

create_bwrap_command_args()ê°€ يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ë§ˆىڑ´يٹ¸ êµ¬ى،°ë¥¼ ê²°ى •ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/bwrap.rs â€” ë§ˆىڑ´يٹ¸ ىˆœى„œ
// 1. ى „ى²´ ى‌½ê¸°: --ro-bind / /
// 2. ى œي•œ ى‌½ê¸°: --tmpfs / ë،œ ى‹œى‍‘, ê°œë³„ --ro-bind ى¶”ê°€
// 3. --dev /dev (ىµœى†Œ ë””ë°”ى‌´ىٹ¤ ë…¸ë“œ: null, zero, urandom ë“±)
// 4. --bind <root> <root> ë،œ ى“°ê¸° ê°€ëٹ¥ ê²½ë،œ ى‍¬ë§ˆىڑ´يٹ¸
// 5. --ro-bind <subpath> <subpath> ë،œ ë³´يک¸ ي•کىœ„ ê²½ë،œ ë³µى›گ

ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ê²©ë¦¬ ي”Œë‍کê·¸:

// ê¸°ë³¸ bubblewrap ي”Œë‍کê·¸ â€” bwrap.rs
vec![
    "--new-session",         // ىƒˆ ى„¸ى…ک ë¦¬ëچ”
    "--die-with-parent",     // ë¶€ëھ¨ ى¢…ë£Œ ى‹œ ي•¨ê»ک ى¢…ë£Œ
    "--unshare-user",        // ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ë¶„ë¦¬
    "--unshare-pid",         // PID ë„¤ى‍„ىٹ¤يژکى‌´ىٹ¤ ë¶„ë¦¬
    "--unshare-net",         // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى°¨ë‹¨ ى‹œى—گë§Œ ى¶”ê°€
    "--proc", "/proc",       // ىƒˆ /proc ë§ˆىڑ´يٹ¸
]

ي”Œë‍«يڈ¼ ê¸°ë³¸ ى‌½ê¸° ê²½ë،œ (restricted ëھ¨ë“œى—گى„œ ى‍گëڈ™ ى¶”ê°€):

const LINUX_PLATFORM_DEFAULT_READ_ROOTS: &[&str] = &[
    "/bin", "/sbin", "/usr", "/etc", "/lib", "/lib64",
    "/nix/store", "/run/current-system/sw",
];

Bubblewrap ى‹¤ي–‰ê¸°ëٹ” ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ /usr/bin/bwrapë¥¼ ë¨¼ى € ى‹œëڈ„ي•کê³ , --argv0ى‌„ ى§€ى›گي•کى§€ ى•ٹëٹ” êµ¬ë²„ى „ى‌´ë©´ ë²¤ëچ”ë§پëگœ bwrapىœ¼ë،œ يڈ´ë°±ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/launcher.rs
fn preferred_bwrap_launcher_for_path(system_bwrap_path: &Path) -> BubblewrapLauncher {
    if !system_bwrap_supports_argv0(system_bwrap_path) {
        return BubblewrapLauncher::Vendored;
    }
    BubblewrapLauncher::System(/* ... */)
}

2.2.2 Seccomp syscall ي•„ي„°ë§پ

ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى°¨ë‹¨ى‌´ ي•„ىڑ”ي•œ ê²½ىڑ° seccomp BPF ي•„ي„°ê°€ ى„¤ى¹کëگœë‹¤. ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ ëھ¨ë“œê°€ ى‍ˆë‹¤:

// codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/landlock.rs
enum NetworkSeccompMode {
    Restricted,    // ëھ¨ë“  ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ syscall ى°¨ë‹¨, AF_UNIXë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©
    ProxyRouted,   // AF_INET/INET6ë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ© (ي”„ë،‌ى‹œ ë¸Œë¦؟ى§€ىڑ©), AF_UNIX ى°¨ë‹¨
}

Restricted ëھ¨ë“œ â€” ى°¨ë‹¨ ëŒ€ىƒپ syscall ëھ©ë،‌:

// ë¬´ى،°ê±´ ى°¨ë‹¨
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_ptrace);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_io_uring_setup);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_io_uring_enter);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_io_uring_register);

// ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê´€ë ¨ ى°¨ë‹¨
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_connect);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_accept);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_accept4);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_bind);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_listen);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_sendto);
deny_syscall(&mut rules, libc::SYS_sendmmsg);
// ... shutdown, getpeername, getsockname, recvmmsg, getsockopt, setsockopt

// socket()/socketpair()ëٹ” AF_UNIXë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©
let unix_only_rule = SeccompRule::new(vec![SeccompCondition::new(
    0,                        // ى²« ë²ˆى§¸ ى‌¸ى‍گ (domain)
    SeccompCmpArgLen::Dword,
    SeccompCmpOp::Ne,
    libc::AF_UNIX as u64,    // AF_UNIXê°€ ى•„ë‹ˆë©´ ى°¨ë‹¨
)?])?;
rules.insert(libc::SYS_socket, vec![unix_only_rule]);

seccomp ي•„ي„° ىƒ‌ى„±:

let filter = SeccompFilter::new(
    rules,
    SeccompAction::Allow,                     // ê¸°ë³¸: ي—ˆىڑ©
    SeccompAction::Errno(libc::EPERM as u32), // ê·œى¹™ ë§¤ى¹ک ى‹œ: EPERM ë°کي™ک
    TargetArch::x86_64,                       // ëکگëٹ” aarch64
)?;
let prog: BpfProgram = filter.try_into()?;
apply_filter(&prog)?;

2.2.3 Landlock ë ˆê±°ى‹œ يڈ´ë°±

Landlock ABI V5 ê¸°ë°ک يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ى œي•œ. يک„ى‍¬ëٹ” bubblewrapى‌´ ê¸°ë³¸ى‌´ى§€ë§Œ, --use-legacy-landlock ي”Œë‍کê·¸ë،œ ي™œى„±ي™”ي•  ىˆک ى‍ˆë‹¤:

// codex-rs/linux-sandbox/src/landlock.rs
fn install_filesystem_landlock_rules_on_current_thread(
    writable_roots: Vec<AbsolutePathBuf>,
) -> Result<()> {
    let abi = ABI::V5;
    let access_rw = AccessFs::from_all(abi);
    let access_ro = AccessFs::from_read(abi);
    let mut ruleset = Ruleset::default()
        .handle_access(access_rw)?
        .create()?
        .add_rules(landlock::path_beneath_rules(&["/"], access_ro))?    // ى „ى²´ ى‌½ê¸°
        .add_rules(landlock::path_beneath_rules(&["/dev/null"], access_rw))? // /dev/null ى“°ê¸°
        .set_no_new_privs(true);
    if !writable_roots.is_empty() {
        ruleset = ruleset.add_rules(
            landlock::path_beneath_rules(&writable_roots, access_rw)    // ي—ˆىڑ© ê²½ë،œ ى“°ê¸°
        )?;
    }
    ruleset.restrict_self()?;
    Ok(())
}

2.3 Windows â€” Restricted Token + ë°©ي™”ë²½

Windows ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ëٹ” ê°€ى‍¥ ë³µى‍،ي•کë‹¤. ى „ىڑ© ë،œى»¬ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گë¥¼ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•کê³ , restricted tokenê³¼ ACLë،œ يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œى‌„ ê²©ë¦¬ي•کë©°, Windows Firewall ê·œى¹™ىœ¼ë،œ ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ë¥¼ ى°¨ë‹¨ي•œë‹¤.

graph TD
    A["setup_orchestrator ى‹¤ي–‰"] --> B["ى „ىڑ© ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ىƒ‌ى„±"]
    B --> B1["CodexSandboxOffline â€” ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى°¨ë‹¨"]
    B --> B2["CodexSandboxOnline â€” ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ي—ˆىڑ©"]
    B1 --> C["ë°©ي™”ë²½ ê·œى¹™ ى„¤ى¹ک"]
    B2 --> C
    C --> D["ACL ى„¤ى • â€” ى‌½ê¸°/ى“°ê¸° ê²½ë،œ ى œى–´"]
    D --> E["CreateRestrictedToken"]
    E --> F["restricted tokenىœ¼ë،œ ي”„ë،œى„¸ىٹ¤ ى‹¤ي–‰"]

2.3.1 ى „ىڑ© ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ي”„ë،œë¹„ى €ë‹‌

ë‘گ ê°œى‌ک ë،œى»¬ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گë¥¼ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•کê³ , CodexSandboxUsers ê·¸ë£¹ى—گ ى¶”ê°€ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/setup_orchestrator.rs
pub const OFFLINE_USERNAME: &str = "CodexSandboxOffline";
pub const ONLINE_USERNAME: &str = "CodexSandboxOnline";

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/sandbox_users.rs
pub fn provision_sandbox_users(/* ... */) -> Result<()> {
    ensure_sandbox_users_group(log)?;
    let offline_password = random_password();   // 24ى‍گ ë‍œëچ¤ ë¹„ë°€ë²ˆيک¸
    let online_password = random_password();
    ensure_sandbox_user(offline_username, &offline_password, log)?;
    ensure_sandbox_user(online_username, &online_password, log)?;
    write_secrets(codex_home, /* ... */)?;       // DPAPIë،œ ى•”يک¸ي™” ى €ى‍¥
    Ok(())
}

ë¯¼ê°گ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ ى‍گëڈ™ ى œى™¸(ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ي”„ë،œي•„ ى‌½ê¸° ى‹œ):

const USERPROFILE_READ_ROOT_EXCLUSIONS: &[&str] = &[
    ".ssh", ".gnupg", ".aws", ".azure", ".kube",
    ".docker", ".config", ".npm", ".pki", ".terraform.d",
];

2.3.2 Restricted Token ىƒ‌ى„±

CreateRestrictedToken Win32 APIë،œ ىµœى†Œ ê¶Œي•œ ي† يپ°ى‌„ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/token.rs
unsafe fn create_token_with_caps_from(
    base_token: HANDLE,
    psid_capabilities: &[*mut c_void],
) -> Result<HANDLE> {
    let flags = DISABLE_MAX_PRIVILEGE   // ëھ¨ë“  يٹ¹ê¶Œ ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™”
              | LUA_TOKEN               // Limited User Account
              | WRITE_RESTRICTED;       // ى“°ê¸° ى œي•œ

    CreateRestrictedToken(
        base_token, flags,
        0, std::ptr::null(),            // SID ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™” ى—†ى‌Œ
        0, std::ptr::null(),            // يٹ¹ê¶Œ ى‚­ى œ ى—†ى‌Œ
        entries.len() as u32,           // ى œي•œ SID: capability + logon + everyone
        entries.as_mut_ptr(),
        &mut new_token,
    );
    set_default_dacl(new_token, &dacl_sids)?;   // يŒŒى‌´ي”„ IPCë¥¼ ىœ„ي•œ DACL
    enable_single_privilege(new_token, "SeChangeNotifyPrivilege")?;
    Ok(new_token)
}

2.3.3 ë°©ي™”ë²½ ê·œى¹™

ىک¤ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گى‌ک ى•„ى›ƒë°”ىڑ´ë“œ يٹ¸ë‍کي”½ى‌„ Windows Firewall COM APIë،œ ى°¨ë‹¨ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/windows-sandbox-rs/src/firewall.rs
const OFFLINE_BLOCK_RULE_NAME: &str = "codex_sandbox_offline_block_outbound";

fn configure_rule(rule: &INetFwRule3, /* ... */) -> Result<()> {
    rule.SetDirection(NET_FW_RULE_DIR_OUT)?;   // ى•„ى›ƒë°”ىڑ´ë“œë§Œ
    rule.SetAction(NET_FW_ACTION_BLOCK)?;      // ى°¨ë‹¨
    rule.SetProtocol(NET_FW_IP_PROTOCOL_ANY.0)?;
    rule.SetLocalUserAuthorizedList(&BSTR::from(local_user_spec))?;
    // read-back ê²€ى¦‌: SIDê°€ ىک¬ë°”ë¥´ê²Œ ى پىڑ©ëگگëٹ”ى§€ ي™•ى‌¸
}

WFP ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê²©ë¦¬ ëڈ„ى‍…

  • ë°©ي™”ë²½ ى •ى±… ىœ يڑ¨ى„± ê²€ى¦‌ (#22353): INetFwPolicy2::LocalPolicyModifyStateë¥¼ ى،°يڑŒي•´, ê´€ë¦¬يک• يک¸ىٹ¤يٹ¸ى—گى„œ ë،œى»¬ ë°©ي™”ë²½ ى •ى±…ى‌´ ë¬´ى‹œëگکëٹ” ê²½ىڑ° elevated setupى‌„ ى‹¤يŒ¨ ى²کë¦¬ي•œë‹¤.

Guardian ى„œي‚· ë¸Œë ˆى‌´ى»¤ InterruptTurn ى•،ى…ک

GuardianRejectionCircuitBreaker(core/src/guardian/mod.rs)ê°€ ي„´ ى¤‘ë‹¨ى‌„ يٹ¸ë¦¬ê±°ي•œë‹¤. ى‌´ى „ 412 ë²”ىœ„ ë°”ê¹¥ى—گى„œ ëڈ„ى‍…ëگœ êµ¬ى،°ى‌´ë‚ک, ى‌´ ë²”ىœ„ ë‚´ى—گى„œ Guardian ي”„ë،¬ي”„يٹ¸ ى؛گى‹œ ي‚¤ ى•ˆى •ي™”(#24891~#24895), ë¦¬ë·° ë©”يٹ¸ë¦­(#24897), ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ permission profile ê°±ى‹ (#22931), auto-review permission profile override ىˆکى •(#23956) ë“±ى‌´ ى پىڑ©ëگگë‹¤.

ي•µى‹¬ ي‌گë¦„: record_denial() يک¸ى¶œ ى‹œ ى—°ى†چ ê±°ë¶€ 3يڑŒ(MAX_CONSECUTIVE_GUARDIAN_DENIALS_PER_TURN) ëکگëٹ” ىµœê·¼ 50يڑŒ ë¦¬ë·° ى¤‘ 10يڑŒ ê±°ë¶€(MAX_RECENT_AUTO_REVIEW_DENIALS_PER_TURN) ى‍„ê³„ë¥¼ ë„کىœ¼ë©´ InterruptTurnى‌„ ë°کي™کي•œë‹¤. review.rsى‌ک record_guardian_denial()ى‌´ ى‌´ë¥¼ ë°›ى•„ GuardianWarning ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ë¥¼ ë°œي–‰ي•کê³  abort_turn_if_active(turn_id, TurnAbortReason::Interrupted)ë،œ ي„´ى‌„ ى¤‘ë‹¨ي•œë‹¤.

deny-read ىƒŒë“œë°•ى‹± ë³´ى،´

Permission profileى‌ک access = none(deny) ى‌½ê¸° ى œي•œى‌´ ى•ˆى „ ëھ…ë ¹ ë°”ى‌´يŒ¨ىٹ¤ى—گى„œ ëˆ„ë‌½ëگکëچک ë²„ê·¸ê°€ ىˆکى •ëگگë‹¤ (#23943). is_known_safe_command()ë،œ ي—ˆىڑ©ëگœ ëھ…ë ¹(cat, ls ë“±)ى‌´ë‌¼ëڈ„ deny-read ي•­ëھ©(**/*.env ë“±)ى‌´ ي™œى„±ي™”ëڈ¼ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ë¥¼ ىœ ى§€ي•œë‹¤. execpolicy allow ê·œى¹™ى—گ ëŒ€ي•´ى„œëڈ„ ëڈ™ى‌¼ ë…¼ë¦¬ê°€ ى پىڑ©ëگœë‹¤. cloud requirementsى‌ک deny-read glob ي•´ى„‌ ىˆکى •(#23729)ëڈ„ ي•¨ê»ک يڈ¬ي•¨ëگگë‹¤.

Windows ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê±°ë¶€ ى‹œ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ ى·¨ى†Œ

Guardian ëکگëٹ” ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ي”„ë،‌ى‹œê°€ ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى ‘ê·¼ى‌„ ê±°ë¶€ي•کë©´ ExecExpirationى‌„ ي†µي•´ ى‹¤ي–‰ى‌„ ى·¨ى†Œي•کëٹ”ëچ°, Windows ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ ى؛،ى²ک ê²½ë،œê°€ يƒ€ى‍„ى•„ى›ƒ ى»´يڈ¬ë„Œيٹ¸ë§Œ ى „يŒŒي•کê³  ى‍ˆى—ˆë‹¤ (#19880). ى‌´ ىˆکى •ىœ¼ë،œ ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê±°ë¶€ ى‹œى—گëڈ„ ى¦‰ى‹œ ى¢…ë£Œëگœë‹¤.

ê¸°يƒ€ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ ê´€ë ¨ ë³€ê²½

  • Windows deny-read يŒ¨ë¦¬ي‹° (#18202): macOS/Linuxى—گى„œë§Œ ى§€ى›گëگکëچک access = none ى‌½ê¸° ى œي•œى‌„ Windowsى—گëڈ„ ى پىڑ©. ë‹¨, unelevated restricted-token ë°±ى—”ë“œëٹ” ى‌½ê¸° ê±°ë¶€ë¥¼ ê°•ى œي•  ىˆک ى—†ى–´ fail-closed ى²کë¦¬ي•œë‹¤.
  • write-root capability SID ë²”ىœ„ ى§€ى • (#21479): ë ˆê±°ى‹œ/elevated ي† يپ°ى‌ک capability SIDë¥¼ ى‹¤يڑ¨ ى“°ê¸° ë£¨يٹ¸ى—گë§Œ ي•œى •ي•œë‹¤.
  • Linux sandbox ى…¸ ى •ë¦¬ ë³´ى،´ (#22729): ى‌¸ي„°ëں½يٹ¸ ى‹œ ى…¸ ى •ë¦¬ ىٹ¤يپ¬ë¦½يٹ¸ê°€ ى‹¤ي–‰ëگکëڈ„ë،‌ ىˆکى •.
  • bundled bwrap ى„¤ى¹ک ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ (#23634): ë²¤ëچ”ë§پëگœ bwrap ë°”ى‌´ë„ˆë¦¬ ê²½ë،œë¥¼ ى„¤ى¹ک ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ى—گى„œ ي•´ى„‌ي•œë‹¤.
  • Unix socket deny ى„¤ى • (#24970): Unix ى†Œى¼“ ê¶Œي•œى—گ deny ê·œى¹™ى‌„ ىک¬ë°”ë¥´ê²Œ ى پىڑ©ي•œë‹¤.
  • Windows sandbox ë،œê·¸ ê°œى„ : rolling file ë،œي…Œى‌´ى…ک(#24117) + ي”¼ë“œë°± ë³´ê³ ى„œى—گ ë،œê·¸ ى²¨ë¶€(#24623).
  • Windows sandbox ë°©ي™”ë²½ ىœ يڑ¨ى„± ê²€ى¦‌ (#22353): ê´€ë¦¬يک• يک¸ىٹ¤يٹ¸ى—گى„œ ë°©ي™”ë²½ ى •ى±… ë¬´يڑ¨ ى‹œ setup ى‹¤يŒ¨ ى²کë¦¬.

Axis 08: Config system

AXIS

008_config-system/08_co_config.md

Overview

Axis 08: Config system â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” configuration system.

    1. System â€” /etc/codex/config.toml (Unix) ëکگëٹ” %ProgramData%\OpenAI\Codex\config.toml (Windows)
    1. User â€” $CODEX_HOME/config.toml
    1. Project â€” CWD ê¸°ى¤€ .codex/config.toml (tree walk + repo root, untrusted ى‹œ disabled)
    1. Session flags â€” CLI --config ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ (dotted-path TOML write)
    1. Legacy managed config â€” managed_config.toml (يŒŒى‌¼ ëکگëٹ” MDM)
    1. MDM managed config â€” macOS managed preferences (defaults ê¸°ë°ک, macOS only)
  • ë³„ëڈ„ë،œ Requirements ى±„ë„گى‌´ config ë ˆى‌´ى–´ى™€ ëڈ…ë¦½ى پىœ¼ë،œ ى‍‘ëڈ™ي•œë‹¤:
  • Cloud requirements â€” ى›گê²© ê´€ë¦¬يک• ى œى•½
  • MDM requirements â€” macOS managed preferences ê¸°ë°ک ى œى•½
  • System requirements â€” /etc/codex/requirements.toml

Topics covered

    1. ë ˆى‌´ى–´ Architecture
  • 1.1 ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ë،œë”© ي‌گë¦„

  • 1.3 Requirements ى پىڑ©
    1. يچ¼ë¯¸ى…ک ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ

  • 2.2 يچ¼ë¯¸ى…ک ى»´يŒŒى‌¼ ي‌گë¦„
  • 2.3 يٹ¹ىˆک ê²½ë،œى™€ ى „ë°© يک¸ي™کى„±

    1. Feature Flag ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ

// config_loader/mod.rs â€” ى§„ى‍…ى گ
pub async fn load_config_layers_state(
    codex_home: &Path,
    cwd: Option<AbsolutePathBuf>,
    cli_overrides: &[(String, TomlValue)],
    overrides: LoaderOverrides,
    cloud_requirements: CloudRequirementsLoader,
) -> io::Result<ConfigLayerStack> {
    // 1) requirements ىˆکى§‘: cloud â†’ MDM â†’ system requirements.toml
    let mut config_requirements_toml = ConfigRequirementsWithSources::default();
    if let Some(requirements) = cloud_requirements.get().await...? {
        config_requirements_toml.merge_unset_fields(
            RequirementSource::CloudRequirements, requirements);
    }
    // macOS MDM requirements
    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    macos::load_managed_admin_requirements_toml(&mut config_requirements_toml, ...);
    // /etc/codex/requirements.toml
    load_requirements_toml(&mut config_requirements_toml, system_requirements_toml_file()?);

    // 2) config ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ى،°ë¦½: system â†’ user â†’ project â†’ session flags â†’ legacy managed
    let mut layers = Vec::<ConfigLayerEntry>::new();
    layers.push(/* system */);
    layers.push(/* user */);
    // project ë ˆى‌´ى–´: cwdى—گى„œ .codex/ يڈ´ëچ”ë¥¼ tree-walkë،œ ê²€ىƒ‰
    if let Some(cwd) = cwd {
        let project_layers = load_project_layers(&cwd, ...);
        layers.extend(project_layers);
    }
    if let Some(cli_overrides_layer) = cli_overrides_layer {
        layers.push(ConfigLayerEntry::new(ConfigLayerSource::SessionFlags, ...));
    }
    // legacy managed_config.toml (يŒŒى‌¼ + MDM)
    ...

    // 3) ConfigLayerStack êµ¬ى„± â€” layers + requirements â†’ ىµœى¢… ىٹ¤يƒ‌
    ConfigLayerStack::new(layers, config_requirements_toml.try_into()?, ...)
}

README / docs

// config/mod.rs â€” ConfigBuilder::build()
pub async fn build(self) -> std::io::Result<Config> {
    let config_layer_stack = load_config_layers_state(
        &codex_home, Some(cwd), &cli_overrides, loader_overrides, cloud_requirements,
    ).await?;

    // effective_config(): disabled ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ى œى™¸, ى‍¬ê·€ TOML ë³‘ي•©
    let merged_toml = config_layer_stack.effective_config();

    // TOML Value â†’ ConfigToml ى—­ى§پë ¬ي™”
    let config_toml: ConfigToml = merged_toml.try_into()?;

    // ConfigToml + ConfigOverrides + requirements â†’ ىµœى¢… Config
    Config::load_config_with_layer_stack(config_toml, harness_overrides, codex_home, config_layer_stack)
}

README / docs

// config/mod.rs â€” load_config_with_layer_stack()
let ConfigRequirements {
    approval_policy: mut constrained_approval_policy,
    sandbox_policy: mut constrained_sandbox_policy,
    web_search_mode: mut constrained_web_search_mode,
    feature_requirements,
    mcp_servers,
    exec_policy: _,
    enforce_residency,
    network: network_requirements,
} = config_layer_stack.requirements().clone();

// ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى„¤ى •ê°’ى‌´ requirement ى œى•½ى—گ ىœ„ë°کëگکë©´ fallback ى پىڑ© + ê²½ê³  ë°œي–‰
apply_requirement_constrained_value(
    "approval_policy", configured_value,
    &mut constrained_approval_policy, &mut startup_warnings)?;

README / docs

fn apply_requirement_constrained_value<T>(
    field_name: &'static str,
    configured_value: T,
    constrained_value: &mut ConstrainedWithSource<T>,
    startup_warnings: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    if let Err(err) = constrained_value.set(configured_value) {
        let fallback_value = constrained_value.get().clone();
        startup_warnings.push(format!(
            "Configured value for `{field_name}` is disallowed by requirements; \
             falling back to required value {fallback_value:?}. Details: {err}"
        ));
        constrained_value.set(fallback_value)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}

README / docs

// config/permissions.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ يƒ€ى‍…
pub struct PermissionsToml {
    pub entries: BTreeMap<String, PermissionProfileToml>,  // ى‌´ë¦„ë³„ ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼
}

pub struct PermissionProfileToml {
    pub filesystem: Option<FilesystemPermissionsToml>,  // يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ê·œى¹™
    pub network: Option<NetworkToml>,                    // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê·œى¹™
}

// يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ê²½ë،œë³„ ى ‘ê·¼ ê¶Œي•œ
pub enum FilesystemPermissionToml {
    Access(FileSystemAccessMode),                        // ë‹¨ىˆœ: read/write/read_write
    Scoped(BTreeMap<String, FileSystemAccessMode>),      // ي•کىœ„ê²½ë،œë³„ ى„¸ë¶„ي™”
}

[permissions.strict]
[permissions.strict.filesystem]
":project_roots" = "read_write"
":tmpdir" = "read_write"
"/usr/local/bin" = "read"

[permissions.strict.network]
enabled = true
mode = "limited"
allowed_domains = ["api.openai.com", "github.com"]

    C --> W["startup_warnings"]

    subgraph "يٹ¹ىˆک ê²½ë،œ ي•´ى„‌"
        S1[":root â†’ Root"]
        S2[":minimal â†’ Minimal"]
        S3[":project_roots â†’ ProjectRoots"]
        S4[":tmpdir â†’ Tmpdir"]
        S5[":unknown â†’ Unknown + ê²½ê³ "]

README / docs

// config/permissions.rs â€” يچ¼ë¯¸ى…ک ى»´يŒŒى‌¼
pub(crate) fn compile_permission_profile(
    permissions: &PermissionsToml,
    profile_name: &str,
    startup_warnings: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> io::Result<(FileSystemSandboxPolicy, NetworkSandboxPolicy)> {
    let profile = resolve_permission_profile(permissions, profile_name)?;

    // يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ: ê°پ ê²½ë،œë¥¼ FileSystemSandboxEntryë،œ ى»´يŒŒى‌¼
    let mut entries = Vec::new();
    if let Some(filesystem) = profile.filesystem.as_ref() {
        for (path, permission) in &filesystem.entries {
            compile_filesystem_permission(path, permission, &mut entries, startup_warnings)?;
        }
    }
    // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬: enabled ى—¬ë¶€ى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ Enabled/Restricted
    let network_sandbox_policy = compile_network_sandbox_policy(profile.network.as_ref());

    Ok((FileSystemSandboxPolicy::restricted(entries), network_sandbox_policy))
}

README / docs

// ى „ë°© يک¸ي™ک يŒŒى„œ: ىƒˆ يٹ¹ىˆک ê²½ë،œ ى¶”ê°€ ى‹œ êµ¬ë²„ى „ى‌´ rejectي•کى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ
fn parse_special_path(path: &str) -> Option<FileSystemSpecialPath> {
    match path {
        ":root"          => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Root),
        ":minimal"       => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Minimal),
        ":project_roots" => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::project_roots(None)),
        ":tmpdir"        => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Tmpdir),
        _ if path.starts_with(':') => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::unknown(path, None)),
        _ => None,
    }
}

README / docs

pub struct NetworkToml {
    pub enabled: Option<bool>,
    pub proxy_url: Option<String>,
    pub enable_socks5: Option<bool>,
    pub mode: Option<NetworkMode>,               // limited | full
    pub allowed_domains: Option<Vec<String>>,     // ي™”ى‌´يٹ¸ë¦¬ىٹ¤يٹ¸
    pub denied_domains: Option<Vec<String>>,      // ë¸”ë‍™ë¦¬ىٹ¤يٹ¸
    pub allow_unix_sockets: Option<Vec<String>>,
    pub dangerously_allow_non_loopback_proxy: Option<bool>,
    pub dangerously_allow_all_unix_sockets: Option<bool>,
    // ...
}

README / docs

// config/managed_features.rs
pub struct ManagedFeatures {
    value: ConstrainedWithSource<Features>,
    pinned_features: BTreeMap<Feature, bool>,  // requirementsê°€ ê³ ى •ي•œ feature
}

impl ManagedFeatures {
    // requirementsى—گى„œ pinned feature يŒŒى‹± â†’ ي›„ë³´ ى •ê·œي™” â†’ ê²€ى¦‌
    pub(crate) fn from_configured(
        configured_features: Features,
        feature_requirements: Option<Sourced<FeatureRequirementsToml>>,
    ) -> std::io::Result<Self> {
        let (pinned_features, source) = match feature_requirements {
            Some(Sourced { value, source }) => (
                parse_feature_requirements(value, &source)?, Some(source)
            ),
            None => (BTreeMap::new(), None),
        };
        // pinned featureë¥¼ candidateى—گ ê°•ى œ ى پىڑ© ي›„ ى‌کى،´ى„± ى •ê·œي™”
        let normalized = normalize_candidate(configured_features, &pinned_features);
        validate_pinned_features(&normalized, &pinned_features, source.as_ref())?;
        Ok(Self { ... })
    }

    // ë³€ê²½ ى‹œëڈ„ ى‹œ pinned feature ى œى•½ ê²€ى¦‌
    pub fn set(&mut self, candidate: Features) -> ConstraintResult<()> {
        let normalized = self.normalize_and_validate(candidate)?;
        self.value.value.set(normalized)
    }
}

README / docs

graph LR
    A["ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ features\n(config.toml)"] --> B["Features::from_sources()"]
    C["ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ features"] --> B
    D["CLI overrides"] --> B
    B --> E["configured_features"]
    F["requirements.toml\nfeature_requirements"] --> G["parse_feature_requirements()"]
    G --> H["pinned_features\nBTreeMap<Feature, bool>"]
    E --> I["normalize_candidate()"]
    H --> I
    I --> J["pin ى پىڑ© + ى‌کى،´ى„± ى •ê·œي™”"]
    J --> K["validate_pinned_features()"]
    K --> L["ManagedFeatures"]

// config/mod.rs â€” Feature ي•©ى„±
let configured_features = Features::from_sources(
    FeatureConfigSource {
        features: cfg.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: None,
        experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch: cfg.experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch,
        experimental_use_unified_exec_tool: cfg.experimental_use_unified_exec_tool,
    },
    FeatureConfigSource {  // ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ ë ˆë²¨ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ
        features: config_profile.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: config_profile.include_apply_patch_tool,
        ...
    },
    feature_overrides,  // CLI --config ë“±
);
let features = ManagedFeatures::from_configured(configured_features, feature_requirements)?;

# config.toml ىکˆى‹œ
[model_providers.my-local]
name = "My Local LLM"
base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1"
wire_api = "responses"
env_key = "MY_LOCAL_API_KEY"

// config/profile.rs
pub struct ConfigProfile {
    pub model: Option<String>,
    pub model_provider: Option<String>,
    pub approval_policy: Option<AskForApproval>,
    pub sandbox_mode: Option<SandboxMode>,
    pub model_reasoning_effort: Option<ReasoningEffort>,
    pub model_reasoning_summary: Option<ReasoningSummary>,
    pub model_verbosity: Option<Verbosity>,
    pub personality: Option<Personality>,
    pub features: Option<FeaturesToml>,
    pub tools: Option<ToolsToml>,
    pub web_search: Option<WebSearchMode>,
    // ...
}

README / docs

# config.toml â€” ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ ى „ي™ک ىکˆى‹œ
profile = "fast"

[profiles.fast]
model = "gpt-4.1-mini"
model_reasoning_effort = "low"
sandbox_mode = "write"

[profiles.careful]
model = "o3"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
approval_policy = "unless-allow-listed"
sandbox_mode = "locked"

// config/edit.rs â€” ى§€ى›گëگکëٹ” يژ¸ى§‘ ى—°ى‚°
pub enum ConfigEdit {
    SetModel { model: Option<String>, effort: Option<ReasoningEffort> },
    SetServiceTier { service_tier: Option<ServiceTier> },
    SetModelPersonality { personality: Option<Personality> },
    ReplaceMcpServers(BTreeMap<String, McpServerConfig>),
    SetSkillConfig { path: PathBuf, enabled: bool },
    SetProjectTrustLevel { path: PathBuf, level: TrustLevel },
    SetPath { segments: Vec<String>, value: TomlItem },     // ى‍„ى‌ک dotted-path ى„¤ى •
    ClearPath { segments: Vec<String> },                     // ى‍„ى‌ک dotted-path ى‚­ى œ
    // ê°پى¢… notice/flag ي† ê¸€...
}

README / docs

// config/agent_roles.rs â€” ë ˆى‌´ى–´ë³„ ى—­ي•  ë³‘ي•©
pub(crate) fn load_agent_roles(
    cfg: &ConfigToml,
    config_layer_stack: &ConfigLayerStack,
    startup_warnings: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> std::io::Result<BTreeMap<String, AgentRoleConfig>> {
    // ë‚®ى‌€ ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ë¶€ي„° ىˆœيڑŒي•کë©° ى—­ي•  ىˆکى§‘
    let layers = config_layer_stack.get_layers(
        ConfigLayerStackOrdering::LowestPrecedenceFirst, false);
    let mut roles: BTreeMap<String, AgentRoleConfig> = BTreeMap::new();
    for layer in layers {
        // 1) [agents.roles] ى„ ى–¸ يŒŒى‹±
        // 2) agents/ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ى—گى„œ .toml يŒŒى‌¼ ى‍گëڈ™ ë°œê²¬
        // 3) ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ â†’ ىƒپىœ„ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ê°€ ي•کىœ„ ي•„ë“œ merge
    }
    Ok(roles)
}

README / docs

// config/schema.rs
pub fn config_schema() -> RootSchema {
    SchemaSettings::draft07()
        .with(|settings| { settings.option_add_null_type = false; })
        .into_generator()
        .into_root_schema_for::<ConfigToml>()
}

// features ي‚¤: ى•Œë ¤ى§„ feature + legacy keyë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©, additional_properties = false
pub(crate) fn features_schema(schema_gen: &mut SchemaGenerator) -> Schema {
    for feature in FEATURES {
        validation.properties.insert(feature.key.to_string(), ...);
    }
    validation.additional_properties = Some(Box::new(Schema::Bool(false)));
    ...
}

graph TD
    subgraph "ConfigToml ى£¼ىڑ” ى„¹ى…ک"
        M["model / model_provider\nëھ¨ëچ¸ ى„ يƒ‌"]
        P["permissions\nيŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œآ·ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤"]

        MCP["mcp_servers\nMCP ى„œë²„ ى •ى‌ک"]
        PRF["profiles\nëھ…ëھ…ëگœ ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼"]
