        PRJ["projects\nي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ë³„ trust_level"]
        TUI["tui\nUI ى„¤ى • (ي…Œë§ˆ, ىƒپيƒœى¤„ ë“±)"]
    end

Main analysis

Codex setup system analysis

Analysis snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” configuration system.

Codex's configuration is not a single file. A 7-layer model is operated, and each layer is recursively merged in TOML format. The key is how to resolve priority conflicts between layers, per-key origin tracking, and policy restrictions based on requirements.

1. Layer architecture

Layer priorities (higher down):

  1. System â€” /etc/codex/config.toml (Unix) or %ProgramData%\OpenAI\Codex\config.toml (Windows)
  2. User â€” $CODEX_HOME/config.toml
  3. Project â€” CWD standard .codex/config.toml (tree walk + repo root, disabled when untrusted)
  4. Session flags â€” CLI --config override (dotted-path TOML write)
  5. Legacy managed config â€” managed_config.toml (file or MDM)
  6. MDM managed config â€” macOS managed preferences (based on defaults, macOS only)

Separately, the Requirements channel operates independently of the config layer:

  • Cloud requirements â€” Remotely managed constraints
  • MDM requirements â€” Restrictions based on macOS managed preferences
  • System requirements â€” /etc/codex/requirements.toml
graph TD
    subgraph "Config Layers (ë‚®â†’ë†’ ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„)"
        SYS["â‘  System config.toml"]
        USR["â‘، User config.toml"]
        PRJ["â‘¢ Project .codex/config.toml"]
        CLI["â‘£ Session flags (CLI --config)"]
        MCF["â‘¤ Legacy managed_config.toml"]
        MDM["â‘¥ MDM managed config (macOS)"]
    end

    subgraph "Requirements (ى •ى±… ى œى•½)"
        CRQ["Cloud requirements"]
        MRQ["MDM requirements"]
        SRQ["System requirements.toml"]
    end

    SYS --> STACK["ConfigLayerStack"]
    USR --> STACK
    PRJ --> STACK
    CLI --> STACK
    MCF --> STACK
    MDM --> STACK

    CRQ --> REQ["ConfigRequirements"]
    MRQ --> REQ
    SRQ --> REQ

    STACK --> MERGE["effective_config()\nTOML ى‍¬ê·€ ë³‘ي•©"]
    STACK --> ORIG["per-key origins()"]
    REQ --> STACK

    MERGE --> CFG["Config ىµœى¢… êµ¬ى،°ى²´"]
    REQ --> CFG

1.1 Layer loading flow

load_config_layers_state() in config_loader/mod.rs assembles the entire layer stack:

// config_loader/mod.rs â€” ى§„ى‍…ى گ
pub async fn load_config_layers_state(
    codex_home: &Path,
    cwd: Option<AbsolutePathBuf>,
    cli_overrides: &[(String, TomlValue)],
    overrides: LoaderOverrides,
    cloud_requirements: CloudRequirementsLoader,
) -> io::Result<ConfigLayerStack> {
    // 1) requirements ىˆکى§‘: cloud â†’ MDM â†’ system requirements.toml
    let mut config_requirements_toml = ConfigRequirementsWithSources::default();
    if let Some(requirements) = cloud_requirements.get().await...? {
        config_requirements_toml.merge_unset_fields(
            RequirementSource::CloudRequirements, requirements);
    }
    // macOS MDM requirements
    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    macos::load_managed_admin_requirements_toml(&mut config_requirements_toml, ...);
    // /etc/codex/requirements.toml
    load_requirements_toml(&mut config_requirements_toml, system_requirements_toml_file()?);

    // 2) config ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ى،°ë¦½: system â†’ user â†’ project â†’ session flags â†’ legacy managed
    let mut layers = Vec::<ConfigLayerEntry>::new();
    layers.push(/* system */);
    layers.push(/* user */);
    // project ë ˆى‌´ى–´: cwdى—گى„œ .codex/ يڈ´ëچ”ë¥¼ tree-walkë،œ ê²€ىƒ‰
    if let Some(cwd) = cwd {
        let project_layers = load_project_layers(&cwd, ...);
        layers.extend(project_layers);
    }
    if let Some(cli_overrides_layer) = cli_overrides_layer {
        layers.push(ConfigLayerEntry::new(ConfigLayerSource::SessionFlags, ...));
    }
    // legacy managed_config.toml (يŒŒى‌¼ + MDM)
    ...

    // 3) ConfigLayerStack êµ¬ى„± â€” layers + requirements â†’ ىµœى¢… ىٹ¤يƒ‌
    ConfigLayerStack::new(layers, config_requirements_toml.try_into()?, ...)
}

1.2 Config final build

ConfigBuilder builds the final Config structure from the layer stack:

// config/mod.rs â€” ConfigBuilder::build()
pub async fn build(self) -> std::io::Result<Config> {
    let config_layer_stack = load_config_layers_state(
        &codex_home, Some(cwd), &cli_overrides, loader_overrides, cloud_requirements,
    ).await?;

    // effective_config(): disabled ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ى œى™¸, ى‍¬ê·€ TOML ë³‘ي•©
    let merged_toml = config_layer_stack.effective_config();

    // TOML Value â†’ ConfigToml ى—­ى§پë ¬ي™”
    let config_toml: ConfigToml = merged_toml.try_into()?;

    // ConfigToml + ConfigOverrides + requirements â†’ ىµœى¢… Config
    Config::load_config_with_layer_stack(config_toml, harness_overrides, codex_home, config_layer_stack)
}

1.3 Requirements apply

Inside Config::load_config_with_layer_stack() the requirements are applied to each field:

// config/mod.rs â€” load_config_with_layer_stack()
let ConfigRequirements {
    approval_policy: mut constrained_approval_policy,
    sandbox_policy: mut constrained_sandbox_policy,
    web_search_mode: mut constrained_web_search_mode,
    feature_requirements,
    mcp_servers,
    exec_policy: _,
    enforce_residency,
    network: network_requirements,
} = config_layer_stack.requirements().clone();

// ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى„¤ى •ê°’ى‌´ requirement ى œى•½ى—گ ىœ„ë°کëگکë©´ fallback ى پىڑ© + ê²½ê³  ë°œي–‰
apply_requirement_constrained_value(
    "approval_policy", configured_value,
    &mut constrained_approval_policy, &mut startup_warnings)?;

apply_requirement_constrained_value automatically falls back to a safe value and collects a warning when a constraint is violated:

fn apply_requirement_constrained_value<T>(
    field_name: &'static str,
    configured_value: T,
    constrained_value: &mut ConstrainedWithSource<T>,
    startup_warnings: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    if let Err(err) = constrained_value.set(configured_value) {
        let fallback_value = constrained_value.get().clone();
        startup_warnings.push(format!(
            "Configured value for `{field_name}` is disallowed by requirements; \
             falling back to required value {fallback_value:?}. Details: {err}"
        ));
        constrained_value.set(fallback_value)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}

2. Permission system

config/permissions.rs compiles TOML-based permission profiles. Create a sandbox policy with two axes: file system and network.

2.1 Permission TOML structure

// config/permissions.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ يƒ€ى‍…
pub struct PermissionsToml {
    pub entries: BTreeMap<String, PermissionProfileToml>,  // ى‌´ë¦„ë³„ ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼
}

pub struct PermissionProfileToml {
    pub filesystem: Option<FilesystemPermissionsToml>,  // يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ê·œى¹™
    pub network: Option<NetworkToml>,                    // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê·œى¹™
}

// يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ê²½ë،œë³„ ى ‘ê·¼ ê¶Œي•œ
pub enum FilesystemPermissionToml {
    Access(FileSystemAccessMode),                        // ë‹¨ىˆœ: read/write/read_write
    Scoped(BTreeMap<String, FileSystemAccessMode>),      // ي•کىœ„ê²½ë،œë³„ ى„¸ë¶„ي™”
}

Example TOML settings:

[permissions.strict]
[permissions.strict.filesystem]
":project_roots" = "read_write"
":tmpdir" = "read_write"
"/usr/local/bin" = "read"

[permissions.strict.network]
enabled = true
mode = "limited"
allowed_domains = ["api.openai.com", "github.com"]

2.2 Permission compilation flow

graph LR
    A["config.toml\n[permissions.NAME]"] --> B["resolve_permission_profile()"]
    B --> C["compile_permission_profile()"]
    C --> D["FileSystemSandboxPolicy"]
    C --> E["NetworkSandboxPolicy"]
    C --> W["startup_warnings"]

    subgraph "يٹ¹ىˆک ê²½ë،œ ي•´ى„‌"
        S1[":root â†’ Root"]
        S2[":minimal â†’ Minimal"]
        S3[":project_roots â†’ ProjectRoots"]
        S4[":tmpdir â†’ Tmpdir"]
        S5[":unknown â†’ Unknown + ê²½ê³ "]
    end
    C --> S1

// config/permissions.rs â€” يچ¼ë¯¸ى…ک ى»´يŒŒى‌¼
pub(crate) fn compile_permission_profile(
    permissions: &PermissionsToml,
    profile_name: &str,
    startup_warnings: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> io::Result<(FileSystemSandboxPolicy, NetworkSandboxPolicy)> {
    let profile = resolve_permission_profile(permissions, profile_name)?;

    // يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ: ê°پ ê²½ë،œë¥¼ FileSystemSandboxEntryë،œ ى»´يŒŒى‌¼
    let mut entries = Vec::new();
    if let Some(filesystem) = profile.filesystem.as_ref() {
        for (path, permission) in &filesystem.entries {
            compile_filesystem_permission(path, permission, &mut entries, startup_warnings)?;
        }
    }
    // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬: enabled ى—¬ë¶€ى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ Enabled/Restricted
    let network_sandbox_policy = compile_network_sandbox_policy(profile.network.as_ref());

    Ok((FileSystemSandboxPolicy::restricted(entries), network_sandbox_policy))
}

2.3 Special paths and forward compatibility

Round-trip the unrecognized :special_path to FileSystemSpecialPath::Unknown to ensure that config load does not fail in older versions:

// ى „ë°© يک¸ي™ک يŒŒى„œ: ىƒˆ يٹ¹ىˆک ê²½ë،œ ى¶”ê°€ ى‹œ êµ¬ë²„ى „ى‌´ rejectي•کى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ
fn parse_special_path(path: &str) -> Option<FileSystemSpecialPath> {
    match path {
        ":root"          => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Root),
        ":minimal"       => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Minimal),
        ":project_roots" => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::project_roots(None)),
        ":tmpdir"        => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Tmpdir),
        _ if path.starts_with(':') => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::unknown(path, None)),
        _ => None,
    }
}

2.4 NetworkToml details

Network settings are translated into NetworkProxyConfig to perform proxy-level network control:

pub struct NetworkToml {
    pub enabled: Option<bool>,
    pub proxy_url: Option<String>,
    pub enable_socks5: Option<bool>,
    pub mode: Option<NetworkMode>,               // limited | full
    pub allowed_domains: Option<Vec<String>>,     // ي™”ى‌´يٹ¸ë¦¬ىٹ¤يٹ¸
    pub denied_domains: Option<Vec<String>>,      // ë¸”ë‍™ë¦¬ىٹ¤يٹ¸
    pub allow_unix_sockets: Option<Vec<String>>,
    pub dangerously_allow_non_loopback_proxy: Option<bool>,
    pub dangerously_allow_all_unix_sockets: Option<bool>,
    // ...
}

3. Feature Flag system

config/managed_features.rs manages feature flags through the ManagedFeatures wrapper. Features pinned by requirements cannot be changed by the user.

3.1 ManagedFeatures structure

// config/managed_features.rs
pub struct ManagedFeatures {
    value: ConstrainedWithSource<Features>,
    pinned_features: BTreeMap<Feature, bool>,  // requirementsê°€ ê³ ى •ي•œ feature
}

impl ManagedFeatures {
    // requirementsى—گى„œ pinned feature يŒŒى‹± â†’ ي›„ë³´ ى •ê·œي™” â†’ ê²€ى¦‌
    pub(crate) fn from_configured(
        configured_features: Features,
        feature_requirements: Option<Sourced<FeatureRequirementsToml>>,
    ) -> std::io::Result<Self> {
        let (pinned_features, source) = match feature_requirements {
            Some(Sourced { value, source }) => (
                parse_feature_requirements(value, &source)?, Some(source)
            ),
            None => (BTreeMap::new(), None),
        };
        // pinned featureë¥¼ candidateى—گ ê°•ى œ ى پىڑ© ي›„ ى‌کى،´ى„± ى •ê·œي™”
        let normalized = normalize_candidate(configured_features, &pinned_features);
        validate_pinned_features(&normalized, &pinned_features, source.as_ref())?;
        Ok(Self { ... })
    }

    // ë³€ê²½ ى‹œëڈ„ ى‹œ pinned feature ى œى•½ ê²€ى¦‌
    pub fn set(&mut self, candidate: Features) -> ConstraintResult<()> {
        let normalized = self.normalize_and_validate(candidate)?;
        self.value.value.set(normalized)
    }
}

3.2 Feature normalization flow

graph LR
    A["ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ features\n(config.toml)"] --> B["Features::from_sources()"]
    C["ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ features"] --> B
    D["CLI overrides"] --> B
    B --> E["configured_features"]
    F["requirements.toml\nfeature_requirements"] --> G["parse_feature_requirements()"]
    G --> H["pinned_features\nBTreeMap<Feature, bool>"]
    E --> I["normalize_candidate()"]
    H --> I
    I --> J["pin ى پىڑ© + ى‌کى،´ى„± ى •ê·œي™”"]
    J --> K["validate_pinned_features()"]
    K --> L["ManagedFeatures"]

Features are synthesized from multiple sources â€” global config, profile config, legacy toggle, CLI override:

// config/mod.rs â€” Feature ي•©ى„±
let configured_features = Features::from_sources(
    FeatureConfigSource {
        features: cfg.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: None,
        experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch: cfg.experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch,
        experimental_use_unified_exec_tool: cfg.experimental_use_unified_exec_tool,
    },
    FeatureConfigSource {  // ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ ë ˆë²¨ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ
        features: config_profile.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: config_profile.include_apply_patch_tool,
        ...
    },
    feature_overrides,  // CLI --config ë“±
);
let features = ManagedFeatures::from_configured(configured_features, feature_requirements)?;

4. Model provider management

4.1 ModelProviderInfo

Model providers consist of built-in defaults + custom extensions:

// model_provider_info.rs
pub struct ModelProviderInfo {
    pub name: String,                               // ي‘œى‹œëھ…
    pub base_url: Option<String>,                   // API ى—”ë“œيڈ¬ى‌¸يٹ¸
    pub env_key: Option<String>,                    // API ي‚¤ ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک
    pub wire_api: WireApi,                          // responses (chatëٹ” ى œê±°ëگ¨)
    pub query_params: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub http_headers: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub env_http_headers: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub request_max_retries: Option<u64>,
    pub stream_max_retries: Option<u64>,
    pub stream_idle_timeout_ms: Option<u64>,
    // ...
}

Built-in providers:

  • openai â€” OpenAI API (default)
  • ollama â€” Ollama OSS
  • lmstudio â€” LM Studio OSS

Custom providers can be added to the [model_providers] table, but built-in IDs cannot be overridden:

# config.toml ىکˆى‹œ
[model_providers.my-local]
name = "My Local LLM"
base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1"
wire_api = "responses"
env_key = "MY_LOCAL_API_KEY"

4.2 Profile system

With ConfigProfile you can convert model + sandbox + feature as one unit:

// config/profile.rs
pub struct ConfigProfile {
    pub model: Option<String>,
    pub model_provider: Option<String>,
    pub approval_policy: Option<AskForApproval>,
    pub sandbox_mode: Option<SandboxMode>,
    pub model_reasoning_effort: Option<ReasoningEffort>,
    pub model_reasoning_summary: Option<ReasoningSummary>,
    pub model_verbosity: Option<Verbosity>,
    pub personality: Option<Personality>,
    pub features: Option<FeaturesToml>,
    pub tools: Option<ToolsToml>,
    pub web_search: Option<WebSearchMode>,
    // ...
}

# config.toml â€” ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ ى „ي™ک ىکˆى‹œ
profile = "fast"

[profiles.fast]
model = "gpt-4.1-mini"
model_reasoning_effort = "low"
sandbox_mode = "write"

[profiles.careful]
model = "o3"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
approval_policy = "unless-allow-listed"
sandbox_mode = "locked"

5. Config editing system

The ConfigEdit enum in config/edit.rs defines the atomic unit of configuration change. ConfigEditsBuilder updates config.toml with an atomic write:

// config/edit.rs â€” ى§€ى›گëگکëٹ” يژ¸ى§‘ ى—°ى‚°
pub enum ConfigEdit {
    SetModel { model: Option<String>, effort: Option<ReasoningEffort> },
    SetServiceTier { service_tier: Option<ServiceTier> },
    SetModelPersonality { personality: Option<Personality> },
    ReplaceMcpServers(BTreeMap<String, McpServerConfig>),
    SetSkillConfig { path: PathBuf, enabled: bool },
    SetProjectTrustLevel { path: PathBuf, level: TrustLevel },
    SetPath { segments: Vec<String>, value: TomlItem },     // ى‍„ى‌ک dotted-path ى„¤ى •
    ClearPath { segments: Vec<String> },                     // ى‍„ى‌ک dotted-path ى‚­ى œ
    // ê°پى¢… notice/flag ي† ê¸€...
}

6. Agent role system

config/agent_roles.rs merges agent role definitions for each layer. Roles are loaded in two paths: TOML declaration + auto-discovery of agents/ directory:

// config/agent_roles.rs â€” ë ˆى‌´ى–´ë³„ ى—­ي•  ë³‘ي•©
pub(crate) fn load_agent_roles(
    cfg: &ConfigToml,
    config_layer_stack: &ConfigLayerStack,
    startup_warnings: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> std::io::Result<BTreeMap<String, AgentRoleConfig>> {
    // ë‚®ى‌€ ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ë¶€ي„° ىˆœيڑŒي•کë©° ى—­ي•  ىˆکى§‘
    let layers = config_layer_stack.get_layers(
        ConfigLayerStackOrdering::LowestPrecedenceFirst, false);
    let mut roles: BTreeMap<String, AgentRoleConfig> = BTreeMap::new();
    for layer in layers {
        // 1) [agents.roles] ى„ ى–¸ يŒŒى‹±
        // 2) agents/ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ى—گى„œ .toml يŒŒى‌¼ ى‍گëڈ™ ë°œê²¬
        // 3) ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ â†’ ىƒپىœ„ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ê°€ ي•کىœ„ ي•„ë“œ merge
    }
    Ok(roles)
}

7. ConfigService â€” Runtime Configuration API

ConfigService in config/service.rs provides CRUD of settings through app-server. Supports layer metadata query, per-key override status, and batch write:

graph LR
    APP["App Server / TUI"] --> SVC["ConfigService"]
    SVC --> READ["ConfigReadParams\në ˆى‌´ى–´ ë©”يƒ€ + per-key origin"]
    SVC --> WRITE["ConfigValueWriteParams\ndotted-path ë‹¨ى‌¼ ى“°ê¸°"]
    SVC --> BATCH["ConfigBatchWriteParams\në‹¤ى¤‘ ي‚¤ atomic ى“°ê¸°"]
    SVC --> TOML["config.toml\natomic file write"]

8. JSON Schema creation

config/schema.rs automatically creates Draft-07 JSON Schema from ConfigToml. The features and mcp_servers keys are treated as custom schemas:

// config/schema.rs
pub fn config_schema() -> RootSchema {
    SchemaSettings::draft07()
        .with(|settings| { settings.option_add_null_type = false; })
        .into_generator()
        .into_root_schema_for::<ConfigToml>()
}

// features ي‚¤: ى•Œë ¤ى§„ feature + legacy keyë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©, additional_properties = false
pub(crate) fn features_schema(schema_gen: &mut SchemaGenerator) -> Schema {
    for feature in FEATURES {
        validation.properties.insert(feature.key.to_string(), ...);
    }
    validation.additional_properties = Some(Box::new(Schema::Bool(false)));
    ...
}

9. ConfigToml main field map

graph TD
    subgraph "ConfigToml ى£¼ىڑ” ى„¹ى…ک"
        M["model / model_provider\nëھ¨ëچ¸ ى„ يƒ‌"]
        P["permissions\nيŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œآ·ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤"]
        F["features\nê¸°ëٹ¥ ي”Œë‍کê·¸"]
        MCP["mcp_servers\nMCP ى„œë²„ ى •ى‌ک"]
        PRF["profiles\nëھ…ëھ…ëگœ ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼"]
        AG["agents\nى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ى—­ي• آ·ىٹ¤ë ˆë“œ ى œي•œ"]
        MEM["memories\në©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى„œë¸Œى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ"]
        SK["skills\nىٹ¤ي‚¬ ي† ê¸€"]
        PL["plugins\nي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ ى„¤ى •"]
        PRJ["projects\nي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ë³„ trust_level"]
        TUI["tui\nUI ى„¤ى • (ي…Œë§ˆ, ىƒپيƒœى¤„ ë“±)"]
    end

Technical Reference

Internal structure â€” config_loader

file Role
config_loader/mod.rs load_config_layers_state() entry point, layer + requirements assembly
config_loader/layer_io.rs managed_config.toml, read MDM config, return LoadedConfigLayers
config_loader/macos.rs macOS managed preferences integration (config + requirements)

Internal structure â€” config module

file Role
config/mod.rs (114KB) Config, ConfigToml, ConfigBuilder, load_config_with_layer_stack()
config/permissions.rs Compile PermissionsToml â†’ FileSystemSandboxPolicy + NetworkSandboxPolicy
config/managed_features.rs ManagedFeatures â€” feature flag + requirements apply constraints
config/profile.rs ConfigProfile â€” Named profile structure
config/edit.rs ConfigEdit enum + ConfigEditsBuilder â€” config.toml atomic edit
config/agent_roles.rs Agent role loading + merging by layer + automatic discovery of agents/ directory
config/schema.rs config_schema() â€” Create Draft-07 JSON Schema
config/service.rs ConfigService â€” Runtime configuration CRUD API
config/types.rs Simple struct/enum type definition (no business logic)

config crate (codex-config)

file size Role
config/src/config_requirements.rs 58KB policy constraint definition (ConfigRequirements)
config/src/constraint.rs 8KB Constrained<T>, ConstrainedWithSource<T> constraint type
config/src/diagnostics.rs 12KB Configuration Diagnostics and Error Reporting
config/src/state.rs 11KB ConfigLayerStack, ConfigLayerEntry types

Config structure core fields

field Type Description
config_layer_stack ConfigLayerStack Full layer stack (provenance)
permissions Permissions Final sandbox policy (approval, filesystem, network)
features ManagedFeatures constrained feature flag
model_provider ModelProviderInfo Active Model Provider
mcp_servers Constrained<HashMap<...>> requirements filtered MCP server
enforce_residency Constrained<Option<ResidencyRequirement>> Data local residency constraints
active_project ProjectConfig trust_level of current project
agent_roles BTreeMap<String, AgentRoleConfig> Merged Agent Roles

Fork Perspective

  • 7-layer layering and TOML merge are good for reuse
  • Per-key origin tracking can be used directly in the settings debugging UI
  • ConfigRequirements is the core of policy application, so it must be taken along with exec-policy and sandbox.
  • The pinned + normalize pattern of ManagedFeatures is suitable for enterprise feature gates.
  • ConfigEdit + atomic write is a safe configuration change pattern in a concurrent environment.
  • permission profile + special path system is closely linked to the sandbox.
  • The MDM layer is exclusive to macOS, so the scope must be determined when forking.
  • cloud requirements (cloud_requirements.rs) is OpenAI dependent and therefore subject to removal
  • The built-in provider list in model_provider_info.rs needs to be replaced when forking.

List of core files

  • codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs â€” Config, ConfigToml, ConfigBuilder
  • codex-rs/core/src/config/permissions.rs â€” Permission compilation
  • codex-rs/core/src/config/managed_features.rs â€” feature flag management
  • codex-rs/core/src/config/profile.rs â€” Profile
  • codex-rs/core/src/config/edit.rs â€” Edit operations
  • codex-rs/core/src/config/agent_roles.rs â€” agent role
  • codex-rs/core/src/config/schema.rs â€” JSON Schema generation
  • codex-rs/core/src/config/service.rs â€” ConfigService API
  • codex-rs/core/src/config/types.rs â€” Type definitions
  • codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs â€” Layer loading
  • codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/layer_io.rs â€” File/MDM I/O
  • codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/macos.rs â€” macOS MDM
  • codex-rs/core/src/model_provider_info.rs â€” Model provider
  • codex-rs/config/src/config_requirements.rs â€” Policy constraints
  • codex-rs/config/src/constraint.rs â€” Constrained type
  • codex-rs/config/src/state.rs â€” ConfigLayerStack

Area Snapshot Current

Change history

Codex ى„¤ى • ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ë¶„ى„‌

ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” configuration system.

Codexى‌ک ى„¤ى •ى‌€ ë‹¨ى‌¼ يŒŒى‌¼ى‌´ ى•„ë‹ˆë‹¤. 7ى¸µ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌´ ىڑ´ىکپëگکê³ , ê°پ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ëٹ” TOML يک•ى‹‌ىœ¼ë،œ ى‍¬ê·€ ë³‘ي•©ëگœë‹¤. ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ê°„ ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„ ى¶©ëڈŒى‌„ ي•´ê²°ي•کëٹ” ë°©ى‹‌, per-key origin ى¶”ى پ, ê·¸ë¦¬ê³  requirementsى—گ ى‌کي•œ ى •ى±… ى œى•½ى‌´ ي•µى‹¬ى‌´ë‹¤.

1. ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ى•„ي‚¤ي…چى²ک

ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„(ى•„ë‍کë،œ ê°ˆىˆکë،‌ ë†’ى‌Œ):

  1. System â€” /etc/codex/config.toml (Unix) ëکگëٹ” %ProgramData%\OpenAI\Codex\config.toml (Windows)
  2. User â€” $CODEX_HOME/config.toml
  3. Project â€” CWD ê¸°ى¤€ .codex/config.toml (tree walk + repo root, untrusted ى‹œ disabled)
  4. Session flags â€” CLI --config ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ (dotted-path TOML write)
  5. Legacy managed config â€” managed_config.toml (يŒŒى‌¼ ëکگëٹ” MDM)
  6. MDM managed config â€” macOS managed preferences (defaults ê¸°ë°ک, macOS only)

ë³„ëڈ„ë،œ Requirements ى±„ë„گى‌´ config ë ˆى‌´ى–´ى™€ ëڈ…ë¦½ى پىœ¼ë،œ ى‍‘ëڈ™ي•œë‹¤:

  • Cloud requirements â€” ى›گê²© ê´€ë¦¬يک• ى œى•½
  • MDM requirements â€” macOS managed preferences ê¸°ë°ک ى œى•½
  • System requirements â€” /etc/codex/requirements.toml
graph TD
    subgraph "Config Layers (ë‚®â†’ë†’ ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„)"
        SYS["â‘  System config.toml"]
        USR["â‘، User config.toml"]
        PRJ["â‘¢ Project .codex/config.toml"]
        CLI["â‘£ Session flags (CLI --config)"]
        MCF["â‘¤ Legacy managed_config.toml"]
        MDM["â‘¥ MDM managed config (macOS)"]
    end

    subgraph "Requirements (ى •ى±… ى œى•½)"
        CRQ["Cloud requirements"]
        MRQ["MDM requirements"]
        SRQ["System requirements.toml"]
    end

    SYS --> STACK["ConfigLayerStack"]
    USR --> STACK
    PRJ --> STACK
    CLI --> STACK
    MCF --> STACK
    MDM --> STACK

    CRQ --> REQ["ConfigRequirements"]
    MRQ --> REQ
    SRQ --> REQ

    STACK --> MERGE["effective_config()\nTOML ى‍¬ê·€ ë³‘ي•©"]
    STACK --> ORIG["per-key origins()"]
    REQ --> STACK

    MERGE --> CFG["Config ىµœى¢… êµ¬ى،°ى²´"]
    REQ --> CFG

1.1 ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ë،œë”© ي‌گë¦„

config_loader/mod.rsى‌ک load_config_layers_state()ê°€ ى „ى²´ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ىٹ¤يƒ‌ى‌„ ى،°ë¦½ي•œë‹¤:

// config_loader/mod.rs â€” ى§„ى‍…ى گ
pub async fn load_config_layers_state(
    codex_home: &Path,
    cwd: Option<AbsolutePathBuf>,
    cli_overrides: &[(String, TomlValue)],
    overrides: LoaderOverrides,
    cloud_requirements: CloudRequirementsLoader,
) -> io::Result<ConfigLayerStack> {
    // 1) requirements ىˆکى§‘: cloud â†’ MDM â†’ system requirements.toml
    let mut config_requirements_toml = ConfigRequirementsWithSources::default();
    if let Some(requirements) = cloud_requirements.get().await...? {
        config_requirements_toml.merge_unset_fields(
            RequirementSource::CloudRequirements, requirements);
    }
    // macOS MDM requirements
    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    macos::load_managed_admin_requirements_toml(&mut config_requirements_toml, ...);
    // /etc/codex/requirements.toml
    load_requirements_toml(&mut config_requirements_toml, system_requirements_toml_file()?);

    // 2) config ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ى،°ë¦½: system â†’ user â†’ project â†’ session flags â†’ legacy managed
    let mut layers = Vec::<ConfigLayerEntry>::new();
    layers.push(/* system */);
    layers.push(/* user */);
    // project ë ˆى‌´ى–´: cwdى—گى„œ .codex/ يڈ´ëچ”ë¥¼ tree-walkë،œ ê²€ىƒ‰
    if let Some(cwd) = cwd {
        let project_layers = load_project_layers(&cwd, ...);
        layers.extend(project_layers);
    }
    if let Some(cli_overrides_layer) = cli_overrides_layer {
        layers.push(ConfigLayerEntry::new(ConfigLayerSource::SessionFlags, ...));
    }
    // legacy managed_config.toml (يŒŒى‌¼ + MDM)
    ...

    // 3) ConfigLayerStack êµ¬ى„± â€” layers + requirements â†’ ىµœى¢… ىٹ¤يƒ‌
    ConfigLayerStack::new(layers, config_requirements_toml.try_into()?, ...)
}

1.2 Config ىµœى¢… ë¹Œë“œ

ConfigBuilderê°€ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ىٹ¤يƒ‌ى—گى„œ ىµœى¢… Config êµ¬ى،°ى²´ë¥¼ ë§Œë“ ë‹¤:

// config/mod.rs â€” ConfigBuilder::build()
pub async fn build(self) -> std::io::Result<Config> {
    let config_layer_stack = load_config_layers_state(
        &codex_home, Some(cwd), &cli_overrides, loader_overrides, cloud_requirements,
    ).await?;

    // effective_config(): disabled ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ى œى™¸, ى‍¬ê·€ TOML ë³‘ي•©
    let merged_toml = config_layer_stack.effective_config();

    // TOML Value â†’ ConfigToml ى—­ى§پë ¬ي™”
    let config_toml: ConfigToml = merged_toml.try_into()?;

    // ConfigToml + ConfigOverrides + requirements â†’ ىµœى¢… Config
    Config::load_config_with_layer_stack(config_toml, harness_overrides, codex_home, config_layer_stack)
}

1.3 Requirements ى پىڑ©

Config::load_config_with_layer_stack() ë‚´ë¶€ى—گى„œ requirementsê°€ ê°پ ي•„ë“œى—گ ى پىڑ©ëگœë‹¤:

// config/mod.rs â€” load_config_with_layer_stack()
let ConfigRequirements {
    approval_policy: mut constrained_approval_policy,
    sandbox_policy: mut constrained_sandbox_policy,
    web_search_mode: mut constrained_web_search_mode,
    feature_requirements,
    mcp_servers,
    exec_policy: _,
    enforce_residency,
    network: network_requirements,
} = config_layer_stack.requirements().clone();

// ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى„¤ى •ê°’ى‌´ requirement ى œى•½ى—گ ىœ„ë°کëگکë©´ fallback ى پىڑ© + ê²½ê³  ë°œي–‰
apply_requirement_constrained_value(
    "approval_policy", configured_value,
    &mut constrained_approval_policy, &mut startup_warnings)?;

apply_requirement_constrained_valueëٹ” ى œى•½ ىœ„ë°ک ى‹œ ى‍گëڈ™ىœ¼ë،œ ى•ˆى „ي•œ ê°’ىœ¼ë،œ fallbackي•کê³  ê²½ê³ ë¥¼ ىˆکى§‘ي•œë‹¤:

fn apply_requirement_constrained_value<T>(
    field_name: &'static str,
    configured_value: T,
    constrained_value: &mut ConstrainedWithSource<T>,
    startup_warnings: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    if let Err(err) = constrained_value.set(configured_value) {
        let fallback_value = constrained_value.get().clone();
        startup_warnings.push(format!(
            "Configured value for `{field_name}` is disallowed by requirements; \
             falling back to required value {fallback_value:?}. Details: {err}"
        ));
        constrained_value.set(fallback_value)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}

2. يچ¼ë¯¸ى…ک ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ

config/permissions.rsê°€ TOML ê¸°ë°ک يچ¼ë¯¸ى…ک ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ى‌„ ى»´يŒŒى‌¼ي•œë‹¤. يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œê³¼ ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ë‘گ ى¶•ىœ¼ë،œ ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ ى •ى±…ى‌„ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•œë‹¤.

2.1 يچ¼ë¯¸ى…ک TOML êµ¬ى،°

// config/permissions.rs â€” ي•µى‹¬ يƒ€ى‍…
pub struct PermissionsToml {
    pub entries: BTreeMap<String, PermissionProfileToml>,  // ى‌´ë¦„ë³„ ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼
}

pub struct PermissionProfileToml {
    pub filesystem: Option<FilesystemPermissionsToml>,  // يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ê·œى¹™
    pub network: Option<NetworkToml>,                    // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ê·œى¹™
}

// يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ê²½ë،œë³„ ى ‘ê·¼ ê¶Œي•œ
pub enum FilesystemPermissionToml {
    Access(FileSystemAccessMode),                        // ë‹¨ىˆœ: read/write/read_write
    Scoped(BTreeMap<String, FileSystemAccessMode>),      // ي•کىœ„ê²½ë،œë³„ ى„¸ë¶„ي™”
}

TOML ى„¤ى • ىکˆى‹œ:

[permissions.strict]
[permissions.strict.filesystem]
":project_roots" = "read_write"
":tmpdir" = "read_write"
"/usr/local/bin" = "read"

[permissions.strict.network]
enabled = true
mode = "limited"
allowed_domains = ["api.openai.com", "github.com"]

2.2 يچ¼ë¯¸ى…ک ى»´يŒŒى‌¼ ي‌گë¦„

graph LR
    A["config.toml\n[permissions.NAME]"] --> B["resolve_permission_profile()"]
    B --> C["compile_permission_profile()"]
    C --> D["FileSystemSandboxPolicy"]
    C --> E["NetworkSandboxPolicy"]
    C --> W["startup_warnings"]

    subgraph "يٹ¹ىˆک ê²½ë،œ ي•´ى„‌"
        S1[":root â†’ Root"]
        S2[":minimal â†’ Minimal"]
        S3[":project_roots â†’ ProjectRoots"]
        S4[":tmpdir â†’ Tmpdir"]
        S5[":unknown â†’ Unknown + ê²½ê³ "]
    end
    C --> S1

// config/permissions.rs â€” يچ¼ë¯¸ى…ک ى»´يŒŒى‌¼
pub(crate) fn compile_permission_profile(
    permissions: &PermissionsToml,
    profile_name: &str,
    startup_warnings: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> io::Result<(FileSystemSandboxPolicy, NetworkSandboxPolicy)> {
    let profile = resolve_permission_profile(permissions, profile_name)?;

    // يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ: ê°پ ê²½ë،œë¥¼ FileSystemSandboxEntryë،œ ى»´يŒŒى‌¼
    let mut entries = Vec::new();
    if let Some(filesystem) = profile.filesystem.as_ref() {
        for (path, permission) in &filesystem.entries {
            compile_filesystem_permission(path, permission, &mut entries, startup_warnings)?;
        }
    }
    // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬: enabled ى—¬ë¶€ى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ Enabled/Restricted
    let network_sandbox_policy = compile_network_sandbox_policy(profile.network.as_ref());

    Ok((FileSystemSandboxPolicy::restricted(entries), network_sandbox_policy))
}

2.3 يٹ¹ىˆک ê²½ë،œى™€ ى „ë°© يک¸ي™کى„±

ë¯¸ى‌¸ى‹‌ :special_pathë¥¼ FileSystemSpecialPath::Unknownىœ¼ë،œ round-tripي•کى—¬ ى‌´ى „ ë²„ى „ى—گى„œëڈ„ config loadê°€ ى‹¤يŒ¨ي•کى§€ ى•ٹê²Œ ë³´ى‍¥ي•œë‹¤:

// ى „ë°© يک¸ي™ک يŒŒى„œ: ىƒˆ يٹ¹ىˆک ê²½ë،œ ى¶”ê°€ ى‹œ êµ¬ë²„ى „ى‌´ rejectي•کى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ
fn parse_special_path(path: &str) -> Option<FileSystemSpecialPath> {
    match path {
        ":root"          => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Root),
        ":minimal"       => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Minimal),
        ":project_roots" => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::project_roots(None)),
        ":tmpdir"        => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::Tmpdir),
        _ if path.starts_with(':') => Some(FileSystemSpecialPath::unknown(path, None)),
        _ => None,
    }
}

2.4 NetworkToml ىƒپى„¸

ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى„¤ى •ى‌€ NetworkProxyConfigë،œ ë³€ي™کëگکى–´ ي”„ë،‌ى‹œ ىˆکى¤€ى‌ک ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى œى–´ë¥¼ ىˆکي–‰ي•œë‹¤:

pub struct NetworkToml {
    pub enabled: Option<bool>,
    pub proxy_url: Option<String>,
    pub enable_socks5: Option<bool>,
    pub mode: Option<NetworkMode>,               // limited | full
    pub allowed_domains: Option<Vec<String>>,     // ي™”ى‌´يٹ¸ë¦¬ىٹ¤يٹ¸
    pub denied_domains: Option<Vec<String>>,      // ë¸”ë‍™ë¦¬ىٹ¤يٹ¸
    pub allow_unix_sockets: Option<Vec<String>>,
    pub dangerously_allow_non_loopback_proxy: Option<bool>,
    pub dangerously_allow_all_unix_sockets: Option<bool>,
    // ...
}

3. Feature Flag ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ

config/managed_features.rsê°€ ManagedFeatures ë‍کيچ¼ë¥¼ ي†µي•´ feature flagë¥¼ ê´€ë¦¬ي•œë‹¤. requirementsى—گ ى‌کي•´ pinëگœ featureëٹ” ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گê°€ ë³€ê²½ي•  ىˆک ى—†ë‹¤.

3.1 ManagedFeatures êµ¬ى،°

// config/managed_features.rs
pub struct ManagedFeatures {
    value: ConstrainedWithSource<Features>,
    pinned_features: BTreeMap<Feature, bool>,  // requirementsê°€ ê³ ى •ي•œ feature
}

impl ManagedFeatures {
    // requirementsى—گى„œ pinned feature يŒŒى‹± â†’ ي›„ë³´ ى •ê·œي™” â†’ ê²€ى¦‌
    pub(crate) fn from_configured(
        configured_features: Features,
        feature_requirements: Option<Sourced<FeatureRequirementsToml>>,
    ) -> std::io::Result<Self> {
        let (pinned_features, source) = match feature_requirements {
            Some(Sourced { value, source }) => (
                parse_feature_requirements(value, &source)?, Some(source)
            ),
            None => (BTreeMap::new(), None),
        };
        // pinned featureë¥¼ candidateى—گ ê°•ى œ ى پىڑ© ي›„ ى‌کى،´ى„± ى •ê·œي™”
        let normalized = normalize_candidate(configured_features, &pinned_features);
        validate_pinned_features(&normalized, &pinned_features, source.as_ref())?;
        Ok(Self { ... })
    }

    // ë³€ê²½ ى‹œëڈ„ ى‹œ pinned feature ى œى•½ ê²€ى¦‌
    pub fn set(&mut self, candidate: Features) -> ConstraintResult<()> {
        let normalized = self.normalize_and_validate(candidate)?;
        self.value.value.set(normalized)
    }
}

3.2 Feature ى •ê·œي™” ي‌گë¦„

graph LR
    A["ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ features\n(config.toml)"] --> B["Features::from_sources()"]
    C["ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ features"] --> B
    D["CLI overrides"] --> B
    B --> E["configured_features"]
    F["requirements.toml\nfeature_requirements"] --> G["parse_feature_requirements()"]
    G --> H["pinned_features\nBTreeMap<Feature, bool>"]
    E --> I["normalize_candidate()"]
    H --> I
    I --> J["pin ى پىڑ© + ى‌کى،´ى„± ى •ê·œي™”"]
    J --> K["validate_pinned_features()"]
    K --> L["ManagedFeatures"]

Featureëٹ” ë‹¤ى¤‘ ى†Œىٹ¤ى—گى„œ ي•©ى„±ëگœë‹¤ â€” global config, profile config, legacy ي† ê¸€, CLI override:

// config/mod.rs â€” Feature ي•©ى„±
let configured_features = Features::from_sources(
    FeatureConfigSource {
        features: cfg.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: None,
        experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch: cfg.experimental_use_freeform_apply_patch,
        experimental_use_unified_exec_tool: cfg.experimental_use_unified_exec_tool,
    },
    FeatureConfigSource {  // ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ ë ˆë²¨ ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ
        features: config_profile.features.as_ref(),
        include_apply_patch_tool: config_profile.include_apply_patch_tool,
        ...
    },
    feature_overrides,  // CLI --config ë“±
);
let features = ManagedFeatures::from_configured(configured_features, feature_requirements)?;

4. ëھ¨ëچ¸ ي”„ë،œë°”ى‌´ëچ” ê´€ë¦¬

4.1 ModelProviderInfo

ëھ¨ëچ¸ ي”„ë،œë°”ى‌´ëچ”ëٹ” built-in ê¸°ë³¸ê°’ + ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى •ى‌ک ي™•ى‍¥ىœ¼ë،œ êµ¬ى„±ëگœë‹¤:

// model_provider_info.rs
pub struct ModelProviderInfo {
    pub name: String,                               // ي‘œى‹œëھ…
    pub base_url: Option<String>,                   // API ى—”ë“œيڈ¬ى‌¸يٹ¸
    pub env_key: Option<String>,                    // API ي‚¤ ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک
    pub wire_api: WireApi,                          // responses (chatëٹ” ى œê±°ëگ¨)
    pub query_params: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub http_headers: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub env_http_headers: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub request_max_retries: Option<u64>,
    pub stream_max_retries: Option<u64>,
    pub stream_idle_timeout_ms: Option<u64>,
    // ...
}

Built-in ي”„ë،œë°”ى‌´ëچ”:

  • openai â€” OpenAI API (ê¸°ë³¸)
  • ollama â€” Ollama OSS
  • lmstudio â€” LM Studio OSS

ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى •ى‌ک ي”„ë،œë°”ى‌´ëچ”ëٹ” [model_providers] ي…Œى‌´ë¸”ى—گ ى¶”ê°€ي•کëگک built-in IDëٹ” ىک¤ë²„ë‌¼ى‌´ë“œ ë¶ˆê°€:

# config.toml ىکˆى‹œ
[model_providers.my-local]
name = "My Local LLM"
base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1"
wire_api = "responses"
env_key = "MY_LOCAL_API_KEY"

4.2 ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ

ConfigProfileë،œ ëھ¨ëچ¸ + ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤ + featureë¥¼ ي•œ ë‹¨ىœ„ë،œ ى „ي™کي•  ىˆک ى‍ˆë‹¤:

// config/profile.rs
pub struct ConfigProfile {
    pub model: Option<String>,
    pub model_provider: Option<String>,
    pub approval_policy: Option<AskForApproval>,
    pub sandbox_mode: Option<SandboxMode>,
    pub model_reasoning_effort: Option<ReasoningEffort>,
    pub model_reasoning_summary: Option<ReasoningSummary>,
    pub model_verbosity: Option<Verbosity>,
    pub personality: Option<Personality>,
    pub features: Option<FeaturesToml>,
    pub tools: Option<ToolsToml>,
    pub web_search: Option<WebSearchMode>,
    // ...
}

# config.toml â€” ي”„ë،œيŒŒى‌¼ ى „ي™ک ىکˆى‹œ
profile = "fast"

[profiles.fast]
model = "gpt-4.1-mini"
model_reasoning_effort = "low"
sandbox_mode = "write"

[profiles.careful]
model = "o3"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
approval_policy = "unless-allow-listed"
sandbox_mode = "locked"

5. Config يژ¸ى§‘ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ

config/edit.rsى‌ک ConfigEdit enumى‌´ ى„¤ى • ë³€ê²½ى‌ک ى›گى‍گى پ ë‹¨ىœ„ë¥¼ ى •ى‌کي•œë‹¤. ConfigEditsBuilderê°€ config.tomlى‌„ atomic writeë،œ ê°±ى‹ ي•œë‹¤:

// config/edit.rs â€” ى§€ى›گëگکëٹ” يژ¸ى§‘ ى—°ى‚°
pub enum ConfigEdit {
    SetModel { model: Option<String>, effort: Option<ReasoningEffort> },
    SetServiceTier { service_tier: Option<ServiceTier> },
    SetModelPersonality { personality: Option<Personality> },
    ReplaceMcpServers(BTreeMap<String, McpServerConfig>),
    SetSkillConfig { path: PathBuf, enabled: bool },
    SetProjectTrustLevel { path: PathBuf, level: TrustLevel },
    SetPath { segments: Vec<String>, value: TomlItem },     // ى‍„ى‌ک dotted-path ى„¤ى •
    ClearPath { segments: Vec<String> },                     // ى‍„ى‌ک dotted-path ى‚­ى œ
    // ê°پى¢… notice/flag ي† ê¸€...
}

6. Agent ى—­ي•  ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ

config/agent_roles.rsê°€ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ë³„ agent ى—­ي•  ى •ى‌کë¥¼ ë³‘ي•©ي•œë‹¤. ى—­ي• ى‌€ TOML ى„ ى–¸ + agents/ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ ى‍گëڈ™ ë°œê²¬ ë‘گ ê²½ë،œë،œ ë،œë”©ëگœë‹¤:

// config/agent_roles.rs â€” ë ˆى‌´ى–´ë³„ ى—­ي•  ë³‘ي•©
pub(crate) fn load_agent_roles(
    cfg: &ConfigToml,
    config_layer_stack: &ConfigLayerStack,
    startup_warnings: &mut Vec<String>,
) -> std::io::Result<BTreeMap<String, AgentRoleConfig>> {
    // ë‚®ى‌€ ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ë¶€ي„° ىˆœيڑŒي•کë©° ى—­ي•  ىˆکى§‘
    let layers = config_layer_stack.get_layers(
        ConfigLayerStackOrdering::LowestPrecedenceFirst, false);
    let mut roles: BTreeMap<String, AgentRoleConfig> = BTreeMap::new();
    for layer in layers {
        // 1) [agents.roles] ى„ ى–¸ يŒŒى‹±
        // 2) agents/ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ى—گى„œ .toml يŒŒى‌¼ ى‍گëڈ™ ë°œê²¬
        // 3) ê°™ى‌€ ى‌´ë¦„ â†’ ىƒپىœ„ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ê°€ ي•کىœ„ ي•„ë“œ merge
    }
    Ok(roles)
}

7. ConfigService â€” ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ ى„¤ى • API

config/service.rsى‌ک ConfigServiceê°€ app-serverë¥¼ ي†µي•´ ى„¤ى •ى‌ک CRUDë¥¼ ى œê³µي•œë‹¤. ë ˆى‌´ى–´ ë©”يƒ€ëچ°ى‌´ي„° ى،°يڑŒ, per-key override ىƒپيƒœ, batch writeë¥¼ ى§€ى›گي•œë‹¤:

graph LR
    APP["App Server / TUI"] --> SVC["ConfigService"]
    SVC --> READ["ConfigReadParams\në ˆى‌´ى–´ ë©”يƒ€ + per-key origin"]
    SVC --> WRITE["ConfigValueWriteParams\ndotted-path ë‹¨ى‌¼ ى“°ê¸°"]
    SVC --> BATCH["ConfigBatchWriteParams\në‹¤ى¤‘ ي‚¤ atomic ى“°ê¸°"]
    SVC --> TOML["config.toml\natomic file write"]

8. JSON Schema ىƒ‌ى„±

config/schema.rsê°€ ConfigTomlى—گى„œ Draft-07 JSON Schemaë¥¼ ى‍گëڈ™ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•œë‹¤. featuresى™€ mcp_servers ي‚¤ëٹ” ى»¤ىٹ¤ي…€ ىٹ¤ي‚¤ë§ˆë،œ ى²کë¦¬ëگœë‹¤:

// config/schema.rs
pub fn config_schema() -> RootSchema {
    SchemaSettings::draft07()
        .with(|settings| { settings.option_add_null_type = false; })
        .into_generator()
        .into_root_schema_for::<ConfigToml>()
}

// features ي‚¤: ى•Œë ¤ى§„ feature + legacy keyë§Œ ي—ˆىڑ©, additional_properties = false
pub(crate) fn features_schema(schema_gen: &mut SchemaGenerator) -> Schema {
    for feature in FEATURES {
        validation.properties.insert(feature.key.to_string(), ...);
    }
    validation.additional_properties = Some(Box::new(Schema::Bool(false)));
    ...
}

Axis 09: MCP orchestration

AXIS

009_mcp-orchestration/09_co_mcp.md

Overview

Axis 09: MCP orchestration â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” MCP orchestration.

  • Codexى‌ک MCP ë ˆى‌´ى–´ëٹ” ë‹¨ىˆœي•œ "ى™¸ë¶€ ëڈ„êµ¬ ى—°ê²°"ى‌´ ى•„ë‹ˆë‹¤. ى„¤ى • ë،œëچ”ê°€ ى—¬ëں¬ config layerى—گى„œ MCP ى„œë²„ ى •ى‌کë¥¼ ëھ¨ىœ¼ê³ , McpManagerê°€ pluginى‌´ ë‚´ë†“ëٹ” ى„œë²„ى™€ codex_appsë¥¼ ë‹¤ى‹œ ي•©ى¹کë©°, McpConnectionManagerê°€ ى‹¤ى œ ى—°ê²°ê³¼ listingê³¼ sandbox ىƒپيƒœ ى „يŒŒë¥¼ ë‹´ë‹¹ي•œë‹¤. ê·¸ ىœ„ى—گى„œ mcp_tool_call.rsê°€ ىٹ¹ى‌¸, Guardian ى—°ê³„, ê²°ê³¼ ى •ê·œي™”
  • ى‌´ ى¶•ى‌´ ى¤‘ىڑ”ي•œ ى‌´ىœ ëٹ” MCPê°€ Codexى‌ک ي™•ى‍¥ى„± ëŒ€ë¶€ë¶„ê³¼ ى§پى ‘ ë‹؟ê¸° ë•Œë¬¸ى‌´ë‹¤. ىٹ¤ي‚¬ى‌´ ىڑ”êµ¬ي•کëٹ” MCP dependency ى„¤ى¹ک, pluginى‌´ ى„ ى–¸ي•کëٹ” MCP ى„œë²„, ChatGPT ê³„ى •ى—گ ë¬¶ى‌¸ codex_apps, OAuth ë،œê·¸ى‌¸, sandbox state ى£¼ى‍…ى‌´ ëھ¨ë‘گ ى‌´ ê²½ë،œë¥¼ ى§€ë‚کê°„ë‹¤. Phase C ê²€ى¦‌ى—گى„œ ë²„ي‹°ë ¤ë©´ "ى–´ë””ى—گ ى½”ë“œê°€ ى‍ˆë‹¤" ىˆکى¤€ى‌´ ى•„ë‹ˆë‌¼, ى„¤ى • ë³‘ي•©ê³¼ يک¸ى¶œ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌´ ى–´ë–¤ ë ˆى‌´ى–´ى—گى„œ ë¶„ë¦¬ëگکëٹ”ى§€ê¹Œ
  • ê¸°ى،´ ë¬¸ى„œى‌ک ê²½ë،œ ي‘œê¸° ى¤‘ 120_codex-cli/...ëٹ” ى‹¤ى œ ى €ى‍¥ى†Œ ê¸°ى¤€ê³¼ ë§‍ى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤. ى‌´ ë¬¸ى„œëٹ” ى‹¤ى œ êµ¬يک„ ê²½ë،œى‌¸ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/... ê¸°ى¤€ىœ¼ë،œ ë‹¤ى‹œ ى •ë¦¬ي•œë‹¤.
  • Codexى‌ک MCP ى„¤ى •ى‌€ ي•œ ë²ˆى—گ ى™„ى„±ëگکى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤. ë¨¼ى € 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rsى™€ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rsê°€ system, user, project, runtime layerë¥¼ ë³‘ي•©ي•´ Config.mcp_serversë¥¼ ë§Œë“ ë‹¤. ى‌´ë•Œ requirementsê°€ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ `const
  • ê·¸ ë‹¤ى‌Œ 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rsى‌ک McpManagerê°€ config.mcp_serversى™€ pluginى‌´ ë…¸ى¶œي•œ MCP ى„œë²„ë¥¼ ي•©ى¹œë‹¤. ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ىœ¼ë،œ with_codex_apps_mcp()ê°€ ChatGPT ى‌¸ى¦‌ê³¼ Feature::Appsê°€ ëھ¨ë‘گ ى‚´ى•„ ى‍ˆى‌„ ë•Œë§Œ codex_appsë¥¼ ى£¼ى‍…ي•œë‹¤. ى¦‰, "ى„¤ى •ى—گى„œ ى„ ى–¸ëگœ MCP"ى™€ "ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ى—گى„œ ى¶”ê°€ëگœ MCP

    participant CFG as Config loader

    CFG->>MCP: constrained mcp_servers

    CALL->>CALL: parse args and lookup metadata

    CONN->>RMCP: call tool with timeout
    RMCP->>SRV: tools call request

    CALL-->>MODEL: CallToolResult

Main analysis

Codex MCP orchestration analysis

Analysis snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” MCP orchestration.

Codex's MCP layer is not just a simple "connection to external tools". The configuration loader collects MCP server definitions from multiple config layers, McpManager re-merges the servers released by plugins and codex_apps, and McpConnectionManager is responsible for actual connection, listing, and sandbox status propagation. Above that, mcp_tool_call.rs covers authorization, Guardian linkage, result normalization, and telemetry.

The reason this axis is important is because MCP directly touches most of Codex's scalability. Installation of the MCP dependency required by the skill, MCP server declared by the plugin, codex_apps tied to the ChatGPT account, OAuth login, and sandbox state injection all pass through this path. To survive Phase C verification, you need to show not just â€œwhere the code is,â€‌ but also what layer the configuration merge and call pipeline are separated from.

Among the path notations in the existing document, 120_codex-cli/... does not meet the actual storage standards. This document is reorganized based on the actual implementation path, 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/....

Overview

Setting up Codex's MCP is not completed in one go. First, 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs and 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs merge the system, user, project, and runtime layers to create Config.mcp_servers. At this time, if requirements exist, constrain_mcp_servers() and filter_mcp_servers_by_requirements() disable the server or fill disabled_reason.

Next, McpManager of 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs combines the MCP servers exposed by config.mcp_servers and plugin. Finally, with_codex_apps_mcp() injects codex_apps only when both ChatGPT authentication and Feature::Apps are alive. In other words, â€œMCP declared in settingsâ€‌ and â€œMCP added at runtimeâ€‌ are in the same map, but the assembly steps are different.

The actual connection lifecycle is handled by 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs. This manager creates an AsyncManagedClient for each server, issues startup events, manages the initial tool snapshot and cache, and when the server advertises codex/sandbox-state as an experimental capability, it provides the sandbox status with a codex/sandbox-state/update request.

Tool calls run in a separate pipeline in 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs. Here, it is determined whether approval is needed, an approval decision is received from the Guardian or user input, and the actual call is passed to Session::call_tool() and McpConnectionManager::call_tool(). The timeout is executed not in this file, but in RmcpClient::run_service_operation_once() by moving tool_timeout_sec in the server settings to ManagedClient.tool_timeout.

Architecture

If you look at Codex MCP in layers, it is as follows.

graph TD
    A["config_loader/mod.rs<br/>config layers and requirements"] --> B["config/mod.rs<br/>Config.mcp_servers"]
    P["plugins/manager.rs<br/>active plugin MCP servers"] --> C["mcp/mod.rs<br/>McpManager"]
    B --> C
    AU["CodexAuth and Feature Apps"] --> C
    C --> D["effective server map<br/>config plus plugin plus codex_apps"]
    D --> E["mcp_connection_manager.rs<br/>McpConnectionManager"]
    E --> F["rmcp-client/rmcp_client.rs<br/>stdio or streamable HTTP client"]
    F --> G["external MCP servers"]
    E --> H["tool and resource listing"]
    E --> I["sandbox state update"]
    H --> J["mcp_tool_call.rs<br/>approval and execution pipeline"]
    J --> K["Guardian or user input"]
    J --> L["normalized CallToolResult"]

The point of this structure is that McpManager and McpConnectionManager have different roles. The former deals with â€œhow to assemble the server map,â€‌ and the latter deals with â€œhow to connect and maintain those servers.â€‌ This separation allows plugins and codex_apps to be exposed to the same surface, while approval policies and tool timeouts can be handled in a common pipeline.

Core components

Configuration collection and requirements constraints

load_config_layers_state() in 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs creates ConfigLayerStack by combining multiple config layers and requirements. After that, Config::load_config_with_layer_stack() in 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs wraps cfg.mcp_servers with constrain_mcp_servers(). Here, servers that do not meet the requirements allowlist are not deleted and remain in the enabled = false and disabled_reason = Requirements states.

This design is important from a documentation perspective. Even though the MCP configuration appears to be "a single [mcp_servers] table", the actual runtime value is not raw TOML but Constrained<HashMap<String, McpServerConfig>> with the requirements applied. Therefore, even if the fork simplifies the config layer, if the requirements-based disable reason is abandoned, operational visibility also disappears.

McpManager and server map assembly

In 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs, configured_mcp_servers() first gets the custom server with config.mcp_servers.get().clone(), then plugins_manager.plugins_for_config(config).effective_mcp_servers() with or_insert. Add: In other words, in case of same name conflict, config takes precedence over plugin.

effective_mcp_servers() applies with_codex_apps_mcp() once more here. codex_apps is not a regular plugin server, but a Streamable HTTP server derived from Config.chatgpt_base_url and CodexAuth. Since apps_enabled_for_auth() in features/src/lib.rs requires both Feature::Apps and CodexAuth::is_chatgpt_auth(), this server is tightly coupled to the OpenAI account model.

Connection lifecycle with McpConnectionManager

McpConnectionManager::new() in 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs selects only enabled servers and creates AsyncManagedClient. Each server sends a Starting event, performs a handshake asynchronously, and sends a Ready or Failed status back to McpStartupUpdateEvent. The entire result is grouped into McpStartupCompleteEvent at the end.

Connection creation varies by transport. If make_rmcp_client() is stdio, it calls RmcpClient::new_stdio_client(), and if it is streamable HTTP, it calls RmcpClient::new_streamable_http_client(). If there is no startup_timeout_sec for each server, startup timeout is corrected to the default value and goes down to start_server_task() and RmcpClient::connect_pending_transport().

The interesting part is the initial display performance. AsyncManagedClient can pre-read the startup cache snapshot for codex_apps, and returns the cached snapshot first from listed_tools() even if the actual server is still initializing. In other words, Codex does not want to leave the tool surface empty while waiting for MCP startup.

listing, namespace normalization, sandbox state injection

The listing is processed in three ways within the same file. list_all_tools() combines the listed_tools() of each ManagedClient to create a fully qualified tool name with qualify_tools(). list_all_resources() and list_all_resource_templates() iterate through the pagination cursor for each server and collect until the end. If a duplicate cursor appears, it immediately stops with a warning error.

Normalization is especially noticeable in codex_apps. normalize_codex_apps_tool_name(), normalize_codex_apps_tool_title(), and normalize_codex_apps_namespace() remove the connector prefix and create a namespace of the type mcp__codex_apps__connector_name__tool. On top of that, qualify_tools() applies sanitize and hash-based disambiguation once more to meet the Responses API name restrictions.

The sandbox state is connected to the SandboxState structure and the codex/sandbox-state capability. When the server advertises this experimental capability in the initialize result, ManagedClient::notify_sandbox_state_change() sends a codex/sandbox-state/update custom request. No action is taken on unsupported servers. In other words, sandbox state injection is based on capability negotiation, not global enforcement.

OAuth and authentication status calculation

121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/auth.rs is responsible for auth status calculation and scope interpretation. compute_auth_statuses() runs determine_streamable_http_auth_status() for each server, and resolve_oauth_scopes() sets scope priorities in the order of explicit, configured, and discovered.

Actual transport and token persistence are in 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/rmcp_client.rs and 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/oauth.rs. RmcpClient first creates OAuth transport if there is an OAuth token stored in the streamable HTTP server, and reconnects with reinitialize_after_session_expiry() when session expiration 404 is received. Therefore, Codex's OAuth is not a "one-time login and it's over", but a long-term connection model that includes refresh before operation and session-expiry recovery.

tool call approval, timeout, normalization, error handling

handle_mcp_tool_call() of 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs first parses JSON arguments and searches tool annotation and connector information with lookup_mcp_tool_metadata(). In the case of codex_apps, the enable status and approval mode for each connector are read through connectors::app_tool_policy().

Approval is gathered in maybe_request_mcp_tool_approval(). If it is a destructive tool or read_only_hint == false and open_world_hint == true, approval is required. Even if it is an auto approve policy, the ARC monitor can check again and return AskUser or SteerModel. If the Guardian is activated, it is sent to review_approval_request(), otherwise it is asked through elicitation or general user input.

Persistent approval only goes to the codex_apps connector tool. persist_codex_app_tool_approval() directly writes apps.<connector_id>.tools.<tool_name>.approval_mode = "approve" in $CODEX_HOME/config.toml and then calls reload_user_config_layer(). General MCP server approval remains only in session memory.

timeout is not calculated directly in this file. In actual timeout enforcement, McpConnectionManager::call_tool() passes ManagedClient.tool_timeout to RmcpClient::call_tool(), and RmcpClient::run_service_operation_once() wraps it in tokio::time::timeout(). In other words, according to the document, "MCP tool call timeout" is the responsibility of the connection manager and rmcp client, not mcp_tool_call.rs.

Normalizing the results also involves two steps. First, McpConnectionManager::call_tool() converts the content block of the rmcp result into a JSON value and repackages it as CallToolResult for Codex. After that, sanitize_mcp_tool_result_for_model() replaces the image block with a text placeholder if the current model does not support image input. Errors are wrapped in the form tool call error: ..., and at the startup stage, auth-required, startup-timeout, and GitHub PAT special cases are converted into messages that are easy for humans to read.

Connection point between plugin and codex_apps

There are two plugin combinations. effective_mcp_servers() of 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/plugins/manager.rs exposes the MCP server of the active plugin, and PluginCapabilitySummary collects connector id and server name provenance. AsyncManagedClient::listed_tools() adds this provenance to the tool description to surface "which plugin this tool is part of".

The codex_apps binding extends to the connector surface. 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/connectors.rs uses with_codex_apps_mcp() separately from the general session manager to temporarily create a McpConnectionManager that only launches codex_apps, and extracts the accessible connector list and cache from it. In other words, codex_apps is an MCP server, but it is also the backend of the app directory and connector permission model.

Data flow

The startup and listing flow is as follows.

sequenceDiagram
    participant CFG as Config loader
    participant MCP as McpManager
    participant PLG as PluginsManager
    participant AUTH as Auth manager
    participant CONN as McpConnectionManager
    participant RMCP as RmcpClient
    participant SRV as MCP server

    CFG->>MCP: constrained mcp_servers
    PLG->>MCP: plugin MCP servers
    AUTH->>MCP: auth for codex_apps decision
    MCP->>CONN: effective server map
    CONN->>RMCP: create per server client
    RMCP->>SRV: initialize handshake
    SRV-->>RMCP: capabilities and server info
    CONN->>SRV: sandbox state update if supported
    CONN->>SRV: list tools resources templates
    CONN-->>CFG: qualified tool map and auth statuses

The tool call flow is a separate pipeline separate from startup.

sequenceDiagram
    participant MODEL as Model
    participant CALL as mcp_tool_call.rs
    participant GUARD as Guardian or user input
    participant SESS as Session
    participant CONN as McpConnectionManager
    participant RMCP as RmcpClient
    participant SRV as MCP server

    MODEL->>CALL: tool call request
    CALL->>CALL: parse args and lookup metadata
    CALL->>GUARD: approval check if needed
    GUARD-->>CALL: accept decline or remember
    CALL->>SESS: call_tool
    SESS->>CONN: server and tool dispatch
    CONN->>RMCP: call tool with timeout
    RMCP->>SRV: tools call request
    SRV-->>RMCP: tool result
    RMCP-->>CONN: normalized rmcp result
    CALL->>CALL: sanitize result for model input mode
    CALL-->>MODEL: CallToolResult

There are four turning points to look at in this flow.

  • Configuration merge branch: config layer and requirements end up in config_loader/mod.rs and config/mod.rs, and plugin and codex_apps additions happen in mcp/mod.rs.
  • Connection branch: stdio and streamable HTTP transport branch in make_rmcp_client() in mcp_connection_manager.rs.
  • Approval branch: Guardian path and direct user input path diverge in maybe_request_mcp_tool_approval() in mcp_tool_call.rs.
  • Timeout branch: startup timeout applies to handshake and initial listing, and tool timeout applies to operation at the time of call.

Technical Reference

Direct answers to key questions

Question Answer supporting file
Where are MCP server settings gathered and merged Config layer merging is done by load_config_layers_state() and Config::load_config_with_layer_stack(), plugin MCP merge is done by configured_mcp_servers(), and codex_apps injection is done by effective_mcp_servers() and with_codex_apps_mcp() 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs, 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs, 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs
Where does connection lifecycle, listing, auth, sandbox state injection take place McpConnectionManager::new() creates the startup lifecycle, list_all_tools, list_all_resources, and list_all_resource_templates are responsible for listing, auth status is provided by compute_auth_statuses(), and sandbox state is provided by notify_sandbox_state_change() 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs, 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/auth.rs
What pipeline does tool call approval, timeout, normalization, and error handling go through? Approval is maybe_request_mcp_tool_approval(), timeout is passed from ManagedClient.tool_timeout to RmcpClient::run_service_operation_once(), normalization is McpConnectionManager::call_tool() and sanitize_mcp_tool_result_for_model(), and error handling is through startup display helper and tool call. Wrapping is in charge 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs, 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs, 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/rmcp_client.rs
What connection does it have with plugin or codex_apps? The plugin combines active plugin MCP servers and tool provenance annotation, and codex_apps connects connector directory, approval persistence, and accessible connector cache. 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/plugins/manager.rs, 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs, 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/connectors.rs

Component Mapping

Component responsibility Core function or type
config layer Collect MCP server raw settings and apply requirements load_config_layers_state, constrain_mcp_servers, filter_mcp_servers_by_requirements
server map assembler Assemble config, plugin, and codex_apps into one map McpManager, configured_mcp_servers, effective_mcp_servers
connection runtime Server startup, tool listing, resource listing, sandbox state update McpConnectionManager, AsyncManagedClient, ManagedClient
rmcp transport stdio and streamable HTTP connection, OAuth refresh, session recovery RmcpClient, new_stdio_client, new_streamable_http_client, reinitialize_after_session_expiry
approval runtime Guardian, elicitation, session remember, persistent remember handle_mcp_tool_call, maybe_request_mcp_tool_approval, persist_codex_app_tool_approval
template layer Normalize acknowledgment text and parameter display render_mcp_tool_approval_template, consequential_tool_message_templates.json

timeout and normalization points

Topic Implementation location observation point
startup timeout mcp_connection_manager.rs, rmcp_client.rs Applies to steps prior to handshake and initial tool listing
tool timeout mcp_connection_manager.rs, rmcp_client.rs tool_timeout_sec is used as the default timeout for each server
tool name normalization mcp_connection_manager.rs Remove codex_apps connector prefix, sanitize, hash-based disambiguation
result normalization mcp_connection_manager.rs, mcp_tool_call.rs Change rmcp content to JSON value and replace image block according to model capability
startup error display mcp_connection_manager.rs Change the auth required, GitHub PAT information, and startup timeout messages to be easier for humans to read

Fork Perspective

  • Separation of McpManager and McpConnectionManager is worth maintaining. The server assembly and connection lifecycle must be separated so that plugin merge and runtime cache can be changed independently.
  • Session expiry recovery and OAuth refresh of rmcp-client are highly versatile. If it is a fork that will continue to use streamable HTTP MCP, it is worth taking.
  • The SandboxState custom request is a pretty good extension. It is more portable than forced coupling because it receives status only when the server advertises capabilities.
  • Preserving disabled_reason based on [x] requirements is beneficial for operational debugging. Reducing it to a simple allowlist filter makes it difficult to explain why it was disabled.
  • codex_apps auto-injection is strongly tied to OpenAI and ChatGPT auth. If it is a general-purpose fork, it is better to hide it behind the feature flag or separate it into a separate adapter.
  • The two-way coupling between connectors.rs and MCP is large. If you don't need an app directory, accessible connector cache, or approval persistence, this layer can be boldly removed.
  • mcp_tool_approval_templates.rs and consequential_tool_message_templates.json are good for UX quality, but are not essential cores. If there is no app connector product line, it can be abbreviated.
  • The automatic installation of the MCP dependency of the skill relies on the premise of a first-party originator. If it is a fork for external distribution, the installation UX needs to be redesigned.

List of core files

path Role
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/mod.rs MCP server map assembly, codex_apps injection, snapshot collection, tool name split helper
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_connection_manager.rs Connection lifecycle, tool resource listing, cache, sandbox state notification, timeout delivery
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_call.rs Approval, Guardian linkage, tool call entry point, result sanitize, approval persistence
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp/auth.rs auth status calculation, OAuth scope priority, login support determination
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/plugins/manager.rs plugin MCP server discovery, provenance summary, active plugin merge
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs MCP server constraints based on requirements, final Config.mcp_servers generation
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config_loader/mod.rs Assembling the config layer stack and requirements stack
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/connectors.rs codex_apps based connector surface, accessible connector cache
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/rmcp-client/src/rmcp_client.rs Transport creation, initialize, timeout execution, OAuth refresh, session recovery
121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/mcp_tool_approval_templates.rs Approval prompt template matching and display parameter rendering

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release â€” re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

Change history

  • 2026-03-26: The 120_codex-cli/... error path was corrected to 121_openai-codex/..., and the settings merge, connection lifecycle, and approval timeout normalization paths were rewritten based on actual implementation.
  • 2026-03-26: The codex_apps, plugin provenance, and connector connection points were separated into separate sections to reveal the decision points to keep or remove when forking.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 009_mcp-orchestration/09_co_mcp.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

Codex MCP ىک¤ى¼€ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë ˆى‌´ى…ک ë¶„ى„‌

ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” MCP orchestration.

codex_apps ê²°ي•©ى‌€ connector surfaceê¹Œى§€ ى‌´ى–´ى§„ë‹¤. 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/connectors.rsëٹ” ى‌¼ë°ک session managerى™€ ë³„ê°œë،œ with_codex_apps_mcp()ë¥¼ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•´ codex_appsë§Œ ë‌„ىڑ´ McpConnectionManagerë¥¼ ى‍„ى‹œë،œ ë§Œë“¤ê³ , ê·¸ ى•ˆى—گى„œ accessible connector ëھ©ë،‌ê³¼ cacheë¥¼ ë½‘ى•„ë‚¸ë‹¤. ى¦‰, codex_appsëٹ” MCP ى„œë²„ى‌´ى§€ë§Œ ëڈ™ى‹œى—گ app directoryى™€ connector ê¶Œي•œ ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌ک ë°±ى—”ë“œى‌´ê¸°ëڈ„ ي•کë‹¤.

ëچ°ى‌´ي„° ي‌گë¦„

ى‹œى‍‘ê³¼ listing ي‌گë¦„ى‌€ ë‹¤ى‌Œê³¼ ê°™ë‹¤.

sequenceDiagram
    participant CFG as Config loader
    participant MCP as McpManager
    participant PLG as PluginsManager
    participant AUTH as Auth manager
    participant CONN as McpConnectionManager
    participant RMCP as RmcpClient
    participant SRV as MCP server

    CFG->>MCP: constrained mcp_servers
    PLG->>MCP: plugin MCP servers
    AUTH->>MCP: auth for codex_apps decision
    MCP->>CONN: effective server map
    CONN->>RMCP: create per server client
    RMCP->>SRV: initialize handshake
    SRV-->>RMCP: capabilities and server info
    CONN->>SRV: sandbox state update if supported
    CONN->>SRV: list tools resources templates
    CONN-->>CFG: qualified tool map and auth statuses

ëڈ„êµ¬ يک¸ى¶œ ي‌گë¦„ى‌€ startupê³¼ ë¶„ë¦¬ëگœ ë³„ëڈ„ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌´ë‹¤.

sequenceDiagram
    participant MODEL as Model
    participant CALL as mcp_tool_call.rs
    participant GUARD as Guardian or user input
    participant SESS as Session
    participant CONN as McpConnectionManager
    participant RMCP as RmcpClient
    participant SRV as MCP server

    MODEL->>CALL: tool call request
    CALL->>CALL: parse args and lookup metadata
    CALL->>GUARD: approval check if needed
    GUARD-->>CALL: accept decline or remember
    CALL->>SESS: call_tool
    SESS->>CONN: server and tool dispatch
    CONN->>RMCP: call tool with timeout
    RMCP->>SRV: tools call request
    SRV-->>RMCP: tool result
    RMCP-->>CONN: normalized rmcp result
    CALL->>CALL: sanitize result for model input mode
    CALL-->>MODEL: CallToolResult

Axis 10: Memory pipeline

AXIS

010_memory-pipeline/10_co_memory.md

Overview

Axis 10: Memory pipeline â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” memory pipeline.

    1. State DB ى¤‘ى‹¬ coordination: SQLite ê¸°ë°ک state DBê°€ job ى†Œىœ ê¶Œ, lease, watermarkى‌„ ê´€ë¦¬ي•کى—¬ ë‹¤ى¤‘ ى„¸ى…ک ê°„ ى¤‘ë³µ ى‍‘ى—…ى‌„ ë°©ى§€ي•œë‹¤.
    1. Progressive disclosure: ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ يŒŒى‌¼ى‌„ memory_summary.md â†’ MEMORY.md â†’ rollout_summaries/ â†’ skills/ ىˆœىœ¼ë،œ ê³„ى¸µي™”ي•کى—¬, ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ê°€ ي•„ىڑ”ي•œ ê¹ٹى‌´ë§Œيپ¼ë§Œ يƒگىƒ‰ي•œë‹¤.
    1. Secret redaction: ى¶”ى¶œëگœ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ى—گى„œ ي† يپ°/ي‚¤/ë¹„ë°€ë²ˆيک¸ë¥¼ ى‍گëڈ™ ى œê±°ي•œë‹¤.

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/start.rs
pub(crate) fn start_memories_startup_task(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    config: Arc<Config>,
    source: &SessionSource,
) {
    // ë‹¤ى‌Œ ى،°ê±´ى—گى„œ skip:
    if config.ephemeral
        || !config.features.enabled(Feature::MemoryTool)
        || matches!(source, SessionSource::SubAgent(_))
    {
        return;
    }

    if session.services.state_db.is_none() {
        warn!("state db unavailable for memories startup pipeline; skipping");
        return;
    }

    let weak_session = Arc::downgrade(session);
    tokio::spawn(async move {
        let Some(session) = weak_session.upgrade() else { return; };
        phase1::prune(&session, &config).await;  // ىک¤ë‍کëگœ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى •ë¦¬
        phase1::run(&session, &config).await;     // Phase 1 ى‹¤ي–‰
        phase2::run(&session, config).await;      // Phase 2 ى‹¤ي–‰
    });
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/project_doc.rs
pub const DEFAULT_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.md";
pub const LOCAL_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.override.md";

fn candidate_filenames<'a>(config: &'a Config) -> Vec<&'a str> {
    let mut names = Vec::with_capacity(2 + config.project_doc_fallback_filenames.len());
    names.push(LOCAL_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME);   // ىµœىڑ°ى„ 
    names.push(DEFAULT_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME); // ê¸°ë³¸
    for candidate in &config.project_doc_fallback_filenames {
        if !candidate.is_empty() && !names.contains(&candidate.as_str()) {
            names.push(candidate);
        }
    }
    names
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs
pub const USER_INSTRUCTIONS_PREFIX: &str = "# AGENTS.md instructions for ";

impl UserInstructions {
    pub(crate) fn serialize_to_text(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "{prefix}{directory}\n\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\n{contents}\n{suffix}",
            prefix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.start_marker(),
            directory = self.directory,
            contents = self.text,
            suffix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.end_marker(),
        )
    }
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/prompts.rs
pub(crate) async fn build_memory_tool_developer_instructions(
    codex_home: &Path,
) -> Option<String> {
    let base_path = memory_root(codex_home);
    let memory_summary_path = base_path.join("memory_summary.md");
    let memory_summary = fs::read_to_string(&memory_summary_path).await.ok()?
        .trim().to_string();
    let memory_summary = truncate_text(
        &memory_summary,
        TruncationPolicy::Tokens(
            phase_one::MEMORY_TOOL_DEVELOPER_INSTRUCTIONS_SUMMARY_TOKEN_LIMIT // 5,000 ي† يپ°
        ),
    );
    if memory_summary.is_empty() { return None; }
    // read_path.md ي…œي”Œë¦؟ىœ¼ë،œ ë Œëچ”ë§پ
    let template = MemoryToolDeveloperInstructionsTemplate {
        base_path: &base_path.display().to_string(),
        memory_summary: &memory_summary,
    };
    template.render().ok()
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
pub(in crate::memories) async fn run(session: &Arc<Session>, config: &Config) {
    // 1. claim eligible rollout jobs
    let Some(claimed_candidates) = claim_startup_jobs(session, &config.memories).await
        else { return; };

    // 2. build one stage-1 request context
    let stage_one_context = build_request_context(session, config).await;

    // 3. run stage-1 extraction jobs in parallel
    let outcomes = run_jobs(session, claimed_candidates, stage_one_context).await;

    // 4. emit metrics and logs
    let counts = aggregate_stats(outcomes);
    emit_metrics(session, &counts);
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/state/src/runtime/memories.rs â€” claim_stage1_jobs_for_startup()
// ي•„ي„° ى،°ê±´:
// - memory_mode = 'enabled'
// - يک„ى‍¬ ى„¸ى…کى‌ک thread_id ى œى™¸
// - updated_at >= now - max_age_days  (ë„ˆë¬´ ىک¤ë‍کëگœ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// - updated_at <= now - min_rollout_idle_hours  (ى•„ى§پ ي™œى„±ى‌¸ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// - COALESCE(stage1_outputs.source_updated_at, -1) < updated_at  (ى‌´ë¯¸ ى²کë¦¬ëگœ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// - COALESCE(jobs.last_success_watermark, -1) < updated_at  (ى‌´ë¯¸ ى„±ê³µي•œ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// ORDER BY updated_at DESC, id DESC
// LIMIT scan_limit (5,000)

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
async fn run_jobs(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    claimed_candidates: Vec<codex_state::Stage1JobClaim>,
    stage_one_context: RequestContext,
) -> Vec<JobResult> {
    futures::stream::iter(claimed_candidates.into_iter())
        .map(|claim| {
            let session = Arc::clone(session);
            let stage_one_context = stage_one_context.clone();
            async move { job::run(session.as_ref(), claim, &stage_one_context).await }
        })
        .buffer_unordered(phase_one::CONCURRENCY_LIMIT)  // ىµœëŒ€ 8ê°œ ëڈ™ى‹œ
        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
        .await
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs â€” job::sample()
async fn sample(session, rollout_path, rollout_cwd, stage_one_context) {
    // 1. ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ ى•„ى‌´ي…œ ë،œë“œ
    let (rollout_items, _, _) = RolloutRecorder::load_rollout_items(rollout_path).await?;

    // 2. ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ê´€ë ¨ response itemsë§Œ ي•„ي„°ë§پ + ى§پë ¬ي™”
    let rollout_contents = serialize_filtered_rollout_response_items(&rollout_items)?;

    // 3. ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ىœˆëڈ„ىڑ°ى‌ک 70%ê¹Œى§€ truncate
    let prompt = Prompt {
        input: vec![/* build_stage_one_input_message() */],
        base_instructions: BaseInstructions {
            text: phase_one::PROMPT.to_string(),  // stage_one_system.md
        },
        output_schema: Some(output_schema()),     // JSON schema ê°•ى œ
        ..
    };

    // 4. ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ى‌‘ë‹µ ىˆکى§‘
    let mut stream = client_session.stream(&prompt, ...).await?;
    // ...

    // 5. secret redaction
    let mut output: StageOneOutput = serde_json::from_str(&result)?;
    output.raw_memory = redact_secrets(output.raw_memory);
    output.rollout_summary = redact_secrets(output.rollout_summary);
    output.rollout_slug = output.rollout_slug.map(redact_secrets);
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
pub fn output_schema() -> Value {
    json!({
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "rollout_summary": { "type": "string" },
            "rollout_slug": { "type": ["string", "null"] },
            "raw_memory": { "type": "string" }
        },
        "required": ["rollout_summary", "rollout_slug", "raw_memory"],
        "additionalProperties": false
    })
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
pub(in crate::memories) async fn prune(session: &Arc<Session>, config: &Config) {
    if let Some(db) = session.services.state_db.as_deref() {
        let max_unused_days = config.memories.max_unused_days;
        match db.prune_stage1_outputs_for_retention(max_unused_days, PRUNE_BATCH_SIZE).await {
            Ok(pruned) => { /* log */ },
            Err(err) => { /* warn */ },
        }
    }
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” run()
pub(super) async fn run(session: &Arc<Session>, config: Arc<Config>) {
    // 1. Claim the global consolidation job
    let claim = job::claim(session, db).await?;

    // 2. Get agent config (sandbox policy ë“±)
    let agent_config = agent::get_config(config.clone());

    // 3. Query stage-1 outputs with selection rules
    let selection = db.get_phase2_input_selection(max_raw_memories, max_unused_days).await?;

    // 4. Sync filesystem artifacts
    sync_rollout_summaries_from_memories(&root, &artifact_memories, ...).await?;
    rebuild_raw_memories_file_from_memories(&root, &artifact_memories, ...).await?;

    // 5. Spawn consolidation sub-agent
    let prompt = agent::get_prompt(config, &selection);
    let thread_id = session.services.agent_control
        .spawn_agent(agent_config, prompt, Some(source)).await?;

    // 6. Handle agent lifecycle (heartbeat + status watch)
    agent::handle(session, claim, new_watermark, raw_memories, thread_id, timer);

    // 7. Emit metrics
}

    pub removed: Vec<Stage1OutputRef>,         // ى‌´ى „ى—گ ى„ يƒ‌ëگگى§€ë§Œ يک„ى‍¬ ë¹ ى§„ ى‍…ë ¥

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/storage.rs

// 1. rollout_summaries/ ëڈ™ê¸°ي™”: ىœ ى§€ ëŒ€ىƒپë§Œ ë‚¨ê¸°ê³  ë‚کë¨¸ى§€ ى‚­ى œ
async fn sync_rollout_summaries_from_memories(root, memories, max) {
    let retained = retained_memories(memories, max);
    let keep = retained.iter().map(rollout_summary_file_stem).collect::<HashSet<_>>();
    prune_rollout_summaries(root, &keep).await?;
    for memory in retained {
        write_rollout_summary_for_thread(root, memory).await?;
    }
    // retainedê°€ ë¹„ى–´ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ MEMORY.md, memory_summary.md, skills/ ëڈ„ ى‚­ى œ
}

// 2. raw_memories.md ى‍¬ىƒ‌ى„±: ىµœى‹  ىˆœىœ¼ë،œ ë³‘ي•©
async fn rebuild_raw_memories_file(root, memories, max) {
    let mut body = String::from("# Raw Memories\n\n");
    body.push_str("Merged stage-1 raw memories (latest first):\n\n");
    for memory in retained {
        // ## Thread `{thread_id}`
        // updated_at: {rfc3339}
        // cwd: {path}
        // rollout_path: {path}
        // rollout_summary_file: {file}
        // {raw_memory content}
    }
}

    // rollout_slug â†’ ى†Œë¬¸ى‍گي™” + يٹ¹ىˆکë¬¸ى‍گ â†’ '_' ë³€ي™ک, ىµœëŒ€ 60ى‍گ
    // ê²°ê³¼: "2026-03-26T14-30-00-aB3x-fix_login_bug"
}

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” agent::get_config()
pub(super) fn get_config(config: Arc<Config>) -> Option<Config> {
    let mut agent_config = config.as_ref().clone();

    agent_config.cwd = memory_root(&config.codex_home);  // memories/ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬

    // ى‍¬ê·€ى پ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ىƒ‌ى„± ى°¨ë‹¨
    agent_config.memories.generate_memories = false;

    // ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ë¶ˆي•„ىڑ”
    agent_config.permissions.approval_policy =
        Constrained::allow_only(AskForApproval::Never);

    // ي•کىœ„ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ى„±/يک‘ى—…/ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ëڈ„êµ¬ ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™”
    agent_config.features.disable(Feature::SpawnCsv);
    agent_config.features.disable(Feature::Collab);
    agent_config.features.disable(Feature::MemoryTool);

    // ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤: codex_homeë§Œ ى“°ê¸° ê°€ëٹ¥, ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى°¨ë‹¨
    let consolidation_sandbox_policy = SandboxPolicy::WorkspaceWrite {
        writable_roots: vec![codex_home],
        network_access: false,       // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى™„ى „ ى°¨ë‹¨
        ..
    };

    agent_config.model = Some(
        config.memories.consolidation_model.clone()
            .unwrap_or(phase_two::MODEL.to_string())  // "gpt-5.3-codex"
    );
    agent_config.model_reasoning_effort = Some(phase_two::REASONING_EFFORT);  // Medium
    Some(agent_config)
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” agent::loop_agent()
async fn loop_agent(db, token, thread_id, mut rx) -> AgentStatus {
    let mut heartbeat_interval =
        tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(phase_two::JOB_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS)); // 90ى´ˆ

    loop {
        let status = rx.borrow().clone();
        if is_final_agent_status(&status) { break status; }

        tokio::select! {
            update = rx.changed() => {
                if update.is_err() { break status; }  // ى±„ë„گ ëپٹê¹€
            }
            _ = heartbeat_interval.tick() => {
                match db.heartbeat_global_phase2_job(&token, JOB_LEASE_SECONDS).await {
                    Ok(true) => {},                    // lease ê°±ى‹  ى„±ê³µ
                    Ok(false) => break Errored(..),    // ى†Œىœ ê¶Œ ىƒپى‹¤
                    Err(err) => break Errored(..),     // DB ىک¤ë¥ک
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs
pub(super) fn get_watermark(
    claimed_watermark: i64,
    latest_memories: &[codex_state::Stage1Output],
) -> i64 {
    latest_memories.iter()
        .map(|m| m.source_updated_at.timestamp())
        .max()
        .unwrap_or(claimed_watermark)
        .max(claimed_watermark)  // ى ˆëŒ€ ë’¤ë،œ ê°€ى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ
}
