// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
pub(crate) async fn run_inline_auto_compact_task(
    sess: Arc<Session>,
    turn_context: Arc<TurnContext>,
    initial_context_injection: InitialContextInjection,
) -> CodexResult<()> {
    let prompt = turn_context.compact_prompt().to_string(); // compact/prompt.md
    // ...
    // 1. ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گê²Œ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ ىڑ”ى•½ ىڑ”ى²­
    // 2. ى‌‘ë‹µى—گى„œ ىڑ”ى•½ ى¶”ى¶œ
    let summary_text = format!("{SUMMARY_PREFIX}\n{summary_suffix}");
    // 3. ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ىˆکى§‘ (ىµœê·¼ 20,000 ي† يپ°ê¹Œى§€)
    let user_messages = collect_user_messages(history_items);
    // 4. ىƒˆ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ êµ¬ى„±: ى´ˆê¸° ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ + ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ + ىڑ”ى•½
    let new_history = build_compacted_history(Vec::new(), &user_messages, &summary_text);
    // 5. ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ êµگى²´
    sess.replace_compacted_history(new_history, reference_context_item, compacted_item).await;
}

// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
fn build_compacted_history_with_limit(
    mut history, user_messages, summary_text, max_tokens,
) -> Vec<ResponseItem> {
    let mut remaining = max_tokens;
    for message in user_messages.iter().rev() {  // ىµœى‹  â†’ ê³¼ê±° ىˆœ
        let tokens = approx_token_count(message);
        if tokens <= remaining {
            selected_messages.push(message.clone());
            remaining -= tokens;
        } else {
            let truncated = truncate_text(message, TruncationPolicy::Tokens(remaining));
            selected_messages.push(truncated);
            break;
        }
    }
    selected_messages.reverse();
    // historyى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ى¶”ê°€ â†’ ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ى—گ ىڑ”ى•½ ى¶”ê°€
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/control.rs
pub(crate) async fn clear_memory_root_contents(memory_root: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    // symlinkى‌¸ ê²½ىڑ° ê±°ë¶€ (ë³´ى•ˆ)
    match tokio::fs::symlink_metadata(memory_root).await {
        Ok(metadata) if metadata.file_type().is_symlink() => {
            return Err(std::io::Error::new(
                std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput,
                format!("refusing to clear symlinked memory root {}", memory_root.display()),
            ));
        }
        // ...
    }
    // ëھ¨ë“  يŒŒى‌¼/ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ ى‚­ى œ
}

$CODEX_HOME/
â”œâ”€â”€ sessions/           # ي™œى„± ى„¸ى…ک ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ YYYY/MM/DD/     # ë‚ ى§œë³„ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬
â”‚       â””â”€â”€ {thread_id}.jsonl
â”œâ”€â”€ archived_sessions/  # ى•„ى¹´ى‌´ë¸Œëگœ ى„¸ى…ک
â””â”€â”€ memories/           # Phase 2 ى¶œë ¥
    â”œâ”€â”€ memory_summary.md
    â”œâ”€â”€ MEMORY.md
    â”œâ”€â”€ raw_memories.md
    â”œâ”€â”€ rollout_summaries/
    â”‚   â””â”€â”€ {timestamp}-{hash}-{slug}.md
    â””â”€â”€ skills/
        â””â”€â”€ {skill-name}/
            â”œâ”€â”€ SKILL.md
            â”œâ”€â”€ scripts/
            â”œâ”€â”€ templates/
            â””â”€â”€ examples/

Main analysis

Codex memory pipeline analysis

Analysis snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” memory pipeline.

Codex's memory system is an asynchronous two-stage pipeline for inter-session learning. Rather than simply â€œstoring conversation history,â€‌ it is an agent-based pipeline that extracts and integrates structured memories from the rollout.

1. Memory architecture overview

1.1 Design philosophy

Codex memory has an â€œagent teaches agentâ€‌ structure. LLM analyzes the user's past sessions (rollout), converts them into structured memories, and injects them into the system prompt for future sessions. Core principles:

  1. 2-Phase Separation: Separates individual rollout extraction (Phase 1) and global integration (Phase 2), achieving both parallel extraction and safe global state update.
  2. State DB-centered coordination: SQLite-based state DB manages job ownership, lease, and watermark to prevent duplicate work between multiple sessions.
  3. Progressive disclosure: Memory files are layered in the following order: memory_summary.md â†’ MEMORY.md â†’ rollout_summaries/ â†’ skills/, and the agent searches only to the required depth.
  4. Secret redaction: Automatically removes tokens/keys/passwords from extracted memory.
  5. No-op allowed: If there is no meaningful learning, an empty result is returned to prevent noise.

1.2 Overall flow

graph TD
    A["session start"] --> B["start_memories_startup_task()"]
    B --> B1{"ephemeral? / MemoryTool disabled? / SubAgent?"}
    B1 -->|"yes"| SKIP["skip"]
    B1 -->|"no"| C["Phase 1: prune â†’ run"]
    C --> C1["claim eligible rollouts from state DB"]
    C1 --> C2["parallel extraction (concurrency cap=8)"]
    C2 -->|"gpt-5.1-codex-mini"| C3["StageOneOutput: raw_memory + rollout_summary + rollout_slug"]
    C3 --> C4["redact_secrets() â†’ state DB persist"]
    C4 --> D["Phase 2: run"]
    D --> D1["claim global consolidation lock"]
    D1 --> D2["load stage-1 outputs + compute selection diff"]
    D2 --> D3["sync filesystem artifacts"]
    D3 --> D4["spawn consolidation sub-agent"]
    D4 -->|"gpt-5.3-codex"| D5["update memory_summary.md / MEMORY.md / skills/"]
    D5 --> D6["heartbeat loop + status watch"]
    D6 --> D7["mark job succeeded/failed in state DB"]

1.3 Entry point

The single entry point into the pipeline is start_memories_startup_task():

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/start.rs
pub(crate) fn start_memories_startup_task(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    config: Arc<Config>,
    source: &SessionSource,
) {
    // ë‹¤ى‌Œ ى،°ê±´ى—گى„œ skip:
    if config.ephemeral
        || !config.features.enabled(Feature::MemoryTool)
        || matches!(source, SessionSource::SubAgent(_))
    {
        return;
    }

    if session.services.state_db.is_none() {
        warn!("state db unavailable for memories startup pipeline; skipping");
        return;
    }

    let weak_session = Arc::downgrade(session);
    tokio::spawn(async move {
        let Some(session) = weak_session.upgrade() else { return; };
        phase1::prune(&session, &config).await;  // ىک¤ë‍کëگœ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى •ë¦¬
        phase1::run(&session, &config).await;     // Phase 1 ى‹¤ي–‰
        phase2::run(&session, config).await;      // Phase 2 ى‹¤ي–‰
    });
}

If the session has already been terminated using Arc::downgrade, the pipeline will abort quietly. The entire pipeline runs in the background asynchronously with tokio::spawn, so it doesn't block user interaction.

2. Loading project instructions (AGENTS.md)

Apart from the memory pipeline, Codex loads project-level instructions through the AGENTS.md file. This mechanism corresponds to the "static" path of memory injection.

2.1 File navigation mechanism

Search algorithm implemented in project_doc.rs:

  1. Determine Project root: Go up in cwd and find project_root_markers (default: .git).
  2. Hierarchical collection: Search for AGENTS.md in all directories from project root to cwd.
  3. Priority: AGENTS.override.md > AGENTS.md > project_doc_fallback_filenames setting value order.
  4. Size Limit: Limit total bytes by setting project_doc_max_bytes. truncate when exceeded.
// codex-rs/core/src/project_doc.rs
pub const DEFAULT_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.md";
pub const LOCAL_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.override.md";

fn candidate_filenames<'a>(config: &'a Config) -> Vec<&'a str> {
    let mut names = Vec::with_capacity(2 + config.project_doc_fallback_filenames.len());
    names.push(LOCAL_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME);   // ىµœىڑ°ى„ 
    names.push(DEFAULT_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME); // ê¸°ë³¸
    for candidate in &config.project_doc_fallback_filenames {
        if !candidate.is_empty() && !names.contains(&candidate.as_str()) {
            names.push(candidate);
        }
    }
    names
}

2.2 Instruction Injection Format

The loaded AGENTS.md content is encapsulated in a UserInstructions structure and injected into the history as a contextual user message fragment:

// codex-rs/core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs
pub const USER_INSTRUCTIONS_PREFIX: &str = "# AGENTS.md instructions for ";

impl UserInstructions {
    pub(crate) fn serialize_to_text(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "{prefix}{directory}\n\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\n{contents}\n{suffix}",
            prefix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.start_marker(),
            directory = self.directory,
            contents = self.text,
            suffix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.end_marker(),
        )
    }
}

It is passed to the model in a form wrapped in marker tags (# AGENTS.md instructions for ... ~ </INSTRUCTIONS>), and when extracting memory, this fragment becomes filtered â€” it is removed along with the developer role message when serializing the rollout in Phase 1.

2.3 Memory read path (Read Path)

There is a separate path to inject memory_summary.md created in the previous session into the developer instructions of the current session:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/prompts.rs
pub(crate) async fn build_memory_tool_developer_instructions(
    codex_home: &Path,
) -> Option<String> {
    let base_path = memory_root(codex_home);
    let memory_summary_path = base_path.join("memory_summary.md");
    let memory_summary = fs::read_to_string(&memory_summary_path).await.ok()?
        .trim().to_string();
    let memory_summary = truncate_text(
        &memory_summary,
        TruncationPolicy::Tokens(
            phase_one::MEMORY_TOOL_DEVELOPER_INSTRUCTIONS_SUMMARY_TOKEN_LIMIT // 5,000 ي† يپ°
        ),
    );
    if memory_summary.is_empty() { return None; }
    // read_path.md ي…œي”Œë¦؟ىœ¼ë،œ ë Œëچ”ë§پ
    let template = MemoryToolDeveloperInstructionsTemplate {
        base_path: &base_path.display().to_string(),
        memory_summary: &memory_summary,
    };
    template.render().ok()
}

The read_path.md template guides the agent to a memory folder navigation strategy:

  • memory_summary.md always included in system prompt (already provided)
  • MEMORY.md is a handbook for keyword search
  • rollout_summaries/ is detailed evidence of individual rollouts
  • skills/<name>/ is a reusable procedure

3. Phase 1: Rollout Extraction

3.1 Operation sequence

The run() function in Phase 1 is executed in a strict four-step sequence:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
pub(in crate::memories) async fn run(session: &Arc<Session>, config: &Config) {
    // 1. claim eligible rollout jobs
    let Some(claimed_candidates) = claim_startup_jobs(session, &config.memories).await
        else { return; };

    // 2. build one stage-1 request context
    let stage_one_context = build_request_context(session, config).await;

    // 3. run stage-1 extraction jobs in parallel
    let outcomes = run_jobs(session, claimed_candidates, stage_one_context).await;

    // 4. emit metrics and logs
    let counts = aggregate_stats(outcomes);
    emit_metrics(session, &counts);
}

3.2 Rollout screening and Job Claiming

Select eligible rollouts by JOINing the threads table and the stage1_outputs and jobs tables in the State DB:

// codex-rs/state/src/runtime/memories.rs â€” claim_stage1_jobs_for_startup()
// ي•„ي„° ى،°ê±´:
// - memory_mode = 'enabled'
// - يک„ى‍¬ ى„¸ى…کى‌ک thread_id ى œى™¸
// - updated_at >= now - max_age_days  (ë„ˆë¬´ ىک¤ë‍کëگœ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// - updated_at <= now - min_rollout_idle_hours  (ى•„ى§پ ي™œى„±ى‌¸ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// - COALESCE(stage1_outputs.source_updated_at, -1) < updated_at  (ى‌´ë¯¸ ى²کë¦¬ëگœ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// - COALESCE(jobs.last_success_watermark, -1) < updated_at  (ى‌´ë¯¸ ى„±ê³µي•œ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// ORDER BY updated_at DESC, id DESC
// LIMIT scan_limit (5,000)

For each candidate, a lease is acquired with try_claim_stage1_job(). Possible results:

Stage1JobClaimOutcome Meaning
Claimed { ownership_token } Lease acquisition successful, extraction in progress
SkippedUpToDate Already has the latest output
SkippedRunning Another worker is holding the lease
SkippedRetryBackoff During backoff period after failure
SkippedRetryExhausted Number of retries exhausted (default 3 times)

3.3 Parallel Sampling

Parallelize claimed jobs with futures::stream::buffer_unordered:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
async fn run_jobs(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    claimed_candidates: Vec<codex_state::Stage1JobClaim>,
    stage_one_context: RequestContext,
) -> Vec<JobResult> {
    futures::stream::iter(claimed_candidates.into_iter())
        .map(|claim| {
            let session = Arc::clone(session);
            let stage_one_context = stage_one_context.clone();
            async move { job::run(session.as_ref(), claim, &stage_one_context).await }
        })
        .buffer_unordered(phase_one::CONCURRENCY_LIMIT)  // ىµœëŒ€ 8ê°œ ëڈ™ى‹œ
        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
        .await
}

3.4 Individual job execution flow

Each job loads the rollout file, filters out items that don't fit in memory, and sends them to the model:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs â€” job::sample()
async fn sample(session, rollout_path, rollout_cwd, stage_one_context) {
    // 1. ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ ى•„ى‌´ي…œ ë،œë“œ
    let (rollout_items, _, _) = RolloutRecorder::load_rollout_items(rollout_path).await?;

    // 2. ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ê´€ë ¨ response itemsë§Œ ي•„ي„°ë§پ + ى§پë ¬ي™”
    let rollout_contents = serialize_filtered_rollout_response_items(&rollout_items)?;

    // 3. ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ىœˆëڈ„ىڑ°ى‌ک 70%ê¹Œى§€ truncate
    let prompt = Prompt {
        input: vec![/* build_stage_one_input_message() */],
        base_instructions: BaseInstructions {
            text: phase_one::PROMPT.to_string(),  // stage_one_system.md
        },
        output_schema: Some(output_schema()),     // JSON schema ê°•ى œ
        ..
    };

    // 4. ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ى‌‘ë‹µ ىˆکى§‘
    let mut stream = client_session.stream(&prompt, ...).await?;
    // ...

    // 5. secret redaction
    let mut output: StageOneOutput = serde_json::from_str(&result)?;
    output.raw_memory = redact_secrets(output.raw_memory);
    output.rollout_summary = redact_secrets(output.rollout_summary);
    output.rollout_slug = output.rollout_slug.map(redact_secrets);
}

3.5 Rollout filtering rules

Not all items in a rollout are included in memory. In sanitize_response_item_for_memories():

  • developer role message â†’ Remove (structural fragments such as system prompt, AGENTS.md, etc.)
  • is_memory_excluded_contextual_user_fragment() in user role message â†’ Remove (environment context, AGENTS.md fragment, etc.)
  • Other user/assistant messages â†’ Preservation
  • should_persist_response_item_for_memories() â†’ Determination of function call, tool output, etc.

3.6 Phase 1 output schema

The model returns structured output enforced as a JSON Schema:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
pub fn output_schema() -> Value {
    json!({
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "rollout_summary": { "type": "string" },
            "rollout_slug": { "type": ["string", "null"] },
            "raw_memory": { "type": "string" }
        },
        "required": ["rollout_summary", "rollout_slug", "raw_memory"],
        "additionalProperties": false
    })
}
field Role
raw_memory Raw memory in detailed Markdown format
rollout_summary One-line summary for routing and indexing
rollout_slug Short identifier for creating file name (optional)

3.7 Stage 1 System Prompt Key Contents

Key instructions for the stage_one_system.md template:

  • No-op gate: "Will a future agent plausibly act better because of what I write here?" â€” Otherwise return an empty field
  • High-signal memory criteria: (1) stable user preference, (2) high leverage procedural knowledge, (3) decision trigger, (4) environment/workflow evidence
  • Secret redaction: Replace token/key/password with [REDACTED_SECRET]
  • Immutable evidence: raw rollout is never modified.

3.8 Prune old memory (Prune)

Before Phase 1 execution, prune() is called to batch-prune any unused stage-1 output that exceeds max_unused_days:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
pub(in crate::memories) async fn prune(session: &Arc<Session>, config: &Config) {
    if let Some(db) = session.services.state_db.as_deref() {
        let max_unused_days = config.memories.max_unused_days;
        match db.prune_stage1_outputs_for_retention(max_unused_days, PRUNE_BATCH_SIZE).await {
            Ok(pruned) => { /* log */ },
            Err(err) => { /* warn */ },
        }
    }
}

4. Phase 2: Global Consolidation

4.1 Operation sequence

Phase 2 is a strictly linear flow consisting of 7 steps:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” run()
pub(super) async fn run(session: &Arc<Session>, config: Arc<Config>) {
    // 1. Claim the global consolidation job
    let claim = job::claim(session, db).await?;

    // 2. Get agent config (sandbox policy ë“±)
    let agent_config = agent::get_config(config.clone());

    // 3. Query stage-1 outputs with selection rules
    let selection = db.get_phase2_input_selection(max_raw_memories, max_unused_days).await?;

    // 4. Sync filesystem artifacts
    sync_rollout_summaries_from_memories(&root, &artifact_memories, ...).await?;
    rebuild_raw_memories_file_from_memories(&root, &artifact_memories, ...).await?;

    // 5. Spawn consolidation sub-agent
    let prompt = agent::get_prompt(config, &selection);
    let thread_id = session.services.agent_control
        .spawn_agent(agent_config, prompt, Some(source)).await?;

    // 6. Handle agent lifecycle (heartbeat + status watch)
    agent::handle(session, claim, new_watermark, raw_memories, thread_id, timer);

    // 7. Emit metrics
}

4.2 Global Lock (Phase 2 Job Claim)

Phase 2 ensures that only one consolidation runs globally:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” job::claim()
let claim = db
    .try_claim_global_phase2_job(session.conversation_id, phase_two::JOB_LEASE_SECONDS)
    .await?;

// ê°€ëٹ¥ي•œ ê²°ê³¼:
// Phase2JobClaimOutcome::Claimed { ownership_token, input_watermark }
// Phase2JobClaimOutcome::SkippedNotDirty   â€” ىƒˆ ى‍…ë ¥ ى—†ى‌Œ
// Phase2JobClaimOutcome::SkippedRunning    â€” ë‹¤ë¥¸ ى›Œى»¤ê°€ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى¤‘

4.3 Input Selection and Diff

Phase 2 doesn't simply fetch the latest N, it computes the diff from the previous successful Phase 2 run:

// codex-rs/state/src/model/memories.rs
pub struct Phase2InputSelection {
    pub selected: Vec<Stage1Output>,           // يک„ى‍¬ ى„ يƒ‌ëگœ ى‍…ë ¥
    pub previous_selected: Vec<Stage1Output>,  // ى‌´ى „ Phase 2ى—گى„œ ى„ يƒ‌ëگگëچک ى‍…ë ¥
    pub retained_thread_ids: Vec<ThreadId>,    // ى‌´ى „ى—گëڈ„ يک„ى‍¬ى—گëڈ„ ى„ يƒ‌ëگœ thread
    pub removed: Vec<Stage1OutputRef>,         // ى‌´ى „ى—گ ى„ يƒ‌ëگگى§€ë§Œ يک„ى‍¬ ë¹ ى§„ ى‍…ë ¥
}

Selection Policy:

  • Exclude memory with last_usage exceeding max_unused_days
  • If there is no last_usage, fallback to generated_at
  • Sort by usage_count highest â†’ most recent last_usage/generated_at

4.4 File system synchronization

Phase 2 synchronizes the DB's stage-1 outputs to the file system:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/storage.rs

// 1. rollout_summaries/ ëڈ™ê¸°ي™”: ىœ ى§€ ëŒ€ىƒپë§Œ ë‚¨ê¸°ê³  ë‚کë¨¸ى§€ ى‚­ى œ
async fn sync_rollout_summaries_from_memories(root, memories, max) {
    let retained = retained_memories(memories, max);
    let keep = retained.iter().map(rollout_summary_file_stem).collect::<HashSet<_>>();
    prune_rollout_summaries(root, &keep).await?;
    for memory in retained {
        write_rollout_summary_for_thread(root, memory).await?;
    }
    // retainedê°€ ë¹„ى–´ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ MEMORY.md, memory_summary.md, skills/ ëڈ„ ى‚­ى œ
}

// 2. raw_memories.md ى‍¬ىƒ‌ى„±: ىµœى‹  ىˆœىœ¼ë،œ ë³‘ي•©
async fn rebuild_raw_memories_file(root, memories, max) {
    let mut body = String::from("# Raw Memories\n\n");
    body.push_str("Merged stage-1 raw memories (latest first):\n\n");
    for memory in retained {
        // ## Thread `{thread_id}`
        // updated_at: {rfc3339}
        // cwd: {path}
        // rollout_path: {path}
        // rollout_summary_file: {file}
        // {raw_memory content}
    }
}

4.5 Rollout Summary file name creation

The file name is generated by extracting the timestamp and short hash from the UUID-based thread_id:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/storage.rs
fn rollout_summary_file_stem_from_parts(thread_id, source_updated_at, rollout_slug) -> String {
    // UUIDى—گى„œ يƒ€ى‍„ىٹ¤يƒ¬ي”„ ى¶”ى¶œ â†’ "2026-03-26T14-30-00"
    // UUID ي•کىœ„ 32ë¹„يٹ¸ â†’ 62ى§„ë²• 4ى‍گë¦¬ short hash
    // rollout_slug â†’ ى†Œë¬¸ى‍گي™” + يٹ¹ىˆکë¬¸ى‍گ â†’ '_' ë³€ي™ک, ىµœëŒ€ 60ى‍گ
    // ê²°ê³¼: "2026-03-26T14-30-00-aB3x-fix_login_bug"
}

4.6 Consolidation Sub-Agent Settings

The sub-agent created by Phase 2 runs in a very restricted environment:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” agent::get_config()
pub(super) fn get_config(config: Arc<Config>) -> Option<Config> {
    let mut agent_config = config.as_ref().clone();

    agent_config.cwd = memory_root(&config.codex_home);  // memories/ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬

    // ى‍¬ê·€ى پ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ىƒ‌ى„± ى°¨ë‹¨
    agent_config.memories.generate_memories = false;

    // ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ë¶ˆي•„ىڑ”
    agent_config.permissions.approval_policy =
        Constrained::allow_only(AskForApproval::Never);

    // ي•کىœ„ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ى„±/يک‘ى—…/ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ëڈ„êµ¬ ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™”
    agent_config.features.disable(Feature::SpawnCsv);
    agent_config.features.disable(Feature::Collab);
    agent_config.features.disable(Feature::MemoryTool);

    // ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤: codex_homeë§Œ ى“°ê¸° ê°€ëٹ¥, ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى°¨ë‹¨
    let consolidation_sandbox_policy = SandboxPolicy::WorkspaceWrite {
        writable_roots: vec![codex_home],
        network_access: false,       // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى™„ى „ ى°¨ë‹¨
        ..
    };

    agent_config.model = Some(
        config.memories.consolidation_model.clone()
            .unwrap_or(phase_two::MODEL.to_string())  // "gpt-5.3-codex"
    );
    agent_config.model_reasoning_effort = Some(phase_two::REASONING_EFFORT);  // Medium
    Some(agent_config)
}

4.7 Heartbeat and health monitoring

While the sub-agent is running, a heartbeat is sent every 90 seconds to renew the lease. Immediate suspension upon failure to renew Lease:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” agent::loop_agent()
async fn loop_agent(db, token, thread_id, mut rx) -> AgentStatus {
    let mut heartbeat_interval =
        tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(phase_two::JOB_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS)); // 90ى´ˆ

    loop {
        let status = rx.borrow().clone();
        if is_final_agent_status(&status) { break status; }

        tokio::select! {
            update = rx.changed() => {
                if update.is_err() { break status; }  // ى±„ë„گ ëپٹê¹€
            }
            _ = heartbeat_interval.tick() => {
                match db.heartbeat_global_phase2_job(&token, JOB_LEASE_SECONDS).await {
                    Ok(true) => {},                    // lease ê°±ى‹  ى„±ê³µ
                    Ok(false) => break Errored(..),    // ى†Œىœ ê¶Œ ىƒپى‹¤
                    Err(err) => break Errored(..),     // DB ىک¤ë¥ک
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

4.8 Watermark management

Watermark tracks the timestamp of the last input processed by Phase 2:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs
pub(super) fn get_watermark(
    claimed_watermark: i64,
    latest_memories: &[codex_state::Stage1Output],
) -> i64 {
    latest_memories.iter()
        .map(|m| m.source_updated_at.timestamp())
        .max()
        .unwrap_or(claimed_watermark)
        .max(claimed_watermark)  // ى ˆëŒ€ ë’¤ë،œ ê°€ى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ
}
  • Use the larger value between the watermark at the time of claim and the latest timestamp of the actually loaded input.
  • If successful, save to DB â†’ Next Phase 2 uses to determine â€œdirty statusâ€‌

5. Conversation history management

5.1 ContextManager structure

The conversation history within a session is managed with ContextManager:

// codex-rs/core/src/context_manager/history.rs
pub(crate) struct ContextManager {
    items: Vec<ResponseItem>,              // oldest â†’ newest ىˆœى„œ
    token_info: Option<TokenUsageInfo>,     // ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ API ى‌‘ë‹µى‌ک ي† يپ° ى‚¬ىڑ©ëں‰
    reference_context_item: Option<TurnContextItem>,  // ë‹¤ى‌Œ ي„´ى‌ک context ê¸°ى¤€ى„ 
}

Core methods:

  • record_items() â€” Applies truncation policy while adding new items to history
  • for_prompt() â€” Normalizes and returns the history to be sent to the model (GhostSnapshot removal, image filtering, etc.)
  • remove_first_item() / remove_last_item() â€” Remove item when context window exceeds
  • replace() â€” Replace entire history after compaction

5.2 History normalization

normalize_history() is called before sending to the model:

  • Remove modalities (e.g. images) not supported by the model
  • Clean up orphan function call output (remove output without a corresponding call)
  • Removed GhostSnapshot item (for UI use only, not needed for model)

6. Context window management and compaction

6.1 Two compaction strategies

Codex performs compaction, compressing the conversation content when the context window is full:

  1. Inline Compaction (compact.rs): Request a history summary from the model and process it locally
  2. Remote Compaction (compact_remote.rs): Processed on the server side when using OpenAI provider.
// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
pub(crate) fn should_use_remote_compact_task(provider: &ModelProviderInfo) -> bool {
    provider.is_openai()  // OpenAIى‌¸ ê²½ىڑ°ë§Œ remote compaction
}

6.2 Inline Compaction Flow

// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
pub(crate) async fn run_inline_auto_compact_task(
    sess: Arc<Session>,
    turn_context: Arc<TurnContext>,
    initial_context_injection: InitialContextInjection,
) -> CodexResult<()> {
    let prompt = turn_context.compact_prompt().to_string(); // compact/prompt.md
    // ...
    // 1. ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گê²Œ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ ىڑ”ى•½ ىڑ”ى²­
    // 2. ى‌‘ë‹µى—گى„œ ىڑ”ى•½ ى¶”ى¶œ
    let summary_text = format!("{SUMMARY_PREFIX}\n{summary_suffix}");
    // 3. ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ىˆکى§‘ (ىµœê·¼ 20,000 ي† يپ°ê¹Œى§€)
    let user_messages = collect_user_messages(history_items);
    // 4. ىƒˆ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ êµ¬ى„±: ى´ˆê¸° ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ + ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ + ىڑ”ى•½
    let new_history = build_compacted_history(Vec::new(), &user_messages, &summary_text);
    // 5. ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ êµگى²´
    sess.replace_compacted_history(new_history, reference_context_item, compacted_item).await;
}

6.3 Initial Context reinjection after compaction

Depending on the compaction type, the initial context injection strategy is different:

// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
pub(crate) enum InitialContextInjection {
    BeforeLastUserMessage,  // mid-turn compaction: ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ى§پى „ى—گ ى£¼ى‍…
    DoNotInject,            // pre-turn compaction: ë‹¤ى‌Œ ي„´ى—گى„œ ى‍گëڈ™ ى‍¬ى£¼ى‍…
}

6.4 User message retention strategy

When compacting, user messages are preserved in reverse chronological order, starting from the most recent, up to a maximum of COMPACT_USER_MESSAGE_MAX_TOKENS (20,000 tokens):

// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
fn build_compacted_history_with_limit(
    mut history, user_messages, summary_text, max_tokens,
) -> Vec<ResponseItem> {
    let mut remaining = max_tokens;
    for message in user_messages.iter().rev() {  // ىµœى‹  â†’ ê³¼ê±° ىˆœ
        let tokens = approx_token_count(message);
        if tokens <= remaining {
            selected_messages.push(message.clone());
            remaining -= tokens;
        } else {
            let truncated = truncate_text(message, TruncationPolicy::Tokens(remaining));
            selected_messages.push(truncated);
            break;
        }
    }
    selected_messages.reverse();
    // historyى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ى¶”ê°€ â†’ ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ى—گ ىڑ”ى•½ ى¶”ê°€
}

7. Memory Trace (Legacy Path)

memory_trace.rs provides a separate path to build memory from raw trace files. Operates independently of the Phase 1/2 pipeline:

// codex-rs/core/src/memory_trace.rs
pub struct BuiltMemory {
    pub memory_id: String,
    pub source_path: PathBuf,
    pub raw_memory: String,
    pub memory_summary: String,
}

pub async fn build_memories_from_trace_files(
    client: &ModelClient,
    trace_paths: &[PathBuf],
    model_info: &ModelInfo,
    effort: Option<ReasoningEffortConfig>,
    session_telemetry: &SessionTelemetry,
) -> Result<Vec<BuiltMemory>> {
    // 1. trace يŒŒى‌¼ ë،œë“œ (JSON array ëکگëٹ” JSONL)
    // 2. BOM ى²کë¦¬, ي•­ëھ© ى •ê·œي™”
    // 3. ي—ˆىڑ©ëگœ trace itemë§Œ ي•„ي„°ë§پ (message: assistant/system/developer/user)
    // 4. client.summarize_memories()ë،œ ىڑ”ى•½ ىƒ‌ى„±
}

Trace loading supports two formats:

  • JSON array: [{...}, {...}] format
  • JSONL: One JSON object per line

When normalizing, extract the payload from the event where payload.type == "response_item" and check whether the message role is one of assistant, system, developer, or user using is_allowed_trace_item().

8. Memory Citation System

There is a citation system that tracks the source when an agent references a memory:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/citations.rs
pub fn parse_memory_citation(citations: Vec<String>) -> Option<MemoryCitation> {
    // <citation_entries> ... </citation_entries> ë¸”ë،‌ يŒŒى‹±
    // <rollout_ids> ëکگëٹ” <thread_ids> ë¸”ë،‌ى—گى„œ UUID ى¶”ى¶œ
}

pub fn get_thread_id_from_citations(citations: Vec<String>) -> Vec<ThreadId> {
    // citationى—گى„œ thread_id ى¶”ى¶œ â†’ usage ى¶”ى پى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©
}

Citation format:

<oai-mem-citation>
<citation_entries>
MEMORY.md:234-236|note=[responses api citation extraction code pointer]
rollout_summaries/2026-02-17T21-23-02-LN3m-weekly_report.md:10-12|note=[weekly report format]
</citation_entries>
<rollout_ids>
019c6e27-e55b-73d1-87d8-4e01f1f75043
</rollout_ids>
</oai-mem-citation>

The extracted thread_id is recorded in the state DB with record_stage1_output_usage(), and usage_count and last_usage of the corresponding memory are updated. This data affects the input selection priority of Phase 2.

9. Memory Usage Telemetry

When the agent reads a memory file, it collects usage metrics:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/usage.rs
enum MemoriesUsageKind {
    MemoryMd,          // memories/MEMORY.md
    MemorySummary,     // memories/memory_summary.md
    RawMemories,       // memories/raw_memories.md
    RolloutSummaries,  // memories/rollout_summaries/
    Skills,            // memories/skills/
}

pub(crate) async fn emit_metric_for_tool_read(invocation: &ToolInvocation, success: bool) {
    // shell/shell_command/exec_command ëڈ„êµ¬ى—گى„œ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ يŒŒى‌¼ ى‌½ê¸° ê°گى§€
    // â†’ codex.memories.usage ى¹´ىڑ´ي„° emit
    //   tags: (kind, tool, success)
}

Parses the command of the tool call to determine whether the read target path is a memory folder, and issues a metric only if it is is_known_safe_command().

10. State DB schema

The persistence of the memory pipeline relies on a SQLite-based state DB.

10.1 Core tables

table Role
threads Session (thread) metadata + memory_mode column
stage1_outputs Phase 1 extraction results (raw_memory, rollout_summary, rollout_slug, etc.)
jobs job ownership, lease, watermark, retry status

10.2 Stage1Output schema

// codex-rs/state/src/model/memories.rs
pub struct Stage1Output {
    pub thread_id: ThreadId,
    pub rollout_path: PathBuf,
    pub source_updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub raw_memory: String,
    pub rollout_summary: String,
    pub rollout_slug: Option<String>,
    pub cwd: PathBuf,
    pub git_branch: Option<String>,
    pub generated_at: DateTime<Utc>,
}

10.3 Memory initialization

Two ways to completely initialize memory data:

// codex-rs/state/src/runtime/memories.rs

// 1. clear_memory_data: stage1_outputs + memory jobs ى‚­ى œ
pub async fn clear_memory_data(&self) -> anyhow::Result<()>;

// 2. reset_memory_data_for_fresh_start: ىœ„ + ê¸°ى،´ threadى‌ک memory_modeë¥¼ 'disabled'ë،œ ë³€ê²½
//    â†’ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒى‌´ ى¦‰ى‹œ ى‍¬ى²کë¦¬ëگکëٹ” ê²ƒى‌„ ë°©ى§€
pub async fn reset_memory_data_for_fresh_start(&self) -> anyhow::Result<()>;

10.4 Memory Root Management

Root initialization of the memory file system also takes security into account:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/control.rs
pub(crate) async fn clear_memory_root_contents(memory_root: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    // symlinkى‌¸ ê²½ىڑ° ê±°ë¶€ (ë³´ى•ˆ)
    match tokio::fs::symlink_metadata(memory_root).await {
        Ok(metadata) if metadata.file_type().is_symlink() => {
            return Err(std::io::Error::new(
                std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput,
                format!("refusing to clear symlinked memory root {}", memory_root.display()),
            ));
        }
        // ...
    }
    // ëھ¨ë“  يŒŒى‌¼/ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ ى‚­ى œ
}

11. Session persistence (Rollout Recording)

Permanence of conversation history is achieved through a rollout recording system.

11.1 Rollout structure

The conversation content of each session is recorded as a JSONL file:

  • session_meta â€” Session metadata (id, source, model, etc.)
  • turn_context â€” Turn boundary marker
  • event_msg â€” lightweight state stream
  • response_item â€” actual message, tool call, tool output

11.2 Rollout path structure

$CODEX_HOME/
â”œâ”€â”€ sessions/           # ي™œى„± ى„¸ى…ک ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ YYYY/MM/DD/     # ë‚ ى§œë³„ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬
â”‚       â””â”€â”€ {thread_id}.jsonl
â”œâ”€â”€ archived_sessions/  # ى•„ى¹´ى‌´ë¸Œëگœ ى„¸ى…ک
â””â”€â”€ memories/           # Phase 2 ى¶œë ¥
    â”œâ”€â”€ memory_summary.md
    â”œâ”€â”€ MEMORY.md
    â”œâ”€â”€ raw_memories.md
    â”œâ”€â”€ rollout_summaries/
    â”‚   â””â”€â”€ {timestamp}-{hash}-{slug}.md
    â””â”€â”€ skills/
        â””â”€â”€ {skill-name}/
            â”œâ”€â”€ SKILL.md
            â”œâ”€â”€ scripts/
            â”œâ”€â”€ templates/
            â””â”€â”€ examples/

12. Matrix system

OpenTelemetry based metrics are built-in throughout the pipeline:

Phase 1 Metrics

metric name Type Description
codex.memory.phase1 counter Count by job status
codex.memory.phase1.e2e_ms timer Phase 1 Total Time Required
codex.memory.phase1.output counter Number of raw memories created
codex.memory.phase1.token_usage histogram Token usage (total/input/cached/output/reasoning)

Phase 2 Metrics

metric name Type Description
codex.memory.phase2 counter Count by job status
codex.memory.phase2.e2e_ms timer Phase 2 Total Time Required
codex.memory.phase2.input counter number of consolidation inputs
codex.memory.phase2.token_usage histogram Token Usage

Memory Usage metrics

metric name Type Description
codex.memories.usage counter Read memory file (by kind/tool/success)

Technical Reference

Phase 1 parameters

Item value Source
model gpt-5.1-codex-mini phase_one::MODEL
reasoning effort Low phase_one::REASONING_EFFORT
concurrency cap 8 phase_one::CONCURRENCY_LIMIT
Context Window Utilization 70% phase_one::CONTEXT_WINDOW_PERCENT
fallback token limit 150,000 phase_one::DEFAULT_STAGE_ONE_ROLLOUT_TOKEN_LIMIT
memory summary injection limit 5,000 tokens phase_one::MEMORY_TOOL_DEVELOPER_INSTRUCTIONS_SUMMARY_TOKEN_LIMIT
job lease 3,600 seconds phase_one::JOB_LEASE_SECONDS
retry backoff 3,600 seconds phase_one::JOB_RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS
Default retry count Episode 3 DEFAULT_RETRY_REMAINING
thread scan limit 5,000 phase_one::THREAD_SCAN_LIMIT
prune batch size 200 phase_one::PRUNE_BATCH_SIZE

Phase 2 parameters

Item value Source
model gpt-5.3-codex phase_two::MODEL
reasoning effort Medium phase_two::REASONING_EFFORT
job lease 3,600 seconds phase_two::JOB_LEASE_SECONDS
retry backoff 3,600 seconds phase_two::JOB_RETRY_DELAY_SECONDS
heartbeat interval 90 seconds phase_two::JOB_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS

Compaction parameters

Item value Source
User message retention limits 20,000 tokens COMPACT_USER_MESSAGE_MAX_TOKENS

Phase 2 Selection Diff

label Meaning
added New inputs that did not exist in the previous Phase 2
retained Input that was also selected in previous Phase 2
removed Inputs previously selected but currently missing from top-N

Filesystem artifacts

path Role
memories/memory_summary.md Top-level summary always injected into system prompt
memories/MEMORY.md Handbook for keyword search (created by Phase 2)
memories/raw_memories.md Phase 1 raw memory merge (newest first, Phase 2 input)
memories/rollout_summaries/ Summary files by rollout
memories/skills/<name>/ Reusable Procedure (SKILL.md + scripts/ + templates/)

Prompt Template

template Use
templates/memories/stage_one_system.md Phase 1 system prompt
templates/memories/stage_one_input.md Phase 1 user message (including rollout details)
templates/memories/consolidation.md Phase 2 consolidation agent prompt
templates/memories/read_path.md Memory read path developer instructions
templates/compact/prompt.md Context compaction prompt
templates/compact/summary_prefix.md compaction summary prefix

Fork Perspective

  • Two-stage separation (extraction â†’ integration) is architecturally clean and has high reuse value.
  • State DB-based job coordination is essential in a multi-session environment
  • Security is considered as a secret redaction step is included.
  • Quantify the â€œvalueâ€‌ of memory with citation-based usage tracking
  • progressive disclosure pattern contributes to token efficiency
  • Security hardening is well implemented, such as symlink rejection and network blocking.
  • The part that uses gpt-5.1-codex-mini in phase 1 and gpt-5.3-codex in phase 2 is model dependent, so abstraction is required.
  • Injection method of memory_tool_developer_instructions (5,000 token limit) cross-references 02_co_prompt
  • You must bring the state DB schema (codex-rs/state/) together.
  • compactionâ€™s inline/remote branch logic is provider dependent

List of core files

file Role
codex-rs/core/src/memories/mod.rs Memory module entry points, constant definitions
codex-rs/core/src/memories/start.rs pipeline entry point (start_memories_startup_task)
codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs Phase 1 overall flow + individual job execution
codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs Phase 2 overall flow + sub-agent management
codex-rs/core/src/memories/storage.rs Filesystem synchronization (raw_memories.md, rollout_summaries/)
codex-rs/core/src/memories/prompts.rs prompt builder (consolidation, stage-1, read path)
codex-rs/core/src/memories/citations.rs memory citation parsing
codex-rs/core/src/memories/control.rs memory root initialization
codex-rs/core/src/memories/usage.rs Memory file read telemetry
codex-rs/core/src/memory_trace.rs trace file based memory build (legacy path)
codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs inline context compaction
codex-rs/core/src/compact_remote.rs remote context compaction
codex-rs/core/src/context_manager/history.rs ContextManager (history management)
codex-rs/core/src/project_doc.rs Navigating and Loading AGENTS.md
codex-rs/core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs Serialize UserInstructions
codex-rs/state/src/model/memories.rs Stage1Output, Phase2InputSelection model
codex-rs/state/src/runtime/memories.rs state DB memory query/mutation
codex-rs/core/templates/memories/ Prompt Template Directory

Codex ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ ë¶„ى„‌

ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” memory pipeline.

Codexى‌ک ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œى‌€ ى„¸ى…ک ê°„ ي•™ىٹµى‌„ ىœ„ي•œ ë¹„ëڈ™ê¸° 2ë‹¨ê³„ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌´ë‹¤. ë‹¨ىˆœ "ëŒ€ي™” ê¸°ë،‌ ى €ى‍¥"ى‌´ ى•„ë‹ˆë‌¼, ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒى—گى„œ êµ¬ى،°ي™”ëگœ ê¸°ى–µى‌„ ى¶”ى¶œي•کê³  ي†µي•©ي•کëٹ” ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ê¸°ë°ک يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌´ë‹¤.

1. ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى•„ي‚¤ي…چى²ک ê°œىڑ”

1.1 ى„¤ê³„ ى² ي•™

Codex ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ëٹ” "ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ê°€ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ë¥¼ ê°€ë¥´ى¹کëٹ”" êµ¬ى،°ë‹¤. ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گى‌ک ê³¼ê±° ى„¸ى…ک(rollout)ى‌„ LLMى‌´ ë¶„ى„‌ي•کى—¬ êµ¬ى،°ي™”ëگœ ê¸°ى–µىœ¼ë،œ ë³€ي™کي•کê³ , ى‌´ë¥¼ ë‹¤ى‹œ ë¯¸ë‍ک ى„¸ى…کى‌ک system promptى—گ ى£¼ى‍…ي•œë‹¤. ي•µى‹¬ ى›گى¹™:

  1. 2ë‹¨ê³„ ë¶„ë¦¬: ê°œë³„ ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ ى¶”ى¶œ(Phase 1)ê³¼ ى „ى—­ ي†µي•©(Phase 2)ى‌„ ë¶„ë¦¬ي•کى—¬, ë³‘ë ¬ ى¶”ى¶œê³¼ ى•ˆى „ي•œ ى „ى—­ ىƒپيƒœ ê°±ى‹ ى‌„ ëھ¨ë‘گ ë‹¬ى„±ي•œë‹¤.
  2. State DB ى¤‘ى‹¬ coordination: SQLite ê¸°ë°ک state DBê°€ job ى†Œىœ ê¶Œ, lease, watermarkى‌„ ê´€ë¦¬ي•کى—¬ ë‹¤ى¤‘ ى„¸ى…ک ê°„ ى¤‘ë³µ ى‍‘ى—…ى‌„ ë°©ى§€ي•œë‹¤.
  3. Progressive disclosure: ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ يŒŒى‌¼ى‌„ memory_summary.md â†’ MEMORY.md â†’ rollout_summaries/ â†’ skills/ ىˆœىœ¼ë،œ ê³„ى¸µي™”ي•کى—¬, ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ê°€ ي•„ىڑ”ي•œ ê¹ٹى‌´ë§Œيپ¼ë§Œ يƒگىƒ‰ي•œë‹¤.
  4. Secret redaction: ى¶”ى¶œëگœ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ى—گى„œ ي† يپ°/ي‚¤/ë¹„ë°€ë²ˆيک¸ë¥¼ ى‍گëڈ™ ى œê±°ي•œë‹¤.
  5. No-op ي—ˆىڑ©: ى‌کë¯¸ ى‍ˆëٹ” ي•™ىٹµى‌´ ى—†ىœ¼ë©´ ë¹ˆ ê²°ê³¼ë¥¼ ë°کي™کي•کى—¬ ë…¸ى‌´ى¦ˆë¥¼ ë°©ى§€ي•œë‹¤.

1.2 ى „ى²´ ي‌گë¦„

graph TD
    A["session start"] --> B["start_memories_startup_task()"]
    B --> B1{"ephemeral? / MemoryTool disabled? / SubAgent?"}
    B1 -->|"yes"| SKIP["skip"]
    B1 -->|"no"| C["Phase 1: prune â†’ run"]
    C --> C1["claim eligible rollouts from state DB"]
    C1 --> C2["parallel extraction (concurrency cap=8)"]
    C2 -->|"gpt-5.1-codex-mini"| C3["StageOneOutput: raw_memory + rollout_summary + rollout_slug"]
    C3 --> C4["redact_secrets() â†’ state DB persist"]
    C4 --> D["Phase 2: run"]
    D --> D1["claim global consolidation lock"]
    D1 --> D2["load stage-1 outputs + compute selection diff"]
    D2 --> D3["sync filesystem artifacts"]
    D3 --> D4["spawn consolidation sub-agent"]
    D4 -->|"gpt-5.3-codex"| D5["update memory_summary.md / MEMORY.md / skills/"]
    D5 --> D6["heartbeat loop + status watch"]
    D6 --> D7["mark job succeeded/failed in state DB"]

1.3 ى§„ى‍…ى گ

يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌ک ë‹¨ى‌¼ ى§„ى‍…ى گى‌€ start_memories_startup_task()ى‌´ë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/start.rs
pub(crate) fn start_memories_startup_task(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    config: Arc<Config>,
    source: &SessionSource,
) {
    // ë‹¤ى‌Œ ى،°ê±´ى—گى„œ skip:
    if config.ephemeral
        || !config.features.enabled(Feature::MemoryTool)
        || matches!(source, SessionSource::SubAgent(_))
    {
        return;
    }

    if session.services.state_db.is_none() {
        warn!("state db unavailable for memories startup pipeline; skipping");
        return;
    }

    let weak_session = Arc::downgrade(session);
    tokio::spawn(async move {
        let Some(session) = weak_session.upgrade() else { return; };
        phase1::prune(&session, &config).await;  // ىک¤ë‍کëگœ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى •ë¦¬
        phase1::run(&session, &config).await;     // Phase 1 ى‹¤ي–‰
        phase2::run(&session, config).await;      // Phase 2 ى‹¤ي–‰
    });
}

Arc::downgradeë¥¼ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•کى—¬ ى„¸ى…کى‌´ ى‌´ë¯¸ ى¢…ë£Œëگœ ê²½ىڑ° يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌´ ى،°ىڑ©ي‍ˆ ى¤‘ë‹¨ëگœë‹¤. ى „ى²´ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ى‌€ tokio::spawnىœ¼ë،œ ë¹„ëڈ™ê¸° ë°±ê·¸ë‌¼ىڑ´ë“œى—گى„œ ى‹¤ي–‰ëگکë¯€ë،œ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ىƒپيک¸ى‍‘ىڑ©ى‌„ ى°¨ë‹¨ي•کى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤.

2. ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ى§€ى‹œ ë،œë”© (AGENTS.md)

ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ê³¼ ë³„ëڈ„ë،œ, Codexëٹ” ي”„ë،œى ‌يٹ¸ ىˆکى¤€ى‌ک ى§€ى‹œى‚¬ي•­ى‌„ AGENTS.md يŒŒى‌¼ى‌„ ي†µي•´ ë،œë”©ي•œë‹¤. ى‌´ ë©”ى»¤ë‹ˆى¦کى‌€ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى£¼ى‍…ى‌ک "ى •ى پ" ê²½ë،œى—گ ي•´ë‹¹ي•œë‹¤.

2.1 يŒŒى‌¼ يƒگىƒ‰ ë©”ى»¤ë‹ˆى¦ک

project_doc.rsى—گى„œ êµ¬يک„ëگœ يƒگىƒ‰ ى•Œê³ ë¦¬ى¦ک:

  1. Project root ê²°ى •: cwdى—گى„œ ىƒپىœ„ë،œ ىک¬ë‌¼ê°€ë©° project_root_markers (ê¸°ë³¸ê°’: .git)ë¥¼ ى°¾ëٹ”ë‹¤.
  2. ê³„ى¸µى پ ىˆکى§‘: project rootë¶€ي„° cwdê¹Œى§€ ëھ¨ë“  ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ى—گى„œ AGENTS.mdë¥¼ يƒگىƒ‰ي•œë‹¤.
  3. ىڑ°ى„ ىˆœىœ„: AGENTS.override.md > AGENTS.md > project_doc_fallback_filenames ى„¤ى •ê°’ ىˆœى„œ.
  4. يپ¬ê¸° ى œي•œ: project_doc_max_bytes ى„¤ى •ىœ¼ë،œ ى´‌ ë°”ى‌´يٹ¸ ى œي•œ. ى´ˆê³¼ ى‹œ truncate.
// codex-rs/core/src/project_doc.rs
pub const DEFAULT_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.md";
pub const LOCAL_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME: &str = "AGENTS.override.md";

fn candidate_filenames<'a>(config: &'a Config) -> Vec<&'a str> {
    let mut names = Vec::with_capacity(2 + config.project_doc_fallback_filenames.len());
    names.push(LOCAL_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME);   // ىµœىڑ°ى„ 
    names.push(DEFAULT_PROJECT_DOC_FILENAME); // ê¸°ë³¸
    for candidate in &config.project_doc_fallback_filenames {
        if !candidate.is_empty() && !names.contains(&candidate.as_str()) {
            names.push(candidate);
        }
    }
    names
}

2.2 ى§€ى‹œى‚¬ي•­ ى£¼ى‍… يک•ى‹‌

ë،œë”©ëگœ AGENTS.md ë‚´ىڑ©ى‌€ UserInstructions êµ¬ى،°ى²´ë،œ ى؛،ىٹگي™”ëگکى–´, contextual user message ي”„ë‍کê·¸ë¨¼يٹ¸ë،œ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ى—گ ى£¼ى‍…ëگœë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/instructions/user_instructions.rs
pub const USER_INSTRUCTIONS_PREFIX: &str = "# AGENTS.md instructions for ";

impl UserInstructions {
    pub(crate) fn serialize_to_text(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "{prefix}{directory}\n\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\n{contents}\n{suffix}",
            prefix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.start_marker(),
            directory = self.directory,
            contents = self.text,
            suffix = AGENTS_MD_FRAGMENT.end_marker(),
        )
    }
}

ë§ˆى»¤ يƒœê·¸(# AGENTS.md instructions for ... ~ </INSTRUCTIONS>)ë،œ ê°گى‹¸ى§„ يک•يƒœë،œ ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گ ى „ë‹¬ëگکë©°, ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى¶”ى¶œ ى‹œى—گëٹ” ى‌´ ي”„ë‍کê·¸ë¨¼يٹ¸ê°€ ي•„ي„°ë§پ ëŒ€ىƒپى‌´ ëگœë‹¤ â€” Phase 1ى—گى„œ ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒى‌„ ى§پë ¬ي™”ي•  ë•Œ developer role ë©”ى‹œى§€ى™€ ي•¨ê»ک ى œê±°ëگœë‹¤.

2.3 ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى‌½ê¸° ê²½ë،œ (Read Path)

ى‌´ى „ ى„¸ى…کى—گى„œ ىƒ‌ى„±ëگœ memory_summary.mdë¥¼ يک„ى‍¬ ى„¸ى…کى‌ک developer instructionsى—گ ى£¼ى‍…ي•کëٹ” ê²½ë،œê°€ ë³„ëڈ„ë،œ ى،´ى‍¬ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/prompts.rs
pub(crate) async fn build_memory_tool_developer_instructions(
    codex_home: &Path,
) -> Option<String> {
    let base_path = memory_root(codex_home);
    let memory_summary_path = base_path.join("memory_summary.md");
    let memory_summary = fs::read_to_string(&memory_summary_path).await.ok()?
        .trim().to_string();
    let memory_summary = truncate_text(
        &memory_summary,
        TruncationPolicy::Tokens(
            phase_one::MEMORY_TOOL_DEVELOPER_INSTRUCTIONS_SUMMARY_TOKEN_LIMIT // 5,000 ي† يپ°
        ),
    );
    if memory_summary.is_empty() { return None; }
    // read_path.md ي…œي”Œë¦؟ىœ¼ë،œ ë Œëچ”ë§پ
    let template = MemoryToolDeveloperInstructionsTemplate {
        base_path: &base_path.display().to_string(),
        memory_summary: &memory_summary,
    };
    template.render().ok()
}

read_path.md ي…œي”Œë¦؟ى‌€ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ى—گê²Œ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ يڈ´ëچ” يƒگىƒ‰ ى „ë‍µى‌„ ى•ˆë‚´ي•œë‹¤:

  • memory_summary.mdëٹ” ي•­ىƒپ system promptى—گ يڈ¬ي•¨ (ى‌´ë¯¸ ى œê³µëگ¨)
  • MEMORY.mdëٹ” keyword ê²€ىƒ‰ىڑ© ي•¸ë“œë¶پ
  • rollout_summaries/ëٹ” ê°œë³„ ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒى‌ک ىƒپى„¸ ى¦‌ê±°
  • skills/<name>/ëٹ” ى‍¬ى‚¬ىڑ© ê°€ëٹ¥ي•œ ى ˆى°¨

3. Phase 1: ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ ى¶”ى¶œ (Rollout Extraction)

3.1 ëڈ™ى‍‘ ىˆœى„œ

Phase 1ى‌ک run() ي•¨ىˆکëٹ” ى—„ê²©ي•œ 4ë‹¨ê³„ ىˆœى„œë،œ ى‹¤ي–‰ëگœë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
pub(in crate::memories) async fn run(session: &Arc<Session>, config: &Config) {
    // 1. claim eligible rollout jobs
    let Some(claimed_candidates) = claim_startup_jobs(session, &config.memories).await
        else { return; };

    // 2. build one stage-1 request context
    let stage_one_context = build_request_context(session, config).await;

    // 3. run stage-1 extraction jobs in parallel
    let outcomes = run_jobs(session, claimed_candidates, stage_one_context).await;

    // 4. emit metrics and logs
    let counts = aggregate_stats(outcomes);
    emit_metrics(session, &counts);
}

3.2 ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ ى„ ë³„ ë°ڈ Job Claiming

State DBى‌ک threads ي…Œى‌´ë¸”ê³¼ stage1_outputs, jobs ي…Œى‌´ë¸”ى‌„ JOINي•کى—¬ ى پê²© ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒى‌„ ى„ ë³„ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/state/src/runtime/memories.rs â€” claim_stage1_jobs_for_startup()
// ي•„ي„° ى،°ê±´:
// - memory_mode = 'enabled'
// - يک„ى‍¬ ى„¸ى…کى‌ک thread_id ى œى™¸
// - updated_at >= now - max_age_days  (ë„ˆë¬´ ىک¤ë‍کëگœ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// - updated_at <= now - min_rollout_idle_hours  (ى•„ى§پ ي™œى„±ى‌¸ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// - COALESCE(stage1_outputs.source_updated_at, -1) < updated_at  (ى‌´ë¯¸ ى²کë¦¬ëگœ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// - COALESCE(jobs.last_success_watermark, -1) < updated_at  (ى‌´ë¯¸ ى„±ê³µي•œ ê±´ ى œى™¸)
// ORDER BY updated_at DESC, id DESC
// LIMIT scan_limit (5,000)

ê°پ ي›„ë³´ى—گ ëŒ€ي•´ try_claim_stage1_job()ىœ¼ë،œ leaseë¥¼ يڑچë“‌ي•œë‹¤. ê°€ëٹ¥ي•œ ê²°ê³¼:

Stage1JobClaimOutcome ى‌کë¯¸
Claimed { ownership_token } lease يڑچë“‌ ى„±ê³µ, ى¶”ى¶œ ى§„ي–‰
SkippedUpToDate ى‌´ë¯¸ ىµœى‹  ى¶œë ¥ى‌´ ى‍ˆى‌Œ
SkippedRunning ë‹¤ë¥¸ ى›Œى»¤ê°€ lease ë³´ىœ  ى¤‘
SkippedRetryBackoff ى‹¤يŒ¨ ي›„ backoff ê¸°ê°„ ى¤‘
SkippedRetryExhausted ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„ يڑںىˆک ى†Œى§„ (ê¸°ë³¸ 3يڑŒ)

3.3 ë³‘ë ¬ ى¶”ى¶œ (Parallel Sampling)

Claimed jobë“¤ى‌„ futures::stream::buffer_unorderedë،œ ë³‘ë ¬ ى²کë¦¬ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
async fn run_jobs(
    session: &Arc<Session>,
    claimed_candidates: Vec<codex_state::Stage1JobClaim>,
    stage_one_context: RequestContext,
) -> Vec<JobResult> {
    futures::stream::iter(claimed_candidates.into_iter())
        .map(|claim| {
            let session = Arc::clone(session);
            let stage_one_context = stage_one_context.clone();
            async move { job::run(session.as_ref(), claim, &stage_one_context).await }
        })
        .buffer_unordered(phase_one::CONCURRENCY_LIMIT)  // ىµœëŒ€ 8ê°œ ëڈ™ى‹œ
        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
        .await
}

3.4 ê°œë³„ Job ى‹¤ي–‰ ي‌گë¦„

ê°پ jobى‌€ ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ يŒŒى‌¼ى‌„ ë،œë“œي•کê³ , ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ى—گ ë¶€ى پي•©ي•œ ي•­ëھ©ى‌„ ي•„ي„°ë§پي•œ ë’¤, ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گ ë³´ë‚¸ë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs â€” job::sample()
async fn sample(session, rollout_path, rollout_cwd, stage_one_context) {
    // 1. ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ ى•„ى‌´ي…œ ë،œë“œ
    let (rollout_items, _, _) = RolloutRecorder::load_rollout_items(rollout_path).await?;

    // 2. ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ê´€ë ¨ response itemsë§Œ ي•„ي„°ë§پ + ى§پë ¬ي™”
    let rollout_contents = serialize_filtered_rollout_response_items(&rollout_items)?;

    // 3. ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ىœˆëڈ„ىڑ°ى‌ک 70%ê¹Œى§€ truncate
    let prompt = Prompt {
        input: vec![/* build_stage_one_input_message() */],
        base_instructions: BaseInstructions {
            text: phase_one::PROMPT.to_string(),  // stage_one_system.md
        },
        output_schema: Some(output_schema()),     // JSON schema ê°•ى œ
        ..
    };

    // 4. ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چ ى‌‘ë‹µ ىˆکى§‘
    let mut stream = client_session.stream(&prompt, ...).await?;
    // ...

    // 5. secret redaction
    let mut output: StageOneOutput = serde_json::from_str(&result)?;
    output.raw_memory = redact_secrets(output.raw_memory);
    output.rollout_summary = redact_secrets(output.rollout_summary);
    output.rollout_slug = output.rollout_slug.map(redact_secrets);
}

3.5 ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒ ي•„ي„°ë§پ ê·œى¹™

ë،¤ى•„ى›ƒى‌ک ëھ¨ë“  ى•„ى‌´ي…œى‌´ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ى—گ يڈ¬ي•¨ëگکëٹ” ê²ƒى‌€ ى•„ë‹ˆë‹¤. sanitize_response_item_for_memories()ى—گى„œ:

  • developer role ë©”ى‹œى§€ â†’ ى œê±° (system prompt, AGENTS.md ë“± êµ¬ى،°ى پ ي”„ë‍کê·¸ë¨¼يٹ¸)
  • user role ë©”ى‹œى§€ ى¤‘ is_memory_excluded_contextual_user_fragment() â†’ ى œê±° (ي™کê²½ ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸, AGENTS.md ي”„ë‍کê·¸ë¨¼يٹ¸ ë“±)
  • ê¸°يƒ€ user/assistant ë©”ى‹œى§€ â†’ ë³´ى،´
  • should_persist_response_item_for_memories() â†’ function call, tool output ë“± يŒگى •

3.6 Phase 1 ى¶œë ¥ ىٹ¤ي‚¤ë§ˆ

ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌€ JSON Schemaë،œ ê°•ى œëگœ structured outputى‌„ ë°کي™کي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
pub fn output_schema() -> Value {
    json!({
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "rollout_summary": { "type": "string" },
            "rollout_slug": { "type": ["string", "null"] },
            "raw_memory": { "type": "string" }
        },
        "required": ["rollout_summary", "rollout_slug", "raw_memory"],
        "additionalProperties": false
    })
}
ي•„ë“œ ى—­ي• 
raw_memory ىƒپى„¸ ë§ˆيپ¬ë‹¤ىڑ´ يک•يƒœى‌ک ى›گى‹œ ê¸°ى–µ
rollout_summary ë‌¼ىڑ°يŒ…آ·ى‌¸ëچ±ى‹±ىڑ© 1ى¤„ ىڑ”ى•½
rollout_slug يŒŒى‌¼ëھ… ىƒ‌ى„±ىڑ© ى§§ى‌€ ى‹‌ë³„ى‍گ (ى„ يƒ‌)

3.7 Stage 1 System Prompt ي•µى‹¬ ë‚´ىڑ©

stage_one_system.md ي…œي”Œë¦؟ى‌ک ي•µى‹¬ ى§€ى¹¨:

  • No-op gate: "Will a future agent plausibly act better because of what I write here?" â€” ى•„ë‹ˆë©´ ë¹ˆ ي•„ë“œ ë°کي™ک
  • High-signal memory ê¸°ى¤€: (1) ى•ˆى •ى پ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى„ يک¸, (2) ê³ ë ˆë²„ë¦¬ى§€ ى ˆى°¨ى پ ى§€ى‹‌, (3) ى‌کى‚¬ê²°ى • يٹ¸ë¦¬ê±°, (4) ي™کê²½/ى›Œيپ¬ي”Œë،œىڑ° ى¦‌ê±°
  • Secret redaction: ي† يپ°/ي‚¤/ë¹„ë°€ë²ˆيک¸ëٹ” [REDACTED_SECRET]ىœ¼ë،œ ëŒ€ى²´
  • Immutable evidence: raw rolloutى‌€ ى ˆëŒ€ ىˆکى •ي•کى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ

3.8 ىک¤ë‍کëگœ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى •ë¦¬ (Prune)

Phase 1 ى‹¤ي–‰ ى „ى—گ prune()ê°€ يک¸ى¶œëگکى–´, max_unused_daysë¥¼ ى´ˆê³¼ي•œ ë¯¸ى‚¬ىڑ© stage-1 outputى‌„ ë°°ى¹ک ى‚­ى œي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase1.rs
pub(in crate::memories) async fn prune(session: &Arc<Session>, config: &Config) {
    if let Some(db) = session.services.state_db.as_deref() {
        let max_unused_days = config.memories.max_unused_days;
        match db.prune_stage1_outputs_for_retention(max_unused_days, PRUNE_BATCH_SIZE).await {
            Ok(pruned) => { /* log */ },
            Err(err) => { /* warn */ },
        }
    }
}

4. Phase 2: ى „ى—­ ي†µي•© (Global Consolidation)

4.1 ëڈ™ى‍‘ ىˆœى„œ

Phase 2ëٹ” 7ë‹¨ê³„ë،œ êµ¬ى„±ëگœ strictly linear flowë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” run()
pub(super) async fn run(session: &Arc<Session>, config: Arc<Config>) {
    // 1. Claim the global consolidation job
    let claim = job::claim(session, db).await?;

    // 2. Get agent config (sandbox policy ë“±)
    let agent_config = agent::get_config(config.clone());

    // 3. Query stage-1 outputs with selection rules
    let selection = db.get_phase2_input_selection(max_raw_memories, max_unused_days).await?;

    // 4. Sync filesystem artifacts
    sync_rollout_summaries_from_memories(&root, &artifact_memories, ...).await?;
    rebuild_raw_memories_file_from_memories(&root, &artifact_memories, ...).await?;

    // 5. Spawn consolidation sub-agent
    let prompt = agent::get_prompt(config, &selection);
    let thread_id = session.services.agent_control
        .spawn_agent(agent_config, prompt, Some(source)).await?;

    // 6. Handle agent lifecycle (heartbeat + status watch)
    agent::handle(session, claim, new_watermark, raw_memories, thread_id, timer);

    // 7. Emit metrics
}

4.2 Global Lock (Phase 2 Job Claim)

Phase 2ëٹ” ى „ى—­ى—گى„œ ë‹¨ ي•کë‚کى‌ک consolidationë§Œ ى‹¤ي–‰ëگکëڈ„ë،‌ ë³´ى‍¥ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” job::claim()
let claim = db
    .try_claim_global_phase2_job(session.conversation_id, phase_two::JOB_LEASE_SECONDS)
    .await?;

// ê°€ëٹ¥ي•œ ê²°ê³¼:
// Phase2JobClaimOutcome::Claimed { ownership_token, input_watermark }
// Phase2JobClaimOutcome::SkippedNotDirty   â€” ىƒˆ ى‍…ë ¥ ى—†ى‌Œ
// Phase2JobClaimOutcome::SkippedRunning    â€” ë‹¤ë¥¸ ى›Œى»¤ê°€ ى‹¤ي–‰ ى¤‘

4.3 Input Selection ë°ڈ Diff

Phase 2ëٹ” ë‹¨ىˆœي‍ˆ ىµœى‹  Nê°œë¥¼ ê°€ى ¸ىک¤ëٹ” ê²ƒى‌´ ى•„ë‹ˆë‌¼, ى‌´ى „ ى„±ê³µى پ Phase 2 ى‹¤ي–‰ê³¼ى‌ک diffë¥¼ ê³„ى‚°ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/state/src/model/memories.rs
pub struct Phase2InputSelection {
    pub selected: Vec<Stage1Output>,           // يک„ى‍¬ ى„ يƒ‌ëگœ ى‍…ë ¥
    pub previous_selected: Vec<Stage1Output>,  // ى‌´ى „ Phase 2ى—گى„œ ى„ يƒ‌ëگگëچک ى‍…ë ¥
    pub retained_thread_ids: Vec<ThreadId>,    // ى‌´ى „ى—گëڈ„ يک„ى‍¬ى—گëڈ„ ى„ يƒ‌ëگœ thread
    pub removed: Vec<Stage1OutputRef>,         // ى‌´ى „ى—گ ى„ يƒ‌ëگگى§€ë§Œ يک„ى‍¬ ë¹ ى§„ ى‍…ë ¥
}

Selection ى •ى±…:

  • max_unused_days ى´ˆê³¼ي•œ last_usage ê°€ى§„ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ى œى™¸
  • last_usage ى—†ىœ¼ë©´ generated_atىœ¼ë،œ fallback
  • usage_count ë†’ى‌€ ىˆœ â†’ ىµœê·¼ last_usage/generated_at ىˆœىœ¼ë،œ ى •ë ¬

4.4 يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œ ëڈ™ê¸°ي™”

Phase 2ëٹ” DBى‌ک stage-1 outputsë¥¼ يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œى—گ ëڈ™ê¸°ي™”ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/storage.rs

// 1. rollout_summaries/ ëڈ™ê¸°ي™”: ىœ ى§€ ëŒ€ىƒپë§Œ ë‚¨ê¸°ê³  ë‚کë¨¸ى§€ ى‚­ى œ
async fn sync_rollout_summaries_from_memories(root, memories, max) {
    let retained = retained_memories(memories, max);
    let keep = retained.iter().map(rollout_summary_file_stem).collect::<HashSet<_>>();
    prune_rollout_summaries(root, &keep).await?;
    for memory in retained {
        write_rollout_summary_for_thread(root, memory).await?;
    }
    // retainedê°€ ë¹„ى–´ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ MEMORY.md, memory_summary.md, skills/ ëڈ„ ى‚­ى œ
}

// 2. raw_memories.md ى‍¬ىƒ‌ى„±: ىµœى‹  ىˆœىœ¼ë،œ ë³‘ي•©
async fn rebuild_raw_memories_file(root, memories, max) {
    let mut body = String::from("# Raw Memories\n\n");
    body.push_str("Merged stage-1 raw memories (latest first):\n\n");
    for memory in retained {
        // ## Thread `{thread_id}`
        // updated_at: {rfc3339}
        // cwd: {path}
        // rollout_path: {path}
        // rollout_summary_file: {file}
        // {raw_memory content}
    }
}

4.5 Rollout Summary يŒŒى‌¼ëھ… ىƒ‌ى„±

يŒŒى‌¼ëھ…ى‌€ UUID ê¸°ë°ک thread_idى—گى„œ يƒ€ى‍„ىٹ¤يƒ¬ي”„ى™€ short hashë¥¼ ى¶”ى¶œي•کى—¬ ىƒ‌ى„±ëگœë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/storage.rs
fn rollout_summary_file_stem_from_parts(thread_id, source_updated_at, rollout_slug) -> String {
    // UUIDى—گى„œ يƒ€ى‍„ىٹ¤يƒ¬ي”„ ى¶”ى¶œ â†’ "2026-03-26T14-30-00"
    // UUID ي•کىœ„ 32ë¹„يٹ¸ â†’ 62ى§„ë²• 4ى‍گë¦¬ short hash
    // rollout_slug â†’ ى†Œë¬¸ى‍گي™” + يٹ¹ىˆکë¬¸ى‍گ â†’ '_' ë³€ي™ک, ىµœëŒ€ 60ى‍گ
    // ê²°ê³¼: "2026-03-26T14-30-00-aB3x-fix_login_bug"
}

4.6 Consolidation Sub-Agent ى„¤ى •

Phase 2ê°€ ىƒ‌ى„±ي•کëٹ” sub-agentëٹ” ë§¤ىڑ° ى œي•œى پى‌¸ ي™کê²½ى—گى„œ ى‹¤ي–‰ëگœë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” agent::get_config()
pub(super) fn get_config(config: Arc<Config>) -> Option<Config> {
    let mut agent_config = config.as_ref().clone();

    agent_config.cwd = memory_root(&config.codex_home);  // memories/ ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬

    // ى‍¬ê·€ى پ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ىƒ‌ى„± ى°¨ë‹¨
    agent_config.memories.generate_memories = false;

    // ىٹ¹ى‌¸ ë¶ˆي•„ىڑ”
    agent_config.permissions.approval_policy =
        Constrained::allow_only(AskForApproval::Never);

    // ي•کىœ„ ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ىƒ‌ى„±/يک‘ى—…/ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ëڈ„êµ¬ ë¹„ي™œى„±ي™”
    agent_config.features.disable(Feature::SpawnCsv);
    agent_config.features.disable(Feature::Collab);
    agent_config.features.disable(Feature::MemoryTool);

    // ىƒŒë“œë°•ىٹ¤: codex_homeë§Œ ى“°ê¸° ê°€ëٹ¥, ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى°¨ë‹¨
    let consolidation_sandbox_policy = SandboxPolicy::WorkspaceWrite {
        writable_roots: vec![codex_home],
        network_access: false,       // ë„¤يٹ¸ى›Œيپ¬ ى™„ى „ ى°¨ë‹¨
        ..
    };

    agent_config.model = Some(
        config.memories.consolidation_model.clone()
            .unwrap_or(phase_two::MODEL.to_string())  // "gpt-5.3-codex"
    );
    agent_config.model_reasoning_effort = Some(phase_two::REASONING_EFFORT);  // Medium
    Some(agent_config)
}

4.7 Heartbeat ë°ڈ ىƒپيƒœ ê°گى‹œ

Sub-agent ى‹¤ي–‰ ى¤‘ 90ى´ˆë§ˆë‹¤ heartbeatë¥¼ ë³´ë‚´ leaseë¥¼ ê°±ى‹ ي•œë‹¤. Lease ê°±ى‹  ى‹¤يŒ¨ ى‹œ ى¦‰ى‹œ ى¤‘ë‹¨:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs â€” agent::loop_agent()
async fn loop_agent(db, token, thread_id, mut rx) -> AgentStatus {
    let mut heartbeat_interval =
        tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(phase_two::JOB_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS)); // 90ى´ˆ

    loop {
        let status = rx.borrow().clone();
        if is_final_agent_status(&status) { break status; }

        tokio::select! {
            update = rx.changed() => {
                if update.is_err() { break status; }  // ى±„ë„گ ëپٹê¹€
            }
            _ = heartbeat_interval.tick() => {
                match db.heartbeat_global_phase2_job(&token, JOB_LEASE_SECONDS).await {
                    Ok(true) => {},                    // lease ê°±ى‹  ى„±ê³µ
                    Ok(false) => break Errored(..),    // ى†Œىœ ê¶Œ ىƒپى‹¤
                    Err(err) => break Errored(..),     // DB ىک¤ë¥ک
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

4.8 Watermark ê´€ë¦¬

Watermarkëٹ” Phase 2ê°€ ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ىœ¼ë،œ ى²کë¦¬ي•œ ى‍…ë ¥ى‌ک يƒ€ى‍„ىٹ¤يƒ¬ي”„ë¥¼ ى¶”ى پي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/phase2.rs
pub(super) fn get_watermark(
    claimed_watermark: i64,
    latest_memories: &[codex_state::Stage1Output],
) -> i64 {
    latest_memories.iter()
        .map(|m| m.source_updated_at.timestamp())
        .max()
        .unwrap_or(claimed_watermark)
        .max(claimed_watermark)  // ى ˆëŒ€ ë’¤ë،œ ê°€ى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œ
}
  • claim ى‹œى گى‌ک watermarkى™€ ى‹¤ى œ ë،œë“œëگœ ى‍…ë ¥ى‌ک ىµœى‹  يƒ€ى‍„ىٹ¤يƒ¬ي”„ ى¤‘ يپ° ê°’ى‌„ ى‚¬ىڑ©
  • ى„±ê³µ ى‹œ DBى—گ ى €ى‍¥ â†’ ë‹¤ى‌Œ Phase 2ê°€ "dirty ى—¬ë¶€" يŒگë‹¨ى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©

5. ëŒ€ي™” ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ ê´€ë¦¬

5.1 ContextManager êµ¬ى،°ى²´

ى„¸ى…ک ë‚´ ëŒ€ي™” ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ëٹ” ContextManagerë،œ ê´€ë¦¬ëگœë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/context_manager/history.rs
pub(crate) struct ContextManager {
    items: Vec<ResponseItem>,              // oldest â†’ newest ىˆœى„œ
    token_info: Option<TokenUsageInfo>,     // ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ API ى‌‘ë‹µى‌ک ي† يپ° ى‚¬ىڑ©ëں‰
    reference_context_item: Option<TurnContextItem>,  // ë‹¤ى‌Œ ي„´ى‌ک context ê¸°ى¤€ى„ 
}

ي•µى‹¬ ë©”ى„œë“œ:

  • record_items() â€” ىƒˆ ى•„ى‌´ي…œى‌„ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ى—گ ى¶”ê°€ي•کë©´ى„œ truncation policy ى پىڑ©
  • for_prompt() â€” ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گ ë³´ë‚¼ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ë¥¼ ى •ê·œي™”ي•کى—¬ ë°کي™ک (GhostSnapshot ى œê±°, ى‌´ë¯¸ى§€ ي•„ي„°ë§پ ë“±)
  • remove_first_item() / remove_last_item() â€” ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ىœˆëڈ„ىڑ° ى´ˆê³¼ ى‹œ ى•„ى‌´ي…œ ى œê±°
  • replace() â€” compaction ي›„ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ ى „ى²´ êµگى²´

5.2 ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ ى •ê·œي™”

ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گ ë³´ë‚´ê¸° ى „ى—گ normalize_history()ê°€ يک¸ى¶œëگکى–´:

  • ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌´ ى§€ى›گي•کى§€ ى•ٹëٹ” modality(ىکˆ: ى‌´ë¯¸ى§€) ى œê±°
  • ê³ ى•„ function call output ى •ë¦¬ (ëŒ€ى‌‘ي•کëٹ” callى‌´ ى—†ëٹ” output ى œê±°)
  • GhostSnapshot ى•„ى‌´ي…œ ى œê±° (UIىڑ©ى‌´ë¯€ë،œ ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گ ë¶ˆي•„ىڑ”)

6. ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ىœˆëڈ„ىڑ° ê´€ë¦¬ ë°ڈ Compaction

6.1 ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ Compaction ى „ë‍µ

Codexëٹ” ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ ىœˆëڈ„ىڑ°ê°€ ê°€ë“‌ ى°¨ë©´ ëŒ€ي™” ë‚´ىڑ©ى‌„ ى••ى¶•ي•کëٹ” compactionى‌„ ىˆکي–‰ي•œë‹¤:

  1. Inline Compaction (compact.rs): ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گê²Œ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ ىڑ”ى•½ى‌„ ىڑ”ى²­ي•کى—¬ ë،œى»¬ى—گى„œ ى²کë¦¬
  2. Remote Compaction (compact_remote.rs): OpenAI ى œê³µى‍گ ى‚¬ىڑ© ى‹œ ى„œë²„ى¸،ى—گى„œ ى²کë¦¬
// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
pub(crate) fn should_use_remote_compact_task(provider: &ModelProviderInfo) -> bool {
    provider.is_openai()  // OpenAIى‌¸ ê²½ىڑ°ë§Œ remote compaction
}

6.2 Inline Compaction ي‌گë¦„

// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
pub(crate) async fn run_inline_auto_compact_task(
    sess: Arc<Session>,
    turn_context: Arc<TurnContext>,
    initial_context_injection: InitialContextInjection,
) -> CodexResult<()> {
    let prompt = turn_context.compact_prompt().to_string(); // compact/prompt.md
    // ...
    // 1. ëھ¨ëچ¸ى—گê²Œ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ ىڑ”ى•½ ىڑ”ى²­
    // 2. ى‌‘ë‹µى—گى„œ ىڑ”ى•½ ى¶”ى¶œ
    let summary_text = format!("{SUMMARY_PREFIX}\n{summary_suffix}");
    // 3. ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ىˆکى§‘ (ىµœê·¼ 20,000 ي† يپ°ê¹Œى§€)
    let user_messages = collect_user_messages(history_items);
    // 4. ىƒˆ ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ êµ¬ى„±: ى´ˆê¸° ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ + ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ + ىڑ”ى•½
    let new_history = build_compacted_history(Vec::new(), &user_messages, &summary_text);
    // 5. ي‍ˆىٹ¤ي† ë¦¬ êµگى²´
    sess.replace_compacted_history(new_history, reference_context_item, compacted_item).await;
}

6.3 Compaction ي›„ Initial Context ى‍¬ى£¼ى‍…

Compaction ىœ يک•ى—گ ë”°ë‌¼ initial context ى£¼ى‍… ى „ë‍µى‌´ ë‹¤ë¥´ë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
pub(crate) enum InitialContextInjection {
    BeforeLastUserMessage,  // mid-turn compaction: ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ى§پى „ى—گ ى£¼ى‍…
    DoNotInject,            // pre-turn compaction: ë‹¤ى‌Œ ي„´ى—گى„œ ى‍گëڈ™ ى‍¬ى£¼ى‍…
}

6.4 ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ë³´ى،´ ى „ë‍µ

Compaction ى‹œ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ëٹ” ىµœê·¼ ê²ƒë¶€ي„° ى—­ىˆœىœ¼ë،œ ىµœëŒ€ COMPACT_USER_MESSAGE_MAX_TOKENS (20,000 ي† يپ°)ê¹Œى§€ ë³´ى،´ëگœë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/compact.rs
fn build_compacted_history_with_limit(
    mut history, user_messages, summary_text, max_tokens,
) -> Vec<ResponseItem> {
    let mut remaining = max_tokens;
    for message in user_messages.iter().rev() {  // ىµœى‹  â†’ ê³¼ê±° ىˆœ
        let tokens = approx_token_count(message);
        if tokens <= remaining {
            selected_messages.push(message.clone());
            remaining -= tokens;
        } else {
            let truncated = truncate_text(message, TruncationPolicy::Tokens(remaining));
            selected_messages.push(truncated);
            break;
        }
    }
    selected_messages.reverse();
    // historyى—گ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ë©”ى‹œى§€ ى¶”ê°€ â†’ ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ى—گ ىڑ”ى•½ ى¶”ê°€
}

7. Memory Trace (ë ˆê±°ى‹œ ê²½ë،œ)

memory_trace.rsëٹ” raw trace يŒŒى‌¼ى—گى„œ ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ë¥¼ ë¹Œë“œي•کëٹ” ë³„ëڈ„ ê²½ë،œë¥¼ ى œê³µي•œë‹¤. Phase 1/2 يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸ê³¼ ëڈ…ë¦½ى پىœ¼ë،œ ëڈ™ى‍‘:

}

Trace ë،œë”©ى‌€ ë‘گ ê°€ى§€ يک•ى‹‌ى‌„ ى§€ى›گي•œë‹¤:

  • JSON array: [{...}, {...}] يک•يƒœ
  • JSONL: ي•œ ى¤„ى—گ ي•کë‚کى‌ک JSON object

ى •ê·œي™” ى‹œ payload.type == "response_item"ى‌¸ ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ى—گى„œ payloadë¥¼ ى¶”ى¶œي•کê³ , is_allowed_trace_item()ىœ¼ë،œ message roleى‌´ assistant, system, developer, user ى¤‘ ي•کë‚کى‌¸ى§€ ي™•ى‌¸ي•œë‹¤.

10.4 Memory Root ê´€ë¦¬

ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ يŒŒى‌¼ى‹œىٹ¤ي…œى‌ک root ى´ˆê¸°ي™”ëڈ„ ë³´ى•ˆى‌„ ê³ ë ¤ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/memories/control.rs
pub(crate) async fn clear_memory_root_contents(memory_root: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    // symlinkى‌¸ ê²½ىڑ° ê±°ë¶€ (ë³´ى•ˆ)
    match tokio::fs::symlink_metadata(memory_root).await {
        Ok(metadata) if metadata.file_type().is_symlink() => {
            return Err(std::io::Error::new(
                std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput,
                format!("refusing to clear symlinked memory root {}", memory_root.display()),
            ));
        }
        // ...
    }
    // ëھ¨ë“  يŒŒى‌¼/ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ ى‚­ى œ
}

Axis 11: Plugin system

AXIS

011_plugin-system/11_co_plugin.md

Overview

Axis 11: Plugin system â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” plugin system.

  • Codexى‌ک ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ى‌€ skillë³´ë‹¤ ي›¨ى”¬ ë¬´ê±°ىڑ´ ي™•ى‍¥ ë‹¨ىœ„ë‹¤. skillى‌´ SKILL.md ë³¸ë¬¸ ى£¼ى‍…ى‌´ë‌¼ë©´, pluginى‌€ MCP ى„œë²„ + app connector + marketplace ë°°يڈ¬ + OAuth ى‌¸ى¦‌ + ëں°يƒ€ى‍„ ëڈ™ê¸°ي™”ê¹Œى§€ يڈ¬ي•¨ي•کëٹ” يŒ¨ي‚¤ى§€ë‹¤.
  • ي•µى‹¬ى‌€ PluginsManager(67KB)ى‌´ë‹¤. ى‌´ ë§¤ë‹ˆى €ê°€ ë،œى»¬ ى„¤ى¹کëگœ ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸, curated marketplace ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸, remote featured ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ى‌„ ي†µي•© ê´€ë¦¬ي•œë‹¤. ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ى‌ک identityëٹ” PluginId(storeى—گى„œ ê´€ë¦¬)ى‌´ê³ , ى‹¤ى œ capabilityëٹ” PluginCapabilitySummaryى—گ app_connector_idsى™€ mcp_server_namesë،œ ë…¸ى¶œëگœë‹¤.
  • stateDiagram-v2
  • [*] --> Discovery : marketplace يƒگىƒ‰
  • Discovery --> Install : resolve + validate
  • Install --> Store : atomic copy to cache
  • Store --> Load : manifest يŒŒى‹± + capability ىˆکى§‘
  • Load --> Injection : developer instructions ى£¼ى‍…
  • Injection --> Sync : remote/curated ëڈ™ê¸°ي™”
  • Sync --> Discovery : SHA ë¹„êµگ ي›„ ى‍¬ë°œê²¬

    1. Plugin ë‌¼ى‌´ي”„ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´

// store.rs â€” PluginId
pub struct PluginId {
    pub plugin_name: String,
    pub marketplace_name: String,
}

impl PluginId {
    pub fn parse(plugin_key: &str) -> Result<Self, PluginIdError> {
        // "github@openai-curated" â†’ plugin_name="github", marketplace_name="openai-curated"
        let Some((plugin_name, marketplace_name)) = plugin_key.rsplit_once('@') else {
            return Err(PluginIdError::Invalid(/* ... */));
        };
        Self::new(plugin_name.to_string(), marketplace_name.to_string())
    }

    pub fn as_key(&self) -> String {
        format!("{}@{}", self.plugin_name, self.marketplace_name)
    }
}

README / docs

fn validate_plugin_segment(segment: &str, kind: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
    if !segment.chars().all(|ch| ch.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || ch == '-' || ch == '_') {
        return Err(format!("invalid {kind}: only ASCII letters, digits, `_`, and `-` are allowed"));
    }
    Ok(())
}

README / docs

// store.rs â€” ë””ë ‰ي† ë¦¬ êµ¬ى،°
pub struct PluginStore { root: AbsolutePathBuf }

impl PluginStore {
    // $CODEX_HOME/plugins/cache/<marketplace>/<plugin>
    pub fn plugin_base_root(&self, plugin_id: &PluginId) -> AbsolutePathBuf { /* ... */ }

    // $CODEX_HOME/plugins/cache/<marketplace>/<plugin>/<version>
    pub fn plugin_root(&self, plugin_id: &PluginId, version: &str) -> AbsolutePathBuf { /* ... */ }

    pub fn is_installed(&self, plugin_id: &PluginId) -> bool {
        self.active_plugin_version(plugin_id).is_some()
    }
}

pub fn active_plugin_version(&self, plugin_id: &PluginId) -> Option<String> {
    let mut discovered_versions = /* read_dir â†’ filter dirs â†’ collect */;
    discovered_versions.sort_unstable();
    if discovered_versions.iter().any(|v| v == "local") {
        Some("local".to_string())
    } else {
        discovered_versions.pop() // ى‚¬ى „ىˆœ ë§ˆى§€ë§‰ = ىµœى‹ 
    }
}

pub fn resolve_marketplace_plugin(
    marketplace_path: &AbsolutePathBuf,
    plugin_name: &str,
    restriction_product: Option<Product>,
) -> Result<ResolvedMarketplacePlugin, MarketplaceError> {
    let marketplace = load_raw_marketplace_manifest(marketplace_path)?;
    let plugin = marketplace.plugins.find(|p| p.name == plugin_name)?;
    
    // ى •ى±… ê²€ى¦‌: NotAvailableى‌´ë©´ ê±°ë¶€
    // ى œي’ˆ ê²Œى‌´يŒ…: products ي•„ë“œê°€ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ restriction_product ë§¤ى¹­ ي™•ى‌¸
    // PluginId ىƒ‌ى„± + source_path ي•´ى„‌
    Ok(ResolvedMarketplacePlugin { plugin_id, source_path, auth_policy })
}

sequenceDiagram
    participant S as Session Start
    participant G as Git
    participant H as GitHub HTTP API
    participant FS as $CODEX_HOME/.tmp/

    S->>G: git ls-remote HEAD SHA
    alt Git ى„±ê³µ
        G->>S: remote_sha
        S->>FS: read local .tmp/plugins.sha
        alt SHA ى‌¼ى¹ک + .git ى،´ى‍¬
            S->>S: skip (ى‌´ë¯¸ ىµœى‹ )
        else SHA ë¶ˆى‌¼ى¹ک
            S->>G: git clone --depth 1
            G->>FS: cloned_repo â†’ temp dir
            S->>FS: atomic rename â†’ .tmp/plugins/
            S->>FS: write .tmp/plugins.sha
        end
    else Git ى‹¤يŒ¨
        S->>H: GitHub API fallback
        H->>S: repo summary â†’ default_branch â†’ HEAD SHA
        H->>S: zipball ë‹¤ىڑ´ë،œë“œ
        S->>FS: extract + activate
    end

README / docs

const OPENAI_PLUGINS_OWNER: &str = "openai";
const OPENAI_PLUGINS_REPO: &str = "plugins";
const CURATED_PLUGINS_RELATIVE_DIR: &str = ".tmp/plugins";
const CURATED_PLUGINS_SHA_FILE: &str = ".tmp/plugins.sha";
const CURATED_PLUGINS_GIT_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);

README / docs

pub fn build_plugin_injections(
    mentioned_plugins: &[PluginCapabilitySummary],
    mcp_tools: &HashMap<String, ToolInfo>,
    available_connectors: &[connectors::AppInfo],
) -> Vec<ResponseItem> {
    mentioned_plugins.iter().filter_map(|plugin| {
        // 1. ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ى—گ ى†چي•œ MCP ى„œë²„ ي•„ي„°ë§پ (CODEX_APPS ى œى™¸)
        let available_mcp_servers = mcp_tools.values()
            .filter(|tool| tool.plugin_display_names.contains(&plugin.display_name))
            .map(|tool| tool.server_name.clone())
            .collect();
        // 2. ي™œى„±ي™”ëگœ app connector ي•„ي„°ë§پ
        let available_apps = available_connectors.iter()
            .filter(|c| c.is_enabled && c.plugin_display_names.contains(&plugin.display_name))
            .map(connector_display_label)
            .collect();
        // 3. DeveloperInstructionsë،œ ë‍کي•‘
        render_explicit_plugin_instructions(plugin, &available_mcp_servers, &available_apps)
            .map(DeveloperInstructions::new)
            .map(ResponseItem::from)
    }).collect()
}

README / docs

## Plugins
### Available plugins
- `GitHub`: Manage repos, issues, and PRs
- `Notion`: Create and edit Notion pages
### How to use plugins
- Discovery: The list above is the plugins available in this session.
- Trigger rules: If the user explicitly names a plugin, prefer capabilities associated with that plugin.
- Preference: When a relevant plugin is available, prefer using capabilities associated with that plugin.

README / docs

// ى¶œë ¥ ىکˆى‹œ:
// Capabilities from the `GitHub` plugin:
// - Skills from this plugin are prefixed with `GitHub:`.
// - MCP servers: `github-mcp-server`.
// - Apps: `GitHub Issues`, `GitHub PRs`.
// Use these plugin-associated capabilities to help solve the task.

// remote.rs â€” featured ي”Œëں¬ê·¸ى‌¸ ى،°يڑŒ (ى‌¸ى¦‌ ى„ يƒ‌ى پ)
pub async fn fetch_remote_featured_plugin_ids(
    config: &Config,
    auth: Option<&CodexAuth>,
    product: Option<Product>,
) -> Result<Vec<String>, RemotePluginFetchError> {
    let url = format!("{base_url}/plugins/featured");
    let request = client.get(&url)
        .query(&[("platform", product.unwrap_or(Product::Codex).to_app_platform())])
        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(10));
    // ى‌¸ى¦‌ى‌´ ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©´ bearer token ى¶”ê°€
    // ...
}
