// codex-rs/core/src/error.rs
let local_reset = resets_at.with_timezone(&Local);

README / docs

graph TD
    CLI[cli] --> Core[core]
    TUI[tui] --> Core
    AppServer[app-server] --> Core
    Core --> GitUtils[git-utils]
    Core --> Otel[otel]
    Core --> Features[features]
    Core --> Login[login]
    Core --> CodexApi[codex-api]
    Core --> Protocol[protocol]
    Login --> KeyringStore[keyring-store]
    CodexApi --> |SSE/WS| OpenAI[OpenAI API]
    Ollama[ollama] --> Core
    LMStudio[lmstudio] --> Core
    Otel --> |OTLP| Statsig[Statsig/OTLP Backend]

Main analysis

Codex residual cross-interest analysis

Overview

Codex (OpenAI) is a Rust-based coding agent that, in addition to the core 12 analysis dimensions (TUI, Prompt, Tool, Skill, Subagent, Surface, Sandbox, Config, MCP, Memory, Plugin, Guardian), includes various cross-cutting concerns such as Git integration, model provider abstraction, error handling, telemetry (OpenTelemetry), feature flags, authentication, voice/multimodal, packaging/distribution, and test infrastructure. In particular, the git-utils, otel, features, and login crates are designed as independent areas of interest, and the event-based asynchronous architecture (async_channel + tokio::sync::watch) runs throughout. Internationalization (i18n) and accessibility (a11y) are not currently implemented.

1. Git/VCS integration

1.1 only crate: git-utils

The core of Git integration is centered on the codex-rs/git-utils/ crate. Module configuration:

module Role
operations.rs Low-level git CLI wrapper (run_git, run_git_for_stdout)
info.rs Collect repository information (branch, commit, remote, diff)
branch.rs merge-base calculation, upstream tracking
ghost_commits.rs Create/Restore Ghost Commit for Snapshot
apply.rs Apply patches based on git apply --3way
errors.rs GitToolingError error type
platform.rs cross-platform symbolic link

1.2 Git command execution pattern

All git commands are executed as std::process::Command (synchronous) or tokio::process::Command (asynchronous) and do not use the git2 library. Timeout of 5 seconds is hardcoded to prevent hangs on large repositories:

// codex-rs/git-utils/src/info.rs
const GIT_COMMAND_TIMEOUT: TokioDuration = TokioDuration::from_secs(5);

async fn run_git_command_with_timeout(args: &[&str], cwd: &Path) -> Option<std::process::Output> {
    let mut command = Command::new("git");
    command
        .env("GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS", "0")  // ى‍ ê¸ˆ ى¶©ëڈŒ ë°©ى§€
        .args(args)
        .current_dir(cwd)
        .kill_on_drop(true);
    let result = timeout(GIT_COMMAND_TIMEOUT, command.output()).await;
    match result {
        Ok(Ok(output)) => Some(output),
        _ => None,
    }
}

1.3 Ghost Commits

This is the core of Codex's undo function. At each agent turn, a full snapshot of the work tree is saved with git commit:

  • Default commit message: "codex snapshot"
  • Automatic exclusion of large files/directories: node_modules, .venv, dist, build, etc.
  • Large untracked file threshold: 10 MiB
  • Large untracked directory threshold: 200 entries.
// codex-rs/git-utils/src/ghost_commits.rs
const DEFAULT_IGNORED_DIR_NAMES: &[&str] = &[
    "node_modules", ".venv", "venv", "env", ".env",
    "dist", "build", ".pytest_cache", ".mypy_cache",
    ".cache", ".tox", "__pycache__",
];

1.4 Diff Strategy

The git_diff_to_remote() function finds the nearest remote SHA and calculates the diff:

  1. Build a branch ancestry (current branch -> default branch -> branch with remotes)
  2. Calculate the distance (number of commits) from the remote SHA and HEAD of each branch
  3. Select the closest remote SHA
  4. Create a diff with git diff --no-textconv --no-ext-diff
  5. Untracked files are added as diff compared to /dev/null

1.5 patch applied

Uses git apply --3way in apply.rs and supports dry-run(--check) mode and revert(-R) mode. Additional git config can be injected through the CODEX_APPLY_GIT_CFG environment variable.

flowchart TD
    A[ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ/ëھ¨ëچ¸ى‌´ يŒ¨ى¹ک ى œى¶œ] --> B[ى‍„ى‹œ يŒŒى‌¼ى—گ diff ى €ى‍¥]
    B --> C{revert ëھ¨ë“œ?}
    C -->|Yes| D[git addë،œ ê²½ë،œ ىٹ¤ي…Œى‌´ى§•]
    C -->|No| E[git apply --3way]
    D --> F[git apply --3way -R]
    E --> G[ê²°ê³¼ يŒŒى‹±: applied/skipped/conflicted]
    F --> G

2. Model routing / provider abstraction

2.1 Provider Registry

Define the provider system in codex-rs/core/src/model_provider_info.rs. 3 built-in providers:

ID name default port Wire API
openai OpenAI - Responses
ollama gpt-oss 11434 Responses
lmstudio gpt-oss 1234 Responses

Chat API (wire_api = "chat") has been completely removed, leaving only a single variant of WireApi, Responses.

2.2 ModelProviderInfo structure

pub struct ModelProviderInfo {
    pub name: String,
    pub base_url: Option<String>,
    pub env_key: Option<String>,
    pub wire_api: WireApi,
    pub query_params: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub http_headers: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub env_http_headers: Option<HashMap<String, String>>,
    pub request_max_retries: Option<u64>,
    pub stream_max_retries: Option<u64>,
    pub stream_idle_timeout_ms: Option<u64>,
    pub requires_openai_auth: bool,
    pub supports_websockets: bool,
    // ...
}

Custom providers can be added/overridden in the model_providers section of ~/.codex/config.toml.

2.3 OSS Provider (Ollama / LM Studio)

Each exists as an independent crate:

  • codex-rs/ollama/: Ollama client, model pull, version check (Responses API support minimum 0.13.4)
  • codex-rs/lmstudio/: LM Studio client, download and load models.

Both follow the ensure_oss_ready() pattern:

// codex-rs/ollama/src/lib.rs
pub async fn ensure_oss_ready(config: &Config) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    let ollama_client = OllamaClient::try_from_oss_provider(config).await?;
    match ollama_client.fetch_models().await {
        Ok(models) => {
            if !models.iter().any(|m| m == model) {
                ollama_client.pull_with_reporter(model, &mut reporter).await?;
            }
        }
        Err(err) => {
            tracing::warn!("Failed to query local models from Ollama: {}.", err);
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}

2.4 Retry/Fallback Settings

Provider-specific retry settings are converted to ApiRetryConfig:

parameters default ىµœëŒ€ê°’
request_max_retries 4 100
stream_max_retries 5 100
stream_idle_timeout_ms 300,000 (5 minutes) -
websocket_connect_timeout_ms 15,000 (15 seconds) -

2.5 Transport Layer

ModelClient (core/src/client.rs) persists for the lifetime of the session and supports two transport layers: SSE and WebSocket. WebSocket optimizes reuse with the prewarm(generate=false) strategy in v2.

flowchart LR
    A[ModelClient] --> B{ى „ى†، ى„ يƒ‌}
    B -->|SSE| C[ResponsesClient / reqwest]
    B -->|WebSocket| D[ResponsesWebsocketClient]
    D --> E[Prewarm: response.create generate=false]
    E --> F[ى‹¤ى œ ىڑ”ى²­ ى‍¬ى‚¬ىڑ©]

3. Error handling/recovery pattern

3.1 CodexErr enumeration

CodexErr, defined in codex-rs/core/src/error.rs, is the central error type with about 20 variants:

pub enum CodexErr {
    TurnAborted,
    Stream(String, Option<Duration>),      // ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„ ê°€ëٹ¥
    ContextWindowExceeded,                  // ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„ ë¶ˆê°€
    UnexpectedStatus(UnexpectedResponseError),
    UsageLimitReached(UsageLimitReachedError),
    ServerOverloaded,
    ConnectionFailed(ConnectionFailedError),
    RetryLimit(RetryLimitReachedError),
    RefreshTokenFailed(RefreshTokenFailedError),
    Sandbox(SandboxErr),
    Fatal(String),
    // ...
}

3.2 Determination of retryability

The is_retryable() method determines whether a per-error retry is possible:

Retry possible No retry possible
Stream, Timeout, UnexpectedStatus TurnAborted, Interrupted, EnvVar
ConnectionFailed, ResponseStreamFailed UsageLimitReached, ServerOverloaded
InternalServerError, InternalAgentDied QuotaExceeded, InvalidRequest
Io, Json, TokioJoin ContextWindowExceeded, RefreshTokenFailed

3.3 ي”„ë،œي† ى½œ ى—گëں¬ ë³€ي™ک

The to_codex_protocol_error() method maps internal errors to client protocol errors (CodexErrorInfo):

CodexErr::ContextWindowExceeded -> CodexErrorInfo::ContextWindowExceeded
CodexErr::UsageLimitReached     -> CodexErrorInfo::UsageLimitExceeded
CodexErr::RetryLimit            -> CodexErrorInfo::ResponseTooManyFailedAttempts
CodexErr::RefreshTokenFailed    -> CodexErrorInfo::Unauthorized

3.4 Usage limit error UX

UsageLimitReachedError displays a different message depending on the user's plan type (Free, Plus, Pro, Team, Enterprise, etc.) and converts the resets_at timestamp to the local time zone.

3.5 SandboxErr

Sandbox errors are in a separate enum:

pub enum SandboxErr {
    Denied { output, network_policy_decision },
    Timeout { output },
    Signal(i32),
    // Linux ى „ىڑ©: SeccompInstall, SeccompBackend, LandlockRestrict
}

3.6 Git error types

pub enum GitToolingError {
    GitCommand { command, status, stderr },
    GitOutputUtf8 { command, source },
    NotAGitRepository { path },
    NonRelativePath { path },
    PathEscapesRepository { path },
    PathPrefix(StripPrefixError),
    Walkdir(WalkdirError),
    Io(io::Error),
}

4. Telemetry / Observability

4.1 exclusive crate: otel

The codex-rs/otel/ crate is dedicated to OpenTelemetry integration.

flowchart TD
    A[OtelProvider] --> B[LoggerProvider / OTLP]
    A --> C[TracerProvider / OTLP]
    A --> D[MetricsClient / Statsig]
    B --> E[OpenTelemetryTracingBridge]
    C --> F[tracing_opentelemetry layer]
    D --> G[ى „ى—­ MetricsClient]

4.2 OtelSettings

pub struct OtelSettings {
    pub environment: String,
    pub service_name: String,
    pub service_version: String,
    pub codex_home: PathBuf,
    pub exporter: OtelExporter,        // ë،œê·¸ ë‚´ë³´ë‚´ê¸°
    pub trace_exporter: OtelExporter,  // يٹ¸ë ˆى‌´ىٹ¤ ë‚´ë³´ë‚´ê¸°
    pub metrics_exporter: OtelExporter, // ë©”يٹ¸ë¦­ ë‚´ë³´ë‚´ê¸°
    pub runtime_metrics: bool,
}

4.3 Export backend

OtelExporter enum:

Transformation Description
None Disable
Statsig Codex internal metrics collection (Statsig OTLP HTTP)
OtlpGrpc gRPC OTLP (endpoint, headers, TLS)
OtlpHttp HTTP OTLP (endpoint, headers, protocol, TLS)

The default metrics export is sent to https://ab.chatgpt.com/otlp/v1/metrics via Statsig.

4.4 Metric naming scheme

Key metrics collected by SessionTelemetry:

  • codex.api_call.count / codex.api_call.duration
  • codex.tool_call.count / codex.tool_call.duration
  • codex.sse_event.count / codex.sse_event.duration
  • codex.websocket_event.count / codex.websocket_event.duration
  • codex.responses_api.overhead_duration / codex.responses_api.inference_time
  • codex.feature.state (feature flag state)

4.5 Tracing Filter

Apply separate filters to logs and traces:

  • Export log: codex_otel.log_only, codex_otel.network_proxy targets
  • Export trace: codex_otel.trace_safe target (excluding sensitive information)
  • Span is always included in trace

4.6 W3C Trace Context

W3C Trace Context propagation is supported in trace_context.rs, and the parent context can be read from the environment variable TRACEPARENT.

5. IDE integration

5.1 VSCode Mention

A VSCode variant of the SessionSource enum exists, which is used as a rollout session source:

// codex-rs/rollout/src/lib.rs
pub static INTERACTIVE_SESSION_SOURCES: LazyLock<Vec<SessionSource>> = LazyLock::new(|| {
    vec![
        SessionSource::Cli,
        SessionSource::VSCode,
        SessionSource::Custom("atlas".to_string()),
        SessionSource::Custom("chatgpt".to_string()),
    ]
});

5.2 App-Server Protocol

Codex defines an LSP-like App-Server Protocol to communicate with various IDE frontends:

  • codex-rs/app-server-protocol/: JSON-RPC based protocol definition
  • codex-rs/app-server/: Server implementation (WebSocket + Unix Domain Socket)
  • codex-rs/app-server-client/: Client library

TypeScript schema is also automatically generated (schema/typescript/) so it can be used type-safely in VSCode extensions, etc.

5.3 Current Status

The code base does not include VSCode extension source code. The App-Server Protocol serves as an IDE integrated interface, and protocol types such as InitializeParams, Settings, and ThreadId are designed to support various surfaces such as VSCode/ChatGPT.

6. Packaging / Distribution

6.1 Rust binary distribution

Releases are automated with the GitHub Actions workflow (rust-release.yml):

Release trigger: Push rust-v*.*.* tags

Build Target:

Runner target
macos-15-xlarge aarch64-apple-darwin
macos-15-xlarge x86_64-apple-darwin
ubuntu-24.04 x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
ubuntu-24.04 x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
ubuntu-24.04-arm aarch64-unknown-linux-musl
ubuntu-24.04-arm aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu

A separate Windows release workflow (rust-release-windows.yml) also exists.

6.2 Build system

Dual build system:

  • Cargo: Basic Rust build (codex-rs/Cargo.toml workspace)
  • Bazel: MODULE.bazel, BUILD.bazel (large-scale monorepo support)

Provide developer convenience commands with justfile:

codex *args:
    cargo run --bin codex -- "$@"
test:
    cargo nextest run --no-fail-fast
clippy *args:
    cargo clippy --tests "$@"

6.3 npm package

The TypeScript SDK is located in sdk/typescript/ and is bundled with tsup. An npm package also exists in 120_codex-cli/.

6.4 Nix

flake.nix and flake.lock exist to support Nix-based reproducible builds.

6.5 LTO settings

Use thin LTO in release builds (instead of full LTO due to 60 minute timeout issues on ARM Ubuntu):

CARGO_PROFILE_RELEASE_LTO: thin

7. Test infrastructure

7.1 Test Strategy

Unit testing: Use the #[cfg(test)] module and the _tests.rs file pattern together. Test files exist for almost all core modules.

Integration Tests: Scenario-specific tests in the tests/suite/ directory:

codex-rs/core/tests/suite/
    exec.rs, compact.rs, review.rs, js_repl.rs,
    agent_jobs.rs, abort_tasks.rs, seatbelt.rs, ...

codex-rs/app-server/tests/suite/v2/
    turn_start.rs, thread_list.rs, plugin_install.rs,
    realtime_conversation.rs, rate_limits.rs, ...

7.2 Test tools

  • cargo nextest: default test runner (parallel execution, --no-fail-fast)
  • Mock Model Server: tests/common/mock_model_server.rs
  • SSE Streaming Fixtures: tests/common/streaming_sse.rs
  • Tracing test: tests/common/tracing.rs

7.3 CI Workflow

Main features of rust-ci.yml:

Job Description
changed Detect change areas (run optional tests)
general cargo fmt format verification
cargo_shear Unused dependency checking
clippy Lint Inspection
test Run cargo nextest test

Based on change detection, it is selectively executed depending on whether codex-rs/, tools/argument-comment-lint/, or .github/ have been changed.

7.4 SDK testing

Jest-based tests in the TypeScript SDK (sdk/typescript/tests/):

  • exec.test.ts: Execution tests
  • run.test.ts: General run tests
  • runStreamed.test.ts: streaming test
  • abort.test.ts: Abort test

8. Authentication/API key management

8.1 only crate: login

The codex-rs/login/ crate is responsible for all authentication logic.

8.2 Authentication Mode

pub enum AuthMode {
    ApiKey,           // OPENAI_API_KEY ي™کê²½ë³€ىˆک
    Chatgpt,          // ChatGPT OAuth ë،œê·¸ى‌¸
    ChatgptAuthTokens, // ChatGPT ى‌¸ى¦‌ ي† يپ°
}

8.3 Device Code Certification

Implement OAuth 2.0 Device Code Flow in device_code_auth.rs:

  1. Request device code + user code with POST /deviceauth/usercode
  2. Display verification URL to user
  3. Wait for token completion at polling interval

8.4 PKCE certification

Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) implementation in pkce.rs:

pub fn generate_pkce() -> PkceCodes {
    let mut bytes = [0u8; 64];
    rand::rng().fill_bytes(&mut bytes);
    let code_verifier = base64::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(bytes);
    let digest = Sha256::digest(code_verifier.as_bytes());
    let code_challenge = base64::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(digest);
    PkceCodes { code_verifier, code_challenge }
}

8.5 Local OAuth server

Handles browser-based logins by running a tiny_http-based local callback server (default port 1455) in server.rs. Security is secured using PKCE code and state parameters.

8.6 API key management

  • Environment variable priority: CODEX_API_KEY > OPENAI_API_KEY
  • Supports custom environment variables with env_key settings for each provider
  • Support programmatic use with experimental_bearer_token (not recommended for security reasons)
  • Keyring Store: codex-rs/keyring-store/ Link OS keychain with crate
  • Save token in auth.json

8.7 Token renewal

The RefreshTokenError / UnauthorizedRecovery type defines the recovery path when token renewal fails. AuthManager manages the authentication status of the overall session.

9. Voice / Multimodal

9.1 Realtime Conversation

Implement WebSocket-based real-time voice conversation in codex-rs/core/src/realtime_conversation.rs:

const AUDIO_IN_QUEUE_CAPACITY: usize = 256;
const USER_TEXT_IN_QUEUE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
const OUTPUT_EVENTS_QUEUE_CAPACITY: usize = 256;

Using RealtimeWebsocketClient, RealtimeAudioFrame, RealtimeEvent, etc. from the codex_api crate:

  • Audio frame input/output (Base64 encoding)
  • Text input
  • handoff event

9.2 Feature flags

Voice/Multimodal related feature flags:

flag stage default
voice_transcription UnderDevelopment false
realtime_conversation UnderDevelopment false
image_detail_original UnderDevelopment false
image_generation UnderDevelopment false

9.3 Image processing

An image utility exists in codex-rs/utils/image/ and supports image input with the view_image tool handler (core/src/tools/handlers/view_image.rs). The type InputModality is defined in the protocol.

9.4 Current status

The voice feature is still under development and is disabled by default. Realtime API WebSocket transport has been converted to legacy (ResponsesWebsockets, ResponsesWebsocketsV2 are Stage::Removed).

10. Event Bus

10.1 async_channel based architecture

Codex does not use a dedicated "event bus" abstraction, but uses async_channel + tokio::sync::watch directly:

// codex-rs/core/src/codex.rs (Session ى´ˆê¸°ي™”)
let (tx_sub, rx_sub) = async_channel::bounded(SUBMISSION_CHANNEL_CAPACITY);
let (tx_event, rx_event) = async_channel::unbounded();
  • tx_sub / rx_sub: User input -> Submission channel to session (bounded)
  • tx_event / rx_event: Event channel from session -> UI (unbounded)

10.2 watch channel

Use tokio::sync::watch to observe state changes:

let (agent_status_tx, agent_status_rx) = watch::channel(AgentStatus::PendingInit);

10.3 Protocol Events

Stream event types defined by the SDK:

type ThreadEvent =
  | ThreadStartedEvent
  | TurnStartedEvent
  | TurnCompletedEvent
  | TurnFailedEvent
  | ItemStartedEvent
  | ItemUpdatedEvent
  | ItemCompletedEvent
  | ThreadErrorEvent;

10.4 App-Server Event

Define an event in the form of a JSON-RPC notification in app-server-protocol:

  • ThreadRealtimeItemAddedNotification
  • CommandExecOutputDeltaNotification
  • ContextCompactedNotification
  • ItemGuardianApprovalReviewStartedNotification
flowchart LR
    U[ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى‍…ë ¥] -->|tx_sub| S[Session Loop]
    S -->|tx_event| T[TUI / App-Server]
    S -->|watch| W[AgentStatus ê´€ى°°ى‍گ]
    S -->|async_channel| R[Realtime Audio Queue]

11. Feature flags

11.1 only crate: features

More than 50 feature flags are defined in codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs.

11.2 Feature enum and Stage

pub enum Feature {
    GhostCommit, ShellTool, JsRepl, CodeMode, UnifiedExec,
    WebSearchRequest, WebSearchCached, ApplyPatchFreeform,
    CodexHooks, GuardianApproval, VoiceTranscription,
    RealtimeConversation, TuiAppServer, FastMode,
    Plugins, Apps, MultiAgentV2, Artifact,
    // ... 50ê°œ ى‌´ىƒپ
}

pub enum Stage {
    UnderDevelopment,
    Experimental { name, menu_description, announcement },
    Stable,
    Deprecated,
    Removed,
}

11.3 Feature stage distribution

stage Example
Stable ShellTool, UnifiedExec, ShellSnapshot, Apps, Plugins, FastMode, Personality
Experimental JsRepl, GuardianApproval, TuiAppServer, PreventIdleSleep
UnderDevelopment CodeMode, VoiceTranscription, RealtimeConversation, MemoryTool, ImageGeneration
Deprecated WebSearchRequest, WebSearchCached
Removed SearchTool, UseLinuxSandboxBwrap, Steer, CollaborationModes, ResponsesWebsockets

11.4 Feature interpretation pipeline

pub fn from_sources(
    base: FeatureConfigSource,
    profile: FeatureConfigSource,
    overrides: FeatureOverrides,
) -> Features {
    let mut features = Features::with_defaults();
    // 1. base config ى پىڑ©
    // 2. profile config ى پىڑ©
    // 3. overrides ى پىڑ©
    // 4. normalize_dependencies() -- ى‌کى،´ى„± ى‍گëڈ™ ي™œى„±ي™”
    features
}

Example of dependency normalization:

  • Automatically activates Collab when SpawnCsv is activated
  • Automatically activates CodeMode when CodeModeOnly is activated
  • JsReplToolsOnly is disabled without JsRepl

11.5 Legacy Compatibility

Maps old key names (web_search, include_apply_patch_tool, etc.) to new feature keys in legacy.rs and generates a deprecation warning.

11.6 Experimental menu

The Stage::Experimental variant has name, menu_description, and announcement fields, allowing the user to toggle experimental features in the TUI's /experimental menu.

11.7 Telemetry integration

pub fn emit_metrics(&self, otel: &SessionTelemetry) {
    for feature in FEATURES {
        if self.enabled(feature.id) != feature.default_enabled {
            otel.counter("codex.feature.state", 1,
                &[("feature", feature.key), ("value", &enabled.to_string())]);
        }
    }
}

12. Internationalization / Accessibility

12.1 Current status: Not implemented

There is no dedicated i18n (internationalization) or a11y (accessibility) infrastructure in the codebase.

  • All user-facing strings are hardcoded in English.
  • No locale detection or translation file system
  • No ARIA properties or screen reader support code (TUI based, so relies on terminal accessibility)
  • The ansi-escape crate handles ANSI escape sequences, but this is for terminal compatibility purposes, not accessibility.

12.2 Related code

The only relevant part is the extent to which the local time zone is used in the time formatting of the error message:

// codex-rs/core/src/error.rs
let local_reset = resets_at.with_timezone(&Local);

Technical Reference

File/module mapping

Topic Critical Path
Git integration codex-rs/git-utils/src/{info,operations,ghost_commits,apply,branch,errors}.rs
model provider codex-rs/core/src/model_provider_info.rs, codex-rs/ollama/, codex-rs/lmstudio/
error handling codex-rs/core/src/error.rs, codex-rs/git-utils/src/errors.rs
telemetry codex-rs/otel/src/{lib,provider,config,events/session_telemetry,metrics/}.rs
IDE integration codex-rs/app-server-protocol/, codex-rs/app-server/
Packaging/Distribution .github/workflows/rust-release*.yml, codex-rs/Cargo.toml, justfile
test codex-rs/core/tests/, codex-rs/app-server/tests/, sdk/typescript/tests/
Certification codex-rs/login/src/{lib,device_code_auth,pkce,server,token_data}.rs
Voice/Multimodal codex-rs/core/src/realtime_conversation.rs, codex-rs/utils/image/
event system codex-rs/core/src/codex.rs (async_channel), sdk/typescript/src/events.ts
Feature flag codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs
Internationalization/Accessibility Not applicable

Dependency graph (crossing concerns)

graph TD
    CLI[cli] --> Core[core]
    TUI[tui] --> Core
    AppServer[app-server] --> Core
    Core --> GitUtils[git-utils]
    Core --> Otel[otel]
    Core --> Features[features]
    Core --> Login[login]
    Core --> CodexApi[codex-api]
    Core --> Protocol[protocol]
    Login --> KeyringStore[keyring-store]
    CodexApi --> |SSE/WS| OpenAI[OpenAI API]
    Ollama[ollama] --> Core
    LMStudio[lmstudio] --> Core
    Otel --> |OTLP| Statsig[Statsig/OTLP Backend]

Judgment

Strengths

  1. Depth of Git integration: Rather than simple command execution, it has sophisticated VCS integration such as ghost commit-based undo, smart diff-to-remote, and merge-base calculation. Dependencies were minimized by using CLI instead of git2.

  2. Telemetry Maturity: Fully accepts the OpenTelemetry standard and supports all 3 axes of log/trace/metric. Sensitive information filtering (trace_safe vs log_only) was considered from the design stage.

  3. Feature flag system: It is a highly complete system that manages more than 50 features in 5 stages and includes dependency normalization, legacy compatibility, experiment menu, and telemetry integration.

  4. Error handling consistency: Retryability determination, protocol conversion, and user plan-specific messages are integrated into the CodexErr central type.

  5. Authentication multi-layer structure: Supports both CLI and IDE by combining Device Code, PKCE, API Key, and Keyring.

Weaknesses

  1. Complete lack of internationalization: All user-facing strings are hard-coded into English.

  2. Accessibility not considered: Because it is TUI-based, it relies on terminal accessibility, but there is no separate a11y support.

  3. Voice function incomplete: Realtime Conversation remains in the UnderDevelopment stage, and legacy WebSocket transport has already been converted to Removed.

  4. Informality of the event system: Since we use async_channel directly without a dedicated event bus, we have to navigate through the code to trace the flow of events.

Singularity

  1. Statsig Built-in: Basic metrics export is sent directly to OpenAI internal Statsig endpoint. The API key is hardcoded in the source code (client-MkRuleRQBd6qakfnDYqJVR9JuXcY57Ljly3vi5JVUIO).

  2. Ghost Commit after Feature Flag: It is disabled by default (default_enabled: false), so to use the undo function, explicit activation is required with [features].undo = true.

  3. Complete removal of Chat Wire API: Displays an explicit error message and migration guide when using wire_api = "chat".

  4. Dual build system: Supports Cargo and Bazel simultaneously, covering both development (Cargo) and large-scale CI (Bazel).

  5. 5 second git timeout: A 5 second hard timeout is applied to all asynchronous git commands to prevent hangs in large monorepos.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 013_etc/13_co_etc.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

1. Git/VCS ي†µي•©

1.2 Git ëھ…ë ¹ ى‹¤ي–‰ يŒ¨ي„´

ëھ¨ë“  git ëھ…ë ¹ى‌€ std::process::Command (ëڈ™ê¸°) ëکگëٹ” tokio::process::Command (ë¹„ëڈ™ê¸°) ë،œ ى‹¤ي–‰ëگکë©°, git2 ë‌¼ى‌´ë¸Œëں¬ë¦¬ë¥¼ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•کى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤. يƒ€ى‍„ى•„ى›ƒ 5ى´ˆê°€ ي•کë“œى½”ë”©ëگکى–´ ëŒ€يک• ى €ى‍¥ى†Œى—گى„œى‌ک ي–‰(hang) ë°©ى§€:

// codex-rs/git-utils/src/info.rs
const GIT_COMMAND_TIMEOUT: TokioDuration = TokioDuration::from_secs(5);

async fn run_git_command_with_timeout(args: &[&str], cwd: &Path) -> Option<std::process::Output> {
    let mut command = Command::new("git");
    command
        .env("GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS", "0")  // ى‍ ê¸ˆ ى¶©ëڈŒ ë°©ى§€
        .args(args)
        .current_dir(cwd)
        .kill_on_drop(true);
    let result = timeout(GIT_COMMAND_TIMEOUT, command.output()).await;
    match result {
        Ok(Ok(output)) => Some(output),
        _ => None,
    }
}

1.3 ê³ ىٹ¤يٹ¸ ى»¤ë°‹ (Ghost Commits)

Codexى‌ک undo ê¸°ëٹ¥ى‌ک ي•µى‹¬ى‌´ë‹¤. ى—گى‌´ى „يٹ¸ ي„´ë§ˆë‹¤ ى‍‘ى—… يٹ¸ë¦¬ى‌ک ى „ى²´ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·ى‌„ git commitىœ¼ë،œ ى €ى‍¥ي•œë‹¤:

  • ê¸°ë³¸ ى»¤ë°‹ ë©”ى‹œى§€: "codex snapshot"
  • ëŒ€يک• يŒŒى‌¼/ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬ ى‍گëڈ™ ى œى™¸: node_modules, .venv, dist, build ë“±
  • ëŒ€يک• ë¯¸ى¶”ى پ يŒŒى‌¼ ى‍„ê³„ê°’: 10 MiB
  • ëŒ€يک• ë¯¸ى¶”ى پ ë””ë ‰ي„°ë¦¬ ى‍„ê³„ê°’: 200ê°œ ي•­ëھ©
// codex-rs/git-utils/src/ghost_commits.rs
const DEFAULT_IGNORED_DIR_NAMES: &[&str] = &[
    "node_modules", ".venv", "venv", "env", ".env",
    "dist", "build", ".pytest_cache", ".mypy_cache",
    ".cache", ".tox", "__pycache__",
];

2.3 OSS ي”„ë،œë°”ى‌´ëچ” (Ollama / LM Studio)

ê°پê°پ ëڈ…ë¦½ يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸ë،œ ى،´ى‍¬:

  • codex-rs/ollama/: Ollama يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸, ëھ¨ëچ¸ pull, ë²„ى „ ي™•ى‌¸ (Responses API ى§€ى›گ ىµœى†Œ 0.13.4)
  • codex-rs/lmstudio/: LM Studio يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸, ëھ¨ëچ¸ ë‹¤ىڑ´ë،œë“œ ë°ڈ ë،œë“œ

ë‘ک ë‹¤ ensure_oss_ready() يŒ¨ي„´ى‌„ ë”°ë¥¸ë‹¤:

// codex-rs/ollama/src/lib.rs
pub async fn ensure_oss_ready(config: &Config) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    let ollama_client = OllamaClient::try_from_oss_provider(config).await?;
    match ollama_client.fetch_models().await {
        Ok(models) => {
            if !models.iter().any(|m| m == model) {
                ollama_client.pull_with_reporter(model, &mut reporter).await?;
            }
        }
        Err(err) => {
            tracing::warn!("Failed to query local models from Ollama: {}.", err);
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}

يŒŒë‌¼ë¯¸ي„° ê¸°ë³¸ê°’ ىµœëŒ€ê°’

3.1 CodexErr ى—´ê±°يک•

codex-rs/core/src/error.rsى—گ ى •ى‌کëگœ CodexErrëٹ” ى•½ 20ê°œى‌ک ë³€يک•ى‌„ ê°–ëٹ” ى¤‘ى•™ ى—گëں¬ يƒ€ى‍…ى‌´ë‹¤:

    TurnAborted,

    Sandbox(SandboxErr),

}

ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„ ê°€ëٹ¥ ى‍¬ى‹œëڈ„ ë¶ˆê°€

3.3 ي”„ë،œي† ى½œ ى—گëں¬ ë³€ي™ک

to_codex_protocol_error() ë©”ى„œë“œê°€ ë‚´ë¶€ ى—گëں¬ë¥¼ يپ´ë‌¼ى‌´ى–¸يٹ¸ ي”„ë،œي† ى½œ ى—گëں¬(CodexErrorInfo)ë،œ ë§¤ي•‘:

3.6 Git ى—گëں¬ يƒ€ى‍…

pub enum GitToolingError {
    GitCommand { command, status, stderr },
    GitOutputUtf8 { command, source },
    NotAGitRepository { path },
    NonRelativePath { path },
    PathEscapesRepository { path },
    PathPrefix(StripPrefixError),
    Walkdir(WalkdirError),
    Io(io::Error),
}

4. ي…”ë ˆë©”يٹ¸ë¦¬ / ê´€ى¸،ى„±

4.1 ى „ىڑ© يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸: otel

codex-rs/otel/ يپ¬ë ˆى‌´يٹ¸ê°€ OpenTelemetry ي†µي•©ى‌„ ى „ë‹´ي•œë‹¤.

flowchart TD
    A[OtelProvider] --> B[LoggerProvider / OTLP]
    A --> C[TracerProvider / OTLP]

4.2 OtelSettings

pub struct OtelSettings {
    pub environment: String,
    pub service_name: String,
    pub service_version: String,
    pub codex_home: PathBuf,
    pub exporter: OtelExporter,        // ë،œê·¸ ë‚´ë³´ë‚´ê¸°
    pub trace_exporter: OtelExporter,  // يٹ¸ë ˆى‌´ىٹ¤ ë‚´ë³´ë‚´ê¸°
    pub metrics_exporter: OtelExporter, // ë©”يٹ¸ë¦­ ë‚´ë³´ë‚´ê¸°

}

8.3 Device Code ى‌¸ى¦‌

device_code_auth.rsى—گى„œ OAuth 2.0 Device Code Flowë¥¼ êµ¬يک„:

  1. POST /deviceauth/usercodeë،œ device code + user code ىڑ”ى²­
  2. ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گى—گê²Œ verification URL ي‘œى‹œ

8.4 PKCE ى‌¸ى¦‌

pkce.rsى—گى„œ PKCE(Proof Key for Code Exchange) êµ¬يک„:

pub fn generate_pkce() -> PkceCodes {
    let mut bytes = [0u8; 64];
    rand::rng().fill_bytes(&mut bytes);
    let code_verifier = base64::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(bytes);
    let digest = Sha256::digest(code_verifier.as_bytes());
    let code_challenge = base64::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(digest);
    PkceCodes { code_verifier, code_challenge }
}

8.5 ë،œى»¬ OAuth ى„œë²„

server.rsى—گى„œ tiny_http ê¸°ë°ک ë،œى»¬ ى½œë°± ى„œë²„(ê¸°ë³¸ يڈ¬يٹ¸ 1455)ë¥¼ ى‹¤ي–‰ي•کى—¬ ë¸Œë‌¼ىڑ°ى € ê¸°ë°ک ë،œê·¸ى‌¸ى‌„ ى²کë¦¬ي•œë‹¤. PKCE ى½”ë“œى™€ state يŒŒë‌¼ë¯¸ي„°ë،œ ë³´ى•ˆى‌„ ي™•ë³´ي•œë‹¤.

10.1 async_channel ê¸°ë°ک ى•„ي‚¤ي…چى²ک

Codexëٹ” ى „ىڑ© "ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ ë²„ىٹ¤" ى¶”ىƒپي™”ë¥¼ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•کى§€ ى•ٹê³ , async_channel + tokio::sync::watchë¥¼ ى§پى ‘ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•œë‹¤:

// codex-rs/core/src/codex.rs (Session ى´ˆê¸°ي™”)

pub enum Feature {
    GhostCommit, ShellTool, JsRepl, CodeMode, UnifiedExec,
    WebSearchRequest, WebSearchCached, ApplyPatchFreeform,
    CodexHooks, GuardianApproval, VoiceTranscription,
    RealtimeConversation, TuiAppServer, FastMode,
    Plugins, Apps, MultiAgentV2, Artifact,

pub enum Stage {
    UnderDevelopment,
    Experimental { name, menu_description, announcement },
    Stable,
    Deprecated,
    Removed,
}

11.3 ي”¼ى²ک ىٹ¤ي…Œى‌´ى§€ ë¶„يڈ¬

ىٹ¤ي…Œى‌´ى§€ ىکˆى‹œ
Stable ShellTool, UnifiedExec, ShellSnapshot, Apps, Plugins, FastMode, Personality
Experimental JsRepl, GuardianApproval, TuiAppServer, PreventIdleSleep
UnderDevelopment CodeMode, VoiceTranscription, RealtimeConversation, MemoryTool, ImageGeneration
Deprecated WebSearchRequest, WebSearchCached
Removed SearchTool, UseLinuxSandboxBwrap, Steer, CollaborationModes, ResponsesWebsockets

11.4 ي”¼ى²ک ي•´ى„‌ يŒŒى‌´ي”„ë‌¼ى‌¸

pub fn from_sources(
    base: FeatureConfigSource,
    profile: FeatureConfigSource,
    overrides: FeatureOverrides,
) -> Features {
    let mut features = Features::with_defaults();
    // 1. base config ى پىڑ©
    // 2. profile config ى پىڑ©
    // 3. overrides ى پىڑ©
    // 4. normalize_dependencies() -- ى‌کى،´ى„± ى‍گëڈ™ ي™œى„±ي™”
    features
}

ى‌کى،´ى„± ى •ê·œي™” ىکˆى‹œ:

11.6 Experimental ë©”ë‰´

Stage::Experimental ë³€يک•ى—گ name, menu_description, announcement ي•„ë“œê°€ ى‍ˆى–´, TUIى‌ک /experimental ë©”ë‰´ى—گى„œ ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گê°€ ى‹¤ي—کى پ ê¸°ëٹ¥ى‌„ ي† ê¸€ي•  ىˆک ى‍ˆë‹¤.

11.7 ي…”ë ˆë©”يٹ¸ë¦¬ ى—°ëڈ™

pub fn emit_metrics(&self, otel: &SessionTelemetry) {
    for feature in FEATURES {
        if self.enabled(feature.id) != feature.default_enabled {
            otel.counter("codex.feature.state", 1,
                &[("feature", feature.key), ("value", &enabled.to_string())]);
        }
    }
}

12. êµ­ى œي™” / ى ‘ê·¼ى„±

12.1 يک„ى‍¬ ىƒپيƒœ: ë¯¸êµ¬يک„

ى½”ë“œë² ى‌´ىٹ¤ى—گ i18n(êµ­ى œي™”) ëکگëٹ” a11y(ى ‘ê·¼ى„±) ى „ىڑ© ى‌¸ي”„ë‌¼ëٹ” ى،´ى‍¬ي•کى§€ ى•ٹëٹ”ë‹¤.

  • ëھ¨ë“  ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ëŒ€ë©´ ë¬¸ى‍گى—´ى‌€ ىکپى–´ ي•کë“œى½”ë”©

Axis 14: Web search

AXIS

014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md

Overview

Axis 14: Web search â€” developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • ë¶„ى„‌ ىٹ¤ëƒ…ىƒ·: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” web search integration.

  • Codexëٹ” OpenAI Responses APIى‌ک ë‚´ى‍¥ web_search ëڈ„êµ¬ë¥¼ ى‚¬ىڑ©ي•œë‹¤. Rust ى½”ى–´ى—گى„œ ToolSpec::WebSearch ë³€يک•ىœ¼ë،œ ى •ى‌کëگکë©°, API ى„œë²„ ى¸،ى—گى„œ ê²€ىƒ‰ى‌„ ىˆکي–‰ي•کê³  ê²°ê³¼ë¥¼ ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë¦¬ë°چىœ¼ë،œ ë°کي™کي•œë‹¤. 3ë‹¨ê³„ ëھ¨ë“œ(live/cached/disabled)ë،œ ë¹„ىڑ©ê³¼ ى†چëڈ„ë¥¼ ى œى–´ي•  ىˆک ى‍ˆىœ¼ë©°, ëڈ„ë©”ى‌¸ ي•„ي„°ë§پ, ى§€ë¦¬ى پ ىœ„ى¹ک, ى»¨ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ يپ¬ê¸° ë“± ى„¸ë°€ي•œ ى„¤ى •ى‌„ ى§€ى›گي•œë‹¤.

Source code snippets

README / docs

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/tools/src/tool_spec.rs:36-48
#[serde(rename = "web_search")]
WebSearch {
    external_web_access: Option<bool>,
    filters: Option<ResponsesApiWebSearchFilters>,
    user_location: Option<ResponsesApiWebSearchUserLocation>,
    search_context_size: Option<WebSearchContextSize>,
    search_content_types: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

README / docs

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/protocol/src/models.rs:1191-1216
pub enum WebSearchAction {
    Search { query: Option<String>, queries: Option<Vec<String>> },
    OpenPage { url: Option<String> },
    FindInPage { url: Option<String>, pattern: Option<String> },
    #[serde(other)]
    Other,
}

README / docs

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/web_search.rs:18-29
pub fn web_search_action_detail(action: &WebSearchAction) -> String {
    match action {
        WebSearchAction::Search { query, queries } => search_action_detail(query, queries),
        WebSearchAction::OpenPage { url } => url.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
        WebSearchAction::FindInPage { url, pattern } => /* ... */,
        WebSearchAction::Other => String::new(),
    }
}

# ~/.codex/config.toml
web_search = "live"        # or "cached" or "disabled"

README / docs

codex --config web_search="live"

README / docs

// sdk/typescript/src/exec.ts
commandArgs.push("--config", `web_search="${args.webSearchMode}"`);

README / docs

# cloud requirements
allowed_web_search_modes = ["cached"]  # live ê²€ىƒ‰ ى°¨ë‹¨

README / docs

struct ResponsesApiWebSearchFilters {
    allowed_domains: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

README / docs

struct ResponsesApiWebSearchUserLocation {
    r#type: WebSearchUserLocationType,  // "approximate"
    country: Option<String>,   // ISO ى½”ë“œ
    region: Option<String>,
    city: Option<String>,
    timezone: Option<String>,  // IANA ى‹œê°„ëŒ€
}

README / docs

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/hosted_spec.rs:20-49
pub fn create_web_search_tool(options: WebSearchToolOptions<'_>) -> Option<ToolSpec> {
    let external_web_access = match options.web_search_mode {
        Some(WebSearchMode::Cached) => Some(false),
        Some(WebSearchMode::Live) => Some(true),
        Some(WebSearchMode::Disabled) | None => None,
    }?;
    Some(ToolSpec::WebSearch { external_web_access: Some(external_web_access), /* filters, location */ })
}

README / docs

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs:2186-2196
fn resolve_web_search_mode(config_toml: &ConfigToml, features: &Features) -> Option<WebSearchMode> {
    if let Some(mode) = config_toml.web_search { return Some(mode); }
    if features.enabled(Feature::WebSearchCached) { return Some(WebSearchMode::Cached); }
    if features.enabled(Feature::WebSearchRequest) { return Some(WebSearchMode::Live); }
    None
}

README / docs

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs:2302-2318
pub(crate) fn resolve_web_search_mode_for_turn(
    web_search_mode: &Constrained<WebSearchMode>,
    permission_profile: &PermissionProfile,
) -> WebSearchMode { /* downgrades when PermissionProfile::Disabled */ }

README / docs

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/event_mapping.rs:183-192
ResponseItem::WebSearchCall { id, action, .. } => {
    let (action, query) = match action {
        Some(action) => (action.clone(), web_search_action_detail(action)),
        None => (WebSearchAction::Other, String::new()),
    };
    Some(TurnItem::WebSearch(WebSearchItem { id, query, action }))
}

README / docs

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/stream_events_utils.rs:184-208
fn response_item_may_include_external_context(item: &ResponseItem) -> bool {
    matches!(item, ResponseItem::ToolSearchCall { .. }
        | ResponseItem::ToolSearchOutput { .. }
        | ResponseItem::WebSearchCall { .. })
}
pub(crate) async fn mark_thread_memory_mode_polluted_if_external_context(...) {
    if !turn_context.config.memories.disable_on_external_context
        || !response_item_may_include_external_context(item) { return; }
    state_db::mark_thread_memory_mode_polluted(...).await;
}

README / docs

// codex-rs/core/src/guardian/prompt.rs
GuardianTranscriptEntryKind::Tool("tool web_search call".to_string()),

// codex-rs/protocol/src/openai_models.rs
web_search_tool_type: WebSearchToolType::Text,  // ê¸°ë³¸ê°’

ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى؟¼ë¦¬ â†’ ToolSpec::WebSearch ى§پë ¬ي™” â†’ OpenAI ى„œë²„
   â†“
[Stage 1] ى؟¼ë¦¬ ë¶„ي•´ (Query Decomposition)

  آ· Bing Index (1ى°¨ ى†Œىٹ¤, MS يŒŒيٹ¸ë„ˆى‹­)
  آ· OAI-SearchBot (ى‍گى²´ يپ¬ë،¤ëں¬, GPTBotê³¼ ë³„ê°œ)

   â†“
[Stage 3] URL ë°©ë¬¸ + يŒ¨ى‹œى§€ ى¶”ى¶œ
  آ· ي›„ë³´ URLى‌ک ى‹¤ى œ يژکى‌´ى§€ ë°©ë¬¸

[Stage 4] LLM ê¸°ë°ک ى‍¬ىˆœىœ„ي™”

   â†“

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/analytics/src/events.rs:72, 673-684

Main analysis

Codex â€” Web Search Implementation

Analysis Snapshot: 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/ â€” web search integration.

Overview

Codex uses the built-in web_search tool of the OpenAI Responses API. Defined in the Rust core as the ToolSpec::WebSearch variant, which performs a search on the API server side and returns the results streaming. Cost and speed can be controlled in three levels of mode (live/cached/disabled), and detailed settings such as domain filtering, geographic location, and context size are supported.

Core type definition

ToolSpec::WebSearch

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/tools/src/tool_spec.rs:36-48
#[serde(rename = "web_search")]
WebSearch {
    external_web_access: Option<bool>,
    filters: Option<ResponsesApiWebSearchFilters>,
    user_location: Option<ResponsesApiWebSearchUserLocation>,
    search_context_size: Option<WebSearchContextSize>,
    search_content_types: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

WebSearchAction (protocol level)

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/protocol/src/models.rs:1191-1216
pub enum WebSearchAction {
    Search { query: Option<String>, queries: Option<Vec<String>> },
    OpenPage { url: Option<String> },
    FindInPage { url: Option<String>, pattern: Option<String> },
    #[serde(other)]
    Other,
}

We classify the web search life cycle into four action types:

  1. Search: Run a keyword-based search
  2. OpenPage: Open a specific URL page
  3. FindInPage: Search for patterns within a page
  4. Other: Other

Display search results

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/web_search.rs:18-29
pub fn web_search_action_detail(action: &WebSearchAction) -> String {
    match action {
        WebSearchAction::Search { query, queries } => search_action_detail(query, queries),
        WebSearchAction::OpenPage { url } => url.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
        WebSearchAction::FindInPage { url, pattern } => /* ... */,
        WebSearchAction::Other => String::new(),
    }
}

Mode settings

Level 3 mode

mode external_web_access Description
live true Live Internet Access, Latest Results
cached false Use only cached search results
disabled (Tools not included) Completely disable web browsing

Setting path

# ~/.codex/config.toml
web_search = "live"        # or "cached" or "disabled"

From the CLI:

codex --config web_search="live"

In the SDK:

// sdk/typescript/src/exec.ts
commandArgs.push("--config", `web_search="${args.webSearchMode}"`);

Cloud requirements integration

Organization administrators can restrict allowed modes with allowed_web_search_modes:

# cloud requirements
allowed_web_search_modes = ["cached"]  # live ê²€ىƒ‰ ى°¨ë‹¨

Filtering options

Domain filter

struct ResponsesApiWebSearchFilters {
    allowed_domains: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

Geographic location

struct ResponsesApiWebSearchUserLocation {
    r#type: WebSearchUserLocationType,  // "approximate"
    country: Option<String>,   // ISO ى½”ë“œ
    region: Option<String>,
    city: Option<String>,
    timezone: Option<String>,  // IANA ى‹œê°„ëŒ€
}

Context size

Control the amount of context in search results with the WebSearchContextSize enum.

Tool assembly (create_web_search_tool)

Hosted tools are built in core/src/tools/hosted_spec.rs â€” not routed through ToolRouter like shell/MCP tools:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/hosted_spec.rs:20-49
pub fn create_web_search_tool(options: WebSearchToolOptions<'_>) -> Option<ToolSpec> {
    let external_web_access = match options.web_search_mode {
        Some(WebSearchMode::Cached) => Some(false),
        Some(WebSearchMode::Live) => Some(true),
        Some(WebSearchMode::Disabled) | None => None,
    }?;
    Some(ToolSpec::WebSearch { external_web_access: Some(external_web_access), /* filters, location */ })
}

WebSearchMode::Disabled or None â†’ function returns None â†’ tool omitted from Responses API tool list for that turn.

Config resolution

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs:2186-2196
fn resolve_web_search_mode(config_toml: &ConfigToml, features: &Features) -> Option<WebSearchMode> {
    if let Some(mode) = config_toml.web_search { return Some(mode); }
    if features.enabled(Feature::WebSearchCached) { return Some(WebSearchMode::Cached); }
    if features.enabled(Feature::WebSearchRequest) { return Some(WebSearchMode::Live); }
    None
}

Per-turn override with permission profile:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs:2302-2318
pub(crate) fn resolve_web_search_mode_for_turn(
    web_search_mode: &Constrained<WebSearchMode>,
    permission_profile: &PermissionProfile,
) -> WebSearchMode { /* downgrades when PermissionProfile::Disabled */ }

Called from core/src/session/turn_context.rs:403 when assembling turn tools.

Event mapping (Responses â†’ TurnItem)

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/event_mapping.rs:183-192
ResponseItem::WebSearchCall { id, action, .. } => {
    let (action, query) = match action {
        Some(action) => (action.clone(), web_search_action_detail(action)),
        None => (WebSearchAction::Other, String::new()),
    };
    Some(TurnItem::WebSearch(WebSearchItem { id, query, action }))
}

Unit tests in core/src/event_mapping_tests.rs:427-501 cover Search, OpenPage, FindInPage variants.

Prevent memory contamination

To prevent web search results from being stored in long-term memory, pollute the thread memory mode when setting disable_on_external_context:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/stream_events_utils.rs:184-208
fn response_item_may_include_external_context(item: &ResponseItem) -> bool {
    matches!(item, ResponseItem::ToolSearchCall { .. }
        | ResponseItem::ToolSearchOutput { .. }
        | ResponseItem::WebSearchCall { .. })
}
pub(crate) async fn mark_thread_memory_mode_polluted_if_external_context(...) {
    if !turn_context.config.memories.disable_on_external_context
        || !response_item_may_include_external_context(item) { return; }
    state_db::mark_thread_memory_mode_polluted(...).await;
}

Invoked from core/src/session/turn.rs:1674 after completed response items.

Change history

date change
2026-06-27 WP40: path:line evidence, fix tool_spec path, memory guard citations

Guardian Integration

Web search calls are recorded in the Guardian transcript:

// codex-rs/core/src/guardian/prompt.rs
GuardianTranscriptEntryKind::Tool("tool web_search call".to_string()),

Feature flags (legacy)

The web search-related feature flags are currently Deprecated:

flag stage
WebSearchRequest Deprecated
WebSearchCached Deprecated

The web_search setting at the config level replaces this. Map the old key names to the new settings in legacy.rs.

Model support

The level of web search support for each model is defined with the WebSearchToolType enum:

// codex-rs/protocol/src/openai_models.rs
web_search_tool_type: WebSearchToolType::Text,  // ê¸°ë³¸ê°’

file reference

file Role
codex-rs/tools/src/tool_spec.rs ToolSpec::WebSearch definition, Responses API serialization
codex-rs/core/src/tools/hosted_spec.rs create_web_search_tool â€” mode â†’ tool inclusion
codex-rs/core/src/web_search.rs Create search action detailed text
codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs resolve_web_search_mode, resolve_web_search_mode_for_turn
codex-rs/core/src/event_mapping.rs Search event â†’ TurnItem::WebSearch
codex-rs/core/src/stream_events_utils.rs external-context memory pollution prevention
codex-rs/protocol/src/models.rs WebSearchAction protocol type
codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs Legacy feature flag
codex-rs/sdk/typescript/src/exec.ts Assembling SDK command line arguments

Server-side pipeline (inside OpenAI)

When Codex calls the web_search tool, the following processing is performed inside the OpenAI Responses API server:

ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گ ى؟¼ë¦¬ â†’ ToolSpec::WebSearch ى§پë ¬ي™” â†’ OpenAI ى„œë²„
   â†“
[Stage 1] ى؟¼ë¦¬ ë¶„ي•´ (Query Decomposition)
  آ· LLMى‌´ ى›گë³¸ ى؟¼ë¦¬ë¥¼ ë¶„ى„‌, ى„œë¸Œى؟¼ë¦¬ë،œ ë¶„ي•´
  آ· ëھ¨يک¸ي•œ ىڑ©ى–´ ëھ…ي™•ي™”, ëڈ™ى‌کى–´ ي™•ى‍¥
  آ· ى‚¬ىڑ©ى‍گى—گê²Œ ë³´ى‌´ى§€ ى•ٹëٹ” ى¤‘ê°„ ë¦¬ë‌¼ى‌´يŒ…
   â†“
[Stage 2] ë‹¤ى¤‘ ى†Œىٹ¤ ê²€ىƒ‰
  آ· Bing Index (1ى°¨ ى†Œىٹ¤, MS يŒŒيٹ¸ë„ˆى‹­)
  آ· OAI-SearchBot (ى‍گى²´ يپ¬ë،¤ëں¬, GPTBotê³¼ ë³„ê°œ)
  آ· Partial Index (ى‍گى²´ ë³´ى،° ى‌¸ëچ±ىٹ¤)
   â†“
[Stage 3] URL ë°©ë¬¸ + يŒ¨ى‹œى§€ ى¶”ى¶œ
  آ· ي›„ë³´ URLى‌ک ى‹¤ى œ يژکى‌´ى§€ ë°©ë¬¸
  آ· HTML â†’ ë³¸ë¬¸ ي…چىٹ¤يٹ¸ â†’ ê´€ë ¨ يŒ¨ى‹œى§€ ë‹¨ىœ„ ى¶”ى¶œ
   â†“
[Stage 4] LLM ê¸°ë°ک ى‍¬ىˆœىœ„ي™”
  آ· Bing ىˆœىœ„ى™€ ëڈ…ë¦½ى پىœ¼ë،œ LLMى‌´ ى‍¬يڈ‰ê°€
  آ· relevance, trust, context ê¸°ë°ک
   â†“
[Stage 5] ى‌‘ë‹µ ىƒ‌ى„± + ى‌¸ىڑ©
  آ· web_search_call ى‌´ë²¤يٹ¸ (action, sources)
  آ· url_citation ى–´ë…¸ي…Œى‌´ى…ک ى‍گëڈ™ ى‚½ى‍…

3 search modes (server side)

mode model Server operation
Non-reasoning gpt-4o Query â†’ Search â†’ Return top results immediately
Agentic Search o3, o4-mini Integration of search into CoT, autonomous decision on additional search after analysis of intermediate results
Deep Research o3-deep-research Browse hundreds of sources in minutes

Role of Codex Client

The Codex client (Rust) does not run the server pipeline directly. Instead:

  1. Serialize ToolSpec::WebSearch to JSON and send to API
  2. Receive web_search_call event from streaming response
  3. Deserialize with WebSearchAction to display UI
  4. Extract citation information from sources array

Analytics / telemetry

Web search turns emit dedicated analytics events:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/analytics/src/events.rs:72, 673-684
AnalyticsEvent::WebSearch(CodexWebSearchEventRequest),
pub(crate) struct CodexWebSearchEventParams {
    pub(crate) web_search_action: Option<WebSearchActionKind>,
    // ...
}

Thread rollups track web_search_count per session (analytics/src/events.rs:807); reducer tests assert count increments when ThreadItem::WebSearch is present (analytics/src/analytics_client_tests.rs:3757).

OTel session telemetry maps ResponseItem::WebSearchCall to "web_search_call" event type (codex-rs/otel/src/events/session_telemetry.rs:1197).

ToolSpec::WebSearch

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/tools/src/tool_spec.rs:36-48
#[serde(rename = "web_search")]
WebSearch {
    external_web_access: Option<bool>,
    filters: Option<ResponsesApiWebSearchFilters>,
    user_location: Option<ResponsesApiWebSearchUserLocation>,
    search_context_size: Option<WebSearchContextSize>,
    search_content_types: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

WebSearchAction (ي”„ë،œي† ى½œ ë ˆë²¨)

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/protocol/src/models.rs:1191-1216
pub enum WebSearchAction {
    Search { query: Option<String>, queries: Option<Vec<String>> },
    OpenPage { url: Option<String> },
    FindInPage { url: Option<String>, pattern: Option<String> },
    #[serde(other)]
    Other,
}

4ى¢… ى•،ى…ک يƒ€ى‍…ىœ¼ë،œ ى›¹ ê²€ىƒ‰ ىˆکëھ…ى£¼ê¸°ë¥¼ ë¶„ë¥کي•œë‹¤:

  1. Search: ي‚¤ى›Œë“œ ê¸°ë°ک ê²€ىƒ‰ ى‹¤ي–‰
  2. OpenPage: يٹ¹ى • URL يژکى‌´ى§€ ى—´ê¸°
  3. FindInPage: يژکى‌´ى§€ ë‚´ يŒ¨ي„´ ê²€ىƒ‰

ê²€ىƒ‰ ê²°ê³¼ ي‘œى‹œ

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/web_search.rs:18-29
pub fn web_search_action_detail(action: &WebSearchAction) -> String {
    match action {
        WebSearchAction::Search { query, queries } => search_action_detail(query, queries),
        WebSearchAction::OpenPage { url } => url.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
        WebSearchAction::FindInPage { url, pattern } => /* ... */,
        WebSearchAction::Other => String::new(),
    }
}

ëھ¨ë“œ ى„¤ى •

3ë‹¨ê³„ ëھ¨ë“œ

ëھ¨ë“œ external_web_access ى„¤ëھ…

ى„¤ى • ê²½ë،œ

يپ´ë‌¼ىڑ°ë“œ ىڑ”êµ¬ى‚¬ي•­ ى—°ëڈ™

Tool assembly (create_web_search_tool)

Hosted tools are built in core/src/tools/hosted_spec.rs â€” not routed through ToolRouter like shell/MCP tools:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/hosted_spec.rs:20-49
pub fn create_web_search_tool(options: WebSearchToolOptions<'_>) -> Option<ToolSpec> {
    let external_web_access = match options.web_search_mode {
        Some(WebSearchMode::Cached) => Some(false),
        Some(WebSearchMode::Live) => Some(true),
        Some(WebSearchMode::Disabled) | None => None,
    }?;
    Some(ToolSpec::WebSearch { external_web_access: Some(external_web_access), /* filters, location */ })
}

WebSearchMode::Disabled or None â†’ function returns None â†’ tool omitted from Responses API tool list for that turn.

Config resolution

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs:2186-2196
fn resolve_web_search_mode(config_toml: &ConfigToml, features: &Features) -> Option<WebSearchMode> {
    if let Some(mode) = config_toml.web_search { return Some(mode); }
    if features.enabled(Feature::WebSearchCached) { return Some(WebSearchMode::Cached); }
    if features.enabled(Feature::WebSearchRequest) { return Some(WebSearchMode::Live); }
    None
}

Per-turn override with permission profile:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/config/mod.rs:2302-2318
pub(crate) fn resolve_web_search_mode_for_turn(
    web_search_mode: &Constrained<WebSearchMode>,
    permission_profile: &PermissionProfile,
) -> WebSearchMode { /* downgrades when PermissionProfile::Disabled */ }

Called from core/src/session/turn_context.rs:403 when assembling turn tools.

Event mapping (Responses â†’ TurnItem)

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/event_mapping.rs:183-192
ResponseItem::WebSearchCall { id, action, .. } => {
    let (action, query) = match action {
        Some(action) => (action.clone(), web_search_action_detail(action)),
        None => (WebSearchAction::Other, String::new()),
    };
    Some(TurnItem::WebSearch(WebSearchItem { id, query, action }))
}

Unit tests in core/src/event_mapping_tests.rs:427-501 cover Search, OpenPage, FindInPage variants.

ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ ىک¤ى—¼ ë°©ى§€

ى›¹ ê²€ىƒ‰ ê²°ê³¼ê°€ ى‍¥ê¸° ë©”ëھ¨ë¦¬ى—گ ى €ى‍¥ëگکى§€ ى•ٹëڈ„ë،‌ disable_on_external_context ى„¤ى • ى‹œ thread memory modeë¥¼ ىک¤ى—¼ ى²کë¦¬ي•œë‹¤:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/core/src/stream_events_utils.rs:184-208
fn response_item_may_include_external_context(item: &ResponseItem) -> bool {
    matches!(item, ResponseItem::ToolSearchCall { .. }
        | ResponseItem::ToolSearchOutput { .. }
        | ResponseItem::WebSearchCall { .. })
}
pub(crate) async fn mark_thread_memory_mode_polluted_if_external_context(...) {
    if !turn_context.config.memories.disable_on_external_context
        || !response_item_may_include_external_context(item) { return; }
    state_db::mark_thread_memory_mode_polluted(...).await;
}

Invoked from core/src/session/turn.rs:1674 after completed response items.

Analytics / telemetry

Web search turns emit dedicated analytics events:

// 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/analytics/src/events.rs:72, 673-684
AnalyticsEvent::WebSearch(CodexWebSearchEventRequest),
pub(crate) struct CodexWebSearchEventParams {
    pub(crate) web_search_action: Option<WebSearchActionKind>,
    // ...
}

Thread rollups track web_search_count per session (analytics/src/events.rs:807); reducer tests assert count increments when ThreadItem::WebSearch is present (analytics/src/analytics_client_tests.rs:3757).

OTel session telemetry maps ResponseItem::WebSearchCall to "web_search_call" event type (codex-rs/otel/src/events/session_telemetry.rs:1197).

snapshot_to_current ëچ¸يƒ€

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release â€” re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs
32

Main analysis

015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md

Reverse Engineering Evidence Policy

This axis is the next step in the installation help parity document. The existing 00 to 14 axis mainly compared the help/config/local-doc surface, and this axis separately records the structures that can be restored from installed binaries and bundles.

Evidence Levels

Level Meaning Claims available
installed-help --help, public command output, version output Command/Flag/Public subcommand exists
safe-local-doc docs, changelog, examples, manifest in the installation package There is a documented schema/path/function description
bundle-key Module key, source path, route name, proto name remaining in minified bundle Internal modules/router/prototypes/components exist
binary-string native binary strings, file, otool, codesign, nm The name crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator is embedded
runtime-trace Results of executing actual commands in synthetic project Checked operational semantics, precedence, and permission enforcement
private-artifact-name Observe only the path/count of private state files An artifact exists. Content/schema claims are prohibited

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native source-path, handler, protobuf, and runtime-trace evidence. Do not call this "full source recovered" unless a real source map or readable source archive is present.

Hard Boundary

The purpose of reverse analysis is to restore product structure, not to extract user state. Next, reading the content is prohibited.

  • auth/token/cookie/credential/login data
  • raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB
  • logs and terminal captures
  • browser profile state, local storage, IndexedDB, session storage
  • memory/goal runtime stores
  • screenshots and browser recordings
  • private mcp.json, settings, config values unless explicitly redacted

For private artifacts, only â€œexistsâ€‌ is used. Field/schema/content assertions are only used when there are public docs, source/bundle evidence, sanitized fixtures, and synthetic runtime traces.

Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)

Single source of truth for pinned ay/cr/gr/ki binaries:

  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md

WP5 trace shards (38â€“41) extend hubs without changing evidence-level definitions above.

Current Upgrade Scope

2026-06-27 pass â€” installed CLI practical RE (goal 4e94e12d-a9b):

Prefix Target Evidence kind

Newly reversed on 2026-05-31 pass (historical baseline):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
gr /Users/jun/.grok/downloads/grok-0.2.14-macos-aarch64, /Users/jun/.grok/bundled, /Users/jun/.grok/docs/user-guide Rust Mach-O strings, crate paths, ACP methods, bundled agents/roles/personas/skills
cr /Users/jun/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5 Webpack module keys, route extraction, proto module names, native helper inventory
ki /Applications/Kiro CLI.app, /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli* signed universal Mach-O binaries, Rust source-path strings, WebKit resources, dashboard/autocomplete assets
ay /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy, /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli Go/native strings, protobuf trajectory/cascade names, local cache/config metadata, helper binaries

Reading Rule For Existing Docs

When a 00~14 page says â€œNoneâ€‌ or â€œNot supportedâ€‌, interpret it as â€œNot exposed in installed helpâ€‌ unless this 15_reverse-engineering axis confirms absence from bundle/binary evidence too.

When a page states runtime semantics from config/cache observation, this axis upgrades or downgrades the claim:

  • bundle-key or binary-string confirms a module exists.
  • runtime-trace is still required for exact precedence, scheduling, isolation, merge, and permission behavior.

015_reverse-engineering/35_harness_source_matrix.md

Harness Source Matrix

This matrix covers source repositories, not installed binary reverse targets. It should be read beside /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness_synthesis_addendum.md.

Evidence Class

Target Evidence class Current boundary
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/hermes-agent Source code, README, package metadata, existing dimensional shards Full source architecture observed; installed/runtime credentialed trace not performed in this pass
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/oh-my-claudecode Source code, Claude plugin manifest, npm metadata, hooks/MCP/team runtime files Host-dependent on Claude Code and Claude Agent SDK
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/oh-my-codex Source code, npm metadata, Rust workspace, Codex plugin manifest, first-party MCP specs Host-dependent on Codex CLI
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/oh-my-openagent Source code, npm metadata, OpenCode plugin entry, Codex Light plugin manifest, docs Host-dependent on OpenCode for Ultimate; Codex Light intentionally reduced
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/121_openai-codex/codex-rs Full Rust workspace (113 crates), Cargo.toml members, core/ session loop Peer-level source runtime; see 000_overview/00_co_crate_graph.md

Codex (co) â€” codex-rs quick matrix

Axis Primary crates Deep doc
Tool runtime core/src/tools/router.rs, tools/, apply-patch 003_tool-runtime/03_co_tool.md
MCP rmcp-client, codex-mcp, mcp-server 009_mcp-orchestration/09_co_mcp.md
Sandbox sandboxing, execpolicy, linux-sandbox 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_co_sandbox.md
App surface app-server*, cli, tui 006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_co_surface.md
Extensions ext/extension-api, ext/goal, ext/guardian 000_overview/00_co_crate_graph.md

Runtime and Capability Matrix

Object Hermes Agent (hermes) Oh My ClaudeCode (omc) Oh My Codex (omx) Oh My OpenAgent (omo)
Runtime ownership Full standalone Python agent loop plus Python CLI, React/Ink TUI, gateway, ACP, MCP, and web surfaces No standalone primary LLM loop; Claude Code plugin/harness with companion npm CLI No standalone primary LLM loop; Codex control plane and setup/runtime wrapper No standalone primary loop in main path; OpenCode plugin plus Codex Light adapter
Tool layer Own tool registry for web, terminal, files, browser, memory, skills, delegation, cron, messaging, Computer Use, and plugin tools Adds t MCP, team runtime, LSP/AST/Python/state/memory/wiki tools around Claude Code Adds first-party MCP servers, CLI parity commands, code-intel/wiki/trace/state tools around Codex Large plugin tool registry: LSP, grep, AST-grep, session/background tools, Team Mode tools, hashline edits
Skill layer Native self-improving skills and skill manager under Hermes profile state Claude-loaded bundled skills plus OMC learned/project memory patterns Codex skills/prompts/agents installed by setup and plugin bundle OpenCode skills plus shared skills and Codex Light components
Agent/subagent layer Real child AIAgent delegation with isolated context, restricted tools, batch parallelism, and backend routing Role agents plus Claude Code Task/tmux/worktree team runtime Codex-native agents, workflow roles, tmux/event-sourced team workers 11-agent Ultimate roster, background agents, Team Mode, and per-agent restrictions; Light omits orchestration
MCP layer Client and server: external MCP ingestion plus hermes mcp serve Plugin/MCP injection for OMC tools and team workflow Six first-party MCP families: state, memory, code-intel, trace, wiki, Hermes coordination 3-tier MCP: built-in runtime-injected, .mcp.json, and skill-embedded per-session MCPs
Memory/state Persistent memory, session DB/profile state, threat-scanned prompt snapshots, skill memory .omc/ project/session/shared memory, notepad/wiki/state tools .omx/ state, memory, trace, wiki, ultragoal/team artifacts .omo task/session/recovery state, todo preservation, session search/read tools
Sandbox/exec Local, Docker, SSH, Modal, Daytona, Singularity; approval and code-execution limits Host permissions plus hook/security controls; no OS-level sandbox boundary found Codex sandbox/approval plus OMX workflow gates and worktree isolation Host-dependent sandbox; plugin permissions/write guards and Codex Light config mutation
App/server surface Messaging gateways, API server, web dashboard, ACP server, MCP server MCP, CLI, hooks, HUD, tmux workers, OpenClaw webhook-style forwarding CLI/API sidecars, HUD, tmux, MCP, Codex plugin bundle OpenCode server attach/start helpers, OpenClaw, Team Mode, web/docs package
Web/search External APIs, xAI X search, browser automation, CDP paths Delegated to Claude Code web tools and optional MCP providers Mostly repo/wiki/provider helper surfaces; no independent general web-search runtime observed Ultimate built-in websearch/context7/grep_app MCPs; Codex Light excludes most web/search MCPs
Peer level vs installed CLIs Peer-level source runtime Not standalone peer; strong Claude Code harness Not standalone peer; strong Codex control plane Not standalone peer; Ultimate is a strong OpenCode harness, Light is reduced

Interpretation

Hermes belongs in a standalone-runtime comparison. OMC, OMX, and OMO belong in a hosted-harness comparison. The practical user-facing capability of the hosted harnesses can be high, but it is the combined host-plus-harness stack that reaches that level, not the harness alone.

Installed CLI (binary) matrix

Read beside 015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md and 00_reverse_evidence_policy.md. These targets have no full source tree in-repo â€” practical RE only.

Prefix Pinned install RE hub Trace index (2026-06-27)
ay /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy 1.0.12 15_ay_reverse.md 31, 32, 22, 38
cr cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533 15_cr_reverse.md 37, 39, 42, 29, 25 (May baseline)
gr grok 0.2.67 15_gr_reverse.md 23, 40, 26â€“28, 30

015_reverse-engineering/20_synthetic_mcp_config_trace.md

Synthetic MCP Config Trace

This pass used disposable temp HOME/workspace directories and dummy /bin/echo MCP commands. It did not start real MCP servers, use credentials, call model sessions, or read private user config.

Cursor Agent

Synthetic files:

  • temp HOME: <tmp>/home/.cursor/mcp.json
  • temp workspace: <tmp>/ws/.cursor/mcp.json

Config shape used:

{"mcpServers":{"homeDummy":{"command":"/bin/echo","args":["home"]}}}

Findings:

Condition Result
temp workspace .cursor/mcp.json only, non-git directory not discovered
temp HOME ~/.cursor/mcp.json homeDummy: not loaded (needs approval)
temp workspace after git init workspaceDummy: not loaded (needs approval)

Implications:

  • Cursor Agent's synthetic trace supports mcpServers as the config root.
  • User-level ~/.cursor/mcp.json is discovered with temp HOME.
  • Project .cursor/mcp.json discovery appears tied to workspace/git detection in this trace.
  • Servers are not auto-loaded; they require approval.

Follow-up runtime trace:

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md used a project-scoped .cursor/mcp.json plus a synthetic stdio MCP server. cursor-agent mcp enable trace wrote an approval entry under temp HOME at .cursor/projects/<project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json; the observed entry was trace-0bab3cead8c72734.

mcp list-tools trace then crossed the pre-approval gate and launched the minimal hand-written server, but it timed out without sending any JSON-RPC initialize frame to that fixture.

A second fixture using @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything succeeded: cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything listed 14 tools. This confirms approval enforcement, project-state write behavior, and standalone no-account stdio MCP tool enumeration.

Grok Build

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/grok mcp add dummy --command /bin/echo --args dummy
  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/grok mcp list --json

Findings:

Surface Result
Add target MCP server 'dummy' saved to ~/.grok/config.toml
JSON list entry includes name, command, args, enabled
Enabled default enabled: true

This confirms that grok mcp add writes user config under temp HOME and that mcp list --json is machine-readable.

Follow-up doctor trace:

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md configured @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything directly in temp ~/.grok/config.toml. grok mcp doctor everything --json reported command found, server started, handshake OK with protocol 2025-06-18, and 13 tools discovered.

Kiro CLI

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name dummy --scope workspace --command /bin/echo --args dummy --disabled --force
  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp list
  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp status --name dummy

Findings:

Surface Result
Write target <tmp>/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json
Listed under default agent bucket kiro_default, despite --scope workspace
Status scope Scope: default, Agent: kiro_default
Disabled flag Disabled: true
Default timeout 120000 ms

Implications:

  • Kiro's CLI writes a workspace config file for the trace.
  • In this run, workspace-scoped server display/status resolved under the default agent bucket.
  • Disabled servers are listed and status-inspectable without launching the command.
  • A later enabled official-server status trace in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md confirmed JSON-array args for @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything, displayed Disabled: false, but still did not report protocol handshake or tool discovery.

AGY CLI

AGY has no public mcp command group in the installed CLI help. No synthetic MCP config trace was run for AGY in this pass. Its MCP evidence remains binary/cache evidence plus public plugin import surface.

Remaining Gaps

  • Cursor MCP project approval behavior and standalone no-account tools/list with an official stdio server are tested in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md.
  • Grok native TOML MCP startup, handshake, and tool discovery are tested in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md.
  • OAuth flows were not tested.
  • Kiro agent-specific MCP routing with a custom synthetic agent is tested in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md; actual server startup and protocol handshake remain separate gaps.
  • AGY MCP activation requires either hidden agentapi knowledge, plugin import, or model/session trace.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 015_reverse-engineering/ for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

ay /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy, /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli Go/native strings, protobuf trajectory/cascade names, local cache/config metadata, helper binaries

Reading Rule For Existing Docs

When a 00~14 page says â€œى—†ى‌Œâ€‌ or â€œى§€ى›گي•کى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œâ€‌, interpret it as â€œinstalled helpى—گى„œ ë…¸ى¶œëگکى§€ ى•ٹى‌Œâ€‌ unless this 15_reverse-engineering axis confirms absence from bundle/binary evidence too.

When a page states runtime semantics from config/cache observation, this axis upgrades or downgrades the claim:

  • bundle-key or binary-string confirms a module exists.
  • runtime-trace is still required for exact precedence, scheduling, isolation, merge, and permission behavior.

015_reverse-engineering/35_harness_source_matrix.md

Harness Source Matrix

This matrix covers source repositories, not installed binary reverse targets. It should be read beside /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness_synthesis_addendum.md.

Evidence Class

Target Evidence class Current boundary
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/hermes-agent Source code, README, package metadata, existing dimensional shards Full source architecture observed; installed/runtime credentialed trace not performed in this pass
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/oh-my-claudecode Source code, Claude plugin manifest, npm metadata, hooks/MCP/team runtime files Host-dependent on Claude Code and Claude Agent SDK
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/oh-my-codex Source code, npm metadata, Rust workspace, Codex plugin manifest, first-party MCP specs Host-dependent on Codex CLI
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/oh-my-openagent Source code, npm metadata, OpenCode plugin entry, Codex Light plugin manifest, docs Host-dependent on OpenCode for Ultimate; Codex Light intentionally reduced
/Users/jun/Developer/codex/121_openai-codex/codex-rs Full Rust workspace (113 crates), Cargo.toml members, core/ session loop Peer-level source runtime; see 000_overview/00_co_crate_graph.md

Codex (co) â€” codex-rs quick matrix

Axis Primary crates Deep doc
Tool runtime core/src/tools/router.rs, tools/, apply-patch 003_tool-runtime/03_co_tool.md
MCP rmcp-client, codex-mcp, mcp-server 009_mcp-orchestration/09_co_mcp.md
Sandbox sandboxing, execpolicy, linux-sandbox 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_co_sandbox.md
App surface app-server*, cli, tui 006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_co_surface.md
Extensions ext/extension-api, ext/goal, ext/guardian 000_overview/00_co_crate_graph.md

Runtime and Capability Matrix

Object Hermes Agent (hermes) Oh My ClaudeCode (omc) Oh My Codex (omx) Oh My OpenAgent (omo)
Runtime ownership Full standalone Python agent loop plus Python CLI, React/Ink TUI, gateway, ACP, MCP, and web surfaces No standalone primary LLM loop; Claude Code plugin/harness with companion npm CLI No standalone primary LLM loop; Codex control plane and setup/runtime wrapper No standalone primary loop in main path; OpenCode plugin plus Codex Light adapter
Tool layer Own tool registry for web, terminal, files, browser, memory, skills, delegation, cron, messaging, Computer Use, and plugin tools Adds t MCP, team runtime, LSP/AST/Python/state/memory/wiki tools around Claude Code Adds first-party MCP servers, CLI parity commands, code-intel/wiki/trace/state tools around Codex Large plugin tool registry: LSP, grep, AST-grep, session/background tools, Team Mode tools, hashline edits
Skill layer Native self-improving skills and skill manager under Hermes profile state Claude-loaded bundled skills plus OMC learned/project memory patterns Codex skills/prompts/agents installed by setup and plugin bundle OpenCode skills plus shared skills and Codex Light components
Agent/subagent layer Real child AIAgent delegation with isolated context, restricted tools, batch parallelism, and backend routing Role agents plus Claude Code Task/tmux/worktree team runtime Codex-native agents, workflow roles, tmux/event-sourced team workers 11-agent Ultimate roster, background agents, Team Mode, and per-agent restrictions; Light omits orchestration
MCP layer Client and server: external MCP ingestion plus hermes mcp serve Plugin/MCP injection for OMC tools and team workflow Six first-party MCP families: state, memory, code-intel, trace, wiki, Hermes coordination 3-tier MCP: built-in runtime-injected, .mcp.json, and skill-embedded per-session MCPs
Memory/state Persistent memory, session DB/profile state, threat-scanned prompt snapshots, skill memory .omc/ project/session/shared memory, notepad/wiki/state tools .omx/ state, memory, trace, wiki, ultragoal/team artifacts .omo task/session/recovery state, todo preservation, session search/read tools
Sandbox/exec Local, Docker, SSH, Modal, Daytona, Singularity; approval and code-execution limits Host permissions plus hook/security controls; no OS-level sandbox boundary found Codex sandbox/approval plus OMX workflow gates and worktree isolation Host-dependent sandbox; plugin permissions/write guards and Codex Light config mutation
App/server surface Messaging gateways, API server, web dashboard, ACP server, MCP server MCP, CLI, hooks, HUD, tmux workers, OpenClaw webhook-style forwarding CLI/API sidecars, HUD, tmux, MCP, Codex plugin bundle OpenCode server attach/start helpers, OpenClaw, Team Mode, web/docs package
Web/search External APIs, xAI X search, browser automation, CDP paths Delegated to Claude Code web tools and optional MCP providers Mostly repo/wiki/provider helper surfaces; no independent general web-search runtime observed Ultimate built-in websearch/context7/grep_app MCPs; Codex Light excludes most web/search MCPs
Peer level vs installed CLIs Peer-level source runtime Not standalone peer; strong Claude Code harness Not standalone peer; strong Codex control plane Not standalone peer; Ultimate is a strong OpenCode harness, Light is reduced

Interpretation

Hermes belongs in a standalone-runtime comparison. OMC, OMX, and OMO belong in a hosted-harness comparison. The practical user-facing capability of the hosted harnesses can be high, but it is the combined host-plus-harness stack that reaches that level, not the harness alone.

Installed CLI (binary) matrix

Read beside 015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md and 00_reverse_evidence_policy.md. These targets have no full source tree in-repo â€” practical RE only.

Prefix Pinned install RE hub Trace index (2026-06-27)

cr cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533 15_cr_reverse.md 37, 39, 42, 29, 25 (May baseline)

015_reverse-engineering/20_synthetic_mcp_config_trace.md

Synthetic MCP Config Trace

This pass used disposable temp HOME/workspace directories and dummy /bin/echo MCP commands. It did not start real MCP servers, use credentials, call model sessions, or read private user config.

Cursor Agent

Synthetic files:

  • temp HOME: <tmp>/home/.cursor/mcp.json
  • temp workspace: <tmp>/ws/.cursor/mcp.json

Config shape used:

{"mcpServers":{"homeDummy":{"command":"/bin/echo","args":["home"]}}}

Findings:

Condition Result
temp workspace .cursor/mcp.json only, non-git directory not discovered
temp HOME ~/.cursor/mcp.json homeDummy: not loaded (needs approval)
temp workspace after git init workspaceDummy: not loaded (needs approval)

Implications:

  • Cursor Agent's synthetic trace supports mcpServers as the config root.
  • User-level ~/.cursor/mcp.json is discovered with temp HOME.
  • Project .cursor/mcp.json discovery appears tied to workspace/git detection in this trace.
  • Servers are not auto-loaded; they require approval.

Follow-up runtime trace:

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md used a project-scoped .cursor/mcp.json plus a synthetic stdio MCP server. cursor-agent mcp enable trace wrote an approval entry under temp HOME at .cursor/projects/<project-slug>/mcp-approvals.json; the observed entry was trace-0bab3cead8c72734.

mcp list-tools trace then crossed the pre-approval gate and launched the minimal hand-written server, but it timed out without sending any JSON-RPC initialize frame to that fixture.

A second fixture using @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything succeeded: cursor-agent mcp list-tools everything listed 14 tools. This confirms approval enforcement, project-state write behavior, and standalone no-account stdio MCP tool enumeration.

Grok Build

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/grok mcp add dummy --command /bin/echo --args dummy
  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/grok mcp list --json

Findings:

Surface Result
Add target MCP server 'dummy' saved to ~/.grok/config.toml
JSON list entry includes name, command, args, enabled
Enabled default enabled: true

This confirms that grok mcp add writes user config under temp HOME and that mcp list --json is machine-readable.

Follow-up doctor trace:

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md configured @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything directly in temp ~/.grok/config.toml. grok mcp doctor everything --json reported command found, server started, handshake OK with protocol 2025-06-18, and 13 tools discovered.

Kiro CLI

Command:

  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp add --name dummy --scope workspace --command /bin/echo --args dummy --disabled --force
  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp list
  • HOME=<tmp>/home /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli-chat mcp status --name dummy

Findings:

Surface Result
Write target <tmp>/ws/.kiro/settings/mcp.json
Listed under default agent bucket kiro_default, despite --scope workspace
Status scope Scope: default, Agent: kiro_default
Disabled flag Disabled: true
Default timeout 120000 ms

Implications:

  • Kiro's CLI writes a workspace config file for the trace.
  • In this run, workspace-scoped server display/status resolved under the default agent bucket.
  • Disabled servers are listed and status-inspectable without launching the command.
  • A later enabled official-server status trace in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md confirmed JSON-array args for @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything, displayed Disabled: false, but still did not report protocol handshake or tool discovery.

AGY CLI

AGY has no public mcp command group in the installed CLI help. No synthetic MCP config trace was run for AGY in this pass. Its MCP evidence remains binary/cache evidence plus public plugin import surface.

Remaining Gaps

  • Cursor MCP project approval behavior and standalone no-account tools/list with an official stdio server are tested in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/29_cr_mcp_handshake_trace.md.
  • Grok native TOML MCP startup, handshake, and tool discovery are tested in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/30_grok_mcp_doctor_trace.md.
  • OAuth flows were not tested.
  • Kiro agent-specific MCP routing with a custom synthetic agent is tested in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/24_ki_agent_mcp_trace.md; actual server startup and protocol handshake remain separate gaps.
  • AGY MCP activation requires either hidden agentapi knowledge, plugin import, or model/session trace.
33
